A little salt, vinegar, calcium, magnesium and potassium (NOT a complete list of ingredients - but no artificial flavors or colors) - NO sugar.  It is a greeny yellow - but before you freak out - it is colored with turmeric!  Hip hip Hoorary!  When you want a little salty instead of sicky sweet.  Honestly.  Don't be afraid - these things are dynamite!  I'm gonna definitely buy them again.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  Only down side to these is the plastic tubing going to landfill.  I'm thinking - spicy dill pickle flavor would be good... In case they are listening...

title: jelly belly buttered popcorn 1 oz packs
text: These are the PERFECT portion control.  Because if I opened a 30 oz bag of these - I WOULD eat them.  Yummy.  You get 30, 1 oz bags.  They are fresh and tasty.  I would totally order this way again.  I really prefer these to one big bag.  If I could rate 10 stars, I would.

title: Dark chocolate covered cherries
text: These are good - but a few are dry-ish.  They arrived in good shape with a tiny lunch box side cooler pack, that was fully thawed and warm when it arrived - however - the cherries were not melted - so worked some.  I don't live in a desert area so that probably helped too. The dark chocolate is shiny - and quite thick.  The cherries inside are dried - sometimes a bit too dry in my opinion.  I would probably purchase again, but honestly the bag is huge - so it will take me a long time to get through it.

title: organic evaporated coconut milk
text: I have purchased this brand/product elsewhere but recently couldn't find it - so purchased here.  I use this in soups, coffee, dips, gravies etc.  NOT fat free - but tasty and an excellent choice for lactose free options. You get a case of 12.  I am sharing 1/2 with my sister.

title: Biscotti bros vanilla biscotti
text: These are crunchy and tasty vanilla but maybe too sweet for my likes.  They come off as more of a sweet cookie.  But if you're into that - you'll love these!

title: zatarains crab boil 6 oz bags
text: We do an annual seafood boil and I always purchased these - because the seasoning is all nicely contained in mesh like bags.  Way tasty - way less messy to clean up.  Won't do the boil without them!

title: Bachans Japanese YUZU bbq sauce
text: I recently saw a cooking segiment on Good Morning America featuring this brand and immediately went to purchase all three - Original, Yuzu and hot and spicy.  If you have not tried this brand and like the japanese bbq style sauce (better than teriyaki) you must order one each of these for yourself - just once and taste.  You can use as marinade, dip or drizzle.  And that is the solid truth!  I tried it expecting good, but was blown away by exceptional.  No, I am NOT on their payroll - but would be proud to be!  I am hooked.  Will be purchasing this brand only when I need an asian style sauce.  Period.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  I never accept any discounts that are not available to ALL shoppers.  My reviews reflect my own personal experience with each product I review - fully and honestly without exception.

title: Trader joes chili lime seasoning
text: I love a good chili lime seasoning.  Trader joes brand, while not my #1 is certainly my second on the list. It's bright and full of chili flavor.  So good on many things.  I use it frequently.  It's worth a try if you have not tried this brand in my opinion.  Time to check dates in your seasoning cupboard and freshen it up!

title: Bachans Original Japanese bbq sauce
text: I purchased all three flavors after seeing a cooking demo on Good Morning America.  This stuff is SOOOO worth the effort to get it - of course, not available locally to me in my tiny rural town.  It's tasty WITHOUT being overly salty as most of these type sauces are.  I purchased the original, Yuzu and the hot and spicy.  I have to say I probably love the hot and spicy the best - but ALL are exceptionally good.  You can use as marinade, dip or drizzle!  The dispenser bottle is a total bonus.  Refrigerate after opening.  I am sold and will certainly buy again.  I just made a batch of wings using the hot and spicy - adding a little crushed red chilis - as fast as we ate them, I may have to find a way to get this stuff in on auto ship - which I never do! I say, try them all once - see what YOU think.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  I never accept any discounts that are not available to ALL shoppers.  My reviews reflect my own personal experience with each product I review - fully and honestly without exception.

title: McCormick organic chili powder
text: Seasonings don't last forever and I needed to replace a bunch of mine.  For some reason couldn't get the organic locally, so I ordered.  It's tastes great - just as chili powder should.  Time to go through your seasoning cupboard and pitch the stuff more than 1 year past date!

title: duncan hines cake kit
text: I call this duncan hines cake kit, lazy cake.  It's just the right size and you have what you need to make it - short of adding the egg.  My personal all time cake favorite is yellow or white cake with chocolate frosting so this fits the bill.  Do I use this kit and eat regularly? No its purely a treat once in a very long time - but easy when I want a treat.

title: Simply organic crushed red pepper
text: Tasty, bright and peppery.  Hmm.  That seems like exactly what you'd hope.  Just the right size- not too big so they expire and retire flavor before you can get to it.

title: Bachans hot and spicy japanese barbecue sauce
text: Bachans hot and spicy japanese barbecue sauce - I saw this in on cooking segment on Good Morning America.  Oh boy.  This stuff is AWESOME.  Can be used as a marinade, dip or topping.  It's not SUPER spicy - but flavor is amazing.  Sometimes these sauces can be salty - this is the perfect balance of salt and flavor.  I purchased the other two flavors after trying this one.  Not available locally for me, but I plan on keeping it on hand because it's so tasty!

title: Planters sweet and spicy dry roasted peanuts
text: Planters sweet and spicy dry roasted peanuts.  The 'sweet' part is true, it's undefined sweet.  The spicy is less so - and NO flavor - just heat. I love heat WITH flavor - so it was overall - very disappointing to say the least. I am not sure what I was expecting, but overall - better taste and flavor.  I would have liked a recognizable sweetness (honey? toffee? - not just nondescript sweetness - heat WITH flavor (jalapeno, chili, even black pepper?) Now, having said all of that - everyone is different.  I can only review my personal experience and this is it.

title: birch benders cup o treats
text: Husband is diabetic and watching carbs.  I purchased some cake in a cup treats for grandsons and bought these for hubby.  Hubby LOVED these.  Said he would totally buy these again. 3 tablespoons water or milk, stir and microwave for 1 min.  Quick treats

title: Batchelors cup a soup creamy leek and potato
text: I like to have a cup of soup every so often and the reviews were so positive on the taste of this one, creamy potato leek.  I have to agree.  It's not grainy at all, very creamy and tasty.  You get 6 boxes of 4.  Delicious.  This order is going to last me for some time, but I would buy it again.  Yum, fast and easy.

title: mini micro popcorn
text: Overall, I like to pop my own corn.  However, these are super handy when the time comes - grandsons with friends and a movie.  Everyone gets their own and keeps their hands to themselves.  Mostly.  Tasty.  Convenient.  Homemade better - but this will do.  Not overly buttery tasting.

title: lemony dill
text: Smells just like you would imagine, plus a little herby.  Tastes good - not bitter.

title: Lemon salt
text: Smelled and tasted good.  I am adding some dried dill weed to make a lemon dill salt.  Just disappointed it is a plastic container - had hoped for glass - better for our environment.  That said - the lemon salt works.

title: Pickled sweet tiny beets
text: Jake and Amos pickled sweet tiny beets!  Ooooo YUMMMMMY.  These are so good and perfect sized, all smaller than a golf ball - most half of that. They are delicious - I am a pickled beet lover though.  I plan to use the juice to pickle some eggs when I'm done.  I also want to add that they are in glass jars.  I was nervous to order - but don't be - they were SERIOUSLY well packed and arrived beautifully.  I am going to order some more before everyone finds these!  Wish they were available locally - you know - like 2 blocks away so I could just go knock on the door.  If you love pickled beets - don't miss these.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Spice World ground ginger
text: I put ginger in about everything I got tired of grating and squeezing.  I accidentally didn't purchase the organic version - but I'll get over it.  It's an easy use squeeze bottle.  Easy Peasy.  Tastes great - recipes, marinades, tea - hot or iced.

title: Ginger People organic ginger juice
text: Yum.  I use this in soups, marinades and even tea - hot or iced.  It's really delicious.  Of course you can buy raw ginger, grate it (no need to peel) and squeeze the juice into whatever you're making - but this is so easy. Can't purchase locally, so I end up ordering it.

title: Del Monte canned french style green beans 50% less sodium
text: Because of an intestinal issue I suffer, once in a while I have to eat mushy stuff.  I honestly don't do canned anything unless I have to.  These are french cut - they are soft but still have slight body.  Sometimes I heat, something I toss with a tiny bit of salad dressing - usually a vinaigrette.  Works.  And purchasing this way (box of 12) is better pricing than purchase locally.  win/win.

title: Le Sueur Premium whole green beans
text: Due to an intestinal issue, every once in awhile I need to have mushy canned stuff and I miss veggies, so this was a sort of compromise.  Honestly, I don't do canned anything unless I have to.  These are good.  They are soft because they are canned, but do have some body.  Taste good.  A stem happens every once in a while.  Purchasing this way (box of 12) is actually more cost effective than local purchase.

title: Pacific foods chicken bone broth
text: I use this a lot in recipes and to drink.  Ordering this way (box of 12) it's actually a better price than current local prices.  It's a rich broth, extra protein (bone broth). Pacific foods is my personal preference for taste.

title: Tuscanini organic tomato paste tube
text: Oooo.. Organic tomato paste.  Yum.  You get 3 tubes.  It is very rich and tasty.  Love and will purchase again.  Great expiration dates, over a year.  I think a really good price compared to local options.

title: Simple red vines
text: Yum.  "Simple' Red vines are so good.  No artificial colors or flavors - real cane sugar.  Are they healthy? No.  It's still sugar, but you get to pass on red dye #5 and other artificial crap.  These are harder to get locally when I am looking for a treat,  so I order them.  I used to sneak these into the movie theater  - Shhhh.  I just hate artificial stuff.  If you like red vines, try these.  They are an upgrade!

title: Cadbury Dairy Milk fruit & Nut Chocolate Bar Single bar
text: I am a huge fan of this bar.  Surprisingly good.  I usually purchase the larger, flatter bar.  These are good, but not quite as good as the larger, flatter bars because the flatter bars seem to have more fruit and nuts.  These are smaller, 'single' bars are thicker chocolate but why less fruit and nuts which are key to these chocolate bars. Maybe it's just that the flatter chocolate makes the fruit and nuts stand out? Not sure. In the first 'single' bar I opened I had 2 pieces of raisins (what happened the whole fruit?) and about 3 chips of almond in a 6" chocolate bar. Eh. I am going to go back to the flatter ones.  Just my personal opinion.  The other disappointment is that expiration date on this box is 10/2022.  It's a box of 48.  Should have had at least a year.

title: red vines mini bites
text: SUPER disappointed when I received these to see they will expire 8/15/22.  I ordered mid June.  Seriously?  Geez.  Should have at least 6 mos.  They sorta taste stale now.  If I had been in the store, I would not have purchased these - due to the expiration date.  In a thicker plastic bag with zipper like closure. I do like that they are the "simple" red vines which means no artificial colors/flavors, cane sugar, wheat flour - radish for color - natural flavors.  Come on guys - these shouldn't have been shipped.

title: Lipton cup of cream of chicken soup
text: This is one of my fav flavors.  Can't get locally.  You get 12 boxes - way too many for me, so I shared with family and friends.  Win/Win.  Tastes good, not super healthy - but I look at it as a change/treat.

title: Simply Asia Japanese inspired Ramen Soy Ginger Chicken broth
text: I love this broth.  I use in recipes and just heat and eat it.  The great news is that it was actually cheaper to purchase this way than locally and delivered to my door.  You get 6 boxes of broth. If you haven't tried it, it's worthy of a try. In my personal opinion that it tastes predominately soy chicken, slight ginger.  It's just a great base for lots of recipes.  When I heat and eat it, I usually add a little ginger juice and some chopped green onions.  My order arrived quickly (I have prime) and in great condition along with other items I ordered. The expiration date is about a year out. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: kirkland signature organic chocolate banana almond drink
text: This is the best protein drink I've tried.  Nothing like artificial - sweeteners included! Hard to find.  It tastes really good and is plant based.  I've tried so many.  This is just the right size - 120 for each drink.  I have intestinal issues and need to have liquid diet and this so perfect.  Can't get this thru the famous big box store via delivery, so I purchase thru Amazon. If you're looking for a truly healthy liquid drink, this is definitely one to try.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: fgo organic hibiscus tea
text: Fgo organic hibiscus tea.  Recommends 5-7 minutes to steep.  Each bag is round and not wrapped.  They are inside of a lined paper bag, ziplock like.  The tea smells floral.  After trying 3 different brands today, this one smells the most like a hibiscus.  It makes a deep, ruby red cup.  It is sharp, tangy taste with a straw like after taste in my opinion.  Surprising to me.  Of all the different brands I've tried lately - this one is probably the best of the unknowns.  It might be good iced. However, I prefer the made in Hawaii - Hibiscus Bliss the best of all.  I probably will not purchase this one again as I am not a fan of the round bags without a string and not individually wrapped.

title: decaf earl gray
text: I love earl grey- it's my fav.  I can't have caffeine so these are decaf.  However, I was bummed to find these are round bags, open - not individually wrapped.  So, you'll need a spoon to retrieve the bag (not a huge deal - but I really prefer a string and tag).  The taste of this brand is very bergamot forward - which for me is perfect.  I probably won't purchased these again because they are all just open rounds in a lined, ziplock like paper bag.  Eh.

title: Tealyra hibiscus tea
text: First, it says to steep for 3-5 mins.  It produces a rich, deep red color tea.  However, the taste is like straw.  A slight tang that tends to the sour.  The real disappointment to me was the fact that the tea bags contained finely ground product.  The picture led me to believe the would be whole flowers.  The product is in individual bags with string and tag.  Honestly, for the price I was hoping for whole flower and not a finely ground, near powder tea.  Sadly, I can't recommend it.

title: Organically Hip HIbiscus
text: I had high hopes for this tea - it has great color - but tastes like a very light straw- even after steeping for 4-6 minutes (!) as described on the box.  It lists on the box that it is organic and caffeine free.  The color was so rich, you would think it would equally rich in flavor.  Disappointing to say the least.  Sadly, I cannot recommend it.  It could have been just a bad box... but I don't know.  Expiration date 10/2023.  If you like a beautiful, deep red colored tea - but no flavor, this is it for you.

title: Watkins coconut extract
text: I've purchased this brand, Watkins, for years.  I have not ever been disappointed.  I purchased this to aid in making a fruity ice cream with a coconut flavor - but not shreds.  Perfect.  It's a big bottle and would last me a life time.

title: chocolate coated toasted coffee beans
text: Ooo. Such a treat.  And this bag is a golden with all the flavors.  White chocolate, milk and dark chocolate.  How can you go wrong.  Just glad I didn't have to buy a 10lb bag.  Just delightful little treats with a morning coffee.  I would definitely buy again.

title: ono seasoning
text: I tried this seasoning for the first time a few months ago and I'm back to get more.  I don't need the measuring spoons, but the set was a better deal than individual purchase.  I have a sensitive stomach to traditional 'season salts' but NO problem with these and they are soooo tasty on everything. I am a huge fan and will keep purchasing these. They have become my go to.  Highly recommend both!

title: dehydrated mini marshmallows
text: I fully realized these were going to be hard, little dehydrated mini marshmallows, but my jar arrived just a little more than HALF full.  They were tough, not crunchy or fresh.  Not sure they'd melt on hot chocolate. Very disappointing. These, went into the trash.  I can't recommend them.

title: Lite Spam
text: Ok.. it's tasty.  No really, it is!  But I always buy the 'lite' version - less fat AND salt instead of just one or the other.  Is it a health food? Nope.  But a really tasty, shelf-stable option for a treat.  Stirfry, musbi, fried as breakfast meat, or a sandwich. Hard to find the 'lite' version locally, so I usually order this way.  Came quickly (I have prime) and in great shape along with other items I ordered.

title: Peanut M&M candybars
text: For some reason the chocolate is sooooo good in these bars.  Peanuts are a bonus.  I actually purchased these for Smore's  I think they'll be perfect.  Be careful! They are addictive!

title: bean thread noodles
text: I purchased these to make Hawaiian chicken long rice soup and they were perfect in every way.  I like that they are wrapped in small bundles.  Easy to use. Tasted great (they pick up whatever you soak them in). Very happy - One package will last me a very long time - but I would buy these again.

title: Mild diced hatch chilis
text: In my opinion, these taste better than run of the mill green chilis.  Love them.  As this Hatch brand is hard to find locally, I end up ordering.  These had about a 1 year out expiration, which is great for me - I'll use them up way before then.  They are great in literally about everything.  Arrived in great shape and quickly (I have prime) with other things I had ordered.

title: hatch whole green chilis
text: I ordered  these because I love this brand and Hatch chilis.  A little bit of heat, but tolerable - just really tasty.  These have about a 1 year on the expiration, which is great for me - they won't last that long.  Not available locally.  Arrived in great shape with other stuff I ordered.  Will definitely order again!

title: whole hatch chilis
text: These are tasty - a little bit of heat - but so tasty.  The cans I received had at least a year out on the expiration and arrived in great shape.  I am very happy as these are impossible to find locally.

title: rocher collection
text: Sent this to my god daughter who lives in another state and is a HUGE Rocher fan.  Needless to say, she was thrilled.  So easy to send stuff this way.  Arrived in good condition along with other items I had ordered for her.

title: Lonolife chicken bone broth
text: I have said this before, but these are some of the best tasting chicken bone broths out there.  This particular kind is great for travel because it is dried and in convenient tubes.  All you need is some hot water and viole! It is expensive - but tasty and handy.

title: chocolate covered fruit sticks
text: Purchased for a friend who lives in another state and loves chocolate.  I love these myself.  They arrived quickly (I have prime) and in good condition along with other items I had ordered.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: campbells tomato soup
text: I sent this to a friend in a different state who needs some pantry help every now and then - campbells tomato soup is one of her favs, so this was a no brainer.  Arrived quickly (I have prime) and in good condition along with other items I had ordered for her.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Mini candy bars
text: Sent to a friend in a different state that needed a bit of chocolate.  Arrived as schedule and in good condition along with some other items I had ordered for her.

title: Happy belly hollandaise mix
text: You get 12 packages - which is probably WAY more than I'll ever use - but it's handy.  Each pack makes about a cup of hollandaise - which is a lot.  I like it.  I'll share most of the box with others and keep 2 or 3 pouches for myself - I don't use it that often.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: green giant canned corn
text: Purchased for a friend who lives in another state and needs a little pantry help from time to time.  She loves canned corn.  Win/win for ease of delivery and ability to send about any shelf stable item I can think of.

title: canned corn
text: I purchased for a friend who needs a little help with pantry items on occasion - these fit the bill.  Was a little trouble with the initial order but seller resent immediately.

title: Lonolife beef and chicken broth
text: This is some of the best instant bone broth out there in my opinion - taste and convenience.  These little tubes are great for travel and easy to use whenever you can get your hands on some boiling water.  Mmmmm.  These were sent to my sister who was suffer covid at the time.  She loved them.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: colvaita organic diced tomatoes
text: I really prefer to buy my tomatoes in these containers or glass - never metal cans.  These are tasty and useful in just about anything.  Love the organic part, of course.  The expiration date is about 1 year out.  Love these.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: sweet heat rib rub
text: Great taste... a little more heat than sweet - I usually add a bit of brown sugar to the mix when using.  Also, I use it on chicken.  Would be 5 stars but the pricing is CRAZY too high.  I can't get this all the time locally, but it's about 1/2 the price when I can.  GRRRR!  But I use it enough that occasionally I have to pay for it this way. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Jeff's natural diced tamed jalapeno peppers
text: These are glass jarred, diced tamed jalapenos.  For whatever reason I can't get them locally - just the tamed rings.  I prefer the diced for most use - just a personal preference.  These taste very good, and just FYI - they 'say' tamed, but they have a pretty good punch of heat.  More than any other brand I've tried.  I love the flavor of jalapeno - but less heat.  These are pretty hot - but fabulous flavor!  They arrived in good shape and quickly (I have prime) along with a few other things I had ordered.  I would definitely buy them again.  They are NOT organic - but 'natural' whatever that means.  Would be great if they would go with organic.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Chick O Stick, bite size
text: I used to love these so much - but these were not quite as crispy as they should have been.  They taste was ok, but stick to your teeth a little too much (they are usually a little sticky).  They are just a tiny bit stale.  With this candy a little makes a bit difference.  They are individually wrapped.

title: Ono hawaiian season salt
text: It's a good season salt.  Says 'natural flavor' which means MSG - if you're sensitive.  I like the chunky grind.  I am not sure it's better than any other 'season salt'... but it works!

title: Ono season salt, spicy
text: It's a season salt, but not very spicy.  Good flavor.  And like the chunky consistency of the salt grind.

title: pineapple tea
text: Purchased as gifts in a Hawaiian gift bag  - perfect.  Comes in a cello wrapped box.  Know it will be well received.

title: Hamakua brand mac nuts chili peppa
text: I love this brand - reasonably priced.  Good sized nuts. And tasty.  The chili peppah is a little disappointing because it's not really spicy or flavorful but - that is my personal opinion.  I love all the other flavors - step out and try something you wouldn't ordinarily.  This is a good brand to try with.

title: gift mac nuts
text: Price is crazy - but I needed for gift bags.  Eh.  Mauna loa is good brand - for flavor and quality in the past.  I have noticed that the nuts have gotten really, really small. Disappointing.  If you have to have them, I guess. Otherwise shop the warehouse store for the best price.

title: mini tacos hard shells
text: Wow!  These are great!  I ordered with some other stuff and they arrived in good shape, all whole and very tasty.  I love a hard shell and love the small size. Especially when I have my grandsons. They're perfect!!  I wish they were available locally, but I live small and rural - so shipping is my only choice.  I was worried after reading all the reviews of broken shells, but I am happy to report mine were whole.  I would say they are worth a try.  I plan to order more and will update my review should new info seem useful!

title: marantha coconut almond butter
text:
1. The coconut flavor is non-existent. MAYBE a tiny hint of the smell of coconut - but it is faint. The taste is even less
2. The expiration date on the jar I received is Sept 2022. Eh. I will end up tossing at least 1/2 of it by then. Was hoping for at least a year. I just don't eat a ton of nut butter - but used to purchasing stuff with at least a year to expire date.
3. As almond butter goes, in my opinion, this is pretty smooth - refrigerate after opening. Not a lot of oil on top. I store my jars upside down in the fridge to make spreading less oily.
4. Glass jar. This is a positive. I try very hard not to purchase plastic jars.
I'm going to continue looking for a coconut almond butter that has a definite coconut essence and taste. And certainly a longer expiration date.
Arrived a little late, but very well package with brown paper waffle wrap so that's an A+. Having said all of these things I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: NOT 100% Kona coffee as advertised
text:
1. Shame on me for not reading the previously posted reviews!
2. NOT KONA COFFEE. it's a blend of anything they'd like
3. Tastes like old regular can coffee
4. Really bummed as I purchased as gifts.
*1 note.. each bag came individually boxed and cello sealed. Like you would like your really delicious 100% KONA coffee to be delivered. Too bad this stuff is junk
5. NO LABEL listing "blend" - one single sticker stating "Aloha gold" yeah. Right.
Grrrrrrrrrrrrr. Always read reviews! After 30 years you'd think I'd have learned.
Cannot recommend this purchase to literally anyone. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Domino super fine sugar
text: I purchase these to use with just about everything - the super fine sugar in this dispenser is so handy.  I don't use a lot of sugar so these 3 containers will last me about 2 or 3 years.  I also purchase the brown sugar variety.  I used to purchase a different brand but they are currently twice (literally for the same size and quantity) the price - so I'm good with Domino.

title: Highkey cookies
text: I started with the snickerdoodle and chocolate chip cookies, then came these nilla waffers My husband is a type 2 diabetic and watching his carbs in addition to medication.  He loves these.  Each cookie is about 1 carb - makes them easy to count.  They are tasty according to him. My sister is a type 2 diabetic too and she loves them as well. Taste good, easy to count the carb load. Pretty much covers it. I never hesitate to update my review if new info seems useful.

title: Ready to eat miracle noodles
text: My husband is a type 2 diabetic and loves these.  This was the first time he'd tried the ready to eat and loved them. The other kind involves lots of rinsing and some people are sensitive to the smell.  Although I will say that the Miracle noodle brand is the least offensive of all in my opinion.

title: Cosmos salted caramel puffed corn
text: I love popcorn.. but not the husks.  I don't usually like caramel corn, but I like this.  And no husks.  Not really popcorn, more like a sweet puffed cheese ball - without the cheese.  It's a huge bag.  I'll probably not get through it.  But it's still good.  If I were to buy again, I'd probably look for a smaller bag.

title: 505 flame roasted green chilis
text: I have used this brand for years.  They are flavorful and spicy.  Medium is our go to.  Hard to get locally, so I am glad to get it here.  3, 16 oz bottles.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: tiny but mighty popcorn
text: I like this popcorn with the smaller kernels.  They don't pop as full as a larger kernel but they are tasty.  Some people think they are easier for people with intestinal issues to digest, might be so - and I do have various issues.  I just like the small popcorn and the taste.

title: Swiss coffee columbian kcups
text: It's really hard to find a good, rich flavored decaf.  I read that the swiss water method for decaf is the only true decaf - therefore I purchased these. It is a rich, full flavor cup!  They are kcup style, for use in single cup makers, plastic bodies with foil tops. There are 18 cups per box, you get 4 boxes - which makes the cost per cup reasonable in my opinion.  This is very hard for me to find locally - so I'm happy to find it here, delivered to my door! Further, there was a slight problem with one of the boxes, the seller responded immediately and was very forthcoming. I will order again. Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Jelly Belly buttered popcorn
text: Oh boy.  I will totally admit I am addicted to this flavor.  Yum.  Jelly Belly brand beans are the perfect smaller size.  The flavors are full and bright.  And this bag arrived nice and fresh.  I'll always buy this flavor.  Harder to find locally, thus the order. A little more spendy this way - but if you like them as much as I do -

title: Pink grapefruit Jelly Belly
text: Very pink grapefruity!  One thing I love about Jelly Belly brand is that they are a small bean.  These taste and smell just like a juicy pink grapefruit.  Love.

title: Jelly belly lemon lime
text: I will be honest, I was hoping these were lime, not lemon lime - but they're citrusy and good.  Fresh and tasty. Jelly bellies are such a nice, small bean.

title: jelly belly citrus mix
text: Oh. These are delightful.  Citrus as you expect, lime, lemon, grapefruit, orange.  Just really good.  I love that jelly bellies are very small. They arrived quickly (I have prime that usually works) and they are fresh. Yum.  I will definitely buy these again.

title: monkfruit sugar free maple syrup
text: Purchased 2 bottles of this, one for my sister and one for my husband - both are diabetic.  I am always on the look for things sweetened with monkfruit or stevia.  They both report it is good.  I would purchase it again.

title: handcrafted caramels
text: Purchased these for a holiday family party - they were loved - buttery, caramel flavor.  A nice size container.  Wax wrapped individual pieces.  I would purchase them again.

title: liquid chocolate covered cherries
text: These are my 83 year old mom's favorite - so these went with her christmas gifts.  They are pure liquid middles - none of that white chalky yucky stuff.  It's not a huge box - so just  perfect for a treat.

title: Himalayan pink salt
text: I purchased this bag to go with a battery operated salt and pepper mill set.  Perfect.  Works as it should in the mill.  Would work with a manual mill too.

title: Happy belly dried fruit trail mix
text: I purchased this a gift for family christmas game and my 83 year old mother chose it!  It is delicious.  I will purchase it again - but it will be a while - it's a huge bag!

title: Sweet and spicy trail mix
text: Purchased this for the holiday family gathering and was raved about.  The perfect blend of sweet and spicy.  A nice big bag.  Would buy again.

title: Duke's original beef sausages
text: I purchased these a gift in our family christmas game and the person who received them was thrilled.  He likes to sip fine whiskey and was really impressed with these for ingredients and flavor.  He said he would purchase these again.  They are packed in bags.  Having said this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Stevia candies
text: I purchased the grape and orange for my husband who is diabetic and like a little sweet once in a while.  They come in really nice metal tins.  He loves the flavors.  They are sweetened with stevia which is one of the very few non sugar sweeteners that are still considered safe.  If you are watching your sugar for whatever reason, you may want to try these.

title: Dr. Johns sugar free drops
text: We have a several diabetics in the family and this was a family christmas bingo prize.  Was well received as all are familiar with this brand.  It's kinda hard to get these days and took a really long time to arrive - not sure why.  But according to our family experts, worth it.

title: Dash waffle maker recipe book
text: I purchased 3 dash waffle makers and 3 cook dash cook books (separate purchases) for a family christmas bingo gift selection room.  I think the cookbooks make the perfect compliment to the waffle makers.  Sometimes you just need to be inspired and while most of us use the internet to look up anything any more, I still like to hold a cookbook - spoken like a true Boomer.  :)  There are lots of various recipes and pics.  If you are giving a Dash waffle maker away - I think the cookbook is lovely compliment.

title: King Leo soft peppermint sticks
text: I was a tad mislead by the 'soft' part - they are not really soft.  They are hard.  They sort of melt something like a buttermint (?) but are not soft.  They are very sweet with a little peppermint and red dye #40.  Eh.  That is a bummer.  They used pure cane sugar, pure peppermint oil and then went artificial with the dye. Product of Mexico for any of you who track that stuff.  1 stick is 40 calories and 10 net carbs.  In my personal opinion they need less sugar and more peppermint and of course, natural dye.  The box I received says 'best by 030624'  I will probably toss these because of the red dye (makes me want to rate 1 star) - sigh - should have looked for ingredient list before purchase.  The did arrive fairly quickly (I have prime that sometimes works) and in great condition - not a single broken stick - considering they are hard - that's a win. Could be good to stir a hot chocolate with - IF they would use a natural dye.  Cute box with 22 individually wrapped sticks.

title: fruit cake stick
text: I actually purchased this as kind of a gag gift  - you know - there's only 1 fruitcake in the world and we just all pass it around - But I have to say that this fruit cake looks SOOO good that if no one chooses it from the prize pile (we play family bingo on christmas eve) I plan on opening it and eating it!  It looks moist and the perfect blend of fruit, nuts and cake!  No joke!  Having said all of that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Raspberry filled candies
text: I purchased these to slip into my Mom's (83 years) christmas gift box.  She loves these and I always try to get one bag each Christmas.  Always a favorite.

title: reeds roll candy
text: These are a blast from my childhood - each candy in the roll is individually wrapped and the flavors bring back great memories... I love the root beer the most, but each one is really a winner on it's own. The candy I received was fresh and delicious! Very happy.

title: Faux swedish fish
text: These are trader Joe's version of Swedish fish... they should NOT be considered so.  These (Trader Joe brand) are super soft- nothing like the real Swedish fish.  I have left the bag open to hopefully kill a little of the softness - then there is the flavor... EH.  The orange fish are, in my personal opinion, are the only ones worth eating. The others sort of all kind of taste the same... maybe vaguely different on the red lobsters.  Listen, these are gummy candies - but oddly super soft.  If you are NOT hoping for real Swedish fish of the old days, you will be disappointed - as I was.  If you are a lover of all candies that are gummy - you'll probably be ok.  They shapes are interesting... some of the colors are interesting - and some are recognizable. While I LOVE Trader Joe's - I will not be purchasing these again.

title: black pepper corns
text: Purchased these to go with battery operated salt and pepper grinders I intend to give as a gift - these pepper corns came in a resealable plastic bag.  I can smell them through the bag - they smell rich and peppery - and it's a huge bag.  If you used a LOT of fresh ground pepper every day it would take a LONG time to get thru this bag!

title: Dark M & M's
text: Super hard to find locally - easy to ship via Amazon!  It's sort of a life time supply - but I share a LOT!  These are milk chocolate m & m's - elevated.  Rich and deep chocolate flavor.

title: Beer Creek soup mix Tortilla
text: I purchased this for a friend that needed help filling her pantry with shelf stable food.  She and her family love Bear Creek soup mixes - she added various things like chopped tomatoes, extra proteins, chopped onions - etc.  They are easy to make and delicious as her family reports to me.

title: Good Sense Organic roasted and salted pumpkin seed
text: I purchased three different brands of pumpkin seeds (also known as pepitas) and I will say that this brand is clearly my hands down favorite.  It's the perfect blend of salt and seeds.  They have been perfectly roasted.  They are organic and msg free - however if allergies are an issue, they are packaged at a plant that processes peanuts, tree nuts, milk wheat, soy and egg.  6oz bag.  The ingredients listed: Organic pepitas, organic sunflower oil and salt.  Packaged in the USA - the packaging does not actually indicate where the pepitas come from though - but they are roasted, seasoned and produced in adherence to USDA standards.  Expiration on my bag: 9/08/22 - they'll never last that long.  Heck, they won't last out the week!  They are the clear winners in our household. Pepitas are good sources of plant based protein, zinc, iron and magnesium. I'll be ordering more soon.  I don't want to be without them!  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Superseed pepitas
text: These are packaged in 4 oz bags with re-closable tops.  You get 6 bags.  They are labeled as premium european pumpkin seeds also known as pepitas.  They are roasted with pink himalayan salt.  Noted on the bag they are produced in America. No msg, sesame or soy.  I am used to the little browner pepitas - these are very interesting.  I included a photo because they are so dark green. I bought 3 different brands.  These are the only green ones.  The seeds are larger, flatter and taste a little earthy in my opinion.  Not super salty.  All in all they are not my personal favorite, but still, an interesting pumpkin seed.

title: kitchen basics chicken bone broth
text: This is the best commercially produced chicken bone broth I've tried to date.  Nothing artificial and terrific taste.  In fact, I might try to set in on automatic delivery it's that good.

title: maple bourbon syrup
text: I purchased this as a gift and they report it devine.  They even like in their coffee  (?) Hmm.  Nice glass jar.  A really great gift.

title: Bourbon and maple candies
text: I purchased these as a gift and they were well received and as I understand too tasty to share!  Ha!  Win/win

title: hard maple candy

title: Butter rum lifesavers
text: Purchased these to share at my Mom's adult living apartment complex for trick or treat.  They were a huge hit with the older crew.  I open one roll and tasted, it was not stale and more delicious than I remembered.  A sweet, buttered rum flavor.  If you haven't tried it - and like a hot buttered rum - you'll love these!

title: organic prunes

title: powdered milk

title: Lemon curd
text: I purchased this to top my vanilla yogurt and it is delicious.  Lemony and a little sweet - but not overly.  Great creamy texture.  It's a win for me!  Glass jar and metal twist lid. title: strawberry and jalapeno jam
text: Man.  They should call this JALAPENO jam with a tiny bit of strawberries.  I like spicy - but this is a bridge too far for me. I would have probably gone with it if there was any strawberry flavor at all.  Now, having said that - there ARE strawberries in there.  But the jalapenos totally take over your taste buds and you can't taste the strawberries!  It was an interesting try but a miss for my tastes.

title: maple pumpkin butter

I'll use it on something, but probably not something I would purchase again.  Nice glass jar and metal twist lid.

title: Organic Maple cream

title: Maple cotton candy
text: First, the  sizing is great.  It's a small. 80 calories in total - nearly all sugar as you would expect. The containers are small plastic cups - but they are NOT easy to open - nail breakers at the least and then edges are very sharp - so don't let kids open them without supervision. Not nearly as good as freshly made - but I didn't expect that.  However, the maple flavor?  Not even close.  It vaguely smells like maple - but it is so faint I don't count that.  Zero maple flavor.  It is a creamy ivory color.  If I didn't know what flavor it was - I would not have been able to guess via scent and flavor.  As a person who really appreciates really great cotton candy, I was excited to try this.  I won't buy it again, but it was a really good try.  I think if you really love cotton candy, it's best fresh.

title: Italian black licorice wheels
text: These taste great... a little stiff and chewy - but I personally like that. The rounds/wheels are about 2" or so in diameter.  Fun to peel.  The only down side for me is the sorbitol.  I wish they remove that.  Then they'd been nearly perfect. No artificial flavors.  All in all, I like them.  They'd be 5 star is they left out the sorbitol.

title: Pacific Foods organic tomato bisque
text: This is the best tasting tomato bisque I have ever had - personal opinion of course.  It's rich and thick - but too thick.  It's hearty as it notes on the label so it doesn't really need anything.  It does contain milk if that is an issue for you.  However you could add some tofu if you'd like for a bit more protein or even your choice of beans. You could add some kale or spinach for a slightly healthier option.  I am not vegan or vegetarian -but like those options. I have a hard time getting this locally so I am happy to have it delivered to my house.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: organic kidney beans
text: These are AWESOME.  Low sodium, in boxes (NOT CANS) - and they are delicious!  I also like the box and not the can.  The beans are organic.  Honestly, there is just nothing to say negative.  I will reorder these as I need them.  I also purchased the pinto beans.  I recently read that eating 3 servings of beans a week can reduce your chance of mental decline by 38%.  Worth a shot - there 's NO risk to eating beans 3x per week!  I work them in to everything/anything several times a week. You get 8 boxes in your order.  If you're on the fence, these are worth a try! Having said all of that,  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Tate's cookies
text: If you like crispy cookies, these are your cookie heaven.  I love the oatmeal/raisin (traditionally like the chewy version on this cookie - until these) and mac/white chocolate chip the best so far.  But honestly, I like them all. Trust me, you will too.  I also appreciate a list of fairly good ingredients and little to no ugly chems. But lets be real - cookies, of any kind, are NOT health food.  They are treats.  So pick what you love the best AND the best you can buy.  Just my opinion.

title: Apres protein drinks
text: I purchased the multi flavor pack to see which flavor(s) I prefer.  So far I like the coffee flavor the best.  My least favorite is the chocolate mint.  They all have a hard to describe bitter aftertaste. My husband is more sensitive to it than I am but I do taste it.  He thinks it has a slightly gritty texture - I do not.  Everyone like something different.  I actually need a protein boost on occasion and I appreciate that these are not stuffed with a bunch of added vitamins and such I don't need. Further, I am appreciating plant based proteins more and more.  Overall I like them.  I do recommend that you refrigerate them before trying and definitely shake very well for at least 30 seconds before opening.  I would purchase again.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Double Donut pumpkin spice kcups
text: You get 4 boxes of these - so LOTS of kcups and they work in your kcup brewer.  But that's where the positive ends.  You can smell the pumpkin spice faintly - but when brewed it tasted like - watery pumpkin spice! I've never tasted any coffee like it. (FYI - these are DECAF - but that doesn't mean NO coffee)  I actually opened the kcup to see if there were coffee grounds in the cup.  There is some brown stuff that looks like coffee? Not sure.  You should be able to smell or taste coffee period.  No coffee flavor at all - as I said, just watery faint pumpkin spice flavor.  What a huge disappointment.  I landed all 4 in the recycle bin.

title: Shitake mushrooms
text: I purchased 2 different types of dried shitakes and was most hopeful of this one because they were so beautiful. However- they are nearly flavorless after rehydration. Going for the darker ones I had purchased that came in a jar for storage

title: Thinsters Toasted Coconut
text:
1. Don't open the bag and leave it open near you - you wont be able to stop
2. They are crispy and delicious - just the perfect coconut taste with little toasty coconut bits
3. Don't open the bag and leave it open near you
4. They are about 1 1/2" rounds
5. One the best things, the ingredients list contains ONLY things you can pronounce - Coconut, wheat flour, sugar, butter, brown sugar and very small amounts of (less than 2%) - whey, baking soda, eggs, salt, soy, lectithin. PERIOD.
6. Don't open the bag and leave it open within arm's reach

This style (in the tub and not the tubes) is great for portion to taste control.  The tubes are better for travel.

title: Simply Heinz
text: I didn't realize that I could still get this at our local big box store - set of three just like this for $8.99.  Yeah.  I am gonna send these directly back without even opening the box.  What a rip off.  I had thought that Heinz had quite making the simply version (no artificial ingredients - no high fructose corn syrup) - but I guess they are just behind in supply.  This is a huge burn on the price - even when you figure cost to ship.  I would pass.

title: chicken tortilla bone broth soup
text: Woo hoo!  This is SUPER SUPER spicy!  Put your chaps on, you're gonna need em!  My husband drinks it straight... WAY too much heat for me.  BUT - it might be really good when making pork carnitas or pastor!  It's delicious in flavor - but be prepared for the heat!  I wish I could find this locally for a more reasonable price -

title: swanson sipping bone broth
text: I use a lot of bone broth and I wanted to try these.  I generally buy the powdered versions for less packaging.  This was stretch for me because of the packaging, however it is delicious.  Tastes like simmered all day long chicken bone broth.

title: sea salt edamame
text: These are salty and crunchy delights.  My husband is diabetic and loves these as night time treat.  Good protein, not a lot of fat and some carbs - but it's plant carbs - not white refined flour or other refined carb. I purchased them on prime day for a good deal so I could try them and now I'm hooked.  I do think they are worthy of a try if you'd like a fairly healthy salty crunchy  snack!  The packs easily fit in a purse or backpack or even in your car glove box.

title: Epic xylitol chewing gum
text: I prefer xylitol chewing gum and I like these in particular because 1 piece is enough to chew at a time.  The peppermint flavor is exceptional in my opinion and last a reasonable few minutes - maybe 10? I keep a pack near my nightstand to chew just before bed - not instead of brushing but keeps me from eating something after I have brushed!  Having said that - I see my family has nearly gone thru this box so I'd better order more! Flat pack is easy to put in your purse and keep one in the car too.  So much better for you than aspartame that is in nearly all gums these days.

title: True lemon lemonade
text: VERY sweet - but it's mostly stevia so that's good.  I usually add real lemon juice to cut the sweet a bit without weakening the flavor.  I do the same with the limeade.

title: Alessi thin breadsticks
text: I just can't get the thin breadsticks locally and I like them - so I ordered them.  I was worried that they would be all broken.  Some where, but mostly good.  They come in separate packages containing a few sticks in each bag.  Fresh and crispy.  I will buy them again.

title: sugar wafer cookies
text: I usually purchase these from costco, but they have been out for a while and my grandsons are coming!  They arrived quickly (I have prime) and in great shape.  Mmmmm... crispy and creamy.  My daughter says they taste like a vanilla cupcake.  So true.  Yummy treat.

title: Sea salt and vinegar pistachios
text: Oooo.  These are the bomb!  Tangy, salty (but low in sodium per serving) and the pistachios are fresh too.  I am addicted to these now.  8 individual packages - each package is 2.5 servings.  Love these.  Try them once and you'll love them too.

title: fruity mentos
text: Love these fruity mentos - these were a reasonable price and delivered to me.  However, they were a bit on the hard side - no so fresh.  Still I eat them.

title: mini moos
text: My son in law likes BOAT loads of cream in his coffee... so I purchased this huge box for their 3 weeks stay.  I'm gonna guess he uses the whole box.  Lots of chemicals.  I would so like it if it were just cream - but then it would be harder to make it shelf stable.

title: killer bee BBQ rub
text: I nice, large, 1 pound bag.  It is an interesting flavor.  A little bbq, very slight sweet tones and salty.  I first tried on chicken wings.  I think it will be tasty on chicken and pork - I would not like on beef - but just my personal preference.

title: True juice powders
text: I really only wanted to try the watermelon flavor.  Just a quick note - there is a tiny tad of sugar, but mostly stevia.  The flavors are great - however - beware - they are super super sweet!  A little goes a long way.

title: true limeade
text: This is really tasty - but it is SUPER sweet.  So a little goes a long way.  The flavor is terrific though!  A tiny bit of sugar, mostly stevia.  Perfect.

title: stir free almond butter
text: I purchased 2, one for me and one for my mom.  Mom loves peanuts and peanut butter but they give her migraines.  I have long been an almond butter fan.  To me, it's like an "upgrade" from peanut butter - but that's just my opinion.  This brand is nice and smooth.  Super smooth for a nut butter.  While it says "no stir" a little stir is still needed.  And while refrigeration is not "required" it will last longer if you put it in the fridge.  No added salt or sugar.  Now.  If I can convince my Mom almond butter IS an upgrade, win/win!  Almond is great on ANY thing you would use peanut butter on.  Almonds are tree nuts and therefore supposed to be better for us when eating nuts - or so they say.

title: Walkers shortbread rounds
text: There is no better shortbread in my opinion.  Nothing artificial, very few ingredients.  Just fine, delicious flavor.  Rich and satisfying with tea.  Just the right texture and melt.  Hard for me to get locally (tiny coastal town), so Amazon allows me to enjoy delivery right to my door.

title: wickles pickle chips
text: I purchased these because I LOVE the wickles relish... but it's so finely chopped it's like a relish paste.  So, I am coarsely chopping the pickle chips and mixing it with the relish paste.  Viole'!  Delicious chunkier relish. If you haven't tried Wickles and you like a little bite with your sweet - Wickles is it.  I spread in on sandwiches, make a tarter sauce my family swears by and even created a bit of vinagrette with some crispy bacon bits.  Sooooo good.  This brand is not available to me locally (tiny coastal town) so Amazon is a great way to get them delivered.  The only issue is they were not very well wrapped ... I am VERY lucky they were not broken or seal popped the way the three bottles were thrown in together with a very thin bubble wrap - along with other items I had ordered in a super hap hazard way.  I'm still shocked the glass jars weren't broken.  Yikes.  All things considered, Wickles is soooo good, it's worth the try!

title: Tiny but mighty microwave popcorn
text: Tasty... but tiny??  Maybe just a little bit... but not much.  No hulls in your teeth... Eh. (Minus one star because the ad says you won't have hulls in your teeth)- You'll still need to floss afterwards.  I like that there isn't anything icky in it...  Doesn't tasty super buttery... but I'll trade that for non of the cancer causing agents.  I toss with a little jalapeno salt and grated parm cheese because that's how I like it.  I do have intestinal issues, but LOVE popcorn.  I did not notice that it was better with this popcorn than the regular, but this is a healthier option due to fewer chemicals. Sometimes you just have to eat what you like even if it might have be no fun later.. sigh...  I would recommend this as a tasty, safer (none of the bad stuff) microwave popcorn.  I had about exactly the same number of un-popped kernels as with any other microwave popcorn I've used - less than 2 tablespoons.  There was plenty of yummy popped corn.  I did notice the kernels are slightly smaller than "regular" popcorn kernels.  I will keep buying this because there's less of the bad stuff and I am a lover of easy popcorn and it tastes pretty good and it's easy.

title: Edible gold dust
text: I purchased to use on some two-bite hand pies (made with a won ton press) just as a little accent.  Worked just as I had hoped.  Even tho the jar is small - a tiny, tiny bit goes a REALLY long way.  I purchased some fine pastry brushes - that really helped..  AND you should know you can't mix with water - i used a tiny drop of vodka (because vodka is clear) and it worked like a dream.  You could use vanilla - or any alcohol based drop.  It was really fun and easy to use with the right brushes and information (mixing with the right medium to make the "paint")  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: wood cake topper
text: This is meant to be a cake topper, but I am incorporating into bridal shower decor and a gift for the bride.  It's small, and very looks homemade, which is it's charm - it's imperfections, are the charm.  I really like it.  Came much quicker than promised.  I was a little disappointed in the styro foam packing (so bad for the environment) but it arrived in perfect condition.  There are much better packing options - I hope this company will stop using the stryo foam asap. Other than that - is just cute.  It is pretty light weight, but stands solidly.

title: dark chocolate foil wrapped eggs
text: These TASTE like a fairly good dark chocolate - but they are HARD.  It's sort of weird.  They don't melt in your mouth really nicely.  They are so hard you can't bite them!  Now, eventually I got it to melt, sort of in my mouth.  I am using them more as decor - in a jar - for the jewel tone foil wrapping.  If I were actually serving them I might not.  Hmmm.

title: coconut peeps with dark chocolate
text: Wow.  These are just awful.  In fact, I checked the expiration date (12/2021) - The coconut flavor is barely noticeable - the dark chocolate has nearly NO flavor! How is that possible?  Further, they are oddly ... grainy...  so weird.  Left an unusual aftertaste.  Overall, just awful doesn't quite cover it.  This flavor was not available locally so I rolled the ridiculous price for Peeps to try because it sounded good.  Now, everyone likes something different and maybe I received a fluke "bad" set.  But in all honesty, I can't recommend these.

title: peeps on a stick
text: Made a mistake... thought I had ordered 4 sets - accidentally only ordered one.. but good thing.  Because I can get the very same thing at my local store for less than $3!  Holy cow! My bad for not checking and taking the easy road of to my door delivery!  They are very cute... quite large... I believe the ad notes 11" and they are that long at least.  They are surprisingly heavy.  I am going to put them in a jar at a wedding shower tea on Easter.  So they will be great.

title: Popped cheezy sorghum
text: I am intestinal issues that a lot of people have... and I love popcorn.  I have looked and looked at this sorghum but haven't tried until now.  I purchased three favorite separately - coconut oil & pink himilayan sea salt, extremely cheezy, and avocado oil and salt.  This review is about the cheezy version.  The cheezy has a little bit of a grainy, coating-your-mouth aftertaste that wasn't my favorite.  It also has quite a number of ingredients - the other flavors have only 3.  Now, for ALL three the thing to note is how super tiny the kernels are!  So tiny.  It's a little hard to eat because it is so tiny.  I am thinking about getting a spoon to eat it.  It's hard to "pinch" like you do with popcorn because they are so tiny!  1/2 cup is a serving... All the flavors have the same calories in that 1/2 cup - 60.  Honestly, it will be hard to eat just 1/2 a cup... but we'll see. The use by date on my bag - 02-22-22.  Sheesh.  It won't last a week here!  I am still on the fence about whether or not it will replace my love of popcorn, however, if it doesn't upset my stomach, it will win.  In all honesty, I tossed the cheezy... Eh.  The best flavor I've tried so far in my opinion is the avocado sea salt.- tastes the most like a lovely teeny tiny popped corn.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: avocado oil and sea salt popped sorghum
text: I have intestinal issues that a lot of people have... and I love popcorn.  I have looked and looked at this sorghum but haven't tried until now.  I purchased three favorite separately - coconut oil & pink himilayan sea salt, extremely cheezy, and avocado oil and salt.  This review is about the avocado oil and sea salt version. IN MY OPINION the avocado version tastes the most like actual tasty popcorn.  The cheezy has a little bit of a grainy aftertaste that wasn't my favorite. The coconut oil version is very, very faintly sweet  - MAYBE a teeny tiny tad of coconut?  It's ok. Now, for ALL three the thing to note is how super tiny the kernels are!  So tiny.  It's a little hard to eat because it is so tiny.  I am thinking about getting a spoon to eat it.  It's hard to "pinch" like you do with popcorn because they are so tiny!  1/2 cup is a serving... All the flavors have the same calories in that 1/2 cup - 60.  Honestly, it will be hard to eat just 1/2 a cup... but we'll see.  There are three ingredients... organic sorghum... avocado oil... and sea salt. Gotta love something processed with only 3 ingredients.  The use by date on my bag - 02-22-22.  Sheesh.  It won't last a week here!  And that's without artificial preservatives. I am still on the fence about whether or not it will replace my love of popcorn, however, if it doesn't upset my stomach, it will win.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: popped sorghum coconut oil and salt
text: I am intestinal issues that a lot of people have... and I love popcorn.  I have looked and looked at this sorghum but haven't tried until now.  I purchased three favorite separately - coconut oil & pink himilayan sea salt, extremely cheezy, and avocado oil and salt.  This review is about the coconut oil version.  It does have a tiny tad of a coconut flavor - although it is very, very faint.  Maybe very slight sweet overtones...  Of the three this flavor is #2 to me.  I like the avocado first and the cheezy last.  The reason being that IN MY OPINION the avocado version tastes the most like actual tasty popcorn.  The cheezy has a little bit of a grainy aftertaste that wasn't my favorite.  Now, for ALL three the thing to note is how super tiny the kernels are!  So tiny.  It's a little hard to eat because it is so tiny.  I am thinking about getting a spoon to eat it.  It's hard to "pinch" like you do with popcorn because they are so tiny!  1/2 cup is a serving... All the flavors have the same calories in that 1/2 cup - 60.  Honestly, it will be hard to eat just 1/2 a cup... but we'll see.  There are three ingredients... organic sorghum... organic coconut oil... and pink himayalan salt. Gotta love something processed with only 3 ingredients.  The use by date on my bag - 02-22-22.  Sheesh.  It won't last a week here!  And that's without artificial preservatives. I am still on the fence about whether or not it will replace my love of popcorn, however, if it doesn't upset my stomach, it will win.  It does taste like a delightful pop of teeny tiny popcorn. Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: grated ginger
text: If you like ginger with your sushi you have got to try this shredded version.  It's sooooo much easier to eat - the shreds are easy to pile on top with a lot (we are ginger addicts) or just a few strands.  Tastes EXACTLY like sliced ginger, just easier to bite and consume.  I also use this in my lemon ginger chicken bone broth that I make in my pressure cooker.  This particular order was a set of 12 small jars.  I share them through out my family - we use a lot.  The expiration date at 10/2022 - This order won't last through the end of this year, 2021 - so that's good.  The arrived quickly and in good shape, each wrapped with bubble wrap.  I just wish they would have been shipped standing up instead of on their sides - but so far I haven't noticed any losses.  You do want to refrigerate after the jars have been opened.  Maybe you don't want to start with an order of 12 jars, but I'm telling you, once you've tried the shreds, you won't go back.  Plus, Ginger people ginger doesn't have artificial dyes or aspartame.  Win/win!

title: Honolulu cookies
text: What can I say? Mmmmmmmm!  These are one of our favorites when we go to Maui and one of our first stops upon arriving.  In this age of covid, we haven't been able to go.  So I am thrilled to be able to get these.  The bags are huge and full of mini chocolate chip shortbread cookies.  Perfectly fresh and delicious.  To be honest, my favorite is the pineapple shortbread cookie - I order those directly from Honolulu cookie company and they are full size.  These are perfect with tea or maybe a bowl of ice cream or just when you need a little treat.  Love love love

title: Alcohol free "gin"
text: I am a huge real gin fan, but as I have aged, I really don't drink much (1 or 2 drinks in a year) and I was looking for something a little refreshing to add to the tonic and fresh lime - I love tonic and lime but is crying for gin!  This stuff is interesting and that's about as good as I can do.  It smells odd.. and tastes even stranger.  There is a pepper bite at the end.  Interesting but not realistic.  Further, in my personal opinion it tastes like chemicals, which are definitely in there.  Check the label.  I was happy it wasn't sweetened with aspartame or sucralose. But even that couldn't save it for me.  It also has a blue hue to it. Which I suppose is a shot at sapphire gin?  I am a Tanqueray 10 fan myself and it's clear - my preference.  So, at 30 bucks a bottle it's an investment - and everyone likes something different. And I think they really tried to come up with something that would work for gin here, but sadly for me, it's a NO GO. Two stars for effort, NO stars for taste. I can't think of a single way to to use it, so I dumped it. Ouch.  I personally will enjoy my 1 or 2 gin and tonics a year with real gin and pass on a more frequent shot of this stuff.  I did give it a good college (so to speak) try.

title: smoked paprika
text: A really subtle but tasty, smoky flavor.  If you haven't tried a "smoked" paprika - once you do, you may never go back.  It's like paprika, but upgraded.  In my opinion anyway.  Worthy of try in my opinion as well.  It is as you would expect - "smoky" but the sweetness of the paprika is a good match that roasted, smoky flavor.

title: Licorice allsorts
text: Delicious and nothing artificial.  Every once in a while an allsort just sounds really good.  The bag is really small.  But in my opinion tasty.

title: Bourbon infused coffee kcups
text: I am not really a bourbon fan - but the thought of roasted beans sitting in old bourbon barrels sounded - interesting?  First, when you open the shipping box you will be hit with the wafting smell of an old bourbon soaked barrel. It's a strong smell.  Then you open the actual coffee box and it's even stronger.  It's almost a varnish like smell. Hmm. Not sure. However, it's really an impressively complex aroma. Deep roasted coffee beans - aged boozy bourbon barrels with the earthy wood tones.  You have to appreciate the complexity in my opinion.  I brewed the first cup this morning.  The bourbon barrel aroma is still strong in the brewed coffee.  And you can certainly taste the overtone of bourbon in the coffee.  You can taste and smell the bourbon - and the barrel.  There are definitely wooden barrel over tones.  I think that people who are very big bourbon fans and love coffee are going to think it is delicious.  In the way that people who love beer brewing love the smell of hops.  Eh.  Not me.  Now, I didn't rate 4 stars because I didn't "like" the coffee.  I had to take one star off for the actual brewing cups.  I have 2 different single brew coffee makers.  BOTH brewers had a really hard time with the cup.  For some reason ( perhaps trying to keep the bourbon aroma a little more contained?) is really hard to lock in.  I removed/wasted one cup thinking it might be a 'bad' cup - but so far they all resist the brewer poke system.  The plastic holder may be too thick over all?? The sealing top is more a plastic vacuum seal than a foil top of most other cups. In any case, it is a struggle to get them securely locked in.  In my personal opinion this is not something I will probably order again unless it would be as a gift for a true bourbon and coffee fan.  The coffee smells and tastes just like it's been sitting in an old bourbon barrel.  And to me a bit too much like wood varnish.  It's a novelty really -not something I would drink every day. I ended up placing the boxes in a thick ziplock plastic bag to kinda control the aroma a bit in my kitchen and pantry.

title: Barrel of cheese balls
text: I purchased these as a bday gift for my 7 year old grandson... they are NOT a healthy treat, but one of his favorites.  He loves these mixed with his popcorn.  He was SO excited to see this giant barrel of cheese balls and immediately shared some with this 10 year old brother.  Random joy.

title: First flush Darjeeling tea leaves
text: On the up side, it is clearly leaves and flowers in the bag.  It is loose tea.  The down side is that I didn't really care for the taste or aroma of the tea when brewed.  It was a bit too straw like in my opinion.  Eh.  It is also a very light tea when brewed.  This is most likely because it is the "first flush."  It was worth a try, but I will not purchase again.

title: Chicken salt
text: I was watching a Chopped episode and this came up as one of the items in the basket.  It was described as so good that I had to find it.  It's an Australian/New Zealand thing - so Amazon is the only option I am aware of.  It is really, really good.  A kind of season salt that would be yummy in/on so many things.  It is not spicy.  In my opinion there is a heavier celery taste than other season salts I have used - I like that - but some may not.  It would be awesome on chicken as a sprinkle.  I am thinking maybe in a bloody mary?  I would like to try a little in my clam dip recipe.  Lots of options.  In my opinion it's good and really worth a try.

title: truffle salt
text: Oh my... I opened it and you could immediately smell the funk - in a good way.  The truffle scent is really tasty.  I can't wait to try it on some potatoes - maybe a steak.  It is really tasty - in my opinion.  If you're a truffle fan, this is really worth trying.

title: Loewensenf Extra Hot German Mustard
text: This is one of my husband's favorite mustards.  It's spicy and smooth - a brownish yellow.  It is also very hard to find locally, so we end up purchasing on Amazon.  It is a glass jar - but arrived in great shape along with other items we had purchased (we have Prime).  If you like a smooth, hot mustard - this is worth considering.

title: Taylors bagged darjeeling tea
text: This was a little bit of disappointment because of the stringless, attached teabags. 2 bags are attached - so you have to separate them.  Good luck.  They don't tear apart easily, therefore about 80% of them, you tear the bag - then it's a real mess.  The tea has been finely ground - NOT my favorite.  I prefer leaf tea.  It smells nice... tastes ok.  It's a darker blush.  But I end up double bagging - adding an outer bag with a string - so it becomes disappointing.  If I had a reusable/washable dunker I would use that.  I won't be purchasing this brand again.

title: super fine sugar
text: Ridiculous price... 11 oz - a little more than 1 1/2 cups per bag! I did read the size.. but was hoping it would hold a bit more.. I was having a hard time finding the super fine sugar locally.  It is very fine and worked well in my recipe - but only if you're desperate for it with the price - I just can't rate 5 stars because it's so ridiculous in price.

title: popcorn kits
text: I gave a friend of mine my commercial quality popcorn maker and so I thought these would be a fun gift. She has a lot of little kids and they are soooo excited to make popcorn and have movie nights this holiday season.  During this time of the virus it's fun to have things that "treats" to do as well.  I've found this brand pops well in the pan of the machine.  Usually need to make 2 or so at a time - (one bag at a time)

title: chocolates
text: I purchased this as a gift for a family that has a few "littles" (kids) as we call them along with some other tasty gifts.  The kids love the little Santas .  They are individually wrapped as Santa - foil.

title: Sriracha cashews
text: My husband loves sriracha - I thought these would be a nice addition to a snack basket I was putting together... was a bit disappointed at how small the bag was... might just put in his stocking instead.

title: Spangler Candy Canes
text: Spangler always makes the prettiest canes... Guilty pleasure.  Good news: they are using vegetable dye for color.  Now, if we could get them to just use cane sugar and remove the corn syrup - I'd be in the 5 star opinion!  They are delicious - but I love peppermint.  They are individually wrapped so perfect for tree decor or stocking adds.  Or.. .just to eat - I put in a glass vase and help myself!  Please just use the cane sugar - no corn syrup!

title: Caramel treats
text: Purchased as a holiday treat for my daughter and grandsons.  They love caramels and they report these are good.  Daughter is using them as "finish your online school - without complaint- and you get 2!"  Will be glad when they can go back to school - but these caramels are delicious treats according to my daughter and grandsons - soft and caramely.  Win/Win/Win!

title: hot and spicy seasoning set
text: I purchased this set for my husband who has to travel for business and loves spicy seasoning when cooking for himself.  His favorite is sriracha.. thought he'd like this set.  5 glass bottles.  Going into his stocking.

title: sriracha packets
text: I purchased these to put into a really big Sriracha mug I purchased as a gift.  Worked great for that.  The packets are all full and none broken.  This person takes Sriracha with him everywhere he goes and uses at most every meal, literally.  So  these packets are convenient.

title: Dr John's healthy sweets
text: I have been purchasing these candies for some time so I ordered these and was very, very surprised to receive a tiny package for the price I have been paying for double or triple the amount in the past.  My husband is diabetic and so is my sister and these creme cicle like discs are favorites.  The list different flavors.. but I have to say to me, they mostly all taste the same or very similar.  But they are sweet without an aftertaste - which is nice for those who cannot have the sugar treats they would like. I am just disappointed that they have become so very too expensive.

title: Cookies in tin
text: Sadly, I gifted these to my Mom, who due to the virus, is removed from us.  The good part is she says the cookies (such as they are) are tasty, the sad part - most were broken and crushed.  NOT good for gifting.  Really disappointing.

title: ghiradelli chocolate treats
text: I sent to my daughter and grandsons as a surprise holiday treat -  Who doesn't love Ghiradelli?  It is a favorite and the holiday flair of these is always welcome.

title: Reeses candy collection
text: I sent this box to my daughter and my grandsons along with some other holiday things... movies, etc... and my youngest (6 years) is having a really hard time eating only 1 of these per day.  He is a HUGE peanut butter fan.

title: 100 calorie Snickers
text: My son in law is a Snickers fan... but doesn't let himself snack a lot - so these 100 calorie size are the cats meow.  My daughter says he loves them.

title: Zebra popcorn in tin
text: Sent this tin along with some other flavors to my daughter and grandsons - and thru in some holiday movies on DVD... my daughter reports that my youngest grandson (6 years) cannot stop eating the zebra popcorn.  She reports that it is surprisingly good.  Hooray!  Win/Win!

title: Popcorn tin
text: I purchased this tin, a different flavor and some christmas DVDs and sent them to my daughter and grandsons.  My daughter reports the popcorn was surprisingly tasty and they are enjoying the holiday joy of it!

title: fig jam
text: This fig jam is really good.  Sweet, but not overly so and crunchy - perfect with so many different things.  Comes the perfect glass jar.  If you like figs and jam... you'll love it - in my opinion.

title: pumpkin spice bread mix
text: Geez.  This is soooo hard to get this locally and it is my favorite.  The bummer is the price.  :(  But at least it's the big box.

title: Fall festive loaf pans
text: These are the perfect touch to give as gifts.  Reusable pans.  Win/win.

title: krusteaz pumpkin spice bread mix
text: Be aware that you purchasing a set of three, SMALL boxes and $7.65 each - double the store price.  My bad.  I thought I was purchasing the large boxes.

title: c&h pourable sugars
text: I started purchasing these years ago.  I don't use much sugar so it takes a long time to use these.  The convenience is just unbeatable.  I purchase the superfine white (quick dissolve - great for making syrups and other mixes).  It's really handy.  If you don't use a lot of refined sugar, these are great.

title: 365 whipped cream
text: As you would expect, easy to use and tasty.  Maybe a bit spendy but once in a while a little whip on top of your coffee is just a treat.

title: organic pears
text: They were pretty hard when I got them - so I set them next to my bananas to ripen just a bit.  They were a bit musty tasting.

title: tropicana oj cartons
text: My 9 year old grandson is a huge fan.  Each carton comes with a straw - easy to poke.  Just the right the amount.  Needs to be refridgerated.

title: Pink salt crackers
text: I gave these crackers 5 stars because they are crackers... but... they are NOT my favs.  They are a bit salty (good) - but a bit tough.  The flavor is not really all that great either.  They might have been better if they were a bit thinner?  I guess I am not a huge multigrain fan - they were all broken as well - supposed to be a 4" or so long cracker... all broken so the longest piece I had was 1".  Doesn't really effect the flavor - but - just in case you were going to try and serve them.

title: smoked oysters with chili
text: My husband who really loves spicy - loves these.  He does recommend that while he loves spicy hot stuff - take care to avoid eating the pepper that is in the can and looks like an oyster.  It's just too hot.

title: Creminelli prosciutto
text: Mmmmm... so delicious and salty.  I am in love with this prosciutto - great with some fig jam or some fresh cheese.  Real treat.  Life is short - try this stuff!

title: Cannonball green olives
text: I am a fan of larger size green olives.  These are firm, salty and delicious.  Glass jar.

title: 365 kalamata pitted olives
text: I love kalamta pitted olives and these are great.  Not mushy and nicely salty.

title: organic sweet onion
text: These are great for a quick cook and add into something.  They are sweeter after cooking.

title: Maille course mustard
text: I love a course, seedy mustard on a meat and cheese board.  These one is perfect - snappy and spicy.  You won't be disappointed.

title: frozen peas and carrots
text: I love peas and carrots and these are good.  The peas are mature size (not baby or new peas).  Easy to add into any recipe, make a salad or heat and eat which is usually how I eat them.

title: artichokes quarters
text: I like these - but sometimes they get the tougher outer layers.  Eh.  They are still good - marinated.  Tasty.

title: herb goat cheese
