text: I love love love goat cheese - it's tangy and this log is a creamy spread with a little herb outer.  It's simply delicious.

title: Boursin herb spread
text: My daughter loves this cheese so when I make a cheese/meat board, I add this for her.  It's herby and creamy - spreads beautifully on a cracker or toast.

title: Organic freezer pops
text: These are "no sugar added" and purchased them for my husband because he loves pops - but is diabetic.  These are NOT no sugar - but you can count them in your diet.  I like the no artificial flavors/colors.  Husband likes the flavors.

title: lactose free 1/2 and 1/2
text: This was such a terrific find.  Love love love.  That rich creamy flavor - without the lactose that can be frightfully bad for me.

title: russet potato
text: These were tasty... but I wish they didn't have any green on the skin - because you can't eat the green skin (unless you like gut pain and diarrhea)...

title: organic honeycrisp apples
text: Mmmmmm!  It's the right season for apples and my grandsons just love honeycrisp.  These were delicious.  Crisp and sweet.  Like a Fuji.. but I think the flesh is a bit crisper - in my opinion.

title: baby bell mushrooms
text: These are soooo thick and rich.  Holds up to a butter saute.  Already sliced - so super easy peasy.

title: organic peaches
text: Remained hard, never ripened  - ultimately molded.  Eh.  Not the right season either.  Best to buy any fruit "in season"

title: organic grapes
text: Washed them.  Cut into bunches.  Put into the fridge.  Two days later I went to pull out a bunch - all moldy!  Yuck.  I've bought lots of organic grapes.  I have never had this happen before - so I can only think it's a variety thing?

title: Organic Valley chocolate milk
text: My 6 year old prefers the other brand ultra pasteurized chocolate milk... but he will drink this one.  It's chocolately and smooth.

title: organic carrots
text: A bit wilted.  But I scrubbed them up - cut them and added a little water and put them in the fridge.  They crisped up a bit and tasted ok.  Nothing like your own homegrown carrots - but ok.  They come with tops.

title: fage fat free greek yogurt
text: This is my go to plain greek yogurt.  I use it whenever sour cream is called for.  My family has never figured it out.  YUM

title: sriracha sky valley brand
text: My husband prefers this sriracha - as he likes the slight chipotle (smoky) flavor.  If you like sriracha - this is definitely worth a try.  I just can't find it locally and he uses about 1 bottle every 1 1/2- 2 weeks.  So it pays to buy in bulk.  Arrived in good time in great shape along with other items I had ordered.

title: baby bella mushrooms
text: Delicious... meaty... full flavor.  They are firmer than the usual white mushrooms and hold up to a butter saute nicely. Already sliced, so super easy.

title: Lindt truffles
text: I purchased these to put into a "thank you" bag for our flight attendants.  I usually bring Moonstruck chocolate bars.. but I couldn't get my hands on any.  These may not be as local or as "la tee dah" but they are still well received.

title: ellenos marionberry
text: Oh boy. This yogurt is the best yogurt I have even eaten.  It's rich, thick and delicious ( yep, sugar and it's NOT fat free) but geez.  If you're gonna eat a yogurt it should be the best.  I love the marionberry and the lemon curd.  Now I'm addicted.  Fair warning!

title: organic step 4 rib eye
text: I had just ordered one of these the week before.  It was delicious and tender.  I then ordered these 2.  Really disappointing.  First, I ordered delivery - so I didn't select them.  So... I got one steak that was about 2 1/2 - 3" thick and the other was about 1 1/2"...  The thicker one was full of gristle.  They were both remarkably tough and chewy - even though they were cooked to a near perfect medium rare - as the one was the week prior.  I am thinking it's better to purchase in person so you can at least get 2 steaks of the same thickness.  Eh.

title: maple syrup
text: This syrup is rich and delicious - as you would expect 100% pure dark maple syrup to be.  It's a bit spendy, but yummy.

title: mini oranges
text: delicious and sweet.  Easy to peel.  This bag held really, really small oranges.  But they are yummy

title: ellenos greek yogurt
text: OMGosh.  This is the BEST greek yogurt out there.  Yeah... it's fat and sugar... but the taste!  The lemon curd and marionberry flavor are my personal favs.  If you're a greek yogurt fan, you cannot miss these - of course... fair warning - once you try them, you'll have a hard time going back to any other kind.  I just wish they were available more places.

title: sushi shrimp roll
text: now, this is not sushi restaurant good.. but it is edible.  One of my husbands favs from Whole Foods.

title: Tobys blue cheese
text: I actually purchased the blue cheese and the ranch.... I liked them both.  Not strictly traditional in taste... but good!  Thick and creamy!  Refrigerated - which, to me, tastes better than shelf dressing.

title: croissants
text: They are a bit dry and not that great in flavor - not buttery enough for me (of course that's my personal opinion)... was hoping for something a bit fresher - but you really need to go to a french bakery to get that.

title: Tobys ranch
text: I purchased Tobys ranch and blue cheese to try...  sounded good on the label.  It's pretty good.  Not strictly traditional in flavor.. but I like it.  It's thick and creamy.  If you like ranch, or blue cheese, they are worth a try.

title: 365 columbian kcup
text: I have had better Colombian flavor coffee - but these work.

title: root vegetable fries
text: Yikes.  I really wanted to like these... I LOVE root veggies... but I usually pan roast them... "fries" is an interesting concept - but I wasn't really a fan of these.  A little "earthy" in taste - mostly the beets anyway...and a bit tough.  Just my opinion.

title: kikka spicy sauce
text: Husband likes spicy with his sushi... he usually uses sriracha..  but he liked this too.

title: Belgian Waffles
text: When these arrived, I didn't realize they should be frozen... they were very soft and thawed when they arrived... so I put them in the fridge.  We didn't try them for a couple days.  They were sorta tough and not very tasty in my personal opinion. Not much in flavor really. Eh.  So. If you purchase these, pop them in the freezer until you're ready to use them.

title: avocado sushi
text: I usually try to get one of these with my seafood sushi choice from Whole Foods... just for a different bite.  The avocado is one of my favorites - creamy and yummy.  If you like avocado, you'll like it!

title: Sun Valley Sriracha
text: Husband considers himself to be an official sriracha expert... ha... This brand is his favorite for heat and flavor.  He describes as sriracha with a touch of a smoky chipotle note - subtle.  With all the heat you want.  He goes through about a bottle a week.  Sheesh.  Brother was here and so he is now a fan so I had to order TWO of these boxes - you get 6 bottles.  One for my brother and one for my husband.  There you go.  If you like sriracha - I would try to pick a single if you can find it locally (we get that brand from Whole Foods when they have it).. and then see if you need to step up to major stock.  Stay safe and well!

title: spitz dill pickle sunflower seeds
text: I love sunflower seeds in the shell - and these are definitely one of tops fav flavors.  It's just a touch of dill, not overwhelming, salty-  tasty.  Can't get this flavor locally - so I pay a bit more because they are shipped to me.  But it's worth it to me.  It's a 1 lb bag.  So for me about 6 servings.  :)

title: toasted coconut almond butter
text: It is a little soupy - even after you mix it... but it's perfect for adding to a bowl of oatmeal.. swirl on toast.. or just on a spoon.. I was worried the coconut would be tough but it tastes delicious - like toasted coconut... not tough.  It is a more grainy almond butter - not chunky - but you can see and taste the grade.  It comes in a very sturdy glass jar... does not need to refridgerated- according to the label.  Hint:  I used a really sturdy chopstick to do the stir.  No drip down the side of the jar.  I like it.  And would definitely buy again.

title: coconut marshmallows
text: My mom finds comfort in marshmallows. .. the toasted coconut version is her favorite.  She's 81.  This is a great treat for her in this time of pandemic and quarantine.  You get 2 bags - but they are small.

title: Hi Chew mixed bag
text: Yum.  As you would expect Hi Chews to be.  A lovely mix of flavors - grape (my personal fav), green apple, strawberry.  They are super chewy - a tiny bit sour - but not a lot.  The grape has a slight concord grape flavor to me.  My grandfather used to grow concord grapes and I can taste that in the grape flavored chews.  The green apple definitely tastes like apple - maybe not so green (my opinion) and the strawberry is the least like the real fruit in taste (again, my personal opinion).  Oh! Wait! Then there's mango.... omg... IT is the worst... tastes like I imagine sweat tastes like. So bad I picked them all out and threw them away!! Yikers. Sooo gross... Huge bag -  1lb 14oz.  I didn't count all the chews - but there are a LOT of them. I'm pleased.  They arrived quickly (I have prime) and in good condition with other items I had ordered.

title: Laffy Taffy grape ropes
text: Mmmmmmm.  Probably not for everyone -  but - if you like laffy taffy generally, these are yummy.  These are Sour - and flavorful grape.  Soft and chewy.  Purple color.  You get 24 in a box.  Around 12" long - or a little less.  80 calories.  I'm gonna have a hard time not eating the whole box in the next 8 hours.  Yikes.

title: simple syrup
text: Received this simple syrup in excellent condition, no leaks - It is yellowish.  The ad photo does show clear liquid.  But I have to say that it tastes delicious - it is smooth and clean tasting - NO grit.  The color is not important.  Perfect for drinks with or without alcohol.  The bottle is glass.  You can purchase a pump that goes in the bottle.  I didn't.  I will definitely purchase this syrup again, no hesitation.

title: Leila Love Macarons
text: I just recently "discovered" macarons after being gifted some from a french bakery.  Mmmmm! Delightful!  I have been hunting them ever since.  I ordered these after reading reviews and a preponderance of hope.  They are delightful.  They arrived a week earlier than estimated.  Each macaron is individually wrapped and arrived in good condition - which says a lot - because they are very delicate. They recommend refrigerating as soon as they arrive, which I did.  They also provide a legend so you can choose/see what flavor you're eating.  The box they come is totally gift worthy.  In fact, as August is my mom's and my birthday month, I ordered her a box too after I tried these.  These are pricey - but in my opinion well worth it.  Now, I am new to macarons - so I don't consider myself a real connoisseur -  I do think they are worth a try if you like macarons.  I would definitely buy these particularly again.

title: cola gummies
text: Yikes.  There are TWELVE bags.  WAY too many for me - so I have to find someone who will admit to liking them and share.  You would think that 5oz bags wouldnt be that big - but  they are 5 serving bags!  I eat one or two gummies - not one or two bags!  If you're into sharing, this is the way to go!

title: hot sauce packet collection
text: Ad says you get 7 different hot sauces, 100 total.  I received 103 and none were broken or empty - so that's good.  I took some pics - there were several more of the Texas Pete and El Yuca packets than other flavors and only 5 of the Louisiana brand.  Mostly they were close except for those mentioned.  In case that matters to you.  It's fun to have the choice while traveling - which is why I purchased them. I appreciated none of them were empty or broken.

title: shelf stable creamer
text: Very disappointing to have received a box that will expire September 29, 2020. Seriously?  Should have been at least into 2021.  I would not order again because the expiration date way too close.  Geez.  I will end up throwing away at least 1/2 the box by that date.  Meh.

title: dill pretzels
text: These were really not my cup of tea... They are twisted pretzel sticks - each about 3" long.  They are "dusted" with a dill like flavor... a tiny bit salty - more sour - which was not a good flavor to me.  Of course this personal taste.  If you like a sour pickle, perhaps these are for you.  The pretzel itself is a little tough - I prefer a bit crispier pretzel.  The bag is pretty small.  Obviously, I won't be purchasing these again - but I am trying to present an honest review of the product and my experience.

title: Wicked minis garden dill oyster crackers
text: Oooo... these are GOOOOOOD.  They are mini oyster crackers that are salty and lightly dill flavored.  I've been eating them right out of the bag when I'm hankering for something salty...  but they would be delicious on a tomato soup.. or a salad.  They are "mini" size - so about half the size of a regular oyster cracker.  And they are very easy to eat.  Dang it.

title: Wickles pickle relish
text: Mmmmmmm..  This stuff is sweet/hot heaven.  Now.  If only they could make the pickle pieces more ... chunky and less like a pickle paste - it would be perfect.  It's awesome in tuna salad... It's great smeared on a grilled cheese sandwich ... it's really good in a family pasta salad recipe that I traded the chopped sweet or bread and butter pickles for this stuff.  It's really elevated relish.  If you haven't tried - it's really worth it if you like sweet/hot.  It's delicious.  I'm a fan.  And Wickles - if you're listening - lets try a nice chunky version!!  Not available locally where I live so I do have to order it.  It arrived fairly quickly in this day and age of covid19 - and all jars were intact - no leakers!

title: dried shitake mushrooms
text: These are slightly earthy and sweet.  They are dried - so rehydrate in warm water for about 1 hour.  I use in my ramen.  They are really small - about 1 1/2 -2".  Plastic jar - wish it was glass.

title: Amazon Fresh Extra Virgin Oil
text: I like to buy this size and brand.  Once you open a bottle of EVO it is good for one month - so this size is about perfect.  I can't use the larger bottles in a month.  This brand is every bit as good as the more expensive brands.

title: Cameron's Coffee
text: I purchased this a  gift for a friend - along with some other brands.  She liked this one -but to be honest it was not her favorite - a little spendy but tasty in her words.

title: Kauai Coffee
text: I sent this to a friend of mine who loves her coffee - and hadn't tried this brand yet.  I love this brand.  Any style/flavor.  Rich - of course, it's a bit pricey.

title: chocolate covered liquid cherries
text: These are delicious dark chocolate pure liquid cherries.  Sooooo good.  AND BONUS... individually wrapped.  They are perfect in every way!

title: pecan pie nut butter
text: I purchased this flavor and bunch of other nutbutters for a friend who cannot chew the nuts she loves anymore - but nut butters were still an option!  She liked this one - but not her number 1 to be honest.

title: McCafe ground coffee
text: In my personal opinion this coffee is one of the best coffee's you can buy at this price point.  Try it once and you'll never go back! It's rich and very expensive tasting - of course - in my opinion.

title: Lindt truffles
text: Purchased for a friend that can't chew stuff really well, but loves chocolate.  These just melt in your mouth like heaven... Her words. That's a 5 star review.

title: Chesters puff corn
text: I can't eat popcorn and it kills me.  This isn't popcorn... but it's popcorn LIKE - without the hulls - which are the problem for me intestinally.  :)  It does sort of take popcorn's place in my mouth.  I keep buying it.  In my opinion the cheese flavor is better than the butter - that's just me.

title: Perrier Lime Mineral water
text: I like the smallish size of these cans as I don't use a lot in one session... I usually mix with a squeeze of fresh lime or a splash of my favorite 100% juice.  Delicious low cal sparkly drink.

title: Justins chocolate nut butter
text: This stuff is great - I use it instead of frosting and my family is none the wiser.  A thin spread, delicious and maybe a tiny bit more healthy than frosting... try it!  Works great on brownies... best to "drizzle" (put some in a sandwich bag and cut the tip - squeeze out) onto cupcakes or other more tender baked goods.

title: almond nut butter
text: I purchased this flavor and bunch of other nutbutters for a friend who cannot chew the nuts she loves anymore - but nut butters were still an option!  Almond butter to me - is like peanut butter elevated!  Haven't tried? You'll never go back.  It's soooo good!

title: flour
text: So in this weird time of hoarding - I have a friend who loves baking but could not get any flour to save her life... so I overpaid a bit in order to surprise her with some of this.  Needless to say - she was overjoyed.

title: trader joes mixed nut butters
text: I purchased this flavor and bunch of other nutbutters for a friend who cannot chew the nuts she loves anymore - but nut butters were still an option!  Of all the nut butters I sent her - THIS ONE was her number one favorite.  I would not have guessed it because it was so many mixed in one.  But she says the texture and taste are unbelievably good.  So I will keep buying this one for her

title: justins cashew butter
text: I purchased this flavor and bunch of other nutbutters for a friend who cannot chew the nuts she loves anymore - but nut butters were still an option!  Cashews were her favorites.  She like this flavor

title: Anna's thin ginger crisps
text: These cookies are super thin and perfect with tea.  Very good ginger flavor.  Crisp and tender. I buy them often.

title: chicken in a biscuit crackers
text: How to describe these... don't taste anything like chicken!  But they are salty and delicious.  They are light colored like a club cracker but a little wider and shorter - and more flavorful.  A blast from my childhood and every bit as good as I remember if not a little more salty!  Great on their own but can stand up to sharp cheese or spread - in my opinion that is!

title: no salt added green beans
text: This is my favorite brand and I don't like the salt - right now - during this difficult shopping time -  people hoarding such things - ordering them online is one of the only ways I get them these days.  In all honesty - I don't love canned vegs - but due to a dietary issue I must eat them temporarily. Shame that it is during a time of crazies at the the grocery store!  Thank goodness for online ordering!

title: simple red vines
text: Whole family are red vine fans... but I am NOT a fan of the artificial colors, etc.  This is a great exchange and NO one can tell the difference if you hand them one out of the box.  I purchase this big box of them and share them around.  They are soft and fresh when they arrive... but we have about 50/50 who like them fresh and the other half prefer them a bit "stale."  So some people slice open the end (I'm one of these folks) - let them sit for a while and eat them chewy.  To each their own.  These are still NOT a "healthy" option - but I consider them a "healthier" option.  Enjoy within reason!

title: toasted coconut marshmallows
text: My mom is HUGE marshmallow fan - and the toasted coconut version is one of her favs, so I sen these to her.  She reports they are DELICIOUS.  Mom is (80 years old) very picky so I consider this a 5 star rating.

title: Tic Tac Gum
text: Like Tic tacs... LOVE LOVE LOVE tic tac gum.  Mostly because I can choose the size of gum I want in my mouth.  If you just want a tiny wad, one or two tic tac gum pieces are perfect.  If you want a more regular amount of gum - 3 or 4 pieces.  The flavor lasts whether you use one or two pieces the same if you choose 3 or 4 pieces... in my opinion.  I just really love them.  Love the option of peppermint or spearmint - by my mood.  I suppose the peppermint is best because there aren't any dyes... eh.  Not sugar free - but then that means NO aspartame which is more important to me.  I will keep buying them!

title: bananas
text: It's hard to say much about bananas - but these were delicious - as bananas are - if you like them!  If you don't get them eaten before they ripen - open, cut and freeze in chunks.  Super great in smoothies - add some milk - a little ice and a bit of peanut butter - Mmmmmm!

title: Arborio Rice
text: This is terrific, most purpose rice.  I cook in my rice cooker.  I don't make sushi - so I can't say it work well for that - but it is very tasty white rice in my opinion.  I do prefer it in the twist top plastic container.

title: organic hamburger
text: Tasty... but maybe a little bit chewy?  Even with the 85% fat content.  I am looking at some other brands -

title: Haas Avocados
text: Honestly, I think that Haas avocados are the very best - taste and creamy texture.  The best flesh in my opinion.  Best when on sale, of course!

title: conventally grown limes
text: The limes were great... fresh, firm and delicious.  I don't purchase organic citrus - because you generally don't eat the skins and they are thick enough.  I save organic purchases for things like apples, berries, potatoes, and other items you eat the skins of - just my personal opinion!

title: Zevia Orange soda
text: I don't drink a lot of soda pop - but if I do - it's Zevia.  Sweetened with stevia - NOTHING artificial.  Tasty and if I want a pop - these are delicious.  I love the orange, grape, root beer and ginger ale.  If you haven't tried it - it is definitely worth a try!

title: Sargento cheese sticks
text: I eat mozzarella cheese sticks regularly with an apple or other piece of fruit and I like Sargento.  To be honest, they are good, but I usually purchase what is on sale.  I would buy these again and have.

title: Lacey swiss
text: I like this swiss because of it's light and delicate flavor.  Thinly sliced - it is just a perfect treat - a nice add to a grilled cheese or the obvious ham and cheese.

title: Organic root vegs
text: I LOVE these.  Organic, easy.  I have to eat my veggies cooked for a dietary reason and I really like these.  I usually gently steam them and just eat them - they are sweet and tasty.  I have also oven roasted them.  Because they are frozen and therefore precooked - it doesn't take long - still tasty.

title: tater puffs
text: 365 Tater puffs are as tasty as you would imagine.  I usually bake them - but in all honesty, deep fried they are best!  Just as a treat!

title: Rib eye step 4
text: I do like that with Whole Foods you can understand the steps of what you are eating.  The rib eye was delicious - grilled to a perfect medium rare.  When I eat beef I usually purchase it from Whole Foods.

title: Organic Harcots
text: I love the convenience of these frozen green beans.  Delicate and tender. I usually gently steam them and then eat them with my fingers. (I know - sorry - but that's how I like them - with my fingers - no butter - no salt.. Just naked - the beans that is.) I only buy organic - to avoid the worst of pesticides.  I buy these fairly regularly and will continue to do so.

title: Oroweat Country White Bread
text: Oroweat is one of my personal favorite brands - and when I am going to indulge in white bread - this is the one I choose.  It's not too fluffy but is white bread.  I like the flavor and texture.  I personally think that Oroweat is one of the better brands - no matter what your flavor choice.

title: Organic Olive Oil Spray
text: I like to use this on cookware and on foil or parchment when oven roasting.  Works great - better for you.  I keep purchasing it!

title: Dannon Oikos triple zero
text: I like this version of Dannon (triple zero) because you get the benefit of nonfat yogurt with no bad adds - delicious - no bitter aftertaste.  Just good for you.  Vanilla is my favorite flavor - but I do like the peach version too.

title: frozen peaches
text: No added sugar, perfect for ice cream or yogurt.  I have purchased these and will continue to do so.

title: diced peaches
text: I like these on top of a little bit of vanilla bean ice cream - but mostly on plain greek yogurt.  just a touch of sweet.  They are nicely cubed and not too mushy - but are tender.  I have and will continue to buy them again.

title: Johnsville breakfast links
text: These are precooked and easily frozen.  In my opinion Johnsonville brand is one of the most tasty.  I like the quick convenience of being precooked and how easily they freeze for later - so I catch them on sale.

title: whipped cream
text: A bit spendy for canned whipped cream, but tasty.  Now, is it worth the price?  I probably will go for a more reasonably priced brand.  Just my personal opinion.

title: Idahoan Mashed Potates Sour cream and chive
text: These potatoes - no matter what flavor you choose are just convenient.  Add 2 cups of water, stir, 4 minutes in a regular microwave - stir and serve.  Can't beat that.  Do they taste and feel just like homemade - no.  But these are a convenience!  I have and will keep purchasing this particular brand!

title: halo oranges
text: These are sweet, delicious, and super easy to peel - just as you would hope.  I buy them all the time when they are available.

title: bacon flavored spam
text: Eh.  You have thought this would be a winner flavor... not for me.  The plain is better - to me this particular flavor of SPAM bears NO relation to bacon what so ever.  Maybe a tiny bit 'smoky?'  I think the plain or lo sodium or even the jalapeno is better than this.  Can't win them all!  Just my opinion.

title: oui yogurt, no fruit
text: This yogurt is ok... its got a gelatin like texture - smooth.  NOT super sweet - which I like.  So if you like a sweeter yogurt, this isn't the one for you.  It comes in a glass jar without a reusable lid.  You could reuse as glasses possibly? It's ok. But I like others better.

title: dr johns creamcicle hard candies
text: My husband is diabetic and this brand is his favorite - he does prefer the various taffies, but they are not available right now... so... we move to these.  To me, they taste sweet and satisfying - but not very flavor specific.  We buy these often... Just a helpful note - if you are going to buy these, you'll need an air tight container.  They really need the air tight container to stay fresh and delicious.

title: frozen berry cherry mix
text: My husband is diabetic and so frozen fruit is a special treat for him.  No sugar added.  You could blend the mix, he prefers just eat them in a bowl.  We buy this brand often and only organic when we do.

title: tempura shrimp
text: I purchased a mini fryer and these were perfect at about 2 minutes.  Crispy, not greasy and every bit as good as the tempura I have had at my favorite Japanese restaurant.  I will keep buying these.  Yum!

title: Glazed donuts holes
text: I did not like the texture or taste.  In fact, I disliked it so much a I checked the 'use by' date - but it was not out of date.  I ended up throwing them out as they just were too dry - and had a chemical back taste to me.  Just my opinion.

title: Oui yogurt
text: This yogurt comes in a glass jar.  It has a gelatin like texture to me... and it is NOT very sweet.  The tiny bit of fruit at the bottom does little to add any sweetness.  So if you like sweet yogurt - you will not like this stuff.  It is ok.  I tried pretty much every flavor.  But I probably won't keep buying it.  It's not my favorite - but everyone likes something different so if you like yogurt it's worth a try in my opinion.

title: buttermilk frozen biscuits
text: Ok.. this is the laziest way to have buttermilk biscuits.. the up side, pull out just one, or as many as you need.  Tasty and super easy.  I keep buying them.

title: Oikos plain greek yogurt
text: There are about 6 million different greek yogurts - Oikos is my solid #1 favorite.  Taste, texture and price.  It's smooth and not bitter.  I sure the plain in exchange for sour cream all of the time - family is completely unaware.  High protein.  I have bought this for years and I will buy it over and over again.

title: multi colored carrots
text: I have to eat only cooked veggies for a bit and these are tasty.  I will buy them again.  Bonus, Organic!

title: Salted Grissini bread sticks
text: I really like these thin, crispy bread sticks.  However, these arrived in my grocery delivery bag 90% broken.  While this does not effect the taste/flavor - but it DOES effect the enjoyment of it.  I will buy them again.

title: organic roma
text: I prefer roma tomatoes because they seem to have fewer seeds and feel firmer to me.  I like the meaty texture and fresh taste.  I will buy them again.

title: Triple Ginger snaps
text: These are soooo good.  They are crispy and tender.  There are little chewy pieces of crystallized ginger... and the cookies taste like ginger.  I love ginger snaps, but some are tough.  These are very crisp - but not at all tough.  Each cookie is about the size of 50 cent piece.  They are are very easy to eat  - maybe too easy.  I will buy these again.

title: wholefood sushi avocado
text: Is it fancy sushi restaurant sushi?  No... but it's close.  And far better than most grocery store sushi.  I order it over and over again.  The avocado roll is one of my favs.  Always fresh and tasty.

title: kozy shack french vanilla rice pudding
text: Man.  I was really hoping this would be good, with a rich vanilla flavor.  Nope.  Very blah.  Didn't taste any different that their regular rice pudding.  Eh.

title: whole foods shrimp sushi
text: Delicious. Perfect bite size.  Is it like a really good sushi restaurant? No - but it's WAY better than regular grocery sushi.  I buy it regularly and will continue to do so.

title: cabbage crunch salad
text: Nope.  I've bought this salad for years.  I purchased it in a delivery order and what arrived was soggy and old.  I tossed it.  Very disappointing but maybe it only purchased in person.  Disappointing.

title: Wedderspoon manuka honey
text: I had purchased a different brand that tasted just awful - was scared to try this brand - but I was SO happy!  Tastes very rich - different that regular honey.  It's really a treat.  I am hoping for some benefit in helping with some intestinal issues I have - but even if that doesn't happen - this honey is just really yummy.  A thicker, slightly grainy looking texture - but is doesn't feel grainy... the taste is slightly floral - but that lovely golden honey flavor is forward.  I just love it.  And so glad after the first purchase I made at more than twice the price - I was brave and tried this brand.  I'm a loyal fan!

title: Cheese -it Snap'd crackers
text: Don't buy these.  They are crack crackers!  They are DELICIOUS.  Just the right thickness - crisp - tasty.  If you open the bag I dare you not to eat the whole bag in one sitting - maybe sharing - or not....  They are perfect comfort crackers!

title: Manukora brand Manuka honey
text: I really, really tried to like this honey - was thinking it was me.. A slightly bitter, chemical after taste and odor.  I even asked others to taste my extremely valuable over $100 jar of honey.  Without commenting myself, each one said exactly the same thing - Ooo.. weird - it's bitter - and .. is that a chemical taste?  I risked purchasing another brand of this expensive honey and was so relieved to find that the new brand is truly wonderful.  Same texture, but MUCH better flavor and odor!  I am sad, but I threw this jar away.  Lesson learned.  If it doesn't taste "good" don't, eat it.  Honey is the only food in the world that doesn't spoil - so I believe this batch must have come in contact with something - unsavory.

title: smoked clams
text: These are delicious and the super tiniest little clams you have ever seen.  Really.  I am thinking about some fancy apps you could make with such adorable tiny clams.  Very smoky - but the clam flavor still comes through.  I recommend opening at room temp - I had refrigerated the unopened can - which made the oil they are packed in a bit less appetizing.  I will buy them again for sure

title: Simply Red Vines
text: No more red dye or other yucky artificial stuff.  I love these when I want a treat.  Still full on sugar - so they're not a "healthy" option - but a "healthier" option without all the artificial stuff.  I get a box, slice a small opening at the top and wait a few days.  I like a "stale" licorice stick -  if you don't, these are super soft and fresh right out of an unopened box.  I will buy these again - and I share them around the family.  (I also sneak them into the movie theater - yup - I just will not eat red dye or other artificial flavors, colors, etc)

title: Variety chocolate
text: Ok.. it's an easy button to order these delivered right to your door, but honestly it's close to the same price as locally and I have grandsons that look forward to a treat when they visit.  Love it.  Fresh and tasty - arrived fast (I have prime) and in excellent condition along with other things I ordered.

title: Jelly Belly Licorice Pastels
text: These are great... fresh.... crunchy on the outside as a pastel should be and chewy black licorice on the inside.  Honestly, they are great - and the only way I like black licorice for whatever reason!  Arrived fast (I have prime) and in great condition.  In all honesty, I would purchase them again.

title: Zevia soda - strawberry
text: I love the ginger ale, grape and rootbeer... but the strawberry isn't my fav.  It's very carbonated - all flavors are.  If you really love strawberry you might like this - just not for me.  I think you can rate on personal preferences - I don't drink soda pop often but when I do, I like this brand because it's Stevia sweetened - no artificial sweetener.  I also like they don't use artificial colors.  If you're gonna drink soda, it's a good option.

title: salted butter
text: Ok... it's salted butter.  It's great.  I love butter - probably not better than any other brand.. but it's butter!

title: Stevia soda
text: I don't drink soda pop often, but if I do, it's only those that are not artificially sweetened or sugared.  This brand is my favorite.  The ginger ale, rootbeer and grape are awesome (in my opinion)  I just recently purchased the strawberry but haven't tried it yet.  The only one I don't really care for is the cola.

title: Zevia
text: I don't drink soda pop often, but if I do, it's only those that are not artificially sweetened or sugared.  This brand is my favorite.  The ginger ale, rootbeer and grape are awesome (in my opinion)  I just recently purchased the strawberry but haven't tried it yet.  The only one I don't really care for is the cola.

title: pumpkin pie spice
text: Smells and tastes just like the best pumpkin pie.  Great on oatmeal... even on buttered toast for a tasty treat.

title: mini cannoli
text: Purchased 2 mini's as a treat for my husband and he devoured them - saying they were delicious.  Each is about 3" in length.

title: colored carrots
text: Came with only purple and white carrots... and I ordered 2 bunches... ONE orange carrot in both bunches.  I would not have chosen them had I been shopping.  They tasted good, I pan roasted them.

title: jarred cheesecake
text: Delicious and creamy...  comes in a reusable jar.  I like that I don't throw away stuff after I've eaten the treat.  Husband uses jars in his shop.

title: bananas
text: Shopper chose the perfect bananas - Just the right amount of green and ripeness.  I am grateful and appreciative.

title: organic squash
text: It's hard to trust someone else to pick your groceries and deliver them.. well..they do as good as you in choosing.  So far, so good.  I've received nice, fresh, firm veggies.  The zucs have had nice skins - not tough.

title: organic green onions
text: When buying groceries to be delivered, it's exciting to see what you get.  The organic green onions are great.  Firm, fresh and tasty.

title: organic potatoes
text: I like that every time I have ordered these delivered, I haven't ever been given a green one.  They are delicious and worth every penny.

title: wild wonders tomatoes
text: I am not a tomato fan... but my husband loves loves loves these.  Eats them when he watches tv... I mix them in his salads.  Mostly he just likes to eat them on their own.  Each time I have purchased them they have been fresh and delicious, according to my husband.

title: sparkling ginger water
text: NO ginger flavor -  and the sparkling water part is ordinary.  A huge disappointment - tossed them.  I didn't even try to give them away cause it was so yucky.

title: lemon pound cake
text: A little dry... but is dense like you would expect with "pound cake."  Nice lemon flavor.  I may have gotten an older piece.

title: natural peppermint
text: These are delicious and natural.  I am not a fan of artificial colors or flavors.  They are small, delicious mints.  Smooth and creamy - but not buttery like a butter mint.  All peppermint.  My grandma would have loved these very much.  I like the small, resealable pouch.  And I would purchase again.

title: rib roast
text: I like to purchase Step 4... but this was really good.  I use a montreal steak seasoning and cook to medium rare temp.  It was tender and tasty.

title: gingerbread cake
text: Yuck - SUPER dry - really disappointing.  Went right into the trash  -  :(

title: raspberry gel filled hard candies
text: These are so tasty - a treat from my mom's childhood memories.  Individually wrapped - fresh and tasty.

title: grated ginger
text: I wanted the sliced ginger but took this as it was the only option.  I used it on top of my sushi and it was easy to eat and delicious.  Love it.  I may not go back to sliced ginger!

title: white cake chocolate frosting cupcake
text: This combo is my personal favorite... white cake, chocolate frosting.  The cake is moist and tasty... the frosting is thick and heavy.. a bit like a ganache..  - a milk chocolate yummy.  A bit spendy.. but oh... what a treat.  I will buy this one again... and again.

title: pumpkin cheesecake cookie
text: These are a sandwich cookie that does sort of taste like pumpkin spice cheesecake... the center is creamy - not gritty.  But... it's easy to eat just one... so, it doesn't bring you back to eat another.

title: butter wreath cookies
text: A good, basic shortbread like butter cookie.  A little sugar on top.  Nice holiday treat.

title: brown butter chocolate cookies
text: These cookies are huge... chewy... and just "ok" in flavor to me - not much chocolate flavor...

title: fire roasted chili salsa
text: This stuff is sooooo yummy.  Must be the fire roasting... just a delicious flavor - some spice but not too much in my personal opinion.  Not super chunky - but there is some texture.  It's definitely one of our favorites.

title: puffin cereal
text: Ok... just personal thoughts.... but the squares are a bit large.... larger than I was expecting.  One is mouthful for me - let alone my 5 year old grandson... He prefers them as snack bites, rather than a breakfast cereal because they are so big.  Flavor is mild.

title: chocolate covered marshmallows
text: My 5 year old grandson, who is a marshmallow expert, loves these.  He likes that they are thinner - and the chocolate is tasty.  They are nice little squares.

title: mini marshmallows
text: My 5 year old grandson LOVES marshmallows and minis are his favs.  These are nice and soft and fresh tasting -

title: pico de gallo
text: Spicy, fresh tasting and chunky.  Everything you want from a pico.  I add it to a milder salsa for controllable heat.

title: bread sticks
text: fresh, crisp and delicious - they are thin, as they should be and are perfect snacking material in my opinion

title: vegan ginger cookie
text: I am not vegan, but love a good cookie so I purchased these.  They are rich tasting and chewy.  They are also very large.  I would prefer a cookie about half the size, but it's easy to cut these in half.  I couldn't eat them all before they were a bit too old.  Next time I might try freezing them and see how that works.

title: Amys mini nacho bean and cheese burritos
text: These are tender and really surprising delicious.  Hard to find.  My grandson who is very picky about his bean and cheese burritos approved these as very delicious - a bigger surprise to me.  I hope they will always have these!  They are a two bite delight - not over stuffed so when you bite them it's just right.  Creamy and smooth.

title: dr johns blackberry strawberry orange suckers
text: My husband is diabetic and says the blackberry is his all time favorite.  All of them are delicious and worth a try if you like a sweet treat but can't have or would like to eat less sugar.  Dr. Johns is my husband's favorite sugar free candy choice.  Sweetened using tooth friendly xylitol.

title: stage 4 rib roast
text: MMMMMMmmmm.... One of the best beef rib roasts I have ever purchased.  Beautiful marbling... cooked up perfectly.  I used montreal steak seasonings as my beef rub.  I cook to medium rare temp.  I then made a stew using the roasted bones.  Sooooo delicious.  I will purchase again without a doubt!

title: bulk whole cloves
text: Purchased these to make the clove and orange holiday decorations with my 2 grandsons.  It is a huge bag... would estimate about 2 cups worth - there are a lot of them are not "whole" but plenty to make up for those.  We made about 6 tangerines and used about 1/3 a cup so there are a lot left.  I will put a pot on the stove with some water and simmer the rest for the delicious fragrance - always welcome for the holidays.  It was handy to get the bulk quantity.  I could only find the very small quantity tins/jars - and basically twice the price!  So this was an awesome option.

title: Lonolife chicken bone broth in a tub
text: I have purchased this in individual tubes of this brand for a long time and it is by far my favorite flavor - rich and delicious - personal opinion but it tastes to me like a whole, slow roasted chicken.  Extra protein - as is with bone broth.  I would say that it's worth a try if you're looking for a tasty bone broth.  I like the individual tubes for traveling - all you need is hot water in cup.  This tub is great for at home.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Gold foil Buttermints
text: These arrived beautifully individually gold foil wrapped.  They have an expiration on the box of 8/25/2020... they are soft and fresh tasting... smooth... a little less buttery and more minty - which to me is perfect - about the size of marble.  Pretty in a holiday bowl.  I would buy them again.

title: Dads root beer hard candies
text: Ok... these are delicious.... and full of artificial everything (wish they could sub out some natural color - and probably should take a star off for that -but I kinda feel that is my personal opinion)  But - delicious.  They are a mouthful (not small) - hard candies.  They taste just as I remember -like the rich, sweet Dad's root beer beverage - but without the carbonation.  I included a pic of the bag I received - excellent expiration date.. the candies are individually wrapped - and are not sticky.  The wrappers are easily opened.  The candies are fresh and look like they were well stored.  They arrived quickly ( I have prime) with other items I had ordered in excellent condition.  I am going to enjoy them without much guilt.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope my review helped you!

title: cosmo nuggets
text: I had to give up one my favorite snacks - popcorn due to hulls.  I thought I'd try these.  They are not a sub for popcorn.. but they are an ok salty, sort of buttery flavored snack.  They are puffy pop/nuggets... but I miss my popcorn!

title: popcorn cones
text: Gave these at Halloween, huge hit!  My little neighbor friend ( 6 year old) asked me to be sure and have them next year.  Ahhh!  The cones are pretty small... and a bit spendy but they are tasty.

title: pickled ginger slices
text: All 6 jars were leaking and open when they arrived - as others have noted - was just hoping it had been addressed.  Apparently not.  Was refunded for order.  Nonetheless, I would not order again.

title: grated ginger
text: For some reason I had thought these would be small jars.... they are HUGE !!  I'm sharing the jars around.  So many things are better with ginger and the grated texture could be used many ways including sushi and hot or ice tea.. ice cream... asian slaw... So many options.  Good thing cause there's so much of it!  There were 6 giant jars (I'm sharing with every one I know).  Came in a separated cardboard crate type box.  All the jars were solidly sealed.  The expire date is posted as 2/2021.  I really love there are NO artificial ingredients!  I recently found out that most commercial pickled ginger contains aspartame - which I refuse to eat.  This ginger does NOT - organic ginger, cane sugar, water, rice vinegar, salt and citric acid.  Perfect.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope my review was helpful to you!

title: chicken bone broth
text: This broth is so delicious.  It's a real surprise - it tastes like a whole roasted chicken - no kidding.  No scary fake ingredients of any kind and because it's a bone broth - a little extra protein - so it's a great snack for diabetics at night.  It's truly yummy.  If you haven't tasted it, it's really worth a shot.  They also make a tasty beef bone broth - but honestly, in my opinion the chicken bone broth is the best!  It's also terrific for travel because they are in nice, liquid free tubes.  Just add hot water and enjoy.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope this info was helpful in some way to you.

title: lono life beef bone broth
text: It is a light colored broth (awesome that they don't add caramel coloring)... tasty - as you would hope for - roasted beef bones. Each box contains 4 tubes, each making 8 oz of broth with added water.  50 calories.  Total fat, 1 gm... less than 5mg total cholesterol... Sodium 550mg... Total carbs .... 0.... no sugar at all.  Protein ... 10g.  Ingredients: beef collagen, chicory root, beef, salt yeast extract, natural flavors, spices.  Box says, NO gluten. NO sugar.  My husband is a type 2 diabetic and travels a lot - these are the perfect travel companion as they are dry in tubes.  The price here is comparable to what I can get locally and I love the home delivery.  The arrived in excellent condition and quickly (I have prime) and the expiration date is really good too... April 29, 2021.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Lono Life chicken broth
text: This broth tastes like a whole, oven roasted chicken... however I did make a mistake.. I was really trying for the bone broth.  I will use this.. but going back for the bone broth.  This broth offers 25 calories per packet - (there are 4 tubes per box - makes 8 oz when mixed with hot water) - 700mg sodium (they do offer a lo so version)  - 2g total carbs - zero sugar and zero dietary fiber. Less than 1gm protein (go for the bone broth - it packs 9 or 10gms of protein).  Ingredients: chicory root, soup base (salt, yeast extract, natural flavors), chicken broth, dried chicken, torula yeast, chicken flavor (yeast extract, salt, maltodextrin, natural flavors), sage, thyme, black pepper.  Arrived quickly and in excellent condition ( I have prime). The expiration on the boxes was excellent as well - Aug 15, 2021.  These tubes are AWESOME for traveling - which is what I mostly use them for.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful!

title: dove chocolates
text: I purchased and sent to friend who is having some chewing challenges temporarily.... very well received.

title: truffle
text: I have a friend who has temporary chewing challenges and these are her favs.  So nice to be able to send her chocolate love right to her door.

title: tea biscuit
text: I had been buying a french butter cookie type tea biscuit for my mom - but one time I purchased these - and they are my mom's hands down favorite.  They are crispy, yet more dense than you would maybe expect - but not tough at all  - to me they taste very much like a fancy graham cracker.  Bottom line, mom loves them with her tea and that's all that matters to me.  Love to be able to send them directly to her doorstep.

title: chocolate kisses, bulk
text: I mailed these to a friend who cannot chew a lot right now.  She LOVES chocolate and these were greatly loved.

title: reeses cups
text: Purchased for a friend who can't really chew a lot right now.  BIG HIT.

title: dried pears
text: Initially I couldn't find this brand locally, now I see it everywhere!  Haha!  Nothing wrong with home delivery!  It's a delicious, crispy dried pear - sadly not terribly healthy - other than just the pear - but on the other hand I would consider it better than eating a candy bar when I crave something sweet.  Each bag contains about 3 servings or so... I could easily eat a whole bag with a good movie.  Makes a great road trip snack because it isn't sticky and it's crunchy - sweet - satisfying.  Happy eating!

title: powdered bone broth
text: This brand is very delicious.... it smells and tastes like the whole oven roasted chicken - it's amazing - every time I make one I am amazed at how good it smells and tastes - just like an oven roasted chicken - but without the chemicals..  Every likes something different - and honestly I didn't really expect much from a powdered mix - but this is good stuff.  If you like bone broth but looking for something easier for travel, I would say this are worth a shot.

title: Pacific Foods ginger chicken bone broth
text:
UPDATE BELOW...I LOVE this flavor and the individual size is so good for many reasons. I just wish I could find it locally more easily. It's a rich chicken bone broth with a light ginger add to the flavor - it's the perfect balance for my tastes - but everyone likes something different! Bottom line is I like the flavor - and the protein punch of a tasty bone broth. Pacific Foods offers the best taste and best quality version in my opinion.
UPDATE: 10/09/2021 Sad to say that I got the wrong soup and it is really a hassle to return it for refund. Looking for a new outlet. The broth is 5 star - but I only like the chicken ginger - NOT the salt free plain chicken. Eh. If they didn't have the right flavor, they should not have just randomly decided I might like the salt free plain chicken. Uh - NO. Now I have to arrange a return. All a waste of my time.
UPDATE: 10/10/21.. I did receive a full refund and will donate the wrong item sent to the food bank. While the Pacific Foods product is a 5 star product, I will be seeking other outlets. I do appreciate the refund as this was not my fault in any way.

title: Hey Girl sleep tea
text: I have been purchasing this tea for my 80 year old mom for some time and my sister just asked for some after having a cup with Mom.  It's a very nice blend - both report that while they can't really say it helped them sleep, it is a tasty, comforting blend.

title: Stur grape acai drink drops
text: I have been buying this brand for years - since they started really.  I battle with my husband as he buys the other brands with artificial colors, flavors and/or sweeteners.  I like this brand as it has none of those things.  As for taste... this flavor is one of husband's (and two grandsons 5 and 8) flavors - however - just FYI - it doesn't taste anything like 'grape' to me!  It tastes almost like... cotton candy to me!  But what the heck.  It does sweeten your water and add some flavor - if you like that sort of thing. You don't need much to flavor either, so the bottle lasts a good amount of time.  I would recommend this brand because the don't use artificial stuff.  They offer many flavors, try them to find your fav.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope this info was helpful to you!

title: Rose City Genmaicha
text: I am a genmaicha tea fan and I have to say that this is particularly tasty version.  It does contain tea sourced from China.  The box was pretty beat up in the shipping process, but the tea bags were fine.  I wish I could find this locally more easily.  I live in a small town with few shopping options - so I rely on Amazon.  If you are a fan of genmaicha tea, I would highly recommend this one for a try.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope my review was helpful to you!

title: chocolate caramel balls
text: Purchased these for a friend who says they are remarkably good!  She was surprised at how tasty they were.  They arrived quickly and in great condition (I have prime).  I would purchase them again for her in an instant.

title: Gogo applesauce pouches
text: I personally love these and the organic version is hard for me to get locally in our small area.  I like to freeze them and eat them frozen.

title: Mt Olive Vidalia pickled onions
text: The jar I received was sticky and there was liquid in the packing material.  I did try the lid and it popped - so I tried them.  They were a bit bitter and not really my favorite.  Of course, that is my subjective opinion on taste - however the condition of the jar was disappointing.  I ended up throwing it away.  I do love other Mt Olive items... like the chunky deli style dill and sweet relish.  Those are worth every penny!  So I think this is just not my cup of tea.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful, however, I am unlikely to purchase these again in all honesty.

title: Simply Jolly Time
text: This is so hard to get locally for me, for whatever reason -so I love that I can it delivered right to my door.  It's a few simple ingredients, easy to make and soooo good. In my personal opinion it's the best micro version out there.  If you're a popcorn fan, this is one to try for sure.

title: North Shore Coconut Peanutbutter
text: This stuff is literally the bomb.  It's SOOOO good... but the price will kill you.  On the up, it's two ingredients... peanuts and coconut.  Period.  It's a bit on the runny side... even after stirring.. but that just makes it easier to dip stuff.  :)  It's sooo good.  I just wish the price was a bit less painful.  I will forever be a fan - just a broke one!

title: marcona almonds
text: For some reason these are rarely available at my local Costco either in store or online. So I end up purchasing through Amazon.  The pricing is substantially higher, but we eat these quite a lot - and I share them with my daughter and her family.  They are not greasy (some marcona almonds are floating in olive oil) but they are nicely salty and crispy.  They are great chopped and added to salads of all kinds - I like to add them chopped to an asian style slaw that I make - a nice, salty crunch.  They are so good by the handful and they travel so well - a great protein source.  As long as I can get my hands on these, I will buy them.

title: coconut peanutbutter
text:
I have been purchasing a MUCH higher priced hawaiian version... but decided to give this one a try. I love it. It's just the right amount of coconut to peanut flavor for me... VERY smooth - not at all gritty. Now, it does add salt and agave .. and the other brand is much simpler - only peanuts and coconut. But it is 6x the price. So, I will purchase this brand more often. It's got a slight salty/sweet tang to it. I love the texture - is much less soupy. Arrived quickly ( I have prime) and in great condition. Having said all of these things I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
UPDATE: I had to drop a couple stars because this most recent order is so... off.... They came in a thin USPS bubble envelope... were quite squished - I don't see any leaks...but they look oddly oily and soupy... that has never been before. And no, it's NOT hot here - I live on the Oregon coast.. today it's foggy and 54 degrees - so - it's not Arizona! I am tempted to return this order... And the other thing is they expire 2/2021. Eh. I would prefer at LEAST one year expiration. So... not sure if I will keep ordering - at least from Amazon. Sadly, I can't get this locally.

title: creamy pralines
text: I used to purchase these from a local candy store that made them fresh daily.... 40 years ago... so... these are ok.  But they are not what I remember - rich and buttery.  These are lacking in full flavor... and I am disappointed in the addition of sorbitol.  ?  Why?  There's corn syrup and sugar!  Not thrilled about the  caramel color too.  Really?  Just brown the sugar properly - no color necessary.  But then, these are mass made.  The pecans are a bit chewy, not crisp and roasty - but there are a fair amount of them. I included a pic.  In my opinion they are just about the right size, although the ones I used to get as a child were a bit larger - but these are 200 calories each and about $2.50 each.  They are creamy - not gritty - but they are a tiny bit rubbery texture wise.  In all honesty, I probably won't order these again, but it was worth a try.  I am just disappointed they aren't richer flavored and the nuts less rubbery. All personal opinion.

title: tiny tootsie pops
text: These are a very easy, quick sugar hit.  They taste just as the regular size ones.  I just wish they would walk away from artificial colors and flavors.  Then they will be a 5 star treat.  In the mean time I like them because they are an easy to pack, fairly reasonable sugar treat.

title: mini sugar daddies
text: Childhood fav in a reasonable serving size.  Each one is about 3" x 1"...  I can eat one and not feel guilty about it.  They taste just as I recall.  Don't recommend them if you have loose teeth.  They are EXCELLENT tooth extractors.  Very sticky and chewy - as you would expect.  I used to use them to remove my baby teeth - haha.  They are a great treat if you liked the really big ones.

title: Genmai cha tea
text: The roasted rice flavor is a bit light...probably not my favorite version - but not terrible.  I am drinking it.  This tea is not super strong by nature... but I do prefer a bit deeper, brighter Genmai cha.  Just my opinion...  arrived quickly (I have prime) and in great condition.

title: mini strawberry jam
text: My grandsons love these.  They are small and easy for them to get into after I open them.  I like the convenience and fun of these.  The jam is delicious.

title: giant reeses cups
text: My son-in-law is a huge chocolate peanutbutter fan and I also put a bit of Reeses in his stocking... these were a special add because they are HUGE!  You can put them on a plate and slice them because they are like small pies!  Haha!  They were a hit and always bring a smile.  These are obviously a treat and not an every day thing.. so great for Christmas.

title: ghirardelli caramel bar
text: Purchased these for my adult daughter's xmas stocking..  her favorite.  They arrived quickly (I have Prime) and in excellent condition.  Of course, they were well received and enjoyed over the holidays.

title: Breakfast in Paris
text: I am a stanch Stash tea supporter, predominantly  earl gray... but Breakfast in Paris (not to be confused with Christmas in Paris - YUCK)  is just a delightful change-up.  There are notes of black tea, bergamot, vanilla, and lavender and similar to earl gray, but slight notes of vanilla and lavender - far from overwhelming - they have master the delightful with this one. The combination is just a nice switch once in awhile.  I have difficulty finding it locally so I have taken to purchasing via Amazon.  If you enjoy tea and a something a bit different, I really think this one is worth a try.

title: Christmas in Paris tea
text: Hmmm... Tea is personal... but ... I was not a fan.  It was an odd blend of mint and chocolate and tea... it didn't work for me.  Having said that... I tried this because I actually really enjoy Breakfast in Paris stash blend.  Very different from this one.  This one wasn't for me... but I always think you should try for yourself.  Tea really is so personal.  Hope this was helpful.

title: dr johns taffies
text: My husband is diabetic and these are on his top 5 list.  They are a bit spendy... but tasty and well worth it in my husband's opinion.  They are chewy, but not tough - and the flavor is bright - again in his opinion.  We've purchased them several times.  He also really likes the suckers and hard cream cicle hard candies.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  And hope this info was helpful to you now!

title: christmas butter mints
text: These were delicious butter mints that we added to christmas goodie bags.  The sweet inspirational sayings were bright and happy!  They were easy to open... they tasted rich and buttery and melted perfectly when eaten.  I would buy them again.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. Hope this was helpful to you!

title: little secrets chocolate wafers
text: These are good... but WAY too expensive.  I'm all about paying a bit more for all natural and certainly "fair trade" items... but these are just not a good enough value for taste and satisfaction... of course, this is only my personal opinion.  The cookie portion is crisp.. the chocolate is nice.. but not the best I've had and for this price I would like it to be more satisfying. The flavors are subtle - and some may prefer that - the flavor balance is close... but light.  Again, my personal opinion.  For some, they may be the perfect balance of all!

title: mallow bits
text: The key here is mallow "bits"... they are like little rocks.  I wish they could make mallows this small that are not crispy.  They sort of melt in hot chocolate... but not really.  Eh.  I threw them out.. but it's just my personal preference.

title: sour ball candy
text: I've read some of the other reviews... and I have to say... In MY opinion.. these sour balls ARE sour... maybe not extremely.. but there is definitely a tang.  The colors are bright.. and clear.  I purchased as some of the filler in our Christmas parade candy bags and they were perfect.  I wanted some old style candies and these fit the bill.  I think candy flavor is perhaps subjective.  They worked for me.. but I respect everyone's opinion.  They were well wrapped...clear - fresh - not melted... and look beautiful in our Christmas treat bags.

title: Dr. John's creamsicle swirl candies
text: Husband is diabetic and HUGE Dr. John's fan... he likes the suckers... and the taffies.  However, he just told me that THESE are his new favorites.  He says they taste really really good.  If you like Dr. John's hard candies, these are worth a try.  I like that there are NO artificial ingredients of any kind.  Win/Win.  Now.. if we could get the price down some.. it would be a 10 star product.

title: fresh and tasty
text: I got a bag that was a little less than 10lbs.  It did not have a whole lot of Dots - if that is your fav... but I think it was a fair mix of all the other candies.  I purchased to mix in santa candy bags - so I taste tested each piece and they were all fresh and tasty - a good value in my opinion.  I read other reviews that were shorted and disappointed in the mix... I did take a chance and I try to be fair in my reviews.  Hope this helped you in some way.

title: coarse dill relish
text:
UPDATE BELOW..... What makes this dill relish so yummy? The coarse grind. It's not the "mush" you get in a fine grind -there's some real texture. And for SOME reason it is impossible to find locally for me. Just FYI.. they also make a coarse or "deli" grind sweet relish too! Oh yeah. It's the bomb. More expensive - but obviously worth it to me. It truly is worth a try if you haven't ever experienced it. Makes everything just a step above. Unlike you like mush. Then you're probably gonna wanna pass. Arrived quickly (I have prime) and in good condition - well bubble wrapped. Hope my review was helpful to you in some way!
UPDATE... 1/18/2023.... Still the best coarse relish available, BUT - both jars expire in about 5 months. Would expect at least 1 year. So, lost a start for that one.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. All of my reviews reflect my honest, personal experience with the reviewed item - your experience may be different. I am not influenced by any outside source. I receive/accept NO free products or discounts that are not available to all shoppers- ever. For some reason our shopper ranks are no longer visible - so, to give you a little more info about me, I am a top 50 reviewer (#30 the highest rank achieved). Those numbers used to fluctuate over time - up and down but I noticed that they stopped updating regularly - perhaps to phase them out. It's a shame because it did help you see who has been around the longest and who is a trustworthy reviewer.  I've been doing reviews for over 25 years with Amazon - over 6,000 reviews posted, those reviews have been viewed well over 50,000 times, including well over 24,000 likes. Bottom line, I pay for all my stuff, just like you do.

title: dr johns sugar free
text: My husband is diabetic and I am constantly looking for things that are not artificially sweetened.  This brand is his favorite... (My personal opinion is that they taste good - but the flavors are not super recognizable - but they are sweet!)  He really loves the taffies and the caramels.. but his "go - to" when he wants a sweet are the lollys.  I do believe they are one of the best options out there.. A couple of things I've learned about these.... put them in an airtight container as soon as they arrive and keep them away from heat.  They'll last as long as you'd like them to that way. No artificial ingredients... and that's the main reason I buy them.  That, and the fact my husband really enjoys them.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. And I really hope my review was helpful to you in some way!

title: pourable brown sugar
text: So... this is not the "packable" brown sugar you usually bake with... but it is PERFECT to use when making a rub... or in tea.. or to sprinkle on top of something you want a little crackle on in the oven.  I LOVE that it is easily pourable and conveniently in a dispenser jar. It's really handy and useful all around.  It's hard to find locally, so I purchase it here. With this seller the price is reasonable and you get three jars.  If you haven't tried it - try it just once.. I wager you'll come back again.  Listen.. it's processed sugar.  It's not "healthy" to use a large amount... but with this dispenser and because "brown" sugar has a richer flavor, I use a little less.  Hope my review was helpful to you!

title: Hawaiian Rainbow Honey
text: This honey is delicious.  The seller/owner is very proud of it and so they should be.  I really admire honey.  It's a true miracle. Really.  The ONLY FOOD IN THE WORLD that does NOT spoil.  You could literally eat 2000 year old honey.  Read up on what a true miracle honey is and what it can do for you.  What bees do for our world.  THEN... support the bee keepers of your area.  Try to buy local whenever you can.  It is NOT easy to keep bees.  And most of the commercial honey you buy (from China especially) has been "mixed" with other things and you are NOT getting 100% honey.  Very bad.  So... if you find a bee keeper you know.. you can trust.  Pay the price.  Buy 100% real honey.  Nothing added.  No flavors... no corn syrup or other thickeners.  Sit back and close your eyes and truly enjoy what you are eating.  I do not know this seller/owner and I am blindly trusting that what they are selling is 100% genuine. I grew up visiting my grandparents on Maui... I live on the mainland so this is not actually "local" for me... but after conversations with the seller/owner and another customer.. I felt comfortable enough to buy just one jar to try.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful..

title: hard to find locally
text: Purchased for my Mom - they are hard to find locally.  She loves them... thin, crisp and a good rye flavor. She likes to eat them with a sliced pear or apple and a slice of sharp cheddar.  I love that Amazon offers nearly all the world delivered to our door.  As hard as they are to find locally I imagine I will order again. They arrived quickly ( I have prime) and in excellent condition right to her door.

title: sriracha cashews
text: My husband travels a LOT and is diabetic.  He loves sriracha and I thought this would be a great travel item.  He LOVES them.  Says the sriracha flavor is not super intense, but good flavor.  I am not a fan of cashews myself, but he loves them.  They are packed in individual serving size packets.  Also handy for traveling.  I have never seen these locally and I realize they are a bit spendy for snacks... but they are delivered right to my door.  Worth it to me.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Ginger preserves
text:
UPDATE below: Wow. If I could rate this 10 stars I would. The ginger preserve is truly delightful. But then, I LOVE ginger. It's spicy... it's got chunks of ginger. It's sweet (obviously - its a preserve!), but the spicy of ginger is the winner here. I actually put this in my tea. It tasty on toast... but my fav is the tea. I wish this was available locally, but I will continue to buy it online. If you love ginger, you are going to love these preserves. I'd buy it in a quart size jar if that was offered. Haha! I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. And I hope this review was helpful to you in some way.
UPDATE: I was so happy with the product that I wanted to share with others... so I sent some to my sister - TWICE. Both times the items arrived broken, VERY poorly packed. Such a HUGE disappointment. It's very rare for Amazon - and they of course made it right. I am having to look for other avenues to purchase this brand. I hate to have to drop my review, but it you can't get it in good condition - it's not worth much. :(

title: florida honeycomb
text: I am a lover of raw honeycomb .. and of bees.  Honey is the ONLY food on earth that does NOT spoil.  It's truly amazing.  When it crystallizes, spread in on toast or heat to re-liquefy.  Without bees AND beekeepers, there would be little to NO food for us to eat.  It has many medicinal properties... some you might be really suprised by... Be careful, never try to treat yourself - always check with a doctor first. Anyway... support your local beekeeper. It is NOT easy to keep bees. It takes real knowledge and skill.  Buy local when you can and buy often.  Now... having said all this... I received my honeycomb quickly (I have prime) and it arrived in perfect condition along with other items I had ordered.  I have included some pics for you to see. The plastic box was closed and there was NO honey leakage.  I was hoping for an slight orange blossom type flavor... but perhaps that is not in season? It IS slightly floral... the comb is tender and the caps are full and delicious.  I am just not sure the flavor is my favorite.  Everyone likes something different and I encourage you to try this particular seller.  I did order some other from a different area and seller and the comb was not fresh... tasting like plastic.  Eh.  Again, I encourage you to try this seller.  I plan to share this with my sister as we are both honeycomb aficionados.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. And I hope my review was helpful to you in some way.  It takes time and effort to write honest, unbiased reviews, especially with photos!

title: vinegar shots
text:
My husband travels A LOT. He gets leg cramps occasionally. It's really hard to "travel" a bottle of vinegar. So these "shot" packets are AWESOME. He firmly believes that they settle leg cramps almost immediately. NOTE: (We are NOT doctors and are not saying they will work for everyone). It seems to work for him. And there may be other health benefits - but again, we are not doctors. You should always check with your doctor first before starting ANY type of "therapy" BEFORE doing it. There could be drug interactions, etc... Just sayin - safety first, we are ALL different. I am mostly reviewing the convenience of these packets for travel! I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
UPDATE: 9/26/2018... Have ordered 2 more times.... husband uses them while traveling. SO much nicer than traveling with a "bottle" of vinegar... which of course you cannot. LOVE these.

title: sriracha to go packets
text: I have purchased "to go" packets of sriracha for many years... There are a couple of different "types" of packets and the one that is shaped like the bottle is the worst (SUPER leaky on arrival!) but this order - THIS TYPE OF PACKET IS THE BEST.  They stay sealed.  So, that makes them a bit tougher to open, BUT I'd rather deal with that, that a leaky mess. Also, I've gone around and around with buying the HUGE number (200+) and a just a few at a time... I've decided that while it costs more to buy the fewer, we are happier with the fewer - 50-100 - which matches my husbands use.  He travels a LOT and I carry a few in my purse for his use while we are out.  These arrived quickly (I have prime) and in good condition.  They arrived in a box, along with other items I ordered, in a zipper closed plastic bag and with a sticker showing a really decent expiration date.  There were EXACTLY 50 packets - which is what I ordered. I don't usually include pics of items that are so obvious, but because the individual item packaging does matter, I decided to include a few pics. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. And I hope that my review was helpful to you in making your purchase decision. It's the only reason I take the time to write reviews!

title: Zatarans crab boil in bags
text: We use this in our annual seafood boil every year.  I like that the spices are mesh bagged. Makes cleanup so much easier.  And the flavor is always perfect.  I recommend them and will buy them again!

title: Cocktail bitters set
text: Ok... I have only tried the orange bitters so far (I add to my mules), but the others smell divine.  I really like have all the options... I was in an airport lounge a lady next to me said that she mixes a little bitters in some club soda when her stomach is upset and it really helps.  I have not tried this but it sounds interesting.  Anyway... if you'd like to have an interesting gin and tonic or a more interesting mule, I think you'd like this set!  They arrived quickly (I have prime) and in excellent shape along with other items I had ordered.  I will update my review if new info would seem useful.

title: Tortuga caribbean mix cake pack
text: Bought these to give as gifts, but I did try each flavor.  I will say that the plain, original is probably my fav, these cakes were very well received. The ones I tried were moist.. and smelled and tasted like rum.  I would buy them again as well as they were well received!  I put a pretty, gold satin ribbon around each one and gifted them.

title: Nutter butter peanutbutter cookies
text: My 4 year grandson thinks they are a treat and calls them "foot" cookies.... I buy them through amazon out of the convenience of delivery to my door and when I am ordering other things.

title: Tortuga Original Rum Cakes
text: Purchased as gifts... there were well received.  To be fair, I also sampled one of the them - it was very moist, very "rummy" tasting.  The cakes are small.. something on the range of a large cupcake... but that is what I was looking for.  The boxes themselves lend well to gift giving - I added a gold ribbon bow and tag.  Perfect for my use.  Hope this info was useful to you in making your decision as that is my whole reason for writing my unbiased, uncompensated reviews!

title: Organic peanut butter and cocoa balls cereal
text: I was really hoping this would be a good mix... but the flavors were sooooo bland!!!  I was hoping for some nice, deep, rich flavors.  Perhaps I was expecting too much?  If you're OK with faint, whispers of flavor, this is the cereal for you!  The "balls" are small... easy to eat.  The mushed up pretty quickly in milk, but I am ok with that...  For me they were "OK" at best - just my opinion!  Hope that helps some

title: M&Ms Almonds
text: I love these.. because of the almonds and they are for whatever reason not easily available locally in my small area.  They are more expensive than the peanut version - but then... these are delivered right to your door and I didn't drive around to 15 places that don't have them.  The bag has a zipper top, so that's nice and keeps them fresh as you enjoy them.

title: Dark roast k-cup
text: Rich, deep taste... not bitter.  I usually buy the "dark" roast because I like the richer flavor... the trick is to find one that isn't bitter.. or at least for me. Says 100% Arabica beans.  The cups work in all "K-cup" makers... I use a non "k" brand single cup maker and these work perfectly. I like the convenience of Prime Pantry. Hope this helps in some way!

title: Fruit flavored xylitol gum
text: Tastes like juicy fruit - a childhood fav... but without the sugar or aspartame.  Xylitol is good for your teeth and gums.  So far, they haven't said it has any bad side effects, so I'm using it.  It's tasty.  I will say that I wish the flavor lasted a bit longer, but in all honesty, it lasts as long as you SHOULD be chewing it.  I guess it's bad for your jaw to just chew and chew - so when the flavor goes, I spit it out.  If you used to love juicy fruit flavor gum, I would say it's really worth a try.

title: Sambal Oelek Chili paste
text: Would have been delicious I am sure... but it was shipped in a paper envelope, and NO additional protection.  Shame.  But I will not buy again.

title: garlic paste
text: Smelled good... maybe a little fermented... but then, jarred "paste" will be a little...  Was not able to actually use as the jar and seal were broken.

title: Stevia water drops
text: Husband LOVES these.  He is soooo picky.  I've been trying to get him drops without aspartame or other artificial sweetners.  He likes them the best of all the others I've tried so far.  Taste is subjective... I do think these are worth trying.

title: tootsie roll midgees
text: Tiny little bites of heaven.  But hey... this is a BIG bag.  They are individually wrapped and made them a great parade throw.

title: soft peppermints
text: These are a little like a butter mint... they melt in your mouth... the only difference is that these are red and white and are "tougher" than the buttery smooth mints you're familiar with.  Probably cheaper locally... I didn't see them initially and as soon as I ordered I did - and they were cheaper.  However, you do pay for the convenience of to your door delivery!  They are individually wrapped.  We used them in our christmas golf cart parade. Worked great.  Just wish they were a bit creamier.

title: peanut butter candy
