title: Best Jerky I've ever had!!!!!! Must try!!!
text: I've been a jerky lover since I was 6. Im 42 now. Tried all brands, flavors and types. This jerky is hands down the best I've ever had. Texture and taste. I love hot jerky and 99% of hot jerky is too hot to enjoy. People's Choice is literally perfect. I set up for monthly delivery.

title: Very spicy hot; one package moldy
text: These tasted good, but were extremely hot.  Too hot for me.  If you like really hot stuff, these may be just what you want.  If you don't, stay away.  The second package I opened was moldy, so maybe these were a little old???

title: Delicious
text: This is good quality maple candy.  I have tried maple candy from other companies, but this is one of the best.

title: Almost melted in early spring heat
text: This took almost a week to reach me after Swiss Colony shipped.  Came by FedEx and they are usually reasonably fast.  I would not recommend ordering this in warm weather, especially if you live in a hot place like Arizona where I live.  They were delivered out in front of my house and they had started to melt when I brought them in.  I think Swiss Colony needs to find a faster way to get things shipped.  As to the product itself, I would say they were just okay, but didn't seem to taste as good as I remember them from years ago.  Maybe my tastes have changed, or the quality of the product is not what it used to be.  Or the product degraded a little during the week-long trip?  Refrigerating did help somewhat.  I used to really love Swiss Colony products years ago, but they have always been a little pricey.

title: Best Tasting Cornbread Ever
text: This is some of the best tasting cornbread ever.  The only thing I don't like is that one of the ingredients is High Fructose Corn syrup, which is very bad for us.

title: Best tasting chocolate ever
text: This is supposed to be milk chocolate, but actually resembles dark chocolate.  Best chocolate I have ever tasted.  Originates from Peru.  I have bought this several times and it is delightful.  Not overly sweet, but not bitter at all, like some darker chocolate can be.  Doesn't have the milky consistency that some American chocolate does.

title: Best Maple Candy on Earth
text: This maple candy comes from the hills above my home town in northern Vermont, one of the best places in the world for quality maple products.  Yes, maple syrup and maple sugar are made in a number of places, but no one makes a better product than David Marvin of Butternut Mountain Farm.  There is just something special about the sugar maples in that area that make the finest maple syrup and maple candy you can find.  Not saying that just because I am from that area either.  There are other good maple products to be found, in other places, but there are subtle differences that make this the best on earth and make it stand above any others.  The package is small and I ate it up fast, but quality maple products are expensive.

title: Our Favorite Cornbread
text: We have always liked this cornbread the best.  I do caution you to realize that the number one ingredient is sugar. Of course sugar makes most things taste better.  My mother used to make cornbread (which we called Johnny Cake) from scratch, but I think I like this better.  Can't remember what I paid last time for it, but the price here seems reasonable.  Of course you can make your own cornbread for a fraction of the price, but will it be as good?  At least you can leave out the sugar or go easy on the sugar.

title: Jar looks bigger in the picture than it actually is
text: These are soft, delicious caramels.  Some of the best I have had.  The container was a little smaller than I expected or remember (as I have had these in the past).  They are top quality, so you won't be disappointed.  They are not overly expensive, but a little more than I like to pay for a "treat".  Mine were gone way too fast.

title: Very Tasty, but more like a candy bar than a cookie
text: These were very tasty, but reminded me of a Twix Candy Bar, with the only real difference being that the chocolate is dark chocolate and better than the milk chocolate of a Twix.  While these are quite good, they aren't anything to rave about.  Will enjoy them, but probably would not buy them again.  They are worth trying, especially if you like Twix.  These seem more like candy than cookies.

title: Very good tasting mustard, but a little on the sweet side
text: I really liked the taste of this mustard and gave it 5 stars, but it doesn't have that much spiciness.  It is better than just plain yellow mustard, but not as spicy as some brown mustards.  It is rather sweet, which is no surprise since it has two kinds of sugar (fair trade organic cane and maple syrup).  I am glad they used some quality and expensive sweeteners, rather than cheaper high fructose corn syrup.  The sugars obviously make it taste better, but does sugar belong in mustard?  Not much else for ingredients, other than the sugar.  I think just mustard and vinegar, as I recall.  The sugars act as a preservative, so there are no  artificial preservatives here.  Worth a try if you aren't familiar with it.

title: Tastes as good as any Thousand Island Dressing and better than cheaper brands
text: I have tried several of the "Happy Belly" products and found them all to be comparable in quality and taste to other brands.  First of all, let me say that I not a big fan of Thousand Island Dressing, but my family uses it and I like it once in a while too.  The taste of this was very good and I enjoyed it.  It might be a little on the sweet side and sugar was not too far down on the list of ingredients, but my guess is that most dressings have sugar, especially Thousand Island.  Dressings are best used in moderation, but if you can't eat your salad with nothing on it, this a tasty addition.

title: Don't taste as good as original Ritz
text: Who doesn't like the original Ritz crackers?  I have always liked them, so thought I would try this WHOLE WHEAT version.  Unfortunately, while they aren't too bad, I didn't like the taste as much, at least compared to original Ritz.  Just not quite a winner in my book, but if you want to eat quality crackers that may be a bit healthier with the whole wheat, these may work for you.  Buy just one box first to try them.  I have 6 boxes and will probably end up giving away several boxes.

title: Good tasting chip; lots of small pieces
text: These are decent tasting chips.  Perhaps not as good as some I have had, but tasty enough to consider getting again.  They seemed to have the right amount of salt too and nice crunchiness.  The only issue I had with these is that many of the chips came pretty badly broken up and it wasn't from the shipping, as they were well packed.  At some point from the time they were packed and when they got to me, they were broken into a lot of pieces.  The bag was not crushed, so it seems like they may have been bagged that way.  Not a big issue for us, as we usually make nachos that we eat with a fork with tortilla chips, so we break them up some anyway.  These are all pre-broken for us!

title: YUMMY; CAN'T GET ENOUGH
text: Very good; nice and cheesy,  but seem to have a lot of salt.  The canister doesn't hold very many, so it is possible to eat a whole can in one sitting or half a can, if you have some self control.  Twice I bought a multi-pack of these and they seemed small and didn't fill the whole can.  Might have been poor production runs or they were old and lost moisture, but sometimes they are plump and delicious and fill up the can fully.  Other times the cans are only 3/4 full.  I love them, but I try not to get them too often.  They seem just as good as when they were originally produced.  They disappeared for several years and now are back.  Get them while you can, as it is possible they could go away again.

title: OKAY, BUT NOT GREAT
text: Both my sister and I thought these bars were just okay, but nothing we would want to try again.  As other reviewers have noted, they are very dry.  I am not a big fan of anything coconut, but the coconut was not overpowering.  My sister's friend liked these a lot, so I guess it is just a matter of taste.  They do contain a lot of good ingredients and low sugar and low carb, so if you need to watch your sugar and carb intake these are good to try.  I am always looking for low carb snacks, as I have diabetes type 2, but these don't taste good enough to me to make it a snack preference.  Probably will not get them again.  Worth trying once; you might like them.

title: Lots of good ingredients; decent taste; low or no added sugar
text: Too often this type of bar contains sugar and often too much sugar.  The ingredient list on these bars does not list sugar or any form of sugar that I could see.  They do contain coconut nectar, which appears to be the only thing that would most likely give it some sweetness.  You might call that a sugar; I don't.  The bars are not overly sweet, but sweet enough to taste good.  The overall taste is reasonably good, although I didn't taste that much peanut butter flavor.  It doesn't actually appear to contain any peanut butter, but contains peanuts, along with other nuts and seeds, all listed as organic.  I have tried several kinds of this type of bar in the past and this is the best tasting so far.  The only issue I have with these bars is the price.  They are almost $4 a bar in a quantity of 8 and that can get expensive really fast if you eat many of these.  For those of you who need to worry about gluten (most of us don't), these are gluten free.  Also they are non-GMO.  I am not a big fan of this type of product, but if you are, you probably won't find anything comparable that is any healthier or any tastier.  The only drawback to these is that they apparently require refrigeration, so they aren't that portable.

title: Delicious; makes a great cereal topping too
text: I am reviewing the walnuts and Berries version of this product.  I give this high marks for taste, convenience and the healthy ingredients.  Walnuts are presumably one of the best nuts for us to eat.  Blueberries are full of antioxidants and cranberries can help prevent urinary infections.  This tastes great too and I found that it works just as well as a cereal topping as it does as a salad topping.  What's not to like?  These are glazed nuts and berries, which means they have sugar added.  They also have sunflower oil, but it appears to be the healthiest kind of sunflower oil.  Because the cost per serving is high, it is far less expensive to buy your own berries and walnuts and then when you add them to your food you aren't getting the sugar and oil that this mixture contains.  Walnuts tend to get rancid fast, so these sealed packs undoubtedly keep the nuts (and the berries) fresher for longer.  I see this as a very convenient item to quickly add a tasty and reasonably healthy topping to your salad or cereal.  Other than the sugar, oil, nuts and berries, there are no other ingredients, so you aren't getting preservatives and other junk.  I would say it is one of the healthier toppings you can get, other than doing your own.

title: Good Quality; Doesn't taste that buttery
text: Mostly I had given up eating microwave popcorn, as it often contains oils and ingredients that are bad for us.  I was attracted to this because it says it contains no artificial preservatives, flavors or dyes and it is non-GMO.  There are not very many ingredients and the only ingredient that I saw that was not so good is palm oil.  I suppose for good popcorn, you need some oil, although you can make your own popcorn in the microwave without any oil, in a paper bag.  The only problem with making your own is that if you want it to taste good, you have to put salt and butter on it afterwards and it is difficult to get the kernels coated consistently with just the right amount of butter and salt.  This popcorn has just the right amount of salt and each kernel has a buttery taste.  The kernels pop up very nicely with very few that don't pop.  The kernels are soft and delicious. No hard hulls, as some cheaper popcorn contains.  This is low fat and low calorie.  This is probably about the healthiest microwave popcorn that you are going to find.  What I didn't like that much is that it didn't taste that buttery.  I like very buttery popcorn, but of course the more butter you put on it, the more fat and calories you add.  If you love popcorn, this is a good choice for you if you don't want to make your own.  Have used this brand for years and they make about the best popcorn there is.  And Orville Redenbacher was a real person (died 1995); not an actor with a made up name.  Some reviewers mentioned a pop-up bowl, but the version I got seems to just be a regular paper bag.

title: This is a BIG can; taste is good; some not so good ingredients
text: As most of the other reviewers have said, the taste of this item is pretty good.  There is adequate meat and the sauce is good (the cheese in the sauce probably helps).  I am a little concerned about some of the ingredients, such as sugar and MSG, but based on the order on the label, the amounts are possibly quite minimal.  Also MSG is controversial; apparently there are no human studies that show it as being harmful in small amounts.  Some people are sensitive to MSG, so you might not want to eat this if you are sensitive to it.  Many processed foods contain some less than desirable items (sugar or some form of it most often and of course salt); this is probably no worse than many other things that most of us consume.  This is not advertised as a health food and it seems safe enough to eat once in a while.  When I ordered this, I didn't realize that it was such a big can.  Unless you can consume the whole thing within a reasonable amount of time, best to avoid this large of a can.  You can, of course, split up the can in several containers and probably freeze some for later use.  This is really best for very large families, restaurants or places where multiple meals need to be served.  To visualize the size, think about 12 to 13 cups in the #10 can that this comes in.

title: Not very tasty
text: I had big hopes for this bar, but it was a little disappointing in taste and consistency.  It is a pleasant enough taste, but not that much flavor and not really "crunchy" as the name implies.  It is more of a soft bar.  It has a some good ingredients and if you need to increase protein especially if you are vegetarian, this could be a good way for you to do so.  I assume it probably has very low sugar, which might be why I didn't like it as much as less healthy bars that are probably more like candy.  It mentions two types of sweeteners on the label - tapioca syrup and brown rice syrup; I don't know anything about either one.  Just didn't care for the taste that much, but you might be okay with it.  Worth trying as it seems quite nutritious.

title: Delicious and Moist
text: I am not a big fan of granola bars, but wanted to try these due to the caffeine boost that they provide, as I don't drink coffee or soda.  Was a little reluctant, as caffeine can keep me awake, so have to take it early in the day.  Also tends to make me urinate more.  These contain about 3 times the caffeine that a soda does, so I was concerned about them maybe making me jittery.  As it turns out, I didn't have any problem with these.  Didn't notice much of any energy boost either, but have only tried one so far.  The cinnamon bun flavor is very good; moist and delicious.  Granola bars tend to be a bit on the dry side, but these are not.  They don't even really taste like granola bars, as they are better.  These are a great pick-me-up and ideal for someone who can't drink coffee.  Being diabetic, I worry some about carbs and I was told to limit myself to 30 carbs for a snack.  These come in at 27 carbs, so are within the recommended limit as a snack item or for a fast breakfast on the run when you can't stop for something else or take the time to drink a cup of coffee.  If you substitute these for coffee in the car, you won't spill hot coffee on yourself either.  Handy item for campers or hikers too.  No need to mess around making coffee to get your caffeine boost.

title: VERY DELICIOUS; BAGS ARE SMALL; A LITTLE PRICEY
text: I am not a big fan of jerky because some of it requires a lot of chewing and is too tough.  This jerky is very tender and delicious.  I immediately went through two bags of it, as it was so good.  Just the right amount of spice; not too hot and not too mild.  The sweetness level was perfect for me too.  There is not much in each bag and it is a little pricey; but it is worth the price for an occasional treat.  Best jerky I have tasted.

title: Great taste; contains too much coumarin
text: I got this because the description made it sound like it was better than Ceylon cinnamon.  I thought I could perhaps make my own cinnamon capsules cheaper than what I pay for the Ceylon cinnamon.  It does have a stronger flavor than Ceylon cinnamon, but I didn't care for the smell which is not as nice as Ceylon cinnamon.  Ceylon is the only "true" cinnamon and most other kinds are all Cassia cinnamon, including this Saigon cinnamon.  Cassia cinnamon contains high amounts of coumarin, at least in comparison to Ceylon cinnamon.  Courmarin can cause liver damage, even in small amounts in sensitive people.  I stopped taking regular Cassia cinnamon some time ago and now only take Ceylon cinnamon, which has very low amounts of courmarin.  Once you have experienced the smell and flavor of the real Ceylon cinnamon, you won't want to use Cassia ever again.  In fairness, this Saigon cinnamon does have a pleasant taste and the fact that it is organic is certainly a plus.  If you must use Cassia cinnamon, this is a great choice; just be aware that the coumarin levels are higher than most other Cassia cinnamon.  The courmarin content is the main reason I can't give this a higher rating.  In comparison to other common Cassia cinnamon, this gets 5 stars.

title: COARSER THAN REGULAR PEPPER
text: This is indeed a coarse ground pepper.  I tend to agree with other reviewers who indicated the flavor is not quite as nice as expected.  It does have some zing.  I like the fact that is is organic, non-GMO and Kosher, which are all positive things.  I don't grind my own pepper so this is good for my uses; as good as any ground pepper and better than some.  Finer ground pepper tends to have a lot of powder, which you won't find here.  This seems like it will be a great topping to season meats, especially steak.

title: Very Convenient; Taste is great
text: Who wants to spend time peeling and chopping garlic?  This is so convenient.  We fried pork chops with this garlic and they came out tasting really good.  Seems like it tastes just about as good as regular garlic.  Very happy with the product.  The same company makes good salad dressing too.

title: Expensive, but well worth the price; a premium candy
text: Best caramel I have ever had.  Very soft and chewy.  Expensive, but well worth it.  Quite gooey, but don't seem to stick to your dental work too much.  They are wax paper wrapped, so they don't stick to the wrapper either.  When you want a premium caramel, you owe it to yourself to give these a try; be careful, as they are addicting.

title: Yummy, but a little pricey
text: These are really tasty, but a little on the expensive side unless you can find them at a regular grocery.  I got them the first time at Walmart, but it was a closeout item there, so couldn't get them again, except online.

title: High Quality Candy; Soft and Buttery; not sticky like most caramels
text: Definitely a gourmet candy.  Best caramel I have ever had.  The first one I ate was very soft and I thought it was because I live in a hot climate and it came right from my outdoor mailbox.  After the product had cooled to room temperature, the consistency was still the same.  Very soft, melt in your mouth.  Unlike most caramels these are not sticky and don't stick to dental work.  They are a pleasure to eat and feel good in your mouth.  Very buttery taste.  Supposedly mocha, which is a mixture of coffee and chocolate, but I didn't really detect the coffee flavor that much.  I am not a big fan of coffee flavored candy, so that was fine with me.  The chocolate is not overpowering, so I would say that the mixture of coffee and chocolate is just about perfect, at least for my taste.  They are wax paper wrapped, so they don't stick to the paper either, when unwrapping.  These are high quality and perfect as a gift.  Better than expected.  I would get them again and would feel confident in giving them as a gift.

title: Good flavor; convenient
text: Used this to make chilli and it came out quite good.  The convenience of having everything chopped and ready is nice.  The only issue with this product is the price; it is a little pricey, but you pay for convenience.  Good quality product with great taste.

title: Not really good for snacking; better as an addition to other food.
text: Would like to have given these 5 stars, as Fisher makes good quality nuts and these seem to measure up to their standards.  The problem that I encountered is that I got these for a snack and then discovered that they probably are not the best nuts for snacking.  They have a taste that takes some getting used to.  I have enjoyed black walnuts in things like cookies or ice cream, but just the plain black walnuts don't really appeal to me.  Walnuts are one of the healthier nuts for us to eat, so I was hoping to use these as a healthy snack.  Unfortunately they did not taste that good to me.  I recommend these for baking and as an addition to other foods (such as salad).  My sister plans to put them on her oatmeal.  I am sure it will jazz up the oatmeal.  As others have pointed out, walnuts have a short shelf life so you need to refrigerate or freeze them if you aren't using them up quickly.

title: Good quality nuts; nice size slices; great for toppings
text: Fisher has always produced quality nuts and these are no exception.  Nice slices and ideal for topping things (salads, desserts, etc.)  Not much you can say about nuts other than crunchy and tasty.  Over time certain nuts can get stale, rancid or too dry, but these were fresh and in perfect condition.  Had some crumbs, but that's to be expected with sliced nuts; there wasn't an excessive amount of crumbs.  Also good for snacking.

title: Not that special, but tasty and appears to be low sugar
text: This particular variety really seemed to me not much more than "glorified" apple sauce.  It tastes about like apple sauce with chunks of apple it it.  Not much strawberry flavor.  It has a good taste, but nothing that special or interesting for me to want to try it again.  This appears to be reasonably low sugar, which is a good thing.  It's only 20 carbs per serving cup.  No high fructose corn syrup and doesn't have a lot of other additives or preservatives.  I would think this would be a somewhat healthy snack to give children that is not overly processed or overly sweetened.  Or a much healthier substitute for a high sugar dessert.  While I probably would not try this combo again, I would like to try some of the other fruit combinations that sound interesting.  This is worth giving a try and if you do try it, try a few of the different varieties to find the one or ones you like the best.

title: Great mixture of quality Ingredients; Tasty with no added salt; as good as it gets for a trail mix
text: Too many trail mixes are loaded with salt and sugar.  Some have those little candy coated chocolate pieces.  No candy pieces in this mix.  The fruit pieces (banana, mango, pineapple and papaya) are sugar glazed, but they are not overly sweet.  The nuts are unsalted and they are nice whole nuts or half nuts; not lots of small nut pieces.  In fact the ingredients do not list salt at all, which is amazing, since so many products do contain at least some salt.  There might be some sodium, as the nutrition label lists 20mg per serving (1/3 cup).  This seems like a healthy snack and certainly much better than many snacks that a person might choose.  Great for kids.  There is a good mixture and distribution of the various ingredients and not too much of any one item.  Cheaper snack mixes include a lot of peanuts because peanuts are cheap.  This has plenty of cashews and Brazil nuts in addition to peanuts.  There were quite a few raisins, but really not excessive.  The only thing in the mix that I didn't care for much was coconut chips.  I am not a big fan of coconut anyway, but they seemed a little tasteless.  There is not much of anything wrong with this mixture, but if you have certain allergies be aware that it is packed on shared equipment with Milk, Egg, Wheat, Soy and other tree nuts.  Could be trace elements of these in the package.  Also because this is a tropical assortment, the ingredients are from several countries:  Brazil, India, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam and USA.  I wouldn't worry about that, as food products sold in the U.S. have to meet certain standards and the product is undoubtedly completely safe.  I think this assortment is about the best you can get, short of mixing your own.  I would buy it again when I want a sweet treat that's not loaded with salt and that is reasonably low carb.  I think this might be suitable for diabetics, which I am.

title: BEST TASTING OLIVES EVER
text: These are best olives I have ever had and it's the only olive that I buy now.  You can buy them online, but with shipping they come in at over $7.00 a jar.  For the taste, they are worth it, but you can find them in some local stores much cheaper, with a little searching.  The Wal-mart near me had them for a while, but discontinued them.  Found them in an upscale grocery and bought several jars at around $4 to $5 a jar.  Again, well worth the price.  If you can't find them any other place, by all means order on line.  I was about to do that, but then was concerned about how long it would take me to go through 6 jars.  Other competitors with Mezzetta just don't measure up.  I do recommend you try a single jar first, as the taste is a little different than other green olives.  I love them; my sister is not as keen on them.

title: NOT A BAD PRETZEL, BUT NOT MY THING
text: To be fair, I have to say that I am not a big fan of bagged crunchy pretzels.  After you have had large soft bakery made pretzels anything else pales in comparison.  That being said, these seem about the same as any other bagged pretzels.  Snyder's makes good products and I was expecting something a little more tasty and interesting, so wanted to give them a try.  If you like a crunchy, bagged pretzel, these are as good as any and probably better than some.  They don't seem overly salty, which is a plus in my mind.  A big plus is the "organic" designation, so if that's important to you and you like this kind of pretzel, I would suggest buying these instead of any that are not designated as organic, as they MIGHT be a little healthier.  I can't think of any reason why you wouldn't like these and they are a nice small size for easy snacking.  Bag didn't seem to have any broken pieces, so quality control must be good on these.

title: Best packaged crunchy, thin chocolate chip cookies I have tasted but most of the cookies were crumbled into bits and pieces
text: I love chocolate chip cookies, but usually am not much of a fan of packaged chocolate chip cookies, as then often aren't very good.  These are an exception.  Thin, crispy and delicious, I couldn't stop eating them.  If you don't have Celiac disease, you probably don't need to eat gluten free, as these are; however, I did read not long ago that certain people are of the opinion that we would all be better off not eating gluten.  I have no idea if gluten has a flavor or makes things taste better or have a different consistency, but if it does any of those things, you will never miss it being gone from these cookies.  I gave these 5-stars because they are so tasty, but unfortunately my box came with the cookies mostly broken into small pieces and crumbs.  There probably wasn't more than 5 or 6 whole cookies in the box.  I don't know if this is a manufacturing problem or shipping, but if you buy these in a store, I recommend shaking the box to make sure you don't hear a lot of crumbs rattling around.  If I had wanted to top some ice cream with these, they were perfectly crumbled for that!  The crumbs tasted as good as the whole cookies.  They were not exceptionally dry, so not quite sure why they broke up so much, as the box only had very slight crushing.

title: BEST TASTING CHIPS I HAVE HAD IN A LONG TIME - ADDICTING
text: I love these chips, but when I tried to buy on Amazon, there are not in stock.  Walmart was the only grocery carrying them and my closest Walmart doesn't have them any more.  Went to another Walmart that was supposed to have them in stock and they didn't have them either.  I wonder if the company has stopped making them or has halted production for now?  I don't like things too spicy or hot and these are ideal; not to hot and not so mild that you feel a little heat.  My wife loves these too.  I hope they come back again.

title: DELICIOUS!
text:
I don't much like sweet potatoes or things made with sweet potatoes, but these are really very good. They have an unusual taste that is hard to describe. It's a little on the sweet side; perhaps too sweet for some; great for others. Popchips (any variety) are not quite as hard or crunchy as regular potato chips, but that seems like a plus to me. They have a nicer consistency than potato chips. I have tried other varieties of Popchips too and I like them all. This one is of the more interesting flavors and it is sure to be a big hit. Popchips are far less greasy than regular chips because they are not fried in fat. Not sure of the exact process, but the result is a wonderful chip that I prefer to regular chips. I wouldn't call these healthy, but certainly better to eat than regular greasy chips.
I would eat Popchips all the time over other chips, except that the bags are small and they are a bit more expensive than other chips. The extra you pay for these is worth it if you like this style of chip. I can't imagine anyone not liking Popchips, although perhaps not everyone would like this flavor. Give it a try and you will most likely be hooked.

title: They laughed when they first saw the size of the container..
text:
When a tremendous canister of cocoa arrived, oh sure, they laughed. "You'll never get through that!"
We're actually almost done with our second can now and no one is laughing anymore.

This is a great cocoa, and you don't need to add sugar/sweeteners at all. Just two generous tablespoons to roughly 14-16 oz of warm milk. It's full-bodied and lovely to sip on colder days. Don't be put off by the size of the can, if you are a regular cocoa drinker you will see your way to the bottom of the can sooner rather than later. Trust me.

title: Delightful
text:
I bought a container of this for my mother on Mother's Day (she gets it tomorrow). But, I bought one for myself, as well.. You know, just to make sure it was suitable for my mother and I had to make sure it wasn't poisoned (insert poker face).

The candies are soft and supple, flavorful and enticing. Definitely worth the cost, these are the real thing. Much like sirens calling the sailors to shore, I find myself back at the kitchen cupboard time and again to have another. I highly recommend them, unless you are trying to diet!

title: Priceless.
text: My partner was tearing through the Christmas wrapping and froze while his mind processed a large box marked Reese's in his hands.  Then, the biggest grin ever once the hampster-on-a-wheel inside his head registered the 36 packages of peanut butter cups inside the box (72 cups total).  Worth every penny.

title: Get em
text: Soft and chewy. Awesome quality and taste. We love them. Gifted them to my mother in law as a house guest gift. They are her favorite now.

title: Great collection of flavors
text:
The cinnamon cream is by far the best. I plan to order just that flavor by itself (assuming it's still in stock).

The pumpkin spice is the next best one. If you like pumpkin spice lattes you'll love these caramels. I would like the spice flavor to be a little stronger, but not complaining.

The caramel apple one is kind of hard to tell the difference from a non-flavored one. Also the package I got was not super fresh, they kind of disintegrated in a strange way as if they were somewhat stale.

Overall great value if you like different flavors. The cinnamon cream ones have a different textured center that makes them really creamy and enjoyable. I would order the whole collection again just for the cinnamon ones.

title: Very tasty but quite sticky
text:
These are very tasty and have about the right amount of salt. They are also extremely sticky and melt easily, even at room temperature. When melted they can be difficult to get out of the wrapper. If shipped in summer months they will likely arrive melty and with the wrappers quite oily. For me it wasn't a big problem, I just stuck them in the fridge.

I prefer to eat them just after being chilled in the fridge as they are not quite as sticky. They become easier to chew.

If the stickiness could be solved, I would give these five stars.

title: Heavy on the ammonium, light on the licorice and salt
text:
I didn't care for these. The value is great, you get a lot of them, but I just couldn't finish more than a handful.

It seems like the production process somehow emphasizes the ammonium part of the ammonium chloride (salt) flavor, rather than the salt part. Also, there is very little licorice flavor as a backdrop, so it seems more like sucking on a piece of raw ammonium.

I read in one of the other reviews that leaving them out of the bag prior to consumption helps eliminate the ammonium flavor, and it does, but for me, that's not enough to make them enjoyable.

If you want quality Dutch salty licorice, go with Gustaf's double salt coins in the grey bag (if you like the salt flavor with a little licorice), Katjes herrings (if you like the licorice flavor with a little salt), or Lakrids by Bulow (no. 2 / salty) if you want the perfect balance between the two.

title: Great flavor, a little too sticky
text:
The flavor of these is perfect - bold black licorice with just enough sweetness (maybe a little overdone but that's fine).

The texture of these is my only nitpick - very chewy and sticky. I don't mind the chewiness, but they stick to the teeth. I normally don't mind a little stickiness but it crosses the line, so I probably won't get them again. If they were a bit cheaper and less sticky I'd be a return customer for sure.

title: Very intense flavor
text:
I like to think that I am a huge fan of licorice flavor. I thought that very strongly until I tried some of these.

I dislike giving them a low rating because the flavor is too intense - that would be punishing them for doing things right. Rather, I was just mistaken about how much I actually like the flavor of licorice. I found them far too intense for my taste, but these could be enjoyable to someone that likes licorice ten times more than I do (perhaps such people exist - I like my black licorice quite a lot and seem to be in the minority with that view).

In short, this is clearly a high-quality product that does exactly what it is intended to do: knock your socks off with licorice flavor. I recently decided I'd rather keep my socks on.

title: The perfect balance of salty licorice flavor
text:
I have tried a litany of licorice products in search of one that is neither so salty it overpowers the licorice flavor, nor too mildly salty as to not even be satisfying. I believe this product has found the perfect balance between the two.

If you are unfamiliar with Dutch salty licorice, you might be a little put off by this at first, so I recommend 'warming up' to the taste of salty licorice with other products like the Katjes herrings with only a slight saltiness. Once you've acquired the taste, then you can try these with a more discerning palate. I submit that you'll not regret spending the time to do so.

If you're already a fan of Dutch licorice, buy these immediately and I would be surprised if they don't become your favorite right away.

I couldn't stand Dutch licorice the first time I tried it (but the first one I tried was a double salt variety). I have since come to enjoy them very much. Gustaf's double salt really hits the spot if you want an intense punch of salt without much licorice flavor, and the Katjes herrings are on the other side with just enough salt to notice. These Lakrids have hit the spot precisely in the middle, and it's fantastic.

Besides all this, the texture is very nice. Some might not like it, but it is sort of a breakaway / plastic feel, except far superior to something like Twizzlers. With those, you're actually eating plastic. With these, it's real licorice and you can tell. My main gripe with most salty licorice is that you can't even taste any licorice flavor, but with these Lakrids, it comes through loud and... savory.

title: Good but not the best
text: This has a little more of the bitter ammonia taste and less of the enjoyable salty licorice taste, so I don’t like these as much as some of the other similar products which have less of the ammonia aftertaste. If you like the ammonia taste (and I understand some do) then these might be your favorite.

title: Taste like plastic
text: I dislike the plastic flavor. Wanted to try them. Disappointed.

title: Delicious
text: Sweet and soft caramel.  Very fresh tasting.

title: Inner seal to food product was broken.
text: The containers inner seal was broken. Not using this one!

title: Inconsistent,  stale, old -tasting.
text: I had ordered a package of 5 or 6 bags, two had good tasting nuts. In fact I really enjoyed them. The others had like an old mildew like taste.

title: Perfect texture and flavor
text: They were much softer carmels than I was expecting but the flavor was wonderful. But the container was much, much smaller than I was expecting. I was going to subscribe to get them every month but cancelled after seeing the size tub they arrived in.

title: It is exactly what it says it is. But it was a little sweet.
text: It was a little too sweet for me. And dried cantaloupe was a little weird tasting.

title: Disappointment
text: The product wasn't as soft or as fresh as expected. I'll not be buying this again.

title: Nut butter
text: I work with students with extreme peanut allergies these were an excellent substitute for protein bars!

title: Bulk pasta
text: Great price on pasta! 8 boxes for less than 10.

title: Fantastic masala!
text:
Individually wrapped bags were as fresh as could be. Flavor is second none.
This is a true Indian Masala Chai. Warming and cozy aromas give way to a very satisfying cup of tea. Add a splash of milk and a fash of sugar for a creamy, cozy-spice treat.

title: If you love cardamom and chai, you will love this tea!
text: Fantastic cardamom chai!! Love that the bags are individually foil wrapped. The taste can't be beat.

title: Sour coffee? Yes.
text:
Not quite a coffee snob here, but do prefer coffee that is complex, smooth, not too bitter, and just roasty enough. This was none of those things. It almost had a sour flavor, to the point that I double checked the expiration and did a nose test on my creamer to make sure that hadn't expired.

I didn't get any notes of vanilla or macadamia. Made one pot, ended up pouring that out and then tossed the whole bag.

Life is too short to suffer through a bad cup of coffee.

title: Big bergamot
text:
Not the most natural Earl Gray.
High bergamot.
Low citrus.

title: Best low bergamot Earl Grey
text: Beautiful Earl Grey, smooth and delicious. I'm not a huge fan of bergamot and although this has it (as do all of this type of tea), it's mild and not overpowering.

title: Great, smooth cuppa!
text:
Smooth and easy to drink. Reminds me of a good cup of diner coffee...simple but hits the spot!

Not bitter or overly roasty.

title: Best ones yet
text:
As a veteran of shirataki/konjac noodles, I can say these are BY FAR the least fishy odored ones yet!

Highly recommend a quick boil for firmness flower by dry pan frying to reduce moisture and allow for sauces to stick to them better.

Great texture. Yes, you know it's not pasta...but these are quite a solid substitute (with a little work) to save the empty carbs and calories!

title: A little goes a long way
text: Love these syrups for flavoring coffees! I add some of this to my almond milk prior to steaming/foamimg...they make excellent flavored cappuccinos. They are concentrated Flavia d very sweet, so a little goes a long way

title: It's delicious
text: Okay, salami? Truffle? What isn't there to like?! Your charcuterie platter needs this salami. Slice super thin, on a bias. Bellisimo!!!

title: DELICIOUS
text:
Delicious!
Taste just like the sugar version of the bread & butter spears. The only thing you'll miss is the empty sugar carbs!

title: Yummy!
text: This is a delicious mustard! Goes great with traditional brats and even better with pretzels!

title: Review from a tea snob
text: I have had tea from China, Japan, Germany, Britain, as well from several other countries. I am an official tea snob. When others exclaim “this tea is great!” I take a sip and think: it’s slightly bitter, and the main notes are not dominant enough. This tea is in my top 10. It is quite smooth and has a good flavor. Here is a tea snobs tip: Never steep your tea in too hot of water. It will make your tea bitter. If you buy hot tea at a coffee and tea house, always ask for a cup of ice with it. They tend to give you almost boiling to boiling hot water. Add a few ice cubes and stir. Then add your tea bag. The water does need to be hot, but not scalding hot. If you don’t like strong tea, and would like to get more cups from your tea bag, then steep your first cup for only a 30 seconds or so. Squeeze the excess water and set aside. Next time you use the same bag steep longer. Each additional cup you make will be slightly weaker. I usually limit this to 3 times and toss.

title: Taste good
text: I didnt realize that these were slices of freeze dried shallots. I really wanted them in smaller pieces so I tried to chop them up...didnt work..hard as a rock and everytime the knife blade hit the slices they went flying!! All over the kitchen lol.. I soaked them in water for a bit then drained them and tried again. Went a little better except for the thicker slices. I wanted them tiny for dips, but I guess if you use them for cooking and the slices work fine. Good product otherwise..just depends on what they are for.

title: For me..not worth it.
text: I have always loved Folgers coffee. I heard about this new one and couldnt wait to try it. I have the med dk roast flavor. I made my first  pot and this one didnt really have that "ahhhh smells good" scent. It was stronger even though I only used 2 scoops instead of 3. It just didnt have that coffee flavor..very mild taste. I even added some sugar free flavored syrup to help it. I think I will stick with the classic roast.. Now, packaging, this canister is not air tight at all. The lid does not snap on or twist it..it just sits on the can loosly so you can never pick it up by the lid. Not a very good design on this one.

The texture is good not hard or too soft and they do not stick to your teeth! Good candy for everyday or to put out for the holiday guests.

title: Yum!

title: Pretty good if you get a chocolate craving
text: This bag has 8 individually wrapped pieces of chocolates. They are a good size about 1 1/2 in square. My first bite, I thought it was overly sweet. It is milk chocolate over a thin layer of a white, vanilla center. It is not a hard or drippy soft filling. When I finished the whole piece, it was ok and the sweetness had mellowed.  They would be nice in a candy dish or jar.

title: Great candy!
text: This is a candy from my childhood. I was so excited when my order came..but..the box was smashed a bit and totally retaped. I opened it and there was the candy tray wrapped in the tiny holed bubble wrap with clear packaging tape around it. When I tried to cut it carefully there was no way to do it because the tape was stuck on the cellophane wrap. I had to tear it off exposing half of the candy to the air. I was worried they would dry out so I had to put them in a zip lock bag..so much for presentation. The candy pieces themselves were not damaged at all. I dont think the damage was from Amazon. The candy was fresh and was just like I remembered them as a child..very yummy! Nice coloring.

title: Nice grain except for mildew aftertaste.
text: I have never had this grain and I love to try new things. The shipping box was a bit crushed up and retaped. I opened the box and there was about a tablespoon of the grain loose, so I checked the bag and found a hole on the bottom of the bag in a fold spot. I got the stuff all over the table looking for it. I then put that bag in a zip lock bag. I had no idea of how to cook and season this so I just added the salt in the beginning. I didnt rinse it either because when I googled it, they said it only helps with the digestion. These are such tiny beads that you would need to use a fine mesh strainer which I dont have. It says to cook until boil, then reduce heat to low and cook 15 mins. After the boil, I turned dow n the heat and stirred it but it had a lot of water on top that I was worried it would be too wattery. Well after 10 mins, the water began to dissapear. As it got thicker the grain began to bubble and splurt out of the pan even as I was mixing it, so be careful not to burn yourself during this part. After the 15 mins it was nice and thick. Could add more water if its too stiff for your liking. I tasted it...to me it had a slight " mildew" aftertaste. I debated on seasoning it ut I treated it like brown rice for my seasons. It hid that mildew flavor very well. The consistancy was good..but began to get hard as it cooled. I added butter and it came out good. I am going to try other seasonings each time I make it. I did like it after this time.

title: Realistic… Maybe a little too realistic for some!
text:
I used beyond beef to make Polish cabbage rolls… This was actually my second attempt cooking with it, the first time I made a vegan meatloaf that was so realistic I couldn’t eat it and threw the whole thing out.

Several months later, I tried a second time.

I’ve been vegetarian since 2004, but even the visual appearance in the packaging was offputting due to its similarity to real meat. The reddish hued block is flecked through with coconut oil “fat”. When you remove it to a bowl, it feels like ground meat. If cooking on the stove top, it shrinks and changes color as it cooks, with a very realistic texture in mouthfeel. As other reviewers have mentioned, this definitely has an odor when removing from the package, but I did not find it overly offensive.

The cabbage roll filling was pleasantly toothsome, and the flavor reminded me of a fast food flame grilled burger.

I definitely look forward to trying this in other uses such as meatballs and sliders in the future.

As it is still a processed food, this will continue to be an occasional treat. And as one who is soy intolerant, I appreciate the fact this is one of the few soy-free options on the market.

Bottom line: if you are looking to reduce your meat intake or a vegan-friendly substitute for ground beef, you might give this a try.

title: The most delicious miso soup I’ve ever had!
text: I first discovered it shortly before PCSing from Japan and I am completely hooked; it is hands-down the most delicious miso soup I have ever had in five years. It taste completely homemade, not instant, and the luscious chunks of fried eggplant add to the overall umami. I only wish I had brought back a suitcase full as the price is exorbitant on Amazon but neither of my local Japanese markets carries this… Buy it, you won’t regret it!

title: Top-grade matcha for a premium tea experience
text:
Having lived in Japan for over four years, I am well familiar with the Japanese tea ceremony and matcha (I have also visited Uji, where some of the most prized tea is grown). Matcha is also popular as a dessert and beverage flavor in Japan.

Unfortunately, all too often in the US, products sold containing matcha also frequently contain sugar or other additives (such as powdered matcha latte mixes).

So I was very happy to see beginning with the packaging that Zenato has gotten it right. The screwtop metal container is essential to keeping the tea fresh; as soon as it is exposed to air, it oxidizes. Upon opening the bag, I was struck by the intense, complex aroma.

I used two scoops of matcha and was pleased to find it made it very smooth cup of matcha ( I made it in a Japanese tea bowl using traditional bamboo utensils for extra authenticity).

Zenato organic matcha is absolutely on par with the more expensive Uji matchas in Japan that sell for as much as $50 for a similar size tin; keep in mind that a little goes a very long way. At Japanese style tea ceremonies, there are two styles of matcha, thin and thick ( if you like a very pronounced, intense tea experience, try using three scoops in place of two).

I have also used this in a number of desserts and savory dishes as well, such as matcha tiramisu. It has a lot of health benefits including vitamins and antioxidants that make it a great add in.

This is absolutely a great value for the money and I will happily purchase again.

title: Old-Fashioned Taste and Texture
text:
I consider myself a “carmaholic”, never met a caramel I didn’t like. However , as with most things, the better the quality the more I appreciate an item. Tara’s Handcrafted Sea Salt Caramels are five star in my opinion. They compare to favorite caramels I have sampled at local markets, specialty shops and candy stores. Real butter, pure vanilla and heavy cream are some of the ingredients cooked together in copper kettles to create these smooth caramels.

If you have ever attempted to make caramel, you know that caramelization is a combination of time and temperature. The flavor and texture of these wonderful caramels is proof that this candy maker does it the right way in copper kettles. Once cooled and cut into bite-size pieces, the candy is wrapped in parchment paper. These are so smooth and creamy and do not stick to the parchment paper or your teeth.

If you love the taste and texture of old-fashioned caramels, I highly recommend Tara’s Handcrafted Sea Salt Caramels, you won’t be disappointed.

title: Delicious and easy alternative to storebought or made-from-scratch challah!
text: This is the second mix of Voilà! Hallah I tried after the Traditional Egg Bread mix. I used my bread machine's dough setting to mix and knead the dough, then used the sundried tomato mix from Jamie Geller's [[ASIN:0062207822 Joy of Kosher: Fast, Fresh Family Recipes]] cookbook to create a savory challah that is a perfect pairing for soups and salads. One thing that surprised me was that this loaf seemed sweeter than the traditional mix, although both call for the same amount of honey (two tablespoons). I'm not sure if it was how I shaped the loaf or the fact that I didn't add extra flour during kneading as I did the first time, but after shaping the loaf and baking, the braid fused together into a blob (it looked fine until I put it in the oven). The loaf of whole wheat Voilà! Hallah I baked today did the same thing. The spelt challah turned out fluffy and flavorful (and a touch sweet, as mentioned above). It was the first time I'd tried making spelt bread; I was pleasantly surprised and would definitely get this again.

title: Just like homemade, but easier!
text:
I was lucky enough to win a sampler pack of Voilà! Hallah containing all three flavors (Traditional, Spelt, and Whole Wheat) recently, and for my first attempt, I used the traditional mix and Jamie Geller's recipe for cran-rosemary challah from [[ASIN:0062207822 Joy of Kosher: Fast, Fresh Family Recipes]] which includes chopped rosemary, grated orange zest, and dried cranberries in a basic challah dough.

I mixed and kneaded entirely by hand (I used the ten-minute suggestion), and ended up with a silky dough. Once the dough was kneaded, I then kneaded in the cranberries, rosemary and orange zest and let the dough rise in a warm place for about 1.5 hours (the box only suggested 45 minutes). I then followed the instructions on the box for dividing and braiding the challah, brushed with egg wash, sprinkled with Swedish pearl sugar, and baked on my [[ASIN:B00008T960 Silpat Non-Stick Baking Mat, 11 5/8 x 16 1/2-inches, Half Sheet Size]].

The result was a gorgeous, fragrant loaf that was moist and fluffy and looked like it came from a bakery (see photos!). I am trying my second loaf of Voilà! Hallah in my bread machine this morning on the dough cycle, then will use the tomato, garlic and herb recipe from Joy of Kosher.

The bread freezes well and I'm also sure it would be fantastic for bread pudding and French toast. Our local Central Market stores have just started carrying Voilà! Hallah, and I will certainly be buying and making it on a regular basis as a time-saving alternative that tastes every bit as good as starting from scratch! Plus it's so easy, the kids can help. There are plenty of great recipes and ideas using Voilà! Hallah mixes on the Tribes-A-Dozen blog at [...] including pumpkin cheese babka, whole wheat rosemary rolls, and baked challah French toast.

title: Complex, some off flavors when eating out of hand
text: I jumped at the chance to review Scharffen Berger Markham Valley Papua New Guinea Semi-Sweet chocolate...I've been a fan of Scharffen Berger for years. I used the Markham Valley in several baked goods, including a flourless cake, cookies, and fudge, as well as sampled some of it straight. At 68%, this is a bold semisweet bordering on bittersweet that is easily one of the most complex dark chocolates I've tried...there are fruity, wine-like notes with a somewhat sour finish when eating it plain, but when used in recipes this is not a notable characteristic. The chocolate is visually appealing and snaps cleanly; however, chocolate is scored in large triangles, which can make it awkward if you're attempting to break off a small piece. When melting it in a double boiler, it did not melt smoothly and remained a bit grainy despite low temps and regular stirring. Verdict: I found the flavor a bit too off-putting when eating it plain, but it makes an interesting (if pricey) addition to baked goods.

title: Jazz up your dishes.
text: A combination of thyme, basil, savory, fennel and lavender, Herbes de Provence is a wonderful addition to meats: to use it as a spice rub on your next poultry or meat dish, simply rub with olive oil and press the Herbes de Provence into the meat. It's also a fantastically easy way to spice up your veggies; it's divine when added with olive oil and coarse sea salt on new red potatoes, or sprinkle on a pan of veggies before oven-roasting. You can incorporate it into soups, homemade breads, or sprinkle on top of pizza. The sky's the limit!

title: The perfect cup of espresso to jump start your day ...
text:
After purchasing a [[ASIN:B0001KO9S8 Gaggia 16103 Evolution Espresso Machine, Silver]] several years ago, I started hunting for the perfect Italian espresso roast. One of my first (and favorite) blends was the Illy Fine Grind Dark Roast. Imported from Italy, Illy is available in whole beans, drip grind (for regular coffeemakers), espresso grind, and pods.

When I took the Illy for a test drive, it produced an espresso with an assertive crema, something that cheaper brands such as Alessi and Lavazza did not. The beans themselves are roasted to perfection, with a caramel-like odor and nuance of flavor that I've yet to match in cheaper blends. I don't consume espresso straight, but instead whip up divine lattes using Illy Fine Grind Dark Roast as the base, although the Illy has so many rich notes, it would probably be great solo. I live in a large metro area, so there are numerous stores that carry Illy, but if you live in a small town, you may have to mail order yours.

Another plus is the fact that it comes in an airtight tin perfect for storing in the freezer (as ground coffee tends to go stale quickly). Yes, this is expensive, but still cheaper than purchasing a burr grinder to grind my own espresso beans (when I pondered buying whole Illy beans and priced a burr grinder, my estimates were between $150-400). Illy also makes a fabulous decaf blend in dark roast ([[ASIN:B0007M3E86 ILLY Ground Decaf Coffee 00493]]) that runs several dollars more, but is perfect for those late-night espresso indulgences. If you're thinking that it's too expensive, consider that a venti latte at Starbucks will set you back about $5 for ONE coffee, versus at least a dozen from a can of really great Italian coffee.

title: Just like homemade
text:
These are by far the best I’ve ever had.
If you like store bought “caramels” like braces or kraft, then these aren’t for you.

I only eat one at a time but I divide it into 3 bites & let it slowly dissolve as I savor the fresh taste and creaminess.
They may seem pricey but if you want good quality caramels, these are it.
Will definitely buy again.

title: Great tea but information inaccurate
text:
I drink tea daily especially green & black and I buy unwrapped bagged tea, loose leaf tea & variety boxes with individually wrapped tea bags.
I like the bagged tea for the variety & because I keep a few in my purse for when I’m out shopping & etc.
It included Darjeeling, Earl Grey, Evening Decaffeinated, Ceylon, and English Breakfast & English No.1.
I enjoy all the teas even the decaf as you’d never know it’s decaffeinated as its that good.
Anyway I can say definitely that the last 1-2 times I didn’t get what the photo & description indicates.
I just thought I’d pass it along for anyone interested in this variety.
It’s too bad that the manufacturer, Amazon or both would care enough to update items.
Amazons return policy isn’t as convenient as it used to be.
Doing a return now is a real pain & a lot of extra time.
Just remember, what you see isn’t always what you get

title: Good but not a true vanilla
text:
This was one of the few brands i tried when I started buying vanilla syrup to have at the house.
I liked the taste & I could find it at world market & Amazon but it was a bit thin and the color and taste was very light.
I realized i had to use a good amount of syrup in order to taste the vanilla just like when I bought a beverage at Starbucks or other coffee shops.
So I looked at the ingredients and it said vanilla flavor & vanilla extract.
I wanted a true vanilla taste so I searched the stores & found nothing, but I found 2 @ Amazon.
Liquid Planet and Sonoma Syrup Co actually use real vanilla bean in their syrup & it's amazing how much better the taste is & i use a lot less.
So if you are looking for a true vanilla taste & you don't mind paying a little extra then you can't go wrong with either one of those brands.

title: Amazing vanilla taste
text:
If you want a great tasting syrup with all natural vanilla bean then you can't go wrong with liquid planet vanilla syrup.
It's amazing how much better a product tastes when they use quality ingredients.
Since trying liquid planet I realized I use a lot less syrup because the flavor comes from real vanilla bean and the other brands I used in the past all had an artificial vanilla flavor.
I don't mind paying a bit more for a product if they use all natural ingredients because the flavor is amazing and there's nothing like the real thing.
I have only tried the vanilla so I can't comment on their other flavors they offer.

title: Recommended!
text:
Really no difference in the flavor than regular pancake mix. My children ate it with no problems and said it tasted the same as regular. Great for my daughter that's allergic to wheat! Give it a try. I paid $6.89 for just one at the local health food store. This is a much better deal!

I wanted to add that I posted this description under Pamela's Pancake mix. I have also used the Chocolate cake mix and the bread mix. Since this is posted under all 3 for some reason I will comment on those too. The Bread mix is very good. It does take a little tweaking, maybe it's my bread machine. It is very good, everyone in the family likes it. We have made pizza crust out of it for my little girl and she loves it. We have made the cookies from the pancake mix and she loves them, we add cinnamon and sugar to them before baking. The Chocolate Cake mix is very good, it is more of a dark chocolate than a milk chocolate. We make cupcakes out of them and freeze them. Nice and fluffy. She loves them too. All in all I would say that if you can't have wheat, Pamela's products are the best choice for flavor and texture. Don't buy that already made bread. It was awful, sorry I don't remember the brand but Amazon sells it.

title: Kinda disappointing
text:
The flavor of these caramels was pretty good, but the mouth feel was off. When you put the caramels into your mouth and begin to chew, your mouth gets absolutely coated. It’s almost like the caramel starts to disintegrate as you chew and it does not hold its integrity.

I was looking for a semi soft caramel that kept is consistency and didn’t coat my mouth with a buttery film.

I tried refrigerating the caramels to add firmness, and it made them slightly more tolerable.

I also tried freezing the caramels to try the texture when frozen, but was left dissatisfied by the overall texture once again.

I believe that the butter content of these is probably too high, as the inside of the container, every wrapper, and every caramel leaves your fingers with a very greasy feel.

title: One of them arrived with mold on it.
text: Bought these on fresh I actually went for the organic ones because hey I supposed they would be better quality. I tend to have problems when I buy on fresh specially with produce because they always bring the produce that is almost past due or in bad condition. As for the food sometimes they bring in opened things or sometimes cans that are in bad condition too but honestly I did not think I would have a problem with these as well. In fact in the picture there are 5 tomatoes and the ones that arrived were only 3, to this point I was a lil upset but whatever, I opened them as soon as they arrived and realized that one of them had mold on it! I really don't tend to care too much but given I got less than what I expected and that at least one of them was in bad condition I would not buy these again maybe, or just a warning they do this a lot where they put one bad fruit or veggies in between the others so they go bad faster and honestly they should check this more! It has happened way too often specially when I buy on fresh and they always have mold on them specially vegetables or fruits and it's always one of them or when they come in bags I don't know why they do this but I assume it's so we are forced to buy more just not happy overall with this happening.

title: Sadly dissapointed
text: I actually happen to love Tang and never in my life did I think that this would not taste like...well tang. I really was looking for that specific orangey flavour and I did not get it at all from this ... well this thing honestly it tasted weird extremely concentrated solution but in a strange sense because I used what was described on the bottle as right amount and it tasted very concentrated but without the orange flavour so I added a bit more and it was worse. Not really what I was expecting at all and never actually tasted the orange Tang flavour that it should've had really, I don't think I will buy this again either and in fact it made me sick as well because of how concentrated it was too very strange. I guess I'll just stick to the powder Tang really.

*Edit: They do work better for cooking certain dessert recipes and taste a lot better than regular marshmallows then cooked but if you don't care about it then I guess it doesn't matter which ones you buy.

title: Reduced one star because it almost flew out of my microwave
text: This is the weirdest thing, I have bought this brand a lot and I like the flavour and everything is good but the only problem I had is from the moment I saw the package I was like how weird, and then as I read the instructions I saw it said to just stick it in the microwave but the container is aluminum the moment I read that I thought how on earth is it going to work in the microwave if it's aluminum it could damage it, but nonetheless I gave it a try I mean what could be the worst that could happen, but to my surprise yes, the thing actually flew up at least twice and the second one it almost touched the ceiling of my microwave so no never buying this packaging again, I do regardless love the soup and flavour but I just wish they actually put more meat on it this one in particular seemed at least big enough to have big chunks and it did but mostly it was just huge chunks of potato on it as well, not the best experience so to speak.

I am happy with my purchase.

title: Sad to give a bad review to this delicious candy from my Mexico but it's bad...
text: The candy itself is not bad, I love this candy and in Mexico it's yum but I bought these here and they taste extremely old, the gummy is not ...well..gummy it feels stiff and hard to chew on, which it is kind of harder gummy but not at all like this here, I feel as though those boxes of candies were left unattended or maybe even frozen for a really long time and then reused because the candy just tasted kinda old to me. Again I used to eat this very often when I lived in Mexico so I'd know... very sad but also, if you don't have sensitive teeth like myself or you don't mind chewing on a harder type of gummy then by all means this candy is awesome. I do still like it and will still eat it because the taste is great of course but honestly I don't think I'll buy this here again.

title: Really great flavour!
text: This powder is yummy! I really liked the flavour and texture also it's great for decorating as well! That being said it does taste like acai I mean you really get it even if you taste a very small amount, I have yet to try it out on shakes but I'm going to try that as well it's really nice! title: Arrived well and super fast
text:

*Edit: This was great I used it to make okonomiyaki (obviously) but it really worked great and tasted perfect, the recipe I was following was a little weird so I had to add a lot of this to make up for it but basically just doubled the portion and it was incredible I literally used 2 cups and the rest of the ingredients (veggies, eggs and whatnot) and I made about 20 okonomiyakis I counted them. And, even better, I ate them for days it really was awesome during quarantine, and I didn't even use up the whole package. So I can make a smaller portion again as well. Great buy!

title: I liked these!
text: I'm just going to say one thing and one thing only... to be quite frank because of the coronavirus I left them outside the fridge and planned on cleaning them after they arrived, I've been doing that for a while for certain things sometimes but my point is I forgot to put them in the fridge and I got super scared when I had to clean them because I thought they'd have lost their flavour, admittedly the flavour wasn't incredible (and believe me I've tried a lot of different caviars before) but it was pretty good for ones bought online and really it felt like it didn't matter that I forgot them for a while before refrigerating and honestly, I think that's kinda important lol, so I'd give this a 5/5 because they retained a pretty decent flavour after that and still in great condition too.

title: Too sweet even if it's natural sugar... flavours are great though.
text:
The Green juice is the only one I truly liked for a healthy choice, but it's on the sweet side. I found these juices to be way too sweet for my taste, I usually buy another green juice from my local store but it's more veggie taste than fruit I like fruits but I found that these juices are just fruits and nothing else, I don't hate it but I feel as though it's way too much, if you are living only on juices and don't mind the sweetness then I guess that's good, but for detox...ehhh I'd clear away from this one, the green juice is great though because it's kind of veggie taste but still extremely sweet which makes me think it's more fruit than vegetables, and again, the taste of that one in particular is really great for a green juice or as far as green juices go, but all the others are very fruity, don't get me wrong I like them, but I have a personal stomach problem and when I eat too many sweets I get acid reflux so again, I would not buy this again, maybe the green one by itself if it was sold as that. The price is good though and if you want something to drink it's ok but again it's more on the sweet side, to be fair the flavours as far as sweet flavours go are great but they are definitely not for dieting or detoxing AT ALL! Also natural sugar doesn't mean it's good in large quantities overall I found this product "okay" but I would not drink to detox just for juice instead of your normal juice I'd buy these, again though if you are like me and are looking for a more healthy choice, I'd rather have a more green variation of this... I am probably not buying these again though, they did give me a terrible acid reaction which means they are way too sweet, I trust my body on that one.

As a side note I noticed they come in glass containers which are very heavy for their size that is, not bad actually that maintains the juice with a good fresh taste but they are a little harder to store since they do use more space, I do like the glass containers but again, they are extremely sweet I would not drink more than one of these a day not even if I was detoxing they are extreme... not a healthy choice sorry.

title: Incredible flavour! Good quality and fantastic snack! My new favourite!
text: I really am so excited to have found these snacks! They are super yummy, easy to store, good amount for the price (something I am super happy about! there's so many in one single bag so I don't run out after one/two weeks!) I guess you could say they work, since they really do give you energy but they also taste really nice I cannot wait to try the blueberry ones, sadly they had run out when I went to buy my second batch, I think this is the perfect healthy snack for everyday, this is basically my new favourite snack!!! Please never change one thing about this product I will continue to buy this indefinitely! Amazing I recommend to everyone reading this review!

title: Amazing flavour!

The fillings were actually just missing for me, some of the more acid and pinneaple tasting ones were really good though. But given that those were 2 in the whole box I would only use these for decoration, they are very pretty, not so keen on taste.

title: What a Fun Gift!
text: I ordered this for my family of 4 to try. The kit includes everything you need to make the churros. The instructions are easy to follow. We had fun making the treats. After doing so, I ordered a kit for my sister's family. I look forward to hearing how much fun they have with the kit.

title: Advent Calendar
text: This type of advent calendar takes me back to my childhood. I'm not particularly religious, but I do enjoy the biblical Christmas story. That's probably because it does remind me of my growing up. This calendar is on par with similar advent calendars sold in the stores. The chocolate is okay - definitely could be better. The price is also pretty similar to what's out there. Last year I was surprised at how much the price has gone up on calendars like this.

title: Tastes Like Water
text: This water tastes like water. There is no flavoring or additives that cause it to taste like anything but water. I did like that you can scratch your name onto the side so you can keep track of your bottle. I am not really sure if any of the added electrolytes, minerals or the pH makes it better than regular water. My first thought was it that it's a little pricey, but when I thought about it, when you compare it to other high end bottles of water at the store, it's  actually more affordable than those.

title: Tastes Great!
text: This is my first time drinking an aloe beverage. I wasn't sure what to expect. It's very sweet and it tastes great! There are some small chunks or pieces in it. I thought it was mango at first, but after reading other reviews, I think it's aloe. Either way, it tastes fine. It says vitamin C has been added to it, but the nutrition label doesn't mention vitamin C. I looked online for the benefits of drinking aloe juice and most of what I found was vague, with some being positive and some negative. From the nutrition label, the only benefit is 60 mg of Calcium per serving. Cane sugar is the 3rd ingredient and it's not a low calorie product. While I do think it's good tasting, I'm not sure on the health benefits.

title: Tastes Good
text: It has been a long time since I've had Jawbreakers. They are pretty much just as I remembered from my childhood days. They may be a little smaller in size, but I'm sure they're safer that way. They are long lasting and taste good. There are tons of them in the box! It will take a long time to get through them all.

title: Yum!
text: What a fun box! My family was quite surprised by the sweet contents of this package! The assortment of candy is fresh. They are chewy but not stale or hard. They arrived loose in the box and not inside any other packaging.

title: Fresh Tasting
text: I am a big fan of mints. I always have some on hand. These are fresh tasting. They are a bit soft which makes them easy to bite into. The  mints are individually wrapped and exactly what you would expect.

title: Strong Lemon Flavor
text: This lemon juice has a very strong lemon flavor. We used it to make cocktails and found it to be an excellent quality. I would definitely reorder this.

title: Fun Assortment of Snacks
text: This is a fun assortment of snacks. The cheesballs are so tiny! Much smaller than regular ones. There's a good mix of sweet and salty snacks. This would be great for lunch boxes or snack time.

title: Snack, Anyone?
text: This is a lot of snacks! There are 4 large boxes of snacks. Each box has 30 individual snacks. The variety is a good mix of sweet and salty. The snacks are fresh and tasty. This would be great for times when you have to bring a snack for the class or sports team.

title: Good Fruit Flavor
text: These K-Cups work well in my Keurig machine. The tea assortment has a nice mix of fruit teas. They have a nice fruit flavor. My daughter has been making iced tea with these and has been very happy with them. I didn't realize that these were sweetened when I ordered them. I am not a fan of sweetened tea of any kind. There wasn't any product info on the box.

title: Noticeable Flavor
text: This coffee has a noticeable flavor. I have had a few of these pods so far. The flavor is stronger than a lot of coffee k-cups out there. I don't love it, but in all fairness, it may just not work well with my coffee creamer. It does seem to have low acid content. It smells good!

title: Yum!
text: I have always loved banana flavored candy such as Laffy Taffy and Runts. These taste just like the banana Runts. They are fun to eat. I have them in a little clear jar on my desk. I would definitely order them again.

title: Fresh Tasting
text: What's not to love about chocolate chip cookies? These are mini sized cookies. They are soft baked so they aren't very messy compared to harder cookies. There are plenty of chocolate chips in each cookie. These are portion controlled which is great for everybody in my opinion. They are fresh tasting and delicious!

title: Interesting Flavors
text: I was really excited to give these cocktail mixers a try. The flavors are interesting and unique. The ingredients look good. They arrived safely and intact. What it came down to was the taste - I just didn't care for the flavors. They weren't bad, I just didn't like them. I tried mixing them with different spirits to see if that was the issue, but that didn't help. I found the spices were just too strong for my preference. To me it's kind of like the whole cilantro thing - you either love it or hate it. Though that is also gene related. I know taste can be subjective and what I like or dislike may be different from what others like or dislike. My palate just may not be refined enough for the flavor combinations.

title: Great Flavor!
text: These protein bars by CLIF are tasty and satisfying. It has a great peanut butter flavor. The texture is pretty good. It's not too hard and crunchy. The list of ingredients isn't over whelming.

title: Spicy
text: I have a few regular recipes that call for jalapenos. Usually I pick up the sliced green ones of the same brand. These are not exactly what I expected. I thought they would be just red jalapenos. If I had looked closer at the picture, I would have seen the carrots & onions. The can is primarily jalapenos. The first time I used some, I just used the jalapenos and I cut them into smaller pieces. The pickled veggies have a good amount of spiciness. There are a lot of cans!!

title: The Beans Taste Great!
text: This is a case of 12 large cans of black beans. I buy this brand frequently, so I know that they're good. The cans are larger than the typical 15 oz (I think that's the size I usually get), but definitely smaller than the 10# cans. These taste great as I would expect. However, as with many of the other reviewers, most of my cans were dented. I've included a couple of pictures. According to the USDA, cans with deep dents should be discarded. I would estimate that at least a third of them have deep dents. The rest are probably okay.

title: Good Crunch
text: These peanut/cassava snacks have a good crunch to them. They are tasty and satisfy the urge for something salty. Though, I would prefer if they had a little more salt. My son has enjoyed eating them. It's nice to have something new and different when it comes to snacking.

title: At Home Bubble Tea!
text: My daughter lives in an apartment at college. She and her roommates had fun making this boba tea. She reported to me that it was easy to make. She followed the directions. It tasted pretty good, not quite as good as what she buys at a nearby boba tea shop, but palatable and tasty. It's the closest she's gotten to making it at home. She says she would order them again.

title: Smooth Tasting
text: These coffee pods are not plastic and come in a resealable bag. I didn't realize that when I ripped open the bag! The coffee pods work well in my Keurig machine. The coffee tastes smooth. It's not very acidic. I have been enjoying these as part of my quiet morning routine.

title: Pretty Good
text: This coffee is pretty good.  It's not bitter. It has a mild, basic taste. It isn't too acidic. It's somewhere between the cheap stuff and the expensive stuff.

title: Strong Smell
