title: The Kraft Standard
text: I'd had non-Kraft caramels from other suppliers.  They were not as good and some were difficult to unwrap.  These are just what I'd hoped for and  will reorder when needed.

title: Tasty, but cumbersome
text: They are pricey, but very tasty.  However, even keeping at room temperature, they stick to the wrapper upon opening.  So, one must scrape with your teeth or use a knife to extract the caramels.  I put one in the fridge to see if that makes it practical.  It did, but you must then let it come to room temperature for the best flavor.  That shouldn't be a long term solution.  They need to come up with a tasty caramel that is easily opened and enjoyed at room temperature.

It’s as if something was added to them (for shelf life maybe?) that affects flavor. There is a slight flavor difference so you can tell what fruit it’s supposed to be, whether apple, pear, pineapple, but the overwhelming taste is the same. Will not be buying again.

title: Weird aftertaste

On the bright side, it mixes great in shakes and smoothies with no soapy aftertaste! Just don’t expect to mix it with just a packet of instant something and think that’s going to be enough - it’s not.

title: No ginger flavor
text: The honey is very sweet and has decent flavor on its own but there is NO taste of ginger. If there's any ginger added to this honey at all, it is so minute as to be nonexistent. By itself this is not a bad honey but about the same quality as cheaper wildflower varieties so I’d advise going for something else.

title: good flavor
text: The flavor is very good! The ginger spice is fairly strong, but in my opinion, it's a good balance between the sharp bite of ginger and the sweet honey. My only minor complaint is that the consistency: it is semi-solid which is fine for making tea but not so great for toast and the like. I prefer a softer spreadable consistency.

title: Addictive

I wasn't sure if I'd like the coconut, and I admit the very first one I was a little unsure about the coconut flavor. After that I discovered the flavors meld so well and the hint of salt is just right. They are SO good! Also, the size packages are perfect for gifting with plenty of individual treats inside. Definitely will buy again!

title: Not worth hype
text: I’m surprised by how mediocre these caramels are. The texture is only slightly better than the cheap stuff usually found covered in chocolate so you don’t notice how grainy it is. The flavor is just ok with hints of saltwater taffy aftertaste, which is just weird. Will not be buying again.

title: Does NOT ”quench”!
text: This stuff is a joke. Not only does it not ”quench” thirst, but it's also weird tasting as soon as the main flavor wears off, which is really fast! First, it's so sour the automatic watering of your mouth *could* be considered to help with dry-mouth, but honestly, most sugary gums are much juicier; then, the consistency is way off, feeling more like chewing a rubberband (ha- no bubbles for sure!); and finally, after about ten minutes or so, the flavor reminds me of those medicine gums and never gets any better. Yuck.

title: Does not live up to the name
text: I know I'm a bit of a snob on coffee flavor, but all the Bestpresso flavors taste about the same and all unpleasant, which is to say ”slightly burnt.” I will say it's better than the super cheap stuff like Folgers or Maxwell House, but honestly, that's not saying much. I guess I'll use what's left for those visitors who truly don't care about coffee flavor.

title: Very nice flavor cooking with salt stones. Not nearly as pink as it shows!
text:

More food using two salt stones.
The flavor is good. There are many minerals in these stones, and there healthy to cook with.

Actually these Himalayan salt stones have been around for many years, and are a primitive way of cooking years ago.
There also anti bacterial.
There is a sweet lite salty taste when cooking with this I think.

After using this stone, Do not run it under water, and Never use oil on Your stone.
I wipe my down with a clean dish towel or paper towel that is damp.
Do not heat up and cook with a wet stone, it can break.
Let it dry first.

If your using this allot the stones will start to go down in size but it does last for years if you use them Right. Remember there salt.

When I have used the stone I do not turn the temp on high to start out, you have to be careful not to hit this when it’s hot on anything, or it will break, although there strong, and this salt Stone seems heavier then the one I already have.

The darker pink the stone is, the richer the flavor, and the higher quality the stone is.
This stone is a bit darker on one end then the other that is fairly light!
A light stone is a less expensive salt stone.

This shows Dark pink salt rocks, but that is Not what you get for this price!
False advertising.
That is the reason I’m giving this 4 stars , although should be less!

I also have darker pink Himalayan lights, and love them.

title: Tasty, many health benefits, always use pepper with this.
text:
I have been using Turmeric for a few years when cooking for the healthy benefits, and there are many. There are minerals in this that are important for your health.
We use it for Arthritis pain, and it works.
Also if you eat a Anti-inflammatory diet that will help to diminish pain allot.

I’m older and do Not take any Meds. We just changed our diet because of my husbands heart attack, and he doesn’t travel anymore since Covid. And doesn’t eat the crap he use too.
No sugar (ok we cheat sometimes), No prepackaged processed food, Lower your salt intake, no fake foods. No potato chips etc. No red meat, No farmed raised fish, eat wild Alaskan salmon, Chicken breasts No skin, a good turkey meat if you eat meat.
No additives in food, No canola oil, no vegetable oil, no corn oil.no soy oil etc.
Actually, Avocado oil takes a high heat but is not heavily processed. Olive oil gets rancid when heated, use it after you cook over your food. Make sure it’s Extra Virgin, First cold pressed. Btw it’s hard to know what is real , as there are so many fakes out there now, it’s tricky.
We also started eating A lot of bean recipes. My husband now says he has never felt this good.

I want to say if your cooking with turmeric and you want the health benefits,
You must use Pepper with this as it will then absorb the turmeric in your body
No pepper, no health benefit. I used my pepper grinder.
When buying turmeric in capsules it should say, with Pepperine, which is Pepper just a fancy name.

I have used turmeric that is darker then this, and some the same color.
I think the color is where it comes from!
If it’s really coming from the rain forest I would think it’s organic. But I don’t like
Anything taken out of the rain forest. It’s my personally opinion.

title: A good food grade coconut is Not, refined as this is. This is  Lower grade coconut oil in my opinion.
text:
There is allot of Buzz words used in this lower grade Jarrow coconut oil.
All coconut oil is organic, as the coconut trees are Not sprayed. But being expeller pressed, and using Dried Copra coconuts is
Not the fresh coconut I would use for cooking or eating..
A good food grade coconut , is Not refined, and put through that high higher temperature, than cold press, and is not
Your higher quality coconut oil. Most of this type comes to the Usa.
Although there is other companies that make a higher grade of refined water steamed coconut oils if you do not want the coconut taste.
Virgin coconut oil is from a fresh right off the tree coconut, and Extra virgin is another Buzz word.
Extra virgin ,cold pressed is a word for a good grade olive oil, but cold pressed is for a healther coconut oil.
There is not any taste to this. This says it does not utilize solvents, but does Not say its steamed or water washed! So how is this expeller pressed, Refined?

I only give this grade to my two big dogs, and a little dog. This is still good for my dogs coat, and helps if they have skin allergies.

The only coconut oil I use to cook with, eat etc Comes from the meat of matured coconuts (not picked too early, not laid out to dry-copra).
But is virgin cold-pressed, and is Unrefined. This Jarrow is none of those to my disappointment.

I also notice Jarrow coconut oil for one tablespoon is 130 calories, and the coconut oil I use is 120 calories for one tablespoon!
Jarrow sat. fat 13g, mine is 12 g, ,but the good fat Monounsaturated 0.5g, and mine is 1g. Which is higher (much better).
A good coconut oil does Not come in a plastic Bpa jar. Coconut oil should come in a Glass jar, and store in a cool dry place.
You do not need to keep a good coconut oil in the frig. Jarrow says after opening, store in Frig!
I personally did not lke the non-taste of this, or the color.
If you are looking for Non-taste coconut oils, there are better ones then this one I believe. (I have bought the non-taste ones before)

I just made a chunky Apple sauce using these nuts.
I just cut up about 3 fresh apples into chunks (do not peel), put a little water in a pan, put in apples, add a hand full of Fisher chopped walnuts, cinnamon, & raisins, And sometimes dried cranberries also. Stir a few times during cooking..simmer with lid on. If I want it thicker after apples are soft, I take the lid off, but keep the flame on low...do not let the liquid burn, and stir. Can also,add more water. if you want it sweeter, add a very little bit of maple syrup...but you really do Not need it..I dont use green apples, but use crisp fresh apples.
You can eat this plain, or over vanilla ice cream, or top with whip cream.Or just eat as is.
Makes a healthy dessert..

title: Organic & the taste is far superior then any other white tea
text:
I love this tea . It is my absolute favorite.
It brings a full flavor to your mouth which is actually romantic, besides being such a healthy tea.

After trying other white organic tea's they have all made me feel sick after drinking them.
This is the only white tea I do not feel sick from after drinking one cup, or more.

I did call the Co. that makes this tea. He said it comes from India, and the full white flower is used in this tea, plus the roses.
White tea has more anti-oxidents then green tea has. White tea is also naturally lower in caffein.
This tea does not get my heart pounding after drinking two or more cups, as organic green tea's, or other tea's do.

Dr. Oz said white tea can help you lose weight, if you drink it everyday, and do not add milk, or anything to it.
I'm not looking to lose weight. I just wanted a wonderful full white tea that did not make me sick, and this is it for me.

This is a hard tea to find. As I notice with Amazon, the boxes change if I only want to order 16 bags , instead of the 100 bags!!!
I have the box of 16, and it looks the same as the 100 bags!

Going to order it anyway, and hope it is the same!!

Granted I am not an avid coffee drinker,however I really look forward to a good morning cup of coffee that won't burn a hole in my stomach.  I usually run one senseo pod through twice and have a perfect cup.  If anything - needs a little more of the flavor (i.e. hazlenut, vanilla) added to it.  But still very good.

title: Caution
text:
I recently bought these. The k-cup pods came all jumbled in one box, unlike in the description photo. Backing up a bit, I also ordered these in bulk last year and there were brown specs/coffee grinds all over the pods and in the box, but I couldn’t find one that had opened, so I requested a returns Now, a year later, I see they’re still being sold like this and thought I’d give them another try.

Once again, they came jumbled in one box unlike the description photo. I didn’t see any coffee grinds at first glance. So I finished the coffee I had from the grocery store and then started on these. Once I started drinking these for a couple of weeks, I noticed some weren’t as good as others. Some tasted stale and/or Didn’t have that normal ‘bite’ I like in the French Roast Starbucks coffee. I dug into the bottom this recent box and there actually was a bit of coffee grinds on the bottom and small bits I’d dirt/debris. I was going to return them but i missed the 30-day deadline.

All I can think of is Starbucks might be selling their older coffee pods or ones that had spilled grinds on them to private buyers, but then those sellers sell them to us on Amazon. They are not undrinkable, but definitely not the normal Starbucks quality or packaging. I think there are fresh and stale mixed in together. So I’m wondering what’s going. I’m going to contact their corporate offices this week. I’ll come back and update this review if I find out anything of value. I just think it’s important to let other people on here know things like this—especially since it is a large coffee purchase. Even though we might think we’re getting a great deal.. if the the quality is not there, then we’re not really getting a great deal.

I’m going to try one more bulk purchase of a different Starbucks flavor and see what happens (both of my previous boxes were French Roast). But I’ll check them out right away and if not fresh or packaged unlike the description, I’ll return them. I’m sure Amazon does not want to sell these on their site if it’s poor quality. It would just mean more returns and expense for them. (They may not even post this.) We’ll see. :)

title: Love It
text:
I just discovered vegetarian baked beans this year and find them just as delicious (possibly better) than the regular—without the unhealthy bacon/meat. (Not that this is the healthiest thing to eat, it’s perfect for when my BB craving leads me to indulge.)

The only downside is how small this particular can is. I thought it would be the regular size. This is more of a one-serving size. But then, all l had to do was read how many ounces it contained on the label. Either way, still a decent price. Still a good product!

title: Good Product and Effective.
text:
I’ve been using Smooth Move, as needed, for about five years. My daughter told me about it. Compared to other products, this has been pretty gentle on the system for me. And it doesn’t make me more constipated days after as laxatives do—as long as you take it as prescribed and as needed. (It shouldn’t be used to lose weight—ultimately it won’t work). I usually need it twice a month, but every now and then, I’ll take it weekly if I’m put on a medication that causes constipation. Its main ingredient is Senna, which is plant-based, so that’s good.

I highly recommend trying it if you have occasional digestive issues. (PS: It can’t hurt to ask your doctor about it.) :)

title: Mushy Center
text:
I LOVE cucumbers. It says one count above, but I ordered six, They were big in size which makes o think that is good, but it actually makes them not as tasty as the small to medium cucumbers. They were firm and I liked that, but the centers were like the centers of cantaloupe.

The problem with ALL of these six had soggy, mushy centers with a weird taste. I tried them raw the way I like. I peel them, cut them in half, quarter them and sometimes add a dash of salt, but have found cucumbers perfectly ripe taste wonderful without any thing else added and make a healthy snack. I also love them diced in salads. Had to throw the first two away because of the center. Then I decided to wash the skins well, cut out the middle the way you would cut out seeds of a cantaloupe and diced them with their skins which added flavor me. So didn’t have to throw them all away.

Maybe got a bad batch due to being out in the cold, but thankfully they’re not too expensive so I didn’t lose too much money. Amazon did refund one. I may try buying one with my next Fresh order and if it’s good I’ll order again. But if I get a bad batch again, I will get them elsewhere from now on.

title: Not A Fan
text:
I usually love this brand of ice cream, but I was sent Coffee flavor instead of Haagen-Dax’s’ Rum Raisin, which is the best anywhere and is seriously dangerous—in a good way. But I’m not a fan of this flavor or most coffee flavored ice cream.

Since it was delivered, I decided to try it (I mean I love ice cream). This tasted like those inexpensive brands—the ones that sell half gallons for less than the price of this, which is just under a pint. It wasn’t creamy or taste-worthy.

Haagen-Dazs ice cream is still one of the best out there. But but I prefer to stick with their specialty flavors.

title: Disappointing
text: Although this loaf was not expired, the slices tasted dry, stale and tasteless. I’ve bought Sara Lee bread before and have found  it delicious. Maybe this was a bad batch or they were compromised in transit from the manufacturer.

title: These Olives Are Okay, But…
text: I like firm olives and some in the were a bit soft, almost mushy, for my liking. They’re probably okay for cooking, but I like them in salads. I very often love Amazon food products, but I wouldn’t by these again. If you don’t mind softer black olives, these are a good buy.

title: Always Fresh
text:
Love this coffee.

Unfortunately, you have to be careful when buying Starbucks. There are some counterfeit sellers and/or sellers who overcharge and sell expired coffee. This pack was good. I try to go by the amount of buyer ratings (over 1,000 is optimal) instead of just by high ratings. A product can have five stars, but only have a very small amount of raters who might be working for the company (they buy, give rave reviews and five stars). These unscrupulous companies count on buyers who see five stars and assume that’s the best product/choice. Just a tip. It’s something I realized after many purchases.

I add a bit of olive oil and pinch of salt (personal preference) before putting them in the oven. Also love them in sandwiches (prepared like hamburgers or paninis).

Then last year a friend had me try these. Loved them.  I rarely eat food packaged in cans, but I make an exception with these. Highly recommend. title: Delicious!
text:
Perfectly salted—not too much or little. I would have given these five stars, but there were more almonds than I would have preferred. Good news—there are no peanuts. Bad news—almonds are the new peanuts. If you love almonds as much or more than other nuts, you’ll want to buy these.

It’s the best deal I’ve ever found for quality mixed nuts.

Gentle warning—these nuts are very addictive. An MNA meeting (Mixed Nuts Anonymous) may become necessary. Help is available.

title: Wonderful Buy
text:
Was so delighted to receive these three lovely stems of blue hydrangeas. When buying flowers online there’s always a risk involved and sadly I’m disappointed more than not. But I figured at this price, I couldn’t lose, and at minimum, I could dry them. I was truly surprised to see the size and quality of these simple, yet elegant blooms (a vase is not included, but these would look lovely in any container including a drinking glass or tin can).

I can’t say what others will receive, because there will always be situations that can come up when buying flowers online, but based on my experience, I will happily buy again for myself and others.

title: Packaging Discrepancies
text:
I’ve never tried nutritional yeast flakes and this tastes pretty good. I mainly bought it as a source of nutrients—especially the B-12, as I am a vegetarian who does not consume dairy products.

My main concern is with the packaging. It’s designed well and eye-pleasing, but there are a few inconsistencies: What is a Tsbp? Do they mean tablespoon (tbsp) or teaspoon (tsp)?! Either way, that’s a pretty big typo for a company, and it doesn’t give me much confidence in Kate Naturals, it’s distributer DOJO or the accuracy of the stated nutrients.

The package also says, “ingredients sourced internationally.” Do they mean the ingredients are sourced ‘out’ to other countries—or they come ‘from’ other countries? If it’s the latter, that would contradicting the company’s description above. (And what countries?)

As much as I want to love it, I remain skeptical and hope the company addresses these discrepancies. If they do, I’d be happy to upgrade my review.

title: ... (1) ocean water and cherry limeade are my two favorite drinks at sonic and (2) I use popsicles instead ...
text: My mom brought me some of these home from the grocery store since (1) ocean water and cherry limeade are my two favorite drinks at sonic and (2) I use popsicles instead of glucose tablets or gel when my sugar goes low to keep from, you know, dying. First impression was not good. I tried the ocean water first. It tasted nothing like ocean water, at all. The cherry limeade was marginally better but still not good. I couldn't quite place the taste. It was almost floral or perfume like. Then I read what others have said and yes, that's it. It tastes how a freshly cleaned public bathroom smells. Even a long time afterward, it leaves this feeling and taste in the throat that's absolutely "cleaner."
