title: Ghastly Freeze-Dried Apple Chips
text:
Ghastly. That is the only way to describe these ONETANG FREEZE-DRIED APPLES (15 Pack Single-Serve Pack, Non GMO, Kosher, No Add Sugar, Gluten free, Vegan, Healthy Snack 0.35 Ounce). The packets contain potato-chip-like apple pieces that are pumped full of air. They are totally lacking in texture, unlike traditional chewy dried apple pieces.

Even worse than the lack of texture is the lack of flavor. The pieces don’t taste like apples, or anything else recognizable. I’ve never before encountered a freeze-dried product that was so utterly tasteless and devoid of texture as these.

The freeze-dried apples are a product of China.

title: “Sweet Vanilla” Is Terrible Idea for Popcorn Flavoring
text:
This SKINNYPOP POPPED POPCORN (Sweet and Salty, Sweet Vanilla Flavors; Individual Snack Sized Bags; Pack of 24, 0.8 Ounce Bags) has excellent texture and crunch. The pack of 24 gives you 12 bags of ready-to-eat “Sweet Vanilla” popcorn, and 12 bags of ready-to-eat “Sweet and Salty” popcorn. The bags are small, but are exactly the right size to add to a brown-bag lunch.

My husband and I really disliked the “Sweet Vanilla” flavor. It seemed unnatural--just a terrible idea for a popcorn flavor--and it definitely tasted like artificial flavoring. It also left a bad aftertaste. The “Sweet and Salty” flavor was okay, but there is quite a bit of sodium (90mg) in a single bag.

title: High Quality Saffron in Tightly-Closing Storage Tube
text:
This SLOFOODGROUP PERSION SAFFRON (from Afghanistan, Grade 1 Quality, 1 Gram) arrives in a 3-inch long tube that closes tightly to preserve the freshness of the saffron threads. The saffron itself seems to be of high quality.

You get one gram of saffron--less than I expected after viewing the lttle pile of saffron shown on the product page. However, one gram of saffron is still a substantial quantity. You use saffron in very small amounts, usually by ¼ teaspoon or less.

title: Half Box Makes Angel Food Cake in Instant Pot
text:
This BETTY CROCKER WHITE ANGEL FOOD CAKE MIX (Pack Of 3 (16oz) Boxes) takes the work out of baking a delicious angel food cake. No need to separate eggs to get a lot of egg whites. You just add water to the mix and beat with an electric mixer until the batter fluffs up, then bake as usual in an angel food cake pan.

I use half a box of this mix to make a half-size angel food cake in my 6-quart Instant Pot. The batter goes into a Wilton 7" Angel Food Cake Pan (available on Amazon), and the pan is covered with tinfoil before going into the Instant Pot for steaming. You can Google "Instant Pot angel food cake recipe" for specific recipes.

title: Dry Hazelnuts Provide Crunchy Experience
text:
This MILANGO DIAMOND FINE HAZELNUT MILK CHOCOLATE (Pack of 100 Gram; 4 Count) consists of 4 packages with each package containing 8 individually wrapped chocolate hazelnut truffles. Each truffle has a whole hazelnut in the middle, but the nuts are so dry that they break into tiny bits when you bite down on the truffle.

The milk chocolate at the bottom of each truffle is soft and creamy, as you would expect from a truffle. However, there are hazelnut bits at the top of each truffle (in addition to the whole hazelnut), and the fractured hazelnuts make the truffle come across as crunchy and rather dry. The chocolate quality is average--about the same as a Hershey bar.

Overall, these truffles didn’t quite deliver the exquisite creamy satisfaction that I expect from a truffle.

title: Brew Fresh Starbucks Coffee in Your Home Coffeemaker
text:
Each of these STARBUCKS MEDIUM PIKE PLACE ROAST GROUND COFFEE CANS holds just enough ground Starbucks coffee to make four (4) cups of coffee. You just open a vacuum-sealed can, dump the contents into your coffee-maker cone, add the appropriate amount of water, and hit the Brew button. Voila! Starbucks Pike Place coffee is ready in minutes.

Four cups translates to two big mugs of coffee. The little cans keep the ground coffee fresh, and they’re extremely convenient to use. You can use this coffee with any drip system. The cans have a "Best by" date, so they're probably NOT ideal for really long-term pantry storage.

title: Slightly Smaller Granules Than Name-Brand Sugar?
text:
Generic or house-brand sugar is pretty much the same as name-brand sugar, though it may be a little lumpy. This Amazon Brand HAPPY BELLY GRANULATED WHITE CANE SUGAR (2 lb.) may have slightly smaller granules than [[ASIN:B00NTIUI3E C&H sugar]], but that’s the only possible difference that I can see.

The Happy Belly Sugar comes in a sealed plastic bag. There were no lumps in the sugar, which poured perfectly smoothly. As long as the price is acceptable, there’s no reason NOT to buy Happy Belly sugar instead of name-brand sugar.

title: Same Ingredients as Mott’s Applesauce, But Different Texture
text:
These HAPPY BELLY (AMAZON BRAND) UNSWEETENED APPLESAUCE CUPS (6 Count) have the same ingredients (apples, water, ascorbic acid) as the familiar [[ASIN:B0005XO8EQ Mott’s Natural No Sugar Added Applesauce Cups]]. However, the Happy Belly applesauce has a different texture-- it is thinner, and sort of runny.

Also, the Happy Belly product doesn’t seem to have as much apple flavor as the Mott’s applesauce. It tastes okay, but I definitely won’t be switching brands, from Mott’s to Happy Belly applesauce, any time soon.

title: Whole Cloves Have Underlying Vinegary Odor
text:
The Amazon Happy Belly brand usually offers good value for the price, but the quality isn’t 100 percent consistent. (For example, I’ve experienced considerable variation in quality and freshness of Happy Belly cashew nuts, although they’re a bargain even when the quality isn’t great). Unfortunately, these HAPPY BELLY WHOLE CLOVES (2 Ounces) seem to be one of the poorer quality Happy Belly products.

When I opened the jar, I found that the cloves had a distinctly vinegary odor underlying the expected intense, strong and sweet aroma of cloves. When I compared the aroma to that of a long-ago-opened jar of Spice Island whole cloves, the latter did NOT have the vinegary odor.

My guess is that the vinegary odor has something to do with the cultivation of the Happy Belly cloves. That is, where the cloves were grown, what (if any) fertilizers were used, and what the weather conditions were like during the growing season. It’s very unlikely that the Happy Belly whole cloves were “gone bad”, because whole spices generally last for a couple of years, and only lose strength as they age.

Still, I don’t like the Happy Belly cloves. I will be sticking with the more expensive Spice Islands brand.

title: Delicious Seasoning for Hamburgers
text:
This Amazon Brand HAPPY BELLY GARLIC PEPPER (24 Ounces) is a delicious seasoning for hamburgers. It’s a mix that’s based on bell peppers (NOT black pepper) and includes onions as well as garlic. The 24-ounce jar is restaurant-sized, and will last my family for at least a year, probably longer.

Note that the seasoning mix contains a fair amount of sodium--100mg in ¼ tsp. This amounts to 4% of an adult’s daily requirement. If you use the seasoning on burgers, you should probably skip adding additional salt. If you’re on a low-salt diet, you should use this seasoning sparingly.

title: Restaurant-Size Jar of Seasoning for Hot Thai Dishes
text: This MCCORMICK CULINARY SRIRACHA SEASONING (22 oz) is a restaurant-size jar of sriracha seasoning for hot Thai and Vietnamese dishes.  Sriracha seasoning generally consists of a combination of chili peppers, distilled vinegar, garlic, sugar, and salt.  This is a tasty version, as you would expect from the McCormick brand.

title: Delicious Organic Blueberry Juice Can Serve Two at Breakfast
text:
This POMONA ORGANIC JUICES PURE BLUEBERRY JUICE (8.4 Ounce Bottle; Pack of 12) is as delicious as it is expensive (about $5.50 per bottle). The pure blueberry juice is both tart and sweet (with no added sugar). One 8.4 oz. bottle has just under 120 calories. To serve two people at breakfast, one bottle could easily be split into two 4-oz. juice glasses, and leave both persons satisfied.

One bottle contains 10mg sodium, 120 mg potassium, and 29 g of carbohydrates (including 24 g sugars). The juice contains vitamins A and C, plus calcium and iron. The pure juice is 100% organic.

title: No Artificial Colors, Flavors, High-Fructose Corn Syrup--But It’ll Cost You
text:
These WILTON NATURALLY FLAVORED ASSORTED SPRINKLES & JIMMIES (5 jars, 8.26 oz. total) have NO artificial colors, flavors, or high-fructose corn syrup, but they’re very expensive (about $18 on Amazon). The set includes 2 jars of jimmies (1 vanilla, 1 chocolate), and 3 jars of decorations (1 pastel rainbow nonpareils, 1 heart confetti, 1 pastel rainbow confetti). The jimmies and nonpareils are 1.76 oz. each; the heart decorations are 1.58 oz. each. You pay about $3.60 for each small shaker-top jar.

The jimmies and decorations taste good. If you’re willing to pay the price for these “natural” jimmies and sprinkles, they look great on iced cupcakes, donuts, or cakes. They also jazz up pudding, ice cream cones, or other desserts. (Note that the pastel colors are very pale.) The main ingredient is sugar (1tsp. of jimmies has 20 calories; 1tsp. of decorations has 15 calories).

title: Extra-Fluffy, Extra-Clean “Sweet Quinoa”
text:
This HAPPY ANDES WHITE ORGANIC QUINOA (Non Gluten, Whole Grain Rice Substitute) is smaller in size than the [[ASIN:B00DDG8KB4 Roland Prewashed Quinoa]] that I usually order. The product description says that it’s a “sweet quinoa” variety--indeed it doesn’t taste as nutty as the Roland product--but it didn’t taste particularly sweet to me.

The Happy Andes quinoa is very, very clean, and required even less rinsing than the prewashed Roland product. Note that it requires 2 cups of water to 1 cup of quinoa for cooking; the more common varieties require 1 cup of water to 1 cup of quinoa.

I made this in my rice cooker, as usual. It came out as an extra-fluffy quinoa that was a nice consistency and change from the usual quinoa. Overall, though, I prefer the taste of the Roland product.

title: Pleasant Alternative to Buttered Popcorn, With Confusing Nutrition Info
text:
ANGIE’S BOOMCHICKAPOP LIGHTLY SWEET MICROWAVE POPCORN is a pleasant alternative to buttered popcorn. There is just a touch of sweetness to add interest, and the kernels pop into very large popcorn pieces. Microwaving in the paper cooking/serving bowl takes just 1½-2 minutes. (Do pay attention to the instructions and stop the microwave when the popping slows, because the popcorn is easy to scorch.) When hot, the popcorn is quite delicious.

The nutrition information is very confusing with regard to the serving size and calories per serving and per bag. You get 35 calories per cup of popped kernels, but how many cups are in a bag?

I finally measured the popcorn from a bag, and came out with something more than 8 cups. This is consistent with “about 2½ servings per bag”, given that 1 serving is 3½ cups of popped kernels--there could easily be about 8¾ cups of popcorn per bag. So with 35 calories per cup of popcorn, each bag must contain about 306¼ calories. But it remains a mystery where the “170 calories per serving” figure comes from, as 3½ cups at 35 calories per cup would come to 122½ calories.)

The label states that there are about 10 servings per “container”. Since there are 4 bags in a box, each bag having 2.5 servings, this comes out to 10 servings per box or “container”.

My family likes this microwave popcorn, so it gets 5 stars, despite the confusing and probably partially erroneous nutrition label.

title: Blend of Anaheim, Jalapeno Peppers Makes Chili Taste Great
text:
This STAGG LAREDO CHILI WITH BEANS (15 Ounce) is quite delicious--the blend of green anaheim and jalapeno peppers makes the chili mildly hot, and very fresh-tasting, Chili pepper, not tomato, is the predominant flavor. The mixture contains a substantial amount of meat (without the fatty, oily layer on top of the opened can that I’ve come to expect from canned chili), and plenty of pinto beans.

One 15-ounce can (about 2 cups) makes a satisfying 600-calorie lunch for one person. My only complaint about this excellent-tasting canned chili is that it is loaded with salt (1980 mg per can, or 82% of the recommended daily amount). Unfortunately, a high salt content is currently the norm for nearly all commercially packaged quick-meal products (e.g., frozen pizza, canned chili, canned spaghetti).

title: Sounds Healthy, Tastes Okay, 90 Calories Per Bottle
text:
This VITA COCO COCONUT WATER tasted fine to me, though I’m totally new to coconut water. My case of 12 large (16.9 oz.) bottles contained clear (faintly yellow-tinged) liquid, and the flavor was neutral and inoffensive. The liquid did NOT taste like coconut at all--my only prior exposure to coconut was the shredded kind used in cakes and macaroons.

The nutrition information on the 2-serving bottle says that a serving contains 460mg potassium, 70mg sodium, and 11mg carbohydrates (including 10mg sugars). I guess that consuming the product after a workout is a good way of replacing electrolytes, but a serving also delivers 45 calories. To me, this is just another form of sugary soda (without carbonation), and a product that sounds more healthy than it actually is.

title: Great Recipe, Simple to Prepare--But Ruined by Excessive Sodium
text:
This ZATARAIN’S SPANISH STYLE RICE WITH BELL PEPPERS & WHITE BEANS is a tasty combination, assembled by a great recipe for a filling side dish--but the dish is completely ruined by excessive saltiness. 0ne 2-serving box delivers 550 calories along with a whopping 1300mg of sodium.

Neither my husband nor I could finish our serving--I was reaching for my water glass after only a couple of forkfuls. (We generally aim at a low-salt diet, so the sodium is no doubt more tolerable for those who eat a “normal” U.S. diet with lots of sodium in commercially prepared foods.)

This side dish is extremely easy to prepare. Just heat water with a little butter to boiling on the stovetop, add the rice mixture, reduce heat, cover and simmer for 10 minutes until the water is mostly absorbed, let stand 5 minutes and serve. The finished texture is perfect.

title: Delicious Cherries, Over-Toasted Almonds, Not Too Hot to Handle
text:
Blue Diamond is a brand that I frequently buy when I’m buying shelled almonds, walnuts, pecans, etc. for homemade ice cream. However, I was disappointed by this snack product, BLUE DIAMOND ALMONDS & FRUIT (Fiery Ghost Pepper Almonds & Tart Cherry, 5 oz Bag). The dried tart cherries are delicious, but the almonds are toasted almost to the point of tasting burnt.

Before sampling the product, I was concerned that the ghost peppers would make it to hot for my digestive system to handle. Happily, the main “fiery” chili pepper flavor derives from much milder jalapenos. The super hot ghost pepper flavor is there, but in the background, so that the snack isn’t uncomfortably hot. (But it’s still very hot, so take care!)

If the almonds weren’t over-toasted, I would like this flavor combination very much. However, the only part that I really liked was the dried cherries.

title: Tastes Like Regular McCormick Black Pepper
text: To me, this MCCORMICK GROUND ORGANIC BLACK PEPPER (3 oz.) is pretty much interchangeable with the regular McCormick ground black pepper that I’ve used in family recipes for many years.  Because it’s organic, it costs a little more.  I trust the McCormick brand, so I would switch to this if I cared about cooking exclusively with organic foods.

title: Big Jar of Fresh, Hot Cayenne Pepper
text:
This big 6-ounce jar of MCCORMICK GROUND CAYENNE RED PEPPER is filled with really fresh, hot, ground cayenne pepper. I have a lot of family-favorite recipes that call for cayenne pepper, so this jar will be put to good use. Cayenne is particularly good in meatloaf recipes.

A large jar like this one takes up extra space on my kitchen countertop, but it provides much better value than a spice-rack-size jar. Cayenne pepper keeps well. If it loses potency over time, you can just use more of it.

title: Large Elbows Are Ideal for Classic Mac and Cheese
text:
These delicious wheat-based (not gluten free) SKINNER LARGE ELBOWS (12 oz) are the perfect size for classic mac and cheese. They’re a high-quality, elbow-shaped, dried pasta that keeps seemingly forever on the pantry shelf. In boiling water, they take about 6-7 minutes to cook “al dente”, a little longer to cook “tender”.

My family loves pasta dishes, regardless of the pasta shape. Elbows add substance to a main dish, and are especially good for catching sauces.

title: High-Quality Dried Pasta Produces Delicious Wheat-Based Fusilli
text:
These wheat-based (not gluten free) SKINNER TWIRLS (12 oz) are quite delicious. They’re a high-quality, fusilli-shaped, dried pasta that keeps seemingly forever on the pantry shelf. The twirls are orange-colored in the package, but produce fat, white fusilli when cooked. In boiling water, they take about 8 minutes to cook “al dente”, or 10 minutes to cook “tender”.

My family loves pasta dishes, especially those using the fusilli shape. Fusilli adds substance to a main dish, and is especially good for catching sauces. I used a package of Skinner twirls in a NYT recipe for pasta with grated cheddar cheese and sliced onions, and it came out great--leaving everyone with the warm, satisfied feeling that comes from homemade macaroni and cheese.

title: Huge Jar Filled With Hot Red Pepper Flakes
text:
This huge plastic jar of SPICE CLASSICS CRUSHED RED PEPPER (12 ounce) is filled with very fresh flakes of hot red pepper. Red pepper flakes are usually made from cayenne peppers, and that’s what these taste like. I doubt that I’ll be able to use all 12 ounces before the flakes lose some potency, but pepper flakes generally last for ages. Besides, when the flakes lose some of their heat, you can just increase the amount you add to your recipe.

The jar is factory-sealed, and has a screw cap that closes tightly to keep the flakes fresh for as long as possible after the seal is broken. The cap has two fliptop choices. One side has an opening that is wide enough to insert a measuring spoon, and the other side has holes for shaking out the flakes. Unfortunately, as another reviewer pointed out, the holes are too small for the flakes to pass through.

The jar label appears to have a coded expiration date. In the black square at the lower left, mine says “31538H 16.52“. It could translate to August (H) something; if you care about the date, you’ll probably have to contact the Spice Classics company to figure it out. (Try Googling “how to read food expiration date codes” for more information on coded expiration dates.)

With spices, I only check the expiration date to make sure that I’m not buying a jar that’s been on the shelf forever. Once a jar is opened by removing the factory seal, the spices inside always deteriorate, some quickly, some more slowly.

title: Huge Jar of Fresh, Fragrant Dried Bay Leaves
text:
This huge plastic jar of SPICE CLASSICS BAY LEAVES (8 ounce) is filled with incredibly fresh and fragrant dried bay leaves. Probably I’ll end up discarding some of them--it’s hard for a single household to use up this many bay leaves before they lose potency--but bay leaves generally last a long time. The big jar will keep my household stocked for a couple of years.

The jar is factory-sealed, and has a screw cap that closes tightly to keep the bay leaves fresh for as long as possible after the seal is broken. My jar, received in late November 2018, has a “best by” date of September 2021 (stamped on the side).

The bay leaves in this jar smell much more fragrant than those in my newly opened small jars of McCormick or Morton & Bassett bay leaves. Some of the leaves are broken, but they’re still perfectly good.

title: Fresh, With Pungent Cumin Smell When Opened
text:
This SPICE CLASSICS GROUND CUMIN (14 Ounce) smelled fresh and pungent when I opened it, exactly like fresh ground cumin should smell. It’s delicious in an herbed meatloaf recipe that’s an old family standby.

The 14-ounce plastic jar is huge, so the cumin will probably lose its flavor before I can use it all. However, in my experience, cumin lasts for a long time if kept tightly closed after opening. This jar comes factory sealed, and has a cap with two fliptop choices. One side has an opening that is wide enough to insert a measuring spoon, and the other side has holes for shaking out the spice.

The jar label does appear to have an expiration date, but it’s coded. In the black square at the lower left, mine says “34422H 13.37“. It could translate to August (H) something, or to December 10, 2022 (34422, or 344th day of 2022), or to something else that you’d have to contact the maker to figure out. (Try Googling “how to read food expiration date codes”.)

With spices, I only check the expiration date to make sure that I’m not buying a jar that’s been on the shelf forever. Once a jar is opened by removing the factory seal, the spice deteriorates quickly. I discard spices after they no longer smell like spices, because they aren’t doing anything for the food.

title: Best Dijon Mustard Ever!
text:
I’m in love! I don't care for the widely distributed [[ASIN:B0014CXN48 Grey Poupon Dijon Mustard]], but I like this MAILLE RICH COUNTRY DIJON MUSTARD (7 oz, 6 Count) even better than my favorite American yellow mustard (see [[ASIN:B002VY3FE6 Raye's Down East Schooner Mustard]]). My husband, who loves all varieties of gourmet mustards, agrees that this Maille Dijon Rich Country Mustard is something extra special.

It has the characteristic Dijon “kick”, along with a delicious mustard flavor. It should work well in any recipe that calls for Dijon mustard. Try it on [[ASIN:B00AACN2NU Snyders Unsalted Mini-Pretzels]] for a quick snack. The price is quite reasonable (about $18 on Amazon for six 7-oz. jars).

It's hardly surprising that this mustard tastes so good, because the Maille company has been making gourmet mustards for 270 years. The company was founded by a vinegar-maker in France in 1720.

title: Bland (Like Other Gluten-Free Pasta), But Excellent Texture
text:
This RONZONI GLUTEN FREE PENNE RIGATE (12 oz) is made from corn, quinoa, brown rice, and white rice. Like other gluten-free pastas, it's bland and almost flavorless. (My household can eat traditional wheat-based pasta, and strongly prefers it.) The pasta texture is excellent, so you get a delicious pasta dish when you combine this gluten-free pasta with a flavorful sauce.

“Penne” refers to the pasta shape (like a hollow quill), and “rigate” refers to the ridges on the quills. The ridges and the hollow tubes are designed to catch and hold your pasta sauce.

title: Specially Processed to Eliminate Stray Cereal Grains; Tastes Same as Regular Product
text:
Oats are naturally gluten-free, because gluten comes from cereal grains (wheat, rye barley, spelt, triticale, etc.). Thus regular Quaker Oats are probably okay for most people who follow gluten-free diets. However, the Quaker Oats company is now offering this QUAKER GLUTEN FREE alternative to regular Quaker Oats. The Gluten Free product is specially processed “to make sure that no stray grains have made their way into your breakfast.” (I assume that the Gluten Free product is prepared with equipment and/or in a factory building that never handles cereal grains.)

I prepare a breakfast bowl of quick-cooking Quaker Oats almost every day. The only difference that I see between the regular product and the Gluten Free product is that the oat flakes of the Gluten Free product appear slightly smaller and more uniform in size than those of the regular product. The taste is identical, and the preparation instructions are identical.

I do find it helpful to adjust the oat and water proportions and the cook time for the two slightly different products, because I use a microwave and need to prevent boiling over. However, I’m not absolutely positive that any adjustment is necessary.

title: Garlic Powder Keeps Well, Works in Most Recipes
text:
This is a very large plastic jar of MCCORMICK GARLIC POWDER (Organic, Non-GMO, Kosher; 16.75 oz.). In my experience, McCormick garlic and onion powders both keep extremely well (for long periods of time) when stored in a cabinet or on a countertop without refrigeration.

I use garlic powder for dry meat rubs (in combination with other dried spices), or as a substitute for fresh or minced garlic in almost any recipe. I’ve used McCormick spices for years, and have never been disappointed as to their quality or flavor.

title: Dry-Roasted Cashews; Lime Flavor Is Barely There
text: These KARMA PREMIUM WRAPPED NATURAL WHOLE CASHEWS (Lime Twist, 8 Ounce) are pretty good, though I can’t really taste the lime flavor.  These are medium-sized, dry-roasted cashews.  Since I love cashews in any form, I like them--though I would probably like them better if they were roasted in the usual fashion (roasted, not dry-roasted).

title: Even Tasted Good With Applesauce
text:
This WICKEDLY PRIME ORGANIC MATCHA GREEN TEA POWDER (Culinary Grade, 4 Ounce) tasted pretty good when added to unsweetened applesauce. (It did make the applesauce taste like veggies, so don’t try this unless you like veggies.)

I’ve never cared for tea (tannin sometimes upsets my stomach), so green tea powder is a way to get some of the many health benefits of green tea without actually drinking tea.

This is a huge packet of green tea powder, but there are a million ways to use it. You can sprinkle it onto bananas, mix it with yogurt, add it to smoothies, etc. The bag is conveniently resealable. Green tea powder needs to be refrigerated after opening.

title: Tastes Almost Like Real Lemonade; Not Too Sweet When Mixed With Bottled Water
text:
This 10-calorie SUNSPLASHERS LEMONADE (120 count) is quite delicious! Some reviewers say that it’s too sweet, but I don’t think so. As long as you mix it with 16.9 fl. oz. water, per the mixing directions, it tastes almost like real lemonade, as if made with fresh lemons. (Mine was only too sweet when I mixed it in a 10 fl. oz. water glass.)

The drink mix is designed to be added to the tall 16.9 oz. size of bottled water--you drink a few sips of water to make room for the mix, add the powder, replace the bottle cap, and shake the bottle until the powder dissolves. Then you have a tall bottle of lemonade.

This product is sweetened with aspartame, but it doesn’t taste artificial or leave an unpleasant chemical aftertaste. The complete ingredients list is as follows: citric acid, maltodextrin, potassium citrate, natural flavors, aspartame* (*phenylketonurics: contains phenylalanine), contains 2% or less of the following: ascorbic acid, salt, magnesium oxide, acesulfame potassium, tricalcium phosphate, artificial color, yellow 5 lake.

title: Terrific Kit With Right Supplies for Colorful Icings
text:
This WILTON COLORFUL ICING KIT (for Decorating Cupcakes, Cookies, and Cakes) has everything you need to make colorful icings for cakes, cupcakes, and cookies. There’s a big tub of ready-to-spread buttercream icing, 8 different gel food colors (4 pastels, 4 primaries), and two packages of disposable decorating bags. (You will need decorating tips (not included) to use the bags.)

The hard-to-find gel colors are the prize for me. My local grocery stores don’t sell gel food colors, even though they’re better than the liquid McCormick food colors that are always available. Gel colors are more intense in color, and they don’t change the consistency of icings or cake batters.

This kit doesn’t come in retail packaging. Instead, it’s a collection of Wilton retail products, grouped together in frustration-free packaging and sold at a special kit price.

title: Set for Experimenting With Extremely Hot Chili Peppers
text:
This set of eight different REBEL HOT SAUCES FROM AUBREY D. (Jalapeno, Chipotle, Habanero, Scotch Bonnet, Red Savina, Jolokia Ghost, Scorpion, and Reaper 51) is a fun way to safely experiment and find out how much chili pepper heat you can personally tolerate!

I’ve been curious for some time about the Ghost pepper (formerly certified by GUINNESS WORLD RECORDS as the world’s hottest chili pepper), and the Carolina Reaper pepper (the current world record holder), but have been afraid to simply bite into a sample of either one. The Reaper is cross-bred for heat, and reportedly has put at least one person into the hospital after eating it. Both of these peppers are many, many times hotter than a Habanero pepper, which is already near the limit of my ability to “stand the heat”.

With this set, I plan to cautiously work may way up the heat ladder. To help with this project, I’ll be consulting the colorful “Rebel-O-Meter” on each box, which has an arrow pointing to the heat level of the chili pepper sauce inside (see uploaded photo).

title: Mess-Free, 90-Calorie, Pick-Me-Up Pouches
text:
These GOGO SQUEEZ BLASTZ FRUIT POUCHES ON THE GO (Raspberry Rush, 3.88 Ounce) give you a mess-free, 90-calorie pick-me-up, wherever you may be (no refrigeration required). The pouches have a wide twist-tab that makes them super easy to open. The pouches contain flavored applesauce (100% fruit (apple, raspberry, cranberry)). Applesauce is boring, but this “raspberry rush” combination is quite tasty.

There’s no sugar added, but most of your 90 calories come from sugars that occur naturally in fruit. One pouch contains 4g dietary fiber, 15g sugars, and 5g fat. There are no preservatives (a big plus for me, because preservatives aggravate my IBS), and no artificial flavors or ingredients. In sum, one of these pouches is a “healthy” sugar snack.

title: Everything You Need for Simple But Striking Cupcakes
text:
Wilton sells an enormous variety of cake, cupcake, and dessert decorating kits. I like this one, the WILTON 2104-3899 METALLIC CUPCAKE DECORATING SET, because it’s so basic (and easy). It provides everything you need to create simple but striking iced cupcakes—colorful foil cupcake liners (in red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple); an assortment of jimmies/sprinkles and fine colored sanding sugars; and a set of primary gel-based (!) food colors (red, yellow, green, and blue).

Both the foil cupcake liners and the gel-based icing colors can be hard to find; they’re certainly scarce in my area. The only cupcake liners available in local stores are thin paper liners in very pale pastel colors (see [[ASIN: B00VN148YO Reynolds Baking Cups]]). Cupcakes made in pastel liners taste good, but cupcakes made in foil liners taste even better because they’re so pleasing to the eye!

Gel-based food colors are desirable because they’re thicker and more intense in color than the liquid food colors sold in grocery stores (see [[ASIN:B007P0FWSI McCormick Food Color Set]]). Liquid food colors give you pastel icing and can thin your icing a little; gel food colors give you saturated colors and won’t thin your icing because you use less of it.

With these supplies, you can make very pretty cupcakes with little extra effort. All you have to do is give a little thought to harmonizing the colored icings, foil liners, and sugar toppings.

title: Excellent Waffle With Only Water Added, But Chocolate Tastes Cheap
text:
This ORGANIC CHOCOLATE CHIP PANCAKE AND WAFFLE MIX BY BIRCH BENDER (Whole Grain, Non-GMO, 96 Ounce) makes a delicious, fluffy Belgian waffle. All you need to do is add water, and a little bit of oil (to keep it from sticking to the waffle iron). I was amazed at how good the basic waffle was, when it wasn’t made with added milk or eggs.

However, the chocolate chips left something to be desired. Maybe I’m a chocolate snob, but I think that [[ASIN:B00932F6Y6 Nestle’s Semi-Sweet Morsels]] taste much better. The chocolate bits in the mix melted properly, but they tasted like the very cheap chocolate that you get from the chocolate bars sold in school money-raising drives.

I checked the “Best if used by” date on my package of pancake and waffle mix, and it showed there were about 15 months to go. Thus the chocolate probably wasn’t stale, though it may have suffered from being stored in a very hot Amazon warehouse.

title: Sugar Replacement Provides Sweetness for Morning Oatmeal
text:
Sugar tastes good on my morning oatmeal, but it often causes a sugar crash (rapid heartbeat, panicky feelings, etc.) a few hours later. To avoid this, I now sweeten cereal with honey or a product like this JARROW FORMULAS XYLI PURE XYLITOL (Supports Gastrointestinal Health, 454 g). Xylitol is a naturally-occurring 5-carbon sugar alcohol found in various fruits and vegetables. It’s about as sweet as sugar, but has 40% fewer calories.

The Xyli Pure product label says it is “ideal for people on sugar-restricted or low-carb diets”. The powder is intended to be used as a sugar replacement in foods and beverages. Use 1-3 teaspoons for desired amount of sweetness, but don’t overdo it, because consumption of more than 8 teaspoons daily can cause diarrhea. For some reason, this product should NOT be fed to dogs.

My experience with Jarrow products has always been good--it’s a brand that I trust to contain the ingredients the label says the product contains, and to do more or less what the label says the product will do.

title: Mildly-Flavored Handmade Caramels With Added Vanilla
text:
If it’s vanilla-flavored, count me in! TARA’S ORIGINAL MADAGASCAR VANILLA CARAMELS (11.5 Ounce) are soft, mildly flavored, melt-in-your-mouth caramels with added Madagascar vanilla. (Madagascar vanilla is what you usually get in bottled vanilla extract. Many (including myself) think that Mexican vanilla tastes even better, but it’s expensive, and you need to buy it from a trusted source to avoid counterfeit, often toxic, products.)

I wish that the flavor were a little stronger, to give the caramels a little more character. The texture is nice and smooth. Each caramel has only 43 calories, so one of them is an almost guilt-free snack. Another thing that I like about these caramels is that they are individually wrapped in waxed paper, not plastic.

title: Mildly-Flavored, Melt-in-Your-Mouth, Handmade Caramels
text:
TARA’S SEA SALT CARAMELS (11.5 Ounce) are mildly flavored handmade caramels that absolutely do melt in your mouth. I wish that the flavor were a little stronger, to give the caramels a little more character. (When I make caramel ice cream, I cook the sugar to a very dark amber color, stopping just before it burns, to get the strongest possible caramel taste.)

As to perfectly smooth texture, these caramels can’t be beat. There are 27 caramels in each pretty plastic screw-top jar (reusable, I think). Each caramel has only 43 calories, so one of them is an almost guilt-free snack.

Something else that I like about these caramels is that they are individually wrapped in waxed paper, not plastic.

title: Tasty Apple/Cranberry Juice in Packable Pouch
text:
This MOTT’S APPLE CRANBERRY 100% JUICE (6 fl. oz. pouches; 8 count (Pack of 4)) tastes good, exactly as you’d expect from the Mott’s brand. The pouches are a nice size for packing into a school lunch or a brown bag lunch. The amount is satisfying, neither too much nor too little.

Each pouch has a plastic straw taped to the back. The straw has a point that helps to pierce the pouch at the circle in the front. My only complaint about this product is that the straw was kind of hard to remove, and the pouch was kind of hard to pierce. I think that small children might have difficulty inserting the straw to drink the juice.

title: Handmade Caramels Have Pecan Bits Inside!
text:
TARA’S PECAN CARAMELS (11.5 Ounce) really taste like pecans, and they have little bits of pecan inside! The caramel flavor is fairly mild—I wish it were a little stronger. The texture is nice and smooth. The caramels are gluten-free, and they definitely taste like handmade candies (which they are).

Three things that I really like about these caramels are: (1) one caramel has only 43 calories; (2) the caramels are individually wrapped in waxed paper, not plastic; and (3) there are 27 caramels in each reusable (screw-top) plastic jar (11.5 oz. size).

title: “Hot to Trot” Trail Mix Is Really Hot, Very Tasty, Probably Healthy
text:
The package back for this WICKEDLY PRIME ORGANIC SPROUTED TRAIL MIX (SPICED MULBERRY APPLE, 8 OUNCES) says: “This trail mix is HOT TO TROT”. That’s no exaggeration—the mix is extremely hot. It has a LOT of cayenne pepper, as well as cinnamon to add heat. I like it a lot!

Sprouted almonds were new to me, but the almonds taste good—like regular almonds but with little crunchy bits. Many health food aficionados say that sprouted nuts and seeds are especially good for you. I don’t know about that, but I’ve always like the way that sprouts and sprouted things taste.

The trail mix delivers 120 calories per ¼ cup. There are 8g fat, 170mg sodium, and 12g carbohydrates (3g fiber; 6g sugar (no added sugar)). As trail mix goes, this one seems fairly healthy to me. It isn’t prepared with oils, and it isn’t too heavy on sugar or salt. One serving makes a fine snack. There are 8 servings per package.

title: Tomatoes Produced Great Lasagna
text:
These LOVESOME DICED TOMATOES (14.5 Ounce) are exceptionally tasty, because of the added onion, celery, and green pepper. The tomato flavor itself is robust—not at all like pale pink supermarket tomatoes. The tomatoes aren’t high-acid, either.

I used a can of these tomatoes in my regular lasagna recipe, and it made one of our best lasagna meals ever.

title: Enough Icing for 55 Cupcakes!
text:
This WILTON CREAMY DECORATOR WHITE ICING TUB (4lb., White; 710-43654) is filled with enough pre-mixed icing to cover 55 cupcakes! It’s a real timesaver when you’re baking for a crowd. (If you’re making cupcakes for school children, just keep in mind that the icing contains soy, and that it is prepared in a facility that processes egg, wheat, milk, and tree nuts).

Once the tub is opened, any unused icing can be stored in the refrigerator, covered, for up to 4 weeks. The tub holds 9 cups of icing. The icing can be tinted using [[ASIN:B0000CFN0Y Wilton Icing Colors]].

title: Hot Honey Tastes Really Good on Morning Oatmeal
text:
MIKE’S HOT HONEY (Pack of 12 Squeeze Packs, 0.75 Ounce Each) tastes really good on my breakfast oatmeal (with milk). The honey has a wonderful flavor, and the chilis add heat quietly in the background (there is no chili taste). Thus “hot honey” is a very accurate description!

The honey is hotter than Tabasco sauce, but not uncomfortably hot—especially not on something like oatmeal, where you can spread it around and temper it with milk. My husband also thinks this product is delicious.

The squeeze packs are kind of expensive (about $19 for 12 packs on Amazon, or about $1.50 per pack), but the product is available in squeeze bottles for less (see, e.g., [[ASIN:B011LWRBES Mike’s Hot Honey, 12 oz. Bottle]]). The squeeze packs are handy for entertaining guests (at a brunch or barbecue, say), or for adding to a brown bag lunch.

title: Morning Granola-Style Oats, Without Extra Work
text:
MAKER OVERNIGHT OATS (Banana & Coffee Organic, No Sugar Added, 5 Count, 2.29 oz Packs) live up to their advertising—there is no cooking required. All you do is put the contents of a packet in a cereal bowl, add milk, and leave the bowl in the refrigerator overnight.

It tastes pretty good, though It has too much sugar (12g from dried organic fruits and nuts) for my taste. The 1-serving protein content (7g) is good, as compared to my usual Quick Quaker oats (5g). One pouch (1 serving) delivers 260 calories (before you add milk). The serving size is satisfying, though it didn’t carry me to lunch; I needed my usual morning snack.

If plain oatmeal isn’t your thing, then you will probably like this product. You get your granola-like fruits and nuts added without having to do the extra work.

title: Mild Cocoa Flavor, Slight Crunchiness, Lots of Calories
text:
These MCCORMICK GOOD MORNING BREAKFAST TOPPERS (with Chia & Flax, Cocoa, Banana, 4.25 Ounce) taste like mildly-cocoa-flavored crisp rice cereal. I thought they might be an interesting change from sugar on my morning oatmeal or Cream of Wheat, but they don’t have much flavor and they add very little crunchiness. I couldn’t taste the banana at all.

One serving (2 Tbsp.) has 90 calories, 80mg sodium, and 6g sugar! No thanks, I’ll stick with just a light sprinkling of white sugar on my hot cereal. The listed contents of this strange product are brown sugar, oats, rice, sunflower seeds, banana, coconut, soy flour, flaxseed, sugar, cocoa liquor, cocoa powder processed with alkali, salt, chia seed, natural flavor & extractives of malt barley.

title: Nice Cup of Coffee With Light Vanilla Flavor
text: These LOVESOME FRENCH VANILLA ROAST K-CUPS (12 Count (Pack of 6)) produce a nice cup of coffee with a light vanilla flavor.  They use Arabica beans, with a medium roast.  Arabica beans aren’t my favorite—I’m a Peet’s/Starbucks coffee snob—but these seem to be of good quality.

title: Chopped Walnuts Are Time-Saver for Busy Baker
text:
These FISHER’S NATURALS CHOPPED WALNUTS (10 oz.) are chopped to the perfect size for adding to cookies, muffins, and cakes. They’re a real time-saver for the busy baker. They taste fresh, and they’re packed in a resealable plastic bag. Fisher’s is a top brand that you can rely on for consistent quality.

The price (about $11.50 on Amazon) is very good, compared to at least one other major brand sold on Amazon (see [[ASIN:B0004MVRFG Diamond Chopped Walnuts, 8 oz.]]). You may be able to find similar chopped walnuts at even lower prices at local stores (e.g., Walmart).

title: Thinly Sliced Almonds Are Just Right for Almond Danish Recipes
text:
These HAPPY BELLY SLICED ALMONDS (30 oz) are very thinly sliced—just right for almond Danish recipes. The slices are uniform, too. The price is reasonable (about $15 on Amazon) for the big 30-ounce bag. The almonds taste good, and seem quite fresh.

I’ve tried several varieties of Happy Belly nuts, and have been very satisfied with all of them. Happy Belly is an Amazon brand that seems to deliver good quality at a modest price.

title: African Pepper Is Extremely Hot—Think Habanero!
text:
This JEB FOODS GOURMET AFRICAN RED PEPPER POWDER (Extremely Hot, 1 lb/16oz) is a huge, restaurant-sized, reclosable bag of ground, red hot African red pepper. It’s delicious, but to say it’s “extremely hot” is almost an understatement. I don’t know where it actually stands on the food science heat scale, but it makes me think of habaneros!

I ruined a vegetable dish (green beans, red bell pepper, shallots) that called for a TABLESPOON of crushed red peppers, by using a TEASPOON (⅓ Tbsp) of this red hot African pepper. The dish was so hot, it seemed dangerous to eat!

In the future, I will use this in place of ground cayenne pepper when I want some extra heat. However, I’ll start by using only half the quantity of cayenne that the recipe calls for. This red hot pepper should be used with extreme caution!

title: Great Value for Money, Especially If You Love Tropical Fruits
text:
This HAPPY BELLY TROPICAL TRAIL MIX (16 oz (Pack of 2)) gives great value for the money, especially if you are fond of tropical fruits. This trail mix contains sweetened dried pineapple, papaya, and mango, raisins, peanuts, cashews, banana chips, Brazil nuts, and coconut chips. I liked the pineapple, raisins, cashews, and peanuts; didn’t care much for the papaya, mango, banana, coconut, and Brazil nuts. Also, I would prefer fewer peanuts, and more cashews.

My favorite Happy Belly Trail Mix is the [[ASIN:B015GK1AJI Happy Belly Cranberry & Nuts Trail Mix]], which has dried cranberries, cashews, and almonds. That one has lots of cashews, which I love.

Generally I think that the Happy Belly line of products is very good, delivering above-average quality nuts at an excellent price. However, the quality is NOT always consistent—one 40-oz. bag of Happy Belly Mixed Nuts that I purchased a few months ago turned out to have some almonds and hazelnuts that were edible, but almost rancid. I think that was an exception, so I will go on buying Happy Belly nuts and trail mixes.

title: Tastes Good, Less Sugar Than Gatorade, Contains Juice
text:
One bottle (100 calories) of this HONEST SPORT ORGANIC ORANGE DRINK (16.9 fl oz bottle, 12 Pack) has 40 fewer calories than my usual NONorganic sports drink, [[ASIN:B00N953VCM Gatorade Lemon-Lime Thirst Quencher (20 fl oz bottle)]]. It has the same amount of sodium (270 mg); a little less potassium (65 mg, not 75 mg); and a LOT less sugar (26g, not 36g).

The biggest difference is that the Honest Sport sugars come from lemon juice and orange juice concentrates, while the Gatorade product contains NO fruit juice. Both products taste good. I like the Gatorade slightly better, no doubt because of the extra sugars. I’m sure that Honest Sport is a somewhat “healthier” drink.

Unfortunately, Honest Sport is much more expensive (12-pack is about $22 on Amazon) than Gatorade (12-pack is about $10 on Amazon) or even Organic Gatorade (12-pack is about $12 on Amazon). But Honest Sport Organic Orange Drink is a good choice if you're willing to pay extra for a less sugary, organic drink, and you really like the taste.

title: Slightly Darker, Stronger Tasting Than Star Extra Virgin Olive Oil
text: This AMAZON FRESH MEDITERRANEAN BLEND EXTRA VIRGIN OLIVE OIL (2 Qt., 3.6 Fl. Oz.) compares favorably to the [[ASIN:B00K7KC678 Star Extra Virgin Olive Oil]] that I’ve been using.  The Amazon oil is slightly darker gold than the Star oil, and the Amazon oil is slightly stronger tasting than the Star oil.

title: Thick Tomato Sauce Isn’t Acidic, Tastes Like Vodka
text:
This thick JAR GOODS CLASSIC VODKA TOMATO SAUCE (16 oz.) tastes good--not acidic, and faintly like vodka. I would definitely buy it again, if the price were comparable to other commercial tomato sauce packaged in jars. But it’s very expensive--one jar is currently selling for about $19.00 on Amazon (with free shipping).

Also, there's a lot of oil in this tomato sauce. It doesn't taste oily, but it’s visibly oily--there was a sheen of oil left behind on all of my kitchen and serving utensils.

One cup of sauce (which is about what I use on a dinner-size serving of spaghetti) has 260 calories, so it’s not going to help out my diet. One cup also has 820 mg of sodium. That's a LOT of sodium, but less than the 920 mg of sodium found in one of my favorites, [[ASIN:B00KOB8WLY Newman’s Own Sockarooni pasta sauce]].

title: Pleasant Strawberry Banana Drink With Only 170 Calories
text:
Like most nutritional drinks, this PRONOURISH NUTRITIONAL DRINK (Strawberry Banana, 8 fl oz, Pack of 24) is a bit chalky, and the strawberry/banana flavor tastes a bit artificial. However, I generally like the strawberry banana drink, and it doesn't have a lasting artificial aftertaste.

I especially like the fact that one bottle has only 170 calories (about right for an afternoon snack). Maybe the drink is higher in salt than it needs to be, at 150mg per bottle.

title: High Smoke Point Is Valuable Feature
text:
What I really like about COCAVO NATURAL cooking oil is the high smoke point, because the smoke from regular cooking oils (e.g., canola oil) will set off my smoke alarm if I’m not careful. When I cook with this oil, my condo doesn't fill up with smoke.

This oil, which is solid at 75 degrees F. and doesn't require refrigeration, looks like mashed avocado in the container. When heated, it becomes a clear, pale green oil. During cooking, it smells like coconut, and the frying odor will take some getting used to if you're accustomed to cooking with vegetable oil.

I tested it by frying eggs, and was pleased to find that it imparted only a faint coconut flavor to the eggs. If you use it for frying strongly-flavored or spicy dishes (e.g., breaded meats, curries), you probably won't taste the oil at all. (Also, I think that I used too much oil for my eggs--a little of this oil goes a long way.)

title: Cacao Coats Everything, Makes Everything Taste Like Chocolate
text:
This KITCHUN NO-GRAIN-OLA (Holy Coco Cacao!, 10 oz.) is very sweet, and the cacao coating that covers everything (nuts, coconut chips, seeds, whatever) makes everything taste like chocolate. The nut, coconut, and seed flavors don't come through at all.

Also, this snack product doesn't seem very healthy to me. A handful (¼ cup) has only 150 calories, but that handful contains 12g fat and 5g sugars (along with 4g protein, which improves it a little). I can't imagine eating this for breakfast “as a cereal with almond milk on top”, as is suggested on the package.

This is okay for snacks, I guess, if you really love cacao.

title: Huge Pecan Halves Are Unsalted, Preservative-Free
text:
These FISHER CHEF’S NATURALS PECAN HALVES (16 Ounce) are huge, preservative-free, and unsalted. They're perfect for cooking, baking, and making butter pecan ice cream (my personal favorite!). The 16-ounce bag has very, very few broken pecan halves.

Because these pecans are so big, they're wonderful for any project that uses pecans as a decorative, finishing touch--for example, sugar cookies with a pecan pressed on top. Priced about $20 per pound on Amazon, they may seem kind of expensive, but they're actually about twice as large as the [[ASIN:B001EQ54QE Diamond Pecan Halves]] that I buy at my local grocery store!

title: Delicious, Filling, Made With Milk & Heavy Cream
text:
This MET-RX ULTRA RECOVERY HIGH PROTEIN MILK SHAKE (Vanilla) is quite delicious, and very filling for a mere 11 fluid ounces . I like the fact that skim milk and heavy whipping cream are the main (first-listed) ingredients. The shake itself is creamy and smooth.

There is a little too much sugar for my taste, but sugar is to be expected in any workout recovery drink. The 30 grams of protein are a big plus. The Vanilla flavor is good, though I think that I'd like the shake a little better if it were flavored with chocolate.

title: Pleasant, Mild-Tasting Colombian Coffee--An Excellent K-Cup Coffee
text:
This MY CUP COUNTS K-CUP COLOMBIAN COFFEE (48 count) makes a pleasant (but very mild) cup of coffee. It's made from medium-roast Arabica beans. To me, it almost doesn't taste like coffee (though maybe that's because I usually drink bold, dark-roast coffee from Peet's and Starbucks). I think this is a "light roast" Colombian, NOT a "full-bodied and rich medium roast coffee". But the caffeine is definitely there, and My Cup Counts makes one of the best cups of K-cup coffee that I've run across.

At just under $30 for 48 K-cups, My Cup Counts Colombian coffee is very reasonably priced (it's comparable to Green Mountain or Dunkin' Donuts K-cups, for example). In addition, you can feel good about buying the My Cup Counts brand, because the company claims that it employs and teaches job skills to adults with developmental disabilities.

title: Shade-Grown Matcha Tea Leaves for "Calm Energy" Effect
text:
Matcha green tea, in its powdered form, is used in the traditional Japanese tea ceremony. These LIPTON MATCHA GREEN TEA BAGS contain non-powdered Matcha green tea leaves. Matcha green tea is shade-grown for a few weeks before harvest, so that the plants will produce extra theanine and caffeine, supposedly to create a "calm energy" effect (for more information, check out the "Matcha" article on Wikipedia).

Caffeine, even in large amounts, has little effect on me, so I cannot say whether this Lipton Matcha green tea is any better or worse than regular green tea (not shade-grown). However, the tea bags contain good-quality tea leaves, and they produce a good cup of green tea.

title: Good to Keep on Emergency Pantry Shelf
text:
Canned meat, whether it's traditional Spam or some other brand, has never been my favorite food. However, I have recipes for Spam--using mustards or other spices in cracker-crumb coatings--that make this processed meat product quite palatable.

Spam is good to keep on the pantry shelf as an emergency food ration. This SPAM LITE READY TO EAT FOOD (12 Ounce) represents a "healthy" improvement over original Spam, because it contains considerably less fat and salt.

title: Makes It Easy to Get Started Using Pasta Attachments for KitchenAid Stand Mixer
text:
This KITCHENAID FRANZESE AUTHENTIC ITALIAN STYLE WHOLE WHEAT PASTA MIX (KFMPD12SPWW, 11.9 oz.) offers an easy way to get started making your own fresh pasta, using the KitchenAid attachments for your KitchenAid stand mixer. There are at least three attachments available that work with the dough from this mix--the gourmet pasta press/pasta extruder (for spaghetti, fusilli, etc.); the pasta roller and cutter (for lasagna, fettuccine, etc.); and the ravioli maker.

You can also make pasta dough from this mix and hand roll and cut it. (You don't need a KitchenAid mixer at all.)

With this mix, all you do is add water and stir well. Then you form the pasta dough into a ball, wrap it, and let the dough rest for 9 minutes. At that point, the dough is ready to press, or to roll and cut. Easy! If you don't want to use the dough right away, you can store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

I like to make my own fresh pasta because most commercial brands of fresh pasta upset my stomach. (I think they use cheap shortening or lard.) If I make my own pasta, I can use only quality ingredients. My household loves pasta, and strongly prefers the taste and texture of fresh pasta to the taste and texture of even the best brands of dried pasta (e.g., Barilla).

title: Disappointing Flavor When Spread on Toast
text:
This 4TH & HEART GRASS-FED GHEE BUTTER (Salted White Truffle, 9 Ounce) is the first ghee that I've tried, and I don't care for it--at least not as a spread on toast. It sinks right into the surface of the toast, so I use more of it than I do with plain butter. That's not good, because one tablespoon of butter has 100 calories, and one tablespoon of this ghee has 135 calories.

The flavor is disappointing. It doesn't really taste like butter. I can barely taste anything mushroom-like, and the added salt just makes it taste too salty. A tablespoon of salted butter has 95mg of sodium, and a tablespoon of this ghee has 120mg of sodium. It does alter the flavor of a fried egg to something slightly "woodsy".

Because I'll be using up this ghee butter in cooking--for frying and greasing baking pans--I rate it at 3 stars ("It's okay" on the official Amazon scale).

title: Alternates Between Tasting Sweet, Tasting Salty--Subtle, Enjoyable Snack Experience
text:
It's so cheap and easy to make fresh popcorn (using a Presto air popper), I've always wondered why anyone would want to purchase grocery-store popcorn. But now I've seen the light, thanks to ANGIE'S BOOMACHICKPOP SWEET AND SALTY KETTLE CORN (14 Ounce).

This pre-popped kettle corn tastes fresh, and the "sweet and salty" flavoring is subtle and intriguing. When I eat it, the popcorn alternates between tasting sweet and tasting salty. How does it do that? Maybe it's just my taste buds taking turns, but it creates a highly enjoyable snack experience.

Two cups of this kettle corn--which amounts to a small bowlful--contain only 140 calories, and of course popcorn is reasonably filling. There are 8g of sugar in a two-cup serving, but that isn't so bad, as snack foods go. There are 110mg of salt, and that's also not so bad. You won't be running for your water bottle after eating several cups of this kettle corn.

title: Lemony Flavor Turned Me Off
text:
Herbal teas vary a lot, and it's normal to like some, and not others. That said, I found this SPECIAL TEA HIBISCUS FLOWERS HERBAL TEA (Single Serve Cups, 30 Gram) to be almost undrinkable. It tasted like "Lemon Zinger gone wrong" (and I like Lemon Zinger well enough, in small quantities).

I've never tried hibiscus flower tea before, so I cannot say whether the lemony flavor is to be expected. Perhaps my cup of tea simply was too strong, given that one K-cup is good for two cups. However, I rate this product at 2 stars ("I don't like it" on the official Amazon scale).

title: Survive on Nuts and Berries!
text:
If you're lost in the woods and need to survive on nuts and berries, you'll be glad you packed along this HAPPY BELLY CRANBERRY & NUTS TRAIL MIX (40 oz.)! The mix has big, plump cashew nuts, large roasted almonds, and chewy dried cranberries. I'd say they're about in equal numbers--certainly there is no skimping on the nuts.

The big recloseable bag contains 38 servings (1/4 cup servings). A serving has 140 calories (80 calories from fat), and makes a good, lasting snack. The nuts are lightly salted (65mg per serving), and the mix overall doesn't taste too sweet (though there are 10g of sugars per serving).

Note that this product is packed on equipment shared with milk, egg, other tree nuts, wheat, peanuts, and soy.

title: Amazingly Good Meal With Next-to-No Preparation, Cleanup
text:
This MOUNTAIN HOUSE HOMESTYLE CHICKEN NOODLE CASSEROLE is kind of amazing. To prepare it, you add 2 cups of boiling water to the package's freeze-dried contents, close the package's zipper-seal, and let the package stand for 8-9 minutes. That's it! When you open the package, it is filled with al dente pasta, as almost all of the water has been absorbed. One package provides a really tasty pasta meal for two, or a really tasty mingy pasta meal for three.

The "Homestyle Chicken Noodle Casserole" contains chicken, pimento. mushrooms, celery, and green peppers. The casserole isn't strongly flavored, but it has discernible chicken chunks, is pleasant-tasting (should appeal to most everyday-cooking palates), doesn't taste overly salty (even if you've been on a low-salt diet), and is reasonably filling. It doesn't taste remotely like "health food".

At an Amazon cost of about $9.50 per 3-serving package, this Mountain House Chicken Noodle Casserole isn't cheap. However, the full package is very lightweight, so it's an excellent choice for backpackers or hikers who must carry their food and trash in and out of the wilderness. These packages might also be a good addition to anyone's pantry, to be used for occasional on-the-run meals that are fast, nutritious, and require next to no preparation or cleanup.

To be prepared for the next S.F. Bay Area earthquake, I recently purchased a couple of the [[ASIN:B014ER40MS Mountain House 3 Day Emergency Food Supply]] boxes. The freeze-dried food tastes good, and has a minimum 12-year shelf life, so it's a more economical choice than canned goods for my household (although it does require water and a means of boiling the water following an earthquake). My household doesn't eat much canned food, so I always have to throw out the canned goods after 2-3 years.

title: Tastes Cloyingly Sweet, Even Though Label Lists No Sugar or Artificial Sweetener
text:
Unquestionably, these HARNEY AND SONS HOT CINNAMON SPICE TEA CAPSULES are filled with high-quality ingredients. However, if you don't care for sweetened tea, you won't like it. It tastes like it's loaded with sugar, even though it leaves no sugary aftertaste. The capsules worked perfectly in my Keurig Elite machine, producing full-bodied cups of strong, sweet tea.

The label lists black tea, orange peel, cinnamon, and sweet cloves as the principal ingredients, along with "natural and artificial flavors". There is no mention of sugar or artificial sweetener. I'm at a loss regarding the source of the cloying sweetness, but I suspect it comes from some artificial flavoring. I doubt that any variety of cloves could taste that sweet.

I love [[ASIN:B004GIE562 Celestial Seasonings India Spice Chai Tea]], and [[ASIN:B00028Q8V0 Bigelow Constant Comment Tea]], but I couldn't drink even one full cup of this Harney and Sons Hot Cinnamon Spice tea. It tastes far, far too sweet, even if it doesn't actually contain sugar. I rate it at 2 stars ("I don't like it" on the official Amazon scale).

title: Pleasant Cup-of-Hot-Soup Accompaniment to Sandwich
text:
At 70 calories, this CAMPBELL'S HOMESTYLE CHICKEN BROTH AND NOODLE MIX FOR K-CUPS won't provide a satisfying light lunch all by itself, but it does make a pleasant cup-of-hot-soup accompaniment to a sandwich.

Each serving consists of a K-cup (for the chicken broth) and an envelope containing dry seasonings and noodles (to be emptied into an 8 oz. brewing cup). Your Keurig machine provides the hot water. You use the "small cup" setting (7.25 oz. on my Keurig Elite) or whatever button is closest to 8.0 oz. of water.

Unless you like soup with a crunch to it, you'll want to let the brewed soup stand for 2-3 minutes--per the instructions--to allow the dried noodles to absorb the hot water. (You'll also want to brew a cup of plain hot water afterwards to clear the broth flavoring from your Keurig, same as you do when you make cocoa or hot apple juice.)

The "homestyle" seasonings are tasty, though the finished soup isn't as good as canned Campbell's Chicken Noodle soup. I rate this K-cup Campbell's Homestyle Chicken Broth and Noodle Mix product at 4 stars ("I like it" on the official Amazon scale).

title: Melt-in-Your-Mouth Diet-Busters
text:
These NOMNOM DELIGHTS GOURMET CARMEL CANDIES are the best caramels ever! They have a full, rich caramel flavor, and a basic caramel chewiness--but if you hold them in your mouth, they melt! There's no way that these caramels will get caught in bridgework or pull out fillings--they're soft, and they dissolve.

I tried both the original and the sea salt versions. It's hard to say which tasted better. Perhaps the Fleur de Sel (sea salt) won in a photo finish, but I'm delighted with the originals that don't have added salt. (I'm on a low-salt diet.)

If I eat one of these caramels, I can't stop until I've eaten at least one more. They're that good! So the only bad thing I can say about these gourmet caramels is that they're real diet-busters.

An extra plus is that the folks who make these are wonderful to deal with. You can expect great customer service if you need to contact them for any reason.

title: Cookies Taste Good, But Decorating Is Difficult
text:
For a packaged cookie mix, this NINJABREAD GINGERBREAD COOKIE KIT makes pretty good cookies. They have a light gingerbread taste, and the icing doesn't leave an unpleasant aftertaste. You will need a couple of extra ingredients to make these: 1/4 cup honey (for the cookies), and 1/2 cup powdered sugar (for the icing).

The mix really does produce 20 cookies. One of mine is a round blob (see the photo), because I didn't want to roll out any more dough. However, I could have gotten another Ninja cookie out of it. Before rolling out the gingerbread dough, I divided it into quarters, so I could tell how thick the dough needed to be to get five cookies per quarter.

The cute Ninja cookie cutters are included. As you can see, I'm not terribly good at decorating cookies. It was harder than I expected to duplicate the package illustrations to make the gingerbread shapes look like Ninjas. Instead of using the included pastry bag, I used an [[ASIN:B00L9X382O OXO Baker's Silicone Decorating Bottle Kit]], which gave pretty good control. I had to chill the icing in the freezer for a little while, because when I mixed it according to the package instructions it was too thin for decorating purposes. All I could get were runny blobs.

Because the decorating was kind of difficult, and the cookies taste like they came from a packaged mix, I rate this item at 4 stars ("I like it" on the official Amazon scale).

title: Delicious, Digestible Hot Sauces That Add Plenty of Heat and "Zing"
text:
GRINGO BANDITO HOT SAUCE packs plenty of heat, whether you choose the original red sauce (hot!) or the newer green (extremely hot!) variety. I really love both sauces (and use them all the time now instead of spicy ketchup), because they add a lot of "zing" without upsetting my moderately sensitive digestive system. With this listing, you get four bottles, two of each kind.

The flavors are intriguing--more interesting than traditional Tabasco sauce or spicy ketchup, and more subtle than fancy hot sauce blends containing weird special ingredients. These hot sauces are also suitably conservative for someone like me who grew up on, and still prefers, plain American cooking. Either variety is delicious on meat loaf or on leftovers that you might otherwise smother in ketchup or barbecue sauce to make them palatable on the umpteenth day.

---GRINGO BANDITO RED SAUCE: It kind of zings in your mouth, then exits with a powerful habanero pepper bite. It tastes a little like traditional chili that is flavored with chili powder. According to the label, it's made from vinegar, water, habanero peppers, jalapeno peppers, red Japanese chili peppers, more peppers, salt, mojo, spices, and xanthan gum. ("Mojo" probably means something like "garlic, cumin, paprika, and/or citrus juice", the usual ingredients in "mojo sauce".) A one-teaspoon serving contains 25mg of sodium (1% daily nutritional value), plus vitamin A.

---GRINGO BANDITO GREEN SAUCE: This is NOT an ordinary tomatillo or green chili sauce. It's different from any other hot sauce that I've ever tasted. Unlike the original red sauce, the burn sticks around for quite a while. This variety includes Serrano peppers, probably for the color. You need to be careful when adding this sauce (to protect your mouth!), but it's just as digestible as the red sauce. According to the label, it's made from water, habanero peppers, serrano peppers, more peppers, vinegar, salt, mojo, spices, and xanthan gum. (As stated above, "mojo" probably means something like "garlic, cumin, paprika, and/or citrus juice", the usual ingredients in "mojo sauce".) A one-teaspoon serving contains 50mg of sodium (2% daily nutritional value), plus vitamin C.

Neither variety of hot sauce has any nutritional value. As the labels say: "Hot Sauce really doesn't have any nutritional value. It's vinegar and peppers, for God's sake. What did you expect? Why are you even trying to determine the nutritional value of Hot Sauce? Just enjoy it!" I think you will.

title: Too Much Salt, Too Little Butter, Too Hard to Open
text:
At under $5.50, the price is certainly right for three big paper "bowls" of this ORVILLE REDENBACHER'S GOURMET NATURALS CLASSIC BUTTER AND SEA SALT MICROWAVE POPCORN. However, the finished popcorn doesn't taste nearly as good as air-popped popcorn made from [[ASIN:B007F1L5CE Orville Redenbacher Gourmet Popcorn kernels]]. Air-popped Orville Redenbacher gourmet popcorn produces larger popped kernels, and, like this microwave popcorn, leaves few unpopped kernels behind.

The Redenbacher microwave popcorn popped very quickly, in a much shorter time than the suggested 2-1/2 minutes. But there was too much sea salt, and I couldn't taste the butter flavor at all. The hot microwave bag was rather difficult to open (even though the instructions and pictures printed on the bag are very clear). Once I got the pull-off plastic strips started, opening the bag was no problem, but the strips had to be picked apart.

Because this popcorn is only average in taste and the bag is not easy to open, I rate the product at 3 stars ("It's okay" on the official Amazon scale). I really expected more from Orville Redenbacher, which is the brand I always buy when I buy popcorn kernels.

title: Makes Wheat-Free, Gluten-Free Cookies That Taste Good
text:
[[VIDEOID:95ce8bd72f8430305c586523f022d84d]] One bag of PAMELA'S PRODUCTS COOKIE MIX made 32 delicious cookie-cutter cookies, when I rolled the dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch. Pamela's cookies are not quite as tasty as [[ASIN:B00BKCYKSU Krusteaz Sugar Cookie Mix]], but they have the advantage of being gluten-free, and wheat-free. They can also be non-dairy, if you use egg and butter substitutes.

To make a regular batch of cookies, you will need one large egg and one stick of butter (8 tbsp.) in addition to the bag of cookie mix. If you want non-dairy cookies, you will need egg-replacer in an amount equivalent to two large eggs, and 8 tbsp. of butter alternative.

To make easy, decorated cookies (like the ones in my accompanying video slide show), you can sprinkle the cookies with colored sugar (not included in the cookie mix package) just before baking. I found a nice little container with several colors of colored sugar at Safeway; it has enough sugar for quite a few batches of cookies. For the cookie shapes, I used [[ASIN:B0000VMIYU Wilton Metal Cookie Cutters]] like these (my larger set has a number of additional shapes). For baking, I used Wilton Baker's Best Cookie Sheets, purchased at a local household goods store, which have a terrific nonstick surface.

You can't really go wrong with this Pamela's Products Cookie Mix, especially since there are a lot of recipes for simple cookie variations included on the package (e.g., refrigerator cookies, cranberry and orange zest cookies, sugar cookie pie crust). My family liked the sugar-sprinkled cookies that I made with this mix very much.

title: Hotter Than Original Recipe; No Ordinary Green Chile Sauce
text:
This GREEN GRINGO BANDITO HOT SAUCE packs even more heat than the original [[ASIN:B009WPKGQC Gringo Bandito Hot Sauce]] (which I really love). Both sauces contain Habanero peppers (!), so it must be the Serrano peppers that make Green Gringo Bandito hotter. Or else it's the Mojo. Magical power? Mojo sauce? Probably it's Mojo sauce, because Mojo is in the ingredients list. That could mean the new Green recipe contains garlic, cumin, paprika, and/or citrus juice.

Whatever the actual ingredients, the GGB flavor mix is subtle and intriguing. This is NOT an ordinary tomatillo or green chile sauce. It's different from any other hot sauce that I've ever tasted. It's delicious on meat loaf, for a change from spicy ketchup, or on leftovers that you might otherwise smother in ketchup or barbecue sauce to make them palatable on the fourth or fifth day.

Do be warned: Green Gringo Bandito Hot Sauce is really, really hot. As the label says, it will "make your dining experience a zinger". Unlike the original recipe, the burn sticks around for quite a while. But like the original, it probably won't upset your digestive system.

Note that this product listing is for Green Gringo Bandito Hot Sauce shipped in Amazon Certified Frustration- Free Packaging.

title: Genuine "Red Hots"--Not Just "Cinnamon Candy"
text:
These are the original Red Hots candy--the real deal, the genuine article. When I wanted to try a recipe for designer home-made ice cream that called for "red hots", I remembered the candies well from my childhood, but couldn't find any at my local grocery store. (My brother, the neighbor kids, and I would buy red hots because they were the cheapest candy we knew of.)

It's sort of a mistake to describe "red hots" as "cinnamon candy". They feel a lot hotter to the taste buds than do cinnamon sprinkles or even the big cinnamon cake/cookie decorations. My designer ice cream, from the [[ASIN:1452104689 Humphry Slocombe Ice Cream Book]] (with no substitutions), came out great! When you order this, you get a big box with 24 little boxes of Red Hots candies inside.

title: High Quality; Great for Pistachio Ice Cream
text:
For homemade pistachio ice cream, I wanted to oven-toast my own fresh pistachios. Locally, I couldn't find any raw pistachios, in or out of the shell--only unsalted, already-roasted, in-the-shell pistachios.

These raw, unsalted, Bayside Candy pistachios are perfect for my needs. The quality is excellent--the pistachios are fresh, uniformly plump, good-tasting. Better yet, I don't have to get out the nutcracker and shell the nuts (some pistachio shells are stubborn to crack). I freeze the 2-lb. bag of shelled pistachios, then thaw and toast the pistachios in small quantities as they're needed. For pistachio ice cream, I use the recipe from [[ASIN:0060187654 The Best Ice Cream Maker Cookbook Ever]].

The pistachios are packed in two individually sealed plastic bags. The price (before shipping) compares quite favorably to the price in my locality for unsalted, roasted-in-the-shell pistachios. Because I don't need the shells for recycling or composting, I'm really happy to buy the shelled pistachios in bulk, and pay for nothing but the nutmeats.

title: Exotic, Floral-Flavored Vanilla Beans
text:
Tahitian vanilla beans don't taste at all like "vanilla" to me. They impart an exotic floral flavor, that makes me think of flowers growing in a lush Tahitian rain forest. Probably the unusual flavor shouldn't have surprised me, because Tahitian beans are not only grown in a different climate from the usual "Bourbon" vanilla beans (grown in Madagascar or Mexico), but they also come from a plant that is a hybrid of the Bourbon vanilla plant and another totally different strain of vanilla plant.

However, my "Tahitian Vanilla" ice cream is tasty, and it's a fun variation on regular vanilla ice cream! These high-quality Tahitian beans are plump and moist, and contain lots of tiny vanilla seeds. The seeds are slightly harder to remove from the pods than the seeds of Bourbon vanilla beans. Also, the Tahitian beans are slightly shorter and flatter than Bourbon vanilla beans. In recipes, one Tahitian vanilla bean is the equivalent of one Bourbon vanilla bean, but your recipe will not taste the same. For beans that have regular vanilla flavor, check out [[ASIN:B0002PHEZC Premium Bourbon-Madagascar Vanilla Beans]] and [[ASIN:B000CPYVGM Award Winning Mexican Vanilla Beans]]. The Mexican beans are my personal favorites.

title: Poppyseed Muffins With Great Crunchy Texture
text:
This mix produces a poppyseed muffin with great crunchy texture. All you need is the mix, a cup of water, and an egg (although you can make an "extra rich & moist" version by adding 1/3 cup vegetable oil to the mix). The box contains a large packet of poppy seeds, which you fold into the batter. The finished muffins look exactly like the photo on the box: lots of poppy seeds!

The muffins have a strong almond flavor, which must come from almond extract, because it has a slight "chemical" or "medicinal" edge. This is fine with me, but some might not like it. Each muffin has 240mg of sodium per muffin, so the mix is okay for persons on a low-to-moderate-salt diet, as long as they don't eat too many!

The box also has instructions for using the mix to make almond poppyseed quick bread or pound cake. I've tried several of the Krusteaz muffin mixes, and they're all good, but this one is probably my favorite.

title: Great Choice for Halloween Candy
text: Jelly Bellies are absolutely the BEST jelly beans!  I purchased these for Halloween candy, because we don't usually have many trick-or-treaters, and adults love the leftovers.  This box has 36 sealed 1-oz. packets (2-3/8" x 5"), each with about 26 jelly beans in assorted flavors.  The flavors vary from packet to packet, but include licorice, buttered popcorn, watermelon, and bubble gum, in addition to the usual vanilla and fruit flavors.  For smaller packets (.35 oz.) that are also suitable for Halloween, take a look at these:  [[ASIN:B000IKDIWY Jelly Belly Jelly Beans, Assorted Flavors, 0.35-Ounce Bags (Pack of 80)]].

title: Sealed Packets With Eleven Terrific Jelly Beans
text: Jelly Bellies are absolutely the BEST jelly beans!  This box has 80 sealed packets (2-1/4" x 3") with 11 jelly beans each, in assorted flavors.  The flavors vary from packet to packet, but include licorice, buttered popcorn, and bubble gum, in addition to the usual vanilla and fruit flavors.  For Halloween treats, I'll probably offer two packets to each child, unless I start to run out of candy!  For larger packets (1 oz.) that are still suitable for Halloween, consider these: [[ASIN:B002VHJ178 Jelly Belly Gold Stripe Thank You Assorted, 1-Ounce Bags (Pack of 36)]].

title: Beautiful Beans at a Beautiful Price
text:
My [[ASIN:B003KYSLMW Cuisinart ICE-21 Ice Cream Maker]] had a recipe for fresh whole vanilla bean ice cream that I wanted to try--until I encountered the $15 price tag on a single bottled bean at the grocery store! So I ordered 7 fresh beans from Amazon (specifically from JR Mushrooms, shipped by OliveNation) for less than half that price.

Seven plump, flexible, aromatic vanilla beans soon arrived. It was a treat just to open the package, because the beans smelled so good. They came in a sealed but resealable plastic bag, which I put into a glass jar in my spice cupboard for long-term storage. The French Vanilla Bean ice cream turned out to be wonderful!

After using up the 7 beans, I placed a second order for 16 beans. These beans came sealed in shrink-wrapped plastic, so the package itself wasn't infused with that marvelous vanilla aroma. However, the beans are the same big, plump, flexible vanilla beans as before, and they make terrific ice cream!

title: Delicious, Highly Spiced Noodle Bowl, Still High in Sodium
text:
One bowl (2 servings) makes a filling lunch for one. The highly spiced chicken flavoring (definitely not for wimps!) is quite tasty. The extra-long, curly noodles are delicious. There are no vegetable or chicken bits in the bowl--the ones shown on the package label are apparently garnishes added for the photo shoot. The ingredients list reveals more beef flavorings than chicken flavorings.

The food prep couldn't be easier: (1) pull back the cover, (2) open the seasonings packet and dump it in, (3) fill the bowl with room temperature water, (4) microwave for 3 minutes, and (5) let cool for at least one minute before eating. One is wise to heed the instructions to wait a bit before eating--my microwaved noodles were still too hot to eat after three or four minutes. The bowl can also be prepared by adding hot water and letting the mixture steep for three minutes.

Unfortunately, the 2-serving bowl--and who eats only half a bowl of noodles?--delivers a whopping 1780 mg of sodium, even though this particular product has 30% less sodium than the manufacturer's regular product. The FDA recommended daily intake for sodium is about 2300 mg of sodium, so if you eat the whole bowl, you have consumed about 77% of your day's recommended sodium. (The package label is reasonably honest, stating that 1 serving delivers 37% of your daily sodium.)

Because my diet is generally a low-salt one (by choice, not for medical reasons), I could tell that some of the spicy flavor was coming from salt rather than from other seasonings. However, the product did not make me immediately thirsty, as most high-salt commercial food products do. For me, this product is worth only three stars ("it's okay"), because I can't eat it on a regular basis and still keep my salt intake down. That's too bad, because a product like this is great to have on the pantry shelf to pull out for a quick meal.
