title: Excellent Ceylon Cinnamon,
text:
This superb True Cinnamon has the fresh scent of Ceylon Cinnamon which is what I expected. It did not disappoint in that manner. The packers at Amazon, though stuffed these at the bottom of a box and overfilled the box with other products, thus crushing these expensive sticks. I called Amazon upon inspection of the bag. It’s not returnable, but Amazon owned up. I wish these were packed in a jar or canister to help protect them.

But I do want to say that if you’re reading poor reviews on this Ceylon, I’m guessing that the poor reviewers may be confused with the difference between the Ceylon Cinnamon and the Cassia. Ceylon (from Sri Lanka) is the healthy kind. Cassia cinnamon (from China) can be toxic if consumed in high quantities. Don’t just take my word alone, research Ceylon… there’s so much solid information available. If you order this one, I hope it comes intact. I’ve found it to be the real deal.

I like roasted nuts and grains so much and they are so good for our bodies. I am so pleased with the dry skillet, low heat, frequent stirring in my non stick skillet. I cook about a cup of grain. I put it in a small glass jar after it cooled. I will use in risotto, stir fry and roasted cherry tomato, garlic, pesto, EVOO pasta sauce. I highly recommend this product. I love this sprinkled on multigrain whole wheat bread lathered with whipped cream cheese. I eat a toasted piece almost every morning.

title: Can be used various ways.

I knew I could buy dried mushrooms and make my own in my food processer. They add a great flavor to soups, stews, hash, eggs, pasta sauces, on and on. You can used a coffee bean blender or a mini food processor. A little goes a long way. I bought the 8 oz. bag and upon receiving, I did not think that was enough. But 8 oz. of dried mushrooms goes on giving. And I also save the broth they make while soaking.

title: Toasted with pine nuts

After a few sandwiches, I did drain the oil into a separate jar for pasta salads. I like it better drier. The flavor are fantastic on salami, pastrami, ham and mortadella. A must try.

It is good on hot dog, in pasta salad, potato salad etc. Try it for yourself. It is great.

People in the South have never heard of it. It is wonderful in chicken salad.

title: I bought this brand of broth at Publix and used ...

I bought Fresh Gourmet Wonton Strips from Amazon Prime and made my own chicken egg rolls and dinner was superb.

title: Great and crispy

I open them then reseal after each use and put in a zipper freezer bag. They will stay crisp for a year this way. Take out what you need and refreeze. They are so worth the price.

I used it in beef tip stroganoff the other night and it was inedible because the smell tainted the taste of the sauce. I use a lot of butter, eggs, half and half, and sour cream in my cooking so I prefer using fresh when possible, but I have used powdered cheese, milk, and egg products with great results. So I had no reason to doubt this as well. After reading the good reviews I ordered it and have used about a cup of the powder. I really have been disappointed with this container. I really feel that this is a one time thing and would like to know if anyone other than me thinks this has a plastic smell?

title: Totally wonderful if not overcooked
text:
Unlike microwave popcorn, you cannot hear the pork skins cooking, so only by trial and error and the wattage of your microwave will you know how long to cook these. Cooked to the proper timing, they will be fluffy, melt in you mouth. 15 seconds too long you will have a stinky unedible product.
It is something that you must try. I cooked mine for 1 minute 45 seconds and they were not all done. I do not know the wattage of my microwave.
So try them for your self. As I said, they are different than popcorn, but have the same results if left too long in the micowave, stinky.

title: Dusty and chewy wafers with no flavor; terrible ingredients
text:
This is the first time I have ever eaten Stroopwafels before, which apparently is a Dutch classic. There is one 5.29 ounce resealable package for the very expensive current price of $10.99. There are about 5 servings per package and with a serving size equaling 4 of the quarter-sized waffles at 140 calories each. Each serving has 5g of fat, 5mg of cholesterol, 75mg of sodium, 21g of carbohydrates that includes 13g of added sugars, and 1g of protein.

I tried the chocolate variety. The waffles were dusty, meaning there was powdered waffle dust on all of the wafers. Even though there is supposed to be caramel in the middle with a real Bourbon vanilla flavor, all I tasted was a chewy / gummy waffle with practically zero flavor. Taste is very subjective but this is definitely not worth eating to me, especially since it is made of junk.

The ingredients are wheat flour, glucose syrup, sugar, palm oil, butter, molasses, cocoa powder that is rainforest alliance certified, soy flour, canola oil, salt, wheat dextrin, natural flavor, baking soda, soy lecithin, citric acid, cinnamon, vanilla bean, and mono- and diglycerides of fatty acids.

UPDATE: I had my boyfriend try these and he gave them 3 stars because he does not taste any caramel. He also doesn't like the high price. He said he would pass on these. He said he purchased other Stroopwafels in the past that he loves more.

title: Like a soft oatmeal cookie with just a whisper of peanut butter flavor which is 8th ingredient
text:
Taste is a very personal thing and I can see where some would love this bar and others will dislike it.

There are 12 1.24-ounce soft-baked oatmeal squares in this package. Each package is one serving of 160 calories with 2g of saturated fat, 140mg of sodium, 10g of total sugars, 22g of total carbs, 0 cholesterol, 3g of dietary fiber, and 3g of protein. The bars are soft and taste plain with a hint of peanut butter. Since I do not like very sweet items or dessert with peanut butter, this is perfect for me. I really like these bars. If they had a little more oat flavor, I would have rated them 5 stars.

The first several ingredients are whole grain oats, sugar, whole wheat flour, canola oil, peanut flour, tapioca syrup, palm kernel and palm oils, peanut butter, vegetable glycerin, honey, etc.

The current price of $3.78 ($4.73 minus 20%) comes to a very reasonable $0.32 cents per bar.

title: Incredibly delicious; 4 servings per bar but impossible to not eat entire bar; PRICEY
text: I would have rated this 5 stars if it were not for the $10 price tag. There is one small 2.82 ounce bar in this package. The bar is apparently four servings as 1/4 of the bar equals one 130 calorie serving coming to $2.50 each. However, it was impossible to eat one serving. I could have eaten the entire bar but ate half of it in seconds.  This is so delicious to me. It reminds me of a nougat bar with nuts except the chocolate is soft and just melts in my mouth. This would have to be no more than $5.99 for me to rate this 5 stars. As for taste, it is off the charts for me.

title: 24 delicious Nutella snacks (12 each variety); breadsticks break easily
text:
There are two 12-packs in the shipment in two boxes. One box has 12 1.8-ounce Nutella with breadsticks containers that are 260 calories each. The other box has 12 1.9-ounce Nutella with salted pretzels containers that are 270 calories each.

The Nutella section is only half full but is enough for all the breadsticks or pretzels in the package unless one overdoes it. I love that the spread is not sweet at all, so this makes for a pleasant snack. I actually prefer the pretzel variety due to the salt (210 mg) on the pretzels, which enhances the flavor of the Nutella. Also, I like the pretzels because they hold up better and do not break when scooping out the Nutella.  The breadstick variety are more plain with only 130 mg of sodium and they break easily when trying to scoop out the Nutella. If they didn't break easy, I would have rated this ensemble 5 stars.

At the current price of $28.04, this comes to $1.17 per serving/container.

UPDATE March 6, 2023 - I gave some of these to my neighbor and he rated this 4.5 stars because he said it is sometimes hard to get the stick out of the Nutella. Other than this, he loves them.

title: All liquid, powder, and sauce; not what I expected for candy
text:
I never had Mexican candy before but I did not expect all liquids, powders, and sauces. There are 13 pieces of "candy" as follows:

1. Chamoy flavored hot liquid

2. Tamarind flavored hot liquid

3. Sour flavored hot liquid

4. Tamarind flavored hot candy with chewing gum--seems like a gel or powder

5. Lemon flavored hot candy with chewing gum--seems like a gel or powder

6. Sweet 'n sour mango flavored powder

7. Sweet 'n sour chamoy flavored powder

8. Sweet 'n watermelon chamoy flavored powder

9. Cherry flavored lollipop with chili powder--but is in all powder form

10.  Mango flavored lollipop with chili powder--but is in all powder form

11. Green apple flavored lollipop with chili powder--but is in all powder form

12. Swinkles salsagheti watermelon flavored hot candy strips with tamarind flavored sauce

13. Swinkles salsagheti mango flavored hot candy strips with tamarind flavored sauce

I tried the mango flavored candy strips which are similar to Twizzlers that look like spaghetti. I threw out the sauce because putting sauce on my candy repulses me. The strips were okay and a little hot with a hint of sweetness. I could only eat a small piece before throwing out the entire package.

This candy is definitely not for me and certainly not what I expected. I should not have ordered this. I hope I can find someone to give this to. However, for someone familiar with Mexican candy, this might be great. I just do not consider liquid, powder, and sauce candy. The current price of $11.99 comes to $.92 cents per item.

title: Fresh seeds with the typical licorice flavor; good for tea, as a digestive, or desserts
text: I love the taste of anise seeds and periodically just eat a few seeds to get the lovely licorice flavor or as a digestive for an upset stomach. This is also great as a tea when steeped in boiling water for 10 minutes. It can also be used in desserts such as biscotti.  These seeds are fresh and fragrant and the current price of $9.99 for 7 ounces is competitively priced.

title: Flavorful cashews; a bit dusty (I suspect due to transit)
text: I've ordered Food to Live nuts and grains before and particularly like their nuts. These cashews are very good and since they are dry roasted with a bit of Himalayan salt, they are more flavorful than raw cashews. My only complaint is that there is more nut dust than typical and I suspect that is due to this being bagged instead of boxed for shipment. The current price of $18.89 ($20.99 minus 10%) comes to a competitively priced $9.45 per pound.

title: Fragrant & flavorful tisane w/turmeric as predominant note; unsafe for those w/high BP
text:
There is .82 ounces of loose leaf organic whole leaf herbal tisane in this resealable pouch. There is enough for about 10 cups. Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this loose leaf herbal tisane is very good. Upon opening the pouch I was hit with a super fragrant turmeric aroma with just a hint of ginger.

The flavor is fresh and tastes as I expected. I brewed one teaspoon in a cup of almost boiling water (208 degree F) for 4 minutes. I do use my own tea bags and infusers. The resulting brew is a slightly cloudy butter yellow and tastes like turmeric with a hint of ginger and lemon. I like the flavor profile.

The ingredients are organic ginger, organic liquorice root, organic lemongrass, organic turmeric, organic orange peel, organic lemon peel, organic lemon oil, and natural mandarin flavoring. Unfortunately, I should not be consuming this tea since I have high blood pressure. Licorice root should be avoided for those with high BP. There are also some other side effects listed on the web. For those who do not have this worry, this is a flavorful tea.

This is distributed out of Great Neck, New York but I have no idea where the ingredients are sourced from.

In summary, I am a tea purist and 99.9% of the time only drink teas (meaning those that are made from the camellia sinensis evergreen leaves). I rarely like tisanes and usually try them and then give them away. However, this herbal tisane is excellent and very flavorful. It is unfortunate it has licorice root. The current price of $5.99 is competitively priced for this small 0.82 ounce amount. This is a quality tisane and fresh too; it's just not for me.

title: Fragrant & flavorful tisane w/lemongrass & ginger notes; unsafe for those w/high BP
text:
There is .57 ounces of loose leaf organic whole leaf herbal tisane in this resealable pouch. There is enough for about 10 cups. Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this loose leaf herbal tisane is very good. Upon opening the pouch I was hit with a super fragrant lemongrass and ginger aroma.

The flavor is fresh and tastes as I expected. I brewed one teaspoon in a cup of almost boiling water (208 degree F) for 4 minutes. I do use my own tea bags and infusers. The resulting brew is a cloudy yellow and tastes like lemongrass and ginger. I love it.

The ingredients are organic ginger, organic lemongrass, organic liquorice root, organic lemon peel, and organic spearmint. Unfortunately, I should not be consuming this tea since I have high blood pressure. Licorice root should be avoided for those with high BP. There are also some other side effects listed on the web. For those who do not have this worry, this is a flavorful tea.

This is distributed out of Great Neck, New York but I have no idea where the ingredients are sourced from.

In summary, I am a tea purist and 99.9% of the time only drink teas (meaning those that are made from the camellia sinensis evergreen leaves). I rarely like tisanes and usually try them and then give them away. However, this herbal tisane is excellent and very flavorful. It is unfortunate it has licorice root. The current price of $5.99 is pricey for this small 0.57 ounce amount but this is a quality tisane and fresh too.

title: Fill line is misprinted; good ingredients but a bit salty & lackluster for the price
text:
For the current price of $17.99, there are four individual 2-ounce (before adding water) cups of soup in this package. At that price, this comes to $4.50 per serving. Each serving is 200 calories with 5g of protein and 790mg of total sodium. There are 4g of dietary fiber and 5g of sugars but none of it is added.

The ingredients are sweet potato vermicelli noodles, organic tomato, non-GMO lentils, celery, organic carrots, organic onions, green onion, organic garlic, sea salt, organic basil, oregano, and black pepper.

There are two ways to prepare this either by adding boiling water to BEYOND the fill line, stirring, then letting it sit covered for 10 minutes or doing the same and microwaving for two minutes and letting it sit for 10. Note that the label on the cap says there is a misprint on the container and the fill line is too low. Therefore, I filled it to about one inch lower than the top.

The soup was lackluster and flat and also a bit salty for my taste. I prefer a hearty soup with beefy (meaning toothful or thick) ingredients. I do love that the ingredients are nutritional but I can make a much better soup from scratch that is tremendously cheaper, more flavorful, and less salty.

title: Some nuts in the bag do not taste or look fresh; they’re good but not great
text:
This is a 15-ounce bag of Brazil nuts. Most of the nuts are fresh tasting but about one quarter of them are browner than I expected and do not taste as fresh as I expected. I have tasted better Brazil nuts in the past. 

The current price of $16.99 is competitively priced. Note that no more than two to three of these nuts should be eaten in one day because of the potential of selenium toxicity as they are high in that trace mineral. I usually eat two a day and take them like pills although I love Brazil nuts and can eat the whole bag if I didn't know better.

title: Utterly delicious; sweet, savory, & crispy; I ate 4 servings in 1 sitting; dangerous & $$$
text:
There are three four-ounce pouches of sweet chili sesame snack mix in this box. They are utterly delicious. So delicious that I ate the entire four-serving package in one sitting. I expected them to be spicy but they are not. They have a sweet and savory flavor and a nice crispy crunch.

Each serving is 120 calories with 150mg of salt, 2g of protein, 20g of carbs 2g of which is added sugar, etc. The ingredients are rice pillows (whole grain rice, rice, sugar, salt, molasses, tocopherols for freshness), oat pillows....., etc. Note that there are 4 servings per bag, which means there are 12 servings over the3 bags. However, it is easy to make this into only three servings by eating the entire bag.

The current price of $19.51 ($22.95 minus 15%) comes to $6.50 per bag or $1.63 per one-ounce serving which is expensive.

title: This stollen is very good but it comes in plastic wrap inside a box w/o any tin
text: My 95-year old friend loves this stollen and I ordered two of these for him on Amazon. The one he got for a gift came in a tin. We thought this one also came in a tin, especially at this high almost $40 price. However, it just comes in plastic wrap in an thin box. The stollen is good and that did not stop us from ordering another one but be aware that the box is not a tin like we expected. Otherwise, this is an excellent but super expensive stollen.

title: Very greasy, salty, & a little spicy; visually unappetizing; more like an Asian Indian sauce
text:
This looks like something my cats threw up and is visually unappetizing. Nevertheless, I heated this up in my steamer for about nine minutes. The sauce separates and has to be stirred even though it does not say that on the package. I put this over my fish and asparagus. It did add flavor but I personally do not care for this and felt nauseous after eating only a few teaspoons of this on my food. I threw out the leftovers. This reminds me of Asian Indian sauces in both look and smell, which I absolutely hate. I feel someone who likes that type of food would like this.

There are supposedly four 1/4 cup servings in this package. Since the sauce is a little thick, I had to dig into the package to extract some that was left behind. In the end there are more like three 1/4 cup servings than four servings in this package. Each serving is a whopping 90 calories with 400mg of sodium--way too much in my opinion. The ingredients are filtered water, heavy cream (cream, milk), red onion, roasted poblano pepper, light sour cream (Grade A cultured cream, skim milk, Vitamin A palmitate), extra virgin olive oil, cilantro, salt, roasted chicken stock (chicken stock, roasted chicken stock, mirepoix stock [made of carrot, celery, and onion stock], dried chicken stock, salt, gelatin, water, white wine), cornstarch, sugar, black pepper, dried garlic, lime juice concentrate, dried roasted garlic, ground jalapeno pepper, lactic acid, and natural flavor.

The current price of $15.44 for two 8-ounce packages is outrageous for this as it comes to $7.72 per pouch.

title: Shockingly delicious & thick; goes from powdered form to creamy in minutes w/water & microwave
text:
There are six microwavable cups in this package for the current price of $17.99, which comes to an expensive $3 each and is the only reason I took off one star. 

When I first saw this, I thought I was going to throw it out after trying it. There is nothing but powder in the cup. I actually thought it was all spice. Nevertheless, I removed the lid, added water up to the fill line (which comes to the half cup mark), stirred it well, then nuked it on high in the microwave for 90 seconds. I then stirred it again and let it sit for three minutes. After three minutes it was thick but not over thick. It is more like a very creamy sauce.

I used this on broccoli but I wanted to just eat it out of the container as is with a spoon after having tasted it. This would be fantastic as a sauce on cooked white rice and that is what I am going to use the remaining containers on.

I expected this to be hot and spicy due to the jalapeno but it was more on the mild side. There are 25 calories per serving and 4 servings in the container. I ate the entire container myself for 100 calories.

The ingredients are vegetables (black bean, onion, green bell pepper, jalapeno pepper, and garlic), salt, spice, citric acid, sodium aluminosilicate, cilantro, and lime peel. Everything but the salt, spice, citric acid, and sodium aluminosilicate are dried. There is 150mg of sodium per serving which is 600mg for the entire package and that equals about 1/4 teaspoon of salt, which is a lot. There is also 1g of protein and 2% of potassium. This is nothing the healthiest thing around but the flavor is outstanding for me and tastes like black beans.

Note that this is manufactured in a facility that processes milk, wheat, egg, soy, peanuts, and tree nuts. I can't wait until I use this on rice.

title: Tasteless “winter / Christmas” sprinkles for cookies or ice cream
text: This is an 8-ounce bag of tiny white snowflakes and red and green jimmies (or what I would call ice-cream sprinkles) that can be used as an ice cream topping or to decorate holiday cookies. They have a nice crunch but they are utterly tasteless. Most sprinkles I tried before are pretty much the same except for chocolate ones. Therefore, I only marked it down two stars for the lack of taste. The current price of $12.99 is pricey too.

title: 27 individually wrapped candies with Scripture wrappers; delicious
text:
This candy is delicious and comes in a nice tin. There were 27 candies in total in my box but based on the nutrition label, I think I was only supposed to get 26 candies. The reason is that one serving is 50 calories and a serving is 2 candies. Each candy is individually wrapped with a different scripture on the wrapper. For example, "And it shall come to pass in the last days, saith God, I will pour out of my Spirit upon all flesh: Acts 2:17."

The candy tastes exactly like it is supposed to of strawberries and cream.

At the current price of $7.99, this comes to $.30 cents per candy which is competitively priced

title: Delicious hard candy with matcha flavoring; packaging in Japanese
text:
This candy is delicious and comes in a very small package. There are 12 candies in total. Each candy is individually wrapped. It tastes like sweet candy with a nice match flavor. According to the sticker on the clear box, each candy is 20 calories. The ingredients are sugar, starch syrup, and matcha powder.

At the current price of $8, this comes to $.67 cents per candy which is expensive for a lozenge the size of a dime.

title: Delicious; the yellow-packaged ones taste like cotton candy to me; packaging in Japanese
text:
This candy is delicious and comes in a very small package. There are 12 candies in total (6 in yellow packages and 6 in red packages). Each candy is individually wrapped. I tried the yellow packaged one and it tastes like cotton candy. According to the sticker on the clear  box, each candy is 20 calories. The yellow is made of sugar and starch syrup. The red is the same but the last ingredient is vegetable juice color.

At the current price of $8, this comes to $.67 cents per candy which is expensive for a lozenge the size of a dime.

title: Fruity tasting tisane; brews to a reddish auburn; contains sugar
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal or other ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this tisane is good. There is one four-ounce pouch of what looks like freeze-dried fruit pieces. This tisane almost looks edible. The ingredients are apple, pineapple, hibiscus, strawberry, banana, natural extracts, cane sugar, and citric acid.

Since this contains cane sugar and natural extracts, this has 5 calories per cup. I actually think it is more calories because it says there are 32 servings per bag and I cannot get 32 servings out of 4 ounces of product. It is more like 16 servings (teaspoons).

I brewed one teaspoon of this with boiling water and steeped it for five minutes. The tea smells and tastes fruity and brews to a reddish auburn hue. The flavor is balanced and light.

I like the taste but I do not like that it contains sugar. I also do not like that they say this supports weight loss and digestive health.

At the current price of $19.99, this comes to $5 per ounce which is pricey.

title: Sweet-smelling green tea with an almost tasteless clear yellow brew
text:
There are 100 teabags in this pouch. When dry, the tea has a light sweet fragrance that smells very nice. I brewed one teaspoon at 175 degrees Fahrenheit for 4 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a clear yellow but the flavor is almost non-existent. This tea comes from China and the only ingredients are organic green tea.

In summary, I would not order this again because of the lack of flavor when brewed. The current price of $11.13 ($13.09 minus 15%) for 100 teabags comes to a super cheap $0.11 cents per cup.

title: Good dark cayenne pepper with the right amount of heat but pricey
text:
Cayenne pepper has many uses including as a spice for soups or rice, or meat, etc. I sometimes use it on my meat and vegetables or even eggs.

This is one 2.5-ounce container. I tasted this and it has just the right amount of heat and adds a good flavor to a dish, providing that is what is desired.

The current price of $9.99 is really pricey for this and is the only reason I took off one star. Note that this is dark cayenne pepper but I'm not one that notices much difference between the lighter version and this.

title: Flavorful tea/tisane with 6.9 calories per cup but it contains licorice; can raise BP
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. This is both a tea and a tisane as it has both ingredients. There is one resealable pouch of 25 tea bags.

The ingredients are organic (black tea, hibiscus, licorice), raspberry, strawberry, natural strawberry extract, and natural blueberry extract. I assume this gets its 6.9 calories per cup from the extract in the blend. The country of origin is India. I did not realize this contained licorice or I would not have ordered it. Licorice should be avoided by those with high BP. Excessive consumption (whatever that means--perhaps several cups per day) of licorice tea can actually cause high BP.

I brewed one tea bag in 8 ounces (6.7 ounces is recommended) for 4 minutes. The brew came out a clear golden amber. The flavor is fruity and smooth and overall very good.

At the current price of $14.99 this comes to a price of $.60 cents per cup.

title: Super minty and delicious decaf green tea / mint tisane
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. This is both a tea and a tisane as it has both ingredients. There is one resealable pouch of 22 tea bags. The organic ingredients are decaf green tea with spearmint and peppermint leaves.

I brewed this with 10 ounces of boiling water and steeped it for 10 minutes as recommended. The tea is fresh, smells "minty" fragrant, and brews to a clear caramel hue. The flavor is super minty but balanced. It is absolutely delicious. What is interesting is that green tea is usually not brewed with boiling water because it creates a bitter brew but herbal ingredients are brewed that way. Nevertheless, there is no bitterness in this tea.

The current price of $10.99 comes to a very reasonable $.50 cents per bag.

title: Light, airy, and tasty; 110 calories per bag; Fuji Apples only ingredient
text: There are eight one-ounce bags in this set. Each bag's only ingredient is Fuji Apples and is 110 calories each. Since these are freeze-dried, they are dehydrated and are light and airy and tasty. Both my boyfriend and I tried them and really liked them. However, the current price of $28.99 comes to $3.62 per bag which is expensive.

title: Fiery hot hot sauce
text: This is as described. This is a 2 liter bottle of XXXTRA HOT SAUCE. I tried it on some rice and it was fiery hot. The first ingredient is habanero pepper followed by water, tomato, salt, onion, acetic acid, etc. The current price of $39.97 comes to almost $20 per liter.

title: Average quality Jasmine tea; lightly fragrant & light tasting; brews to a clear amber
text:
This is a four ounce bag of Jasmine pearls. Upon opening, there was only a faint aroma of Jasmine and almost a sour smell as well. I brewed one teaspoon to about 8 ounces of 175 degree Fahrenheit water for 4 minutes. This tea has a faint jasmine flavor to it and is a clear amber brew. It is not the most flavorful Jasmine tea I ever tried and certainly not my favorite.

The current price of $10.49 ($14.99 minus 30%) is competitively priced. This tea is from China and the only ingredient is jasmine pearls green tea.

title: Good tisane; tastes tropical/coconutty like it should; brews to a clear orange auburn
text:
[[VIDEOID:177f76504bbd78fde6445714c553d23f]] Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this tisane is good. There are 12 K-cups in this box. The ingredients are organic rooibos, organic cinnamon, organic ginger, organic cardamom, and natural coconut flavor.

This tea brews to a clear orange auburn hue. The flavor is definitely tropical and tastes like coconut. It also has a very balanced flavor profile.

The current price of $11.99 ($14.99 minus $3) comes to a very expensive $1 per K-cup.

title: Deep coffee-colored brew with a subtle earthy but smooth flavor profile
text:
I love all things tea and have many different teas. This tea is an organic Pu-erh tea from a tea garden in Yunnan, China. There are 120 triangular shaped corn fiber tea bags in this package.

It does not say if this is raw or ripe Pu-erh tea but based on the current price of $15.19 ($18.99 minus 20%), which comes to $0.13 cents per tea bag, I would say this is ripe Pu-erh. Based on my Internet research, the healthline site says "Raw pu-erh is made from non-fermented green tea leaves that were picked, quickly roasted, sun-dried and then steamed to be compressed into round disks called cakes. After which the cakes are aged until the tea's taste is properly transformed." The Mansatea site says, "Raw pu-erh can be aged upwards of 50 years or more. Younger raw pu-erh is aged anywhere from a few months to a few years, whereas aged pu-erh is generally aged anywhere from a decade to upwards of 50 years or more. Unlike raw pu-erh, ripe pu-erh goes through an accelerated aging process."

I brewed one tea bag at 212 degrees Fahrenheit for 4 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The coffee-color brew was clear and the flavor subtly earthy with a very smooth finish. Most Pu-erh has a stronger earthly and slightly smoky and complex flavor profile. However, I really like the subtle flavor of this one, although real Pu-erh aficionados probably will not like this.

title: There is sediment in this brew; tea has to be strained
text:
[[VIDEOID:26e4f91755f0a5bf8d71bd4d89576de2]] This is a tisane because it is not a true tea like teas made from the camellia sinensis plant. Nevertheless, there is sediment in this brew. I actually had to strain this after brewing it. The flavor is very good but I absolutely hate sediment (mushed up leaves) in my brew. The tisane brews to a medium amber that would be clear if particles weren't floating in it.

There are 12 K-cups in the box. The ingredients are organic chamomile, organic lime tree leaves, organic peppermint, organic rosehip, and organic lemon flavor. The current price of $10.29 ($13.29 minus $3) comes to a pricey $0.86 cents per cup. If I did not have to strain this tea, I would have rated it 5 stars for flavor.

title: Tastes exactly as mate should when not brewing it like the Argentinians
text: [[VIDEOID:cef61208e320a038f48d7005eab82176]] I consider this a tisane because it is not a true tea like teas made from the camellia sinensis plant. Nevertheless, the flavor is good. There are 12 K-cups in the box. The ingredients are organic roasted yerba mate and it tastes like it too. It is a bit smoky but flavorful and relatively strong but not overpowering. It brews to a deep caramel amber. However, the current price of $10.29 ($13.29 minus $3) comes to a pricey $0.86 cents per cup.

title: Brew is very bitter due to high temperature of Keurig; brews to a muddy looking tan
text:
[[VIDEOID:8dcd14215b9ce58d8218ca2ca3a738ae]] This is organic green tea. There are 12 K-cups in this box. The brew is a muddy or cloudy looking tan. I just discovered that all Keurigs apparently brew at 192 degrees (except for the Keurig Elite which apparently can be adjusted from 187 to 192). That temperature setting is really too high for green tea. Green teas should be brewed between 175 and 185 degrees. This tea tastes very bitter to me and that ruins the brew. If it were not so bitter, this would probably be great. What a disappointment.

The current price of $10.99 ($13.99 minus $3) comes to an expensive $.92 cents per K-cup.

title: Tasteless tea; brews to a clear golden yellow 1st brewing; clear amber 2nd brewing
text:
This is a tasteless oolong tea with no flavor profile that I can detect. It tastes like I am drinking hot water. I brewed this with 185-degree water for 4 minutes. I used one heaping teaspoon but the instructions say 2 grams per cup. It appears 2 grams of this tea is about a half a teaspoon. Nevertheless, with more tea, it should have a stronger flavor profile but it did not. Also, the leaves only on a few of the "nodules" expanded but most did not. The brew is clear golden yellow and the flavor is absent.

The bag says each serving can be brewed twice. I usually brew my tea once but I brewed this twice, trying 190-degree water, and it was almost as tasteless but had more of a bitter tinge to it. It says this can be brewed between 185 and 190 degrees but the higher temperature did not bring out any more flavor. Most of the leaves unfurled during the second brewing but some still remained in pebble form -- see photo. Also, the second brewing produced a clear amber brew instead of the golden yellow of the first brew -- see photo.

I had high hopes for this tea but am disappointed. The ingredients are organic oolong and ginseng leaves. The current price of $16.95 for 4 ounces comes to $4.24 per ounce.

title: Rooibos tisane with a hint of vanilla; brews to reddish auburn - 2.5 stars
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this tisane contains organic rooibos with organic vanilla and orange flavor. There are 12 K-cups in this box. The brew is a reddish auburn. I can taste the vanilla but not the orange. It is definitely not my cup of tea. Nevertheless, it does taste as I expect it to with the ingredients listed (minus the orange flavor).

The current price of $10.99 ($13.99 minus $3) comes to an expensive $.92 cents per K-cup.

title: Typical sardines in olive oil; skin & bones included but all are edible
text:
There is one serving of bristling wild sardines in this package that is 220 calories. There are 380 mg of sodium per serving and 17 grams of protein (see nutrition label photo). It is packaged in olive oil.

This tastes subtle but good. The fish includes skin and bones but all are edible and I ate it as is on my salad (see photo). The current price of $6.49 is very expensive for a 3.75 ounce package. This is a product of Scotland and is imported by Culinary Collective in Lynnwood Washington. I took off one star for the high price.

title: Tasty whole grain pillows & airy puffs w/ a couple petite pretzels; pricey
text:
This is a tasty mix of whole grain pillows & airy puffs  of golden corn with a couple petite pretzels and other morsels thrown in. I do not like peanut butter much but the flavor of peanut butter is subtle in this mix. Those who want an intense peanut taste will not get it here. Nevertheless, these are addictive and there is a hint of sweetness in every one-ounce serving.

This 4-ounce bag contains 4 servings at 140 calories each. There is zero cholesterol and 125mg sodium and 4g of sugar. At the current price of $7.99, this comes to an expensive $2 per ounce.

title: Nice selection of three green teas; the one with licorice may not be for those with hypertension...
text:
This is a nice selection of three green teas. There are three boxes with 16 tea bags each (jasmine, Japanese, and white elderflower) for a total of 48 tea bags. 

INGREDIENTS
- Japanese Green - organic green tea

- Jasmine Green - organic green tea scented with organic jasmine blossoms

- White elderflower - organic white tea, organic jasmine green tea, organic toasted brown rice, organic Indian sarsaparilla root, organic licorice root, and organic elderflower extract

I tried the elderflower tea brewed for 3 minutes with just boiling water. Usually I do less than a boil, but the box recommends boiling water. It came out very nice and tastes mostly like licorice tea tastes. Since I have high blood pressure, this is the only tea that I have to be careful drinking. The other two are perfect for me. I had tried their mint sage tea (actually a tisane), and loved it. In fact, my boyfriend loved that one so much, he ordered more.

This is the sixth tea of this brand I tried and their teas are always to my liking. The current price of $12.87 comes to 4.29 per box or $.27 cents per tea bag. The only reason I took off one star is because of the elderflower tea that has licorice. Even though there is green tea in this blend, the addition of licorice is a problem for those with hypertension or heart disease as it can increase BP or lead to irregular heart rhythm (arrhythmia). I doubt there is enough in this tea to do damage, but I do avoid licorice for that reason and some other herbal blends.

title: Almost flavorless & no cheddar cheese flavor but I really like the crunch
text: These are almost flavorless and I definitely do not taste the cheddar cheese flavor; however, I really like the crunch and find these very addictive. It is for that reason I rate these 4 stars. For flavor, these get 2 stars. The entire bag is 360 calories as there are 3 servings of 120 calories each per bag (see nutrition label photo). The current price of $5.99 for this 2.2 ounce bag is also pricey. I really, really like these but at that price and with so many calories for such a small amount that is mostly flavorless, I'll pass.

title: Salty salt, made in Mexico that is supposedly free of microplastics
text: The box says this is 100% unrefined organic sea salt. It is also made in Mexico (hence Colima Sea Salt) and is supposedly microplastic free. It tastes great and is a very salty salt and has a nice texture to the granules as I expect of sea salt. The current price of $18 is pricey for 11 ounces, however.

title: Great on fish but good on pasta too; adds a bit of heat and spice; smells a bit fishy
text:
This has an interesting, almost fishy aroma to me but it actually adds flavor and heat to fish and other dishes. I used it on my pasta (see photo). The ingredients are red chili pepper, orange peel, seaweed, white sesame, black sesame, Japanese pepper, and ginger. I suspect the fishy aroma is from the seaweed.

I actually like this and the current price of $7.99 for a 6-ounce jar is competitively priced to me. Note that the card in the cap has to be removed to use the dispensing holes on either side.

title: Fresh and delicious Italian seasoning; the oregano really comes though
text: I actually love this seasoning. The ingredients are basil, oregano, parsley, rosemary, thyme, marjoram, and bay leaves. It tastes fresh and delicious and the oregano is the predominant flavor (to my palate) in this mix. This is great on pizza, potatoes, soups, etc. I love it. Also, the current price of $8.99 for 2.19 ounces is competitively priced for this.

title: Sparkling water from Korea; no English writing on the cans
text:
There is no English on these cans except for "sparkling water" written on the front. Based on the Amazon site, this is from the Chojeong Mineral Spring in South Korea. There are six 8.33 fluid-ounce cans of carbonated sparkling water in this package. It tastes like any carbonated sparkling water I tasted previously.

The current price of $14.90 for 6 comes to $2.48 per can which is very expensive. I took off one star for the price and lack of English on the can. Note that the plastic packaging does have an English label on the bottom. This is zero calories and zero everything per that label. This is manufactured in a facility that also processes milk, soybean, peach, and wheat.

title: Delicious, tangy but sweet, etc.; a nice addition to hamburgers, cheese, omelets, etc.
text:
At the current price of $10 for 6.35 ounces, this is boutique pricing but it is also delicious. The ingredients are sugar, onion, vinegar, water, pectin, raisins, and lemon juice. It is 45 calories for one tablespoon and goes great on hamburgers, cheese, omelets, etc.

I tried this on a cracker and it was sweet and tangy and just overall delicious.

UPDATE: I tried this on scrambled eggs and this changed the flavor profile of the meal. It was a nice change and tasted good. See photo.

title: Green tea with a subtle roasted flavor; brews to a clear yellow
text:
This tea has a nice aroma. I brewed three grams (one heaping teaspoon) at 185 degrees for one minute in 8 ounces of water. It recommends boiling water but boiling water makes green tea bitter, so I never use water that hot. The resulting brew was clear yellow and the flavor tasted slightly smoky/roasted. The bag says the leaves can be brewed several times. I usually only brew my leaves once, but I did brew this twice and it was fine.

This tea is grown in the Chaoshan area of China.

In summary, I like this tea but I do not like that they make claims on the tea package saying this gives an energy boost, prevents bad breath, is detox slimming, is a powerful antioxidant, and provides digestion support. I also do not like that this is distributed by a biotech company. Per my Internet research, "Biotechnology (biotech) companies derive their products from the extraction or manipulation of living organisms." This makes me wonder if they manipulated the tea in some way.

At the current price of $13.47 per this 5.3-ounce bag, this is reasonably priced.

title: Tastes like any reconstituted mushroom; adds texture to a dish but limited flavor; a bit chewy
text:
This is a 3.53 ounce bag of premium mixed dried mushrooms (porcini mushroom, shiitake slices, oyster mushrooms, and agaricus bisporus). This is a product of China.

Per the instructions, I soaked them in warm water for 30 minutes until soft and then strained them through a coffee filter reserving some liquid. I then rinsed the mushrooms under cold water and added them to my rice dish. They gave the dish texture but not much flavor--even with the juices. Also, one of the mushrooms (I think the porcini) was a bit chewy in texture. However, I find the lack of flavor and sometimes chewy texture is typical of many mushrooms whether dried or fresh.

Note that there are two freshness packets in the bag (desiccant) that have to be thrown out. Be sure not to eat that.

The current price of $15.94 ($19.93 minus 20%) comes to $4.52 per ounce.

title: Adds a savory flavor to rice, pasta, soups, etc.; only 40 calories for 2 tablespoons; it's VEGAN
text: Nutritional yeast is a nice option to add savory flavors to dishes such as rice, pasta, soups, etc. for those who are vegan. I'm not vegan but I like the depth this adds to dishes. It is 40 calories per two tablespoons with 30 servings per container. At the current price of $19.97 this comes to $.67 cents per serving.

title: Only a one-serving packet; tea is smooth & slightly vegetal flavor; brew is cloudy & light yellow
text:
This is NOT a 3.5 ounce pouch of tea. There is only one serving of tea (one pack) that can be brewed twice. I brewed this tea at 175 degrees for 1 minute per the instructions. Personally, I prefer to brew tea for 3-4 minutes. The brew is cloudy and a faint yellow color. The tea has a very subtle flavor and has a vegetal overtone.

This is decent tea and I love that it is from Japan. The current price of $4.99 is expensive as it is not 3.5 ounces. I received only one one-serving packet. My 2-star rating is due to the false advertising on the packet size. The tea itself, is 3 stars to me.

title: Tastes like any reconstituted mushroom; adds texture to a dish but limited flavor
text:
This is a 4.23 ounce bag of classical mixed dried mushrooms (suillus granulatus, shiitake slices, wood ear, and oyster mushrooms). This is a product of China.

Per the instructions, I soaked them in warm water for 30 minutes until soft and then strained them through a coffee filter reserving some liquid. I then rinsed the mushrooms under cold water and added them to my rice dish. They gave the dish texture but not much flavor--even with the juices. However, I find that is typical of all mushrooms whether dried or fresh.

Note that there is a freshness packet in the bag (desiccant) that has to be thrown out. Be sure not to eat that.

The current price of $17.59 ($21.99 minus 20%) comes to $4.16 per ounce.

title: Brews to a golden amber; strong roasted flavor; very nice
text:
This tea looks like grains of rice. It has a smoky aroma. I brewed one teaspoon at 195 degrees for 3 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The resulting brew was clear golden amber and the flavor tasted roasted or slightly smoky.

My boyfriend and I both like this tea. This tea is manufactured in Kerala, India. The ingredients are organic green tea.

In summary, we really like this tea. At $22.99 per 3.5-ounce bag, this is pricey.

title: Superb apple cider vinegar; intensely tangy and packs a punch
text: This vinegar is intense and flavorful. It is very tangy as a good vinegar should be. The current price of $11.69 ($12.99 minus 10%) comes to $.69 cents per ounce as it is a 16.9 fluid-ounce bottle.

title: Four varieties/ two pounds of date; all have pits & taste fresh
text:
There are two pounds of dates in this package. These dates taste delicious and fresh but realize there is no internal plastic packaging in the box, so they are not likely to stay fresh for long. There are four types of organic dates in this package - Zahidi, Medjool, Halawy, and Barhi. Note that each date has a pit inside.

The current price of $39.99 comes to $20 per pound or $10 per date variety. I rated this 4 stars only because of the high price and the lack of an internal freshness package.

title: Tasty whole peppers
text:
My boyfriend and I tried these and love them. They are very tasty. He said "there is a lot of sugar in this, right?" There actually is sugar. I taste the vinegar more.

The ingredients are roasted red pepper, water, distilled vinegar, sugar, salt, and garlic cloves. There are 60 calories per serving and 3 servings per 24.3 fluid ounce jar.

At the current price of $9.99 for one jar, these are pricey but good.

title: Light/sweet vegetal fragrance/taste; 1st and 2nd brew cloudy fluorescent yellow
text:
This tea looks like finely mowed blades of grass. It has a light vegetal fragrance with sweet notes. Per the instructions, I brewed one teaspoon at 180 degrees Fahrenheit for 3 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a cloudy fluorescent yellow and the flavor was lightly sweet and vegetal. The second brew was the same hue. I almost always feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking Sencha tea. I love that this comes from Japan instead of China. The only ingredients are organic green tea.

In summary, I like this tea. The current price of $22.99 for a 3.53-ounce bag, is on the low side of competitively priced.

title: Sweet vegetal fragrance/taste; 1st brew cloudy fluorescent yellow; 2nd brew muddy green
text:
This tea looks like finely mowed blades of grass. It has a strong but sweet vegetal aroma. Per the instructions, I brewed one teaspoon at 180 degrees Fahrenheit for 3 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a cloudy fluorescent yellow and the flavor was lightly sweet and vegetal. The second brew was a muddy green. I almost always feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking Sencha tea. I love that this comes from Japan instead of China. The only ingredients are organic green tea.

In summary, I like this tea. The current price of $22.99 for a 3.5-ounce bag, is on the low side of competitively priced.

title: Tastes like regular kettle corn with a crunchy coating; not sweet tasting; stains mouth/hands
text:
This tastes like regular kettle corn with a crunchy coating. Although corn syrup and sugar are the first two ingredients, it does not taste like a candy coating to me as it is not sweet. It does stain my mouth and hands.

There are supposedly 26 servings per container at 110 each serving. Since this is a 26-ounce jug, that means one serving equals one ounce or approximately 2/3 cup per the label. It contains 29% in added sugars.

The current price of $13.99 is competitively priced. This is perfect for the 4th of July in the red and blue colors. The white is the kettle corn itself which is barely noticeable through the color. Personally, I prefer a flavored sweet taste on popcorn (like cherry or something else). The coating on this one does not add any flavor but adds crunch and lots of calories. Otherwise, this is as expected. To me this is 3.5 stars rounded down to 3 because of the extra calories for no more flavor.

UPDATE - I gave this to my neighbor to try and he did not like it. He rated it 2.5 stars because he said there is no taste and he expected something with flavor. It tastes just like regular kettle corn.

title: Rich coffee that is not as mild as I expected but still very good; bag not resealable
text:
My boyfriend and I tried this coffee. This is supposedly two pounds of light ground arabica coffee but it tasted stronger to us. We used 3-1/2 scoops to 8 cups of water. Perhaps we added too much. It tastes much better with cream. We both like it.

Overall, we think this is good coffee. The current price of $24.99 is competitively priced for this as it comes to $12.50 per pound. The biggest negative is that this does not come in a resealable bag.

title: Fresh tasting macadamia nuts but they are diced for recipes
text: These macadamia nuts are fresh and taste great. However, they are not whole. They are all diced. I thought I was going to get whole nuts. These are perfect for recipes. I took off one star because the description is incorrect and because the bag is not resealable. The ingredients are dry roasted Hawaiian macadamia nuts. The current price of $16.99 is competitively priced for this.

title: More like English muffins than pancakes; dry and pasty; needs more liquid in recipe
text:
My boyfriend and I are actually disappointed in these. These were very pasty and gluey and came out more like English muffins than pancakes. It seems they needed more liquid.

There are two packages in this set. The green package with wild mesquite flour is 20.3 ounces and the orange package is 18.5 ounces. I decided to make the pancakes from the orange package with 1-1/3 cups of mix and 2/3 cups of water and 1 large egg and mixed them like the instructions said. I waited 10 minutes as instructed and then put the batter into molds (see photo).

First of all, the batter was not pourable. It was a sticky mess that was hard to get off the spoon. It was as if there wasn't enough liquid. This has healthy ingredients and taste-wise they were good. Texture and pourability-wise they were not. I will add more water next time.

The current price of $25.99 is pricey as well as it comes to $13 per package. Supposedly there are 9 servings per package but I doubt that unless they are silver dollar size pancakes.

title: Good cayenne pepper with the right amount of heat but pricey
text:
Cayenne pepper has many uses including as a spice for soups or rice, or meat, etc. I sometimes use it on my meat and vegetables or even eggs.

This is one 2.5-ounce container. I tasted this and it has just the right amount of heat and adds a good flavor to a dish, providing that is what is desired.

The current price of $8.99 is very pricey for this and is the only reason I took off one star.

title: Fragrant pepper with typical pepper taste & mild intensity; grinder required
text:
The color of this pepper is black and the flavor has that typical pepper taste but with a mild intensity. It smells and tastes fresh and is not overly powerful. I decided to use my mortar and pestle to "grind" it but a grinder works well with this.

Overall this is a great black pepper for those, like me, who prefer a milder pepper. The ingredients are Ceylon black peppercorns. The current price of $4.99 is pricey for two ounces.

title: Mildly flavorful, but also the least toxic, cinnamon powder from Sri Lanka
text:
This is one pound of organic Ceylon cinnamon powder sourced from Sri Lanka. When I opened the package I noticed a much subtler aroma than cassia cinnamon, which is the one predominantly on the market.

This cinnamon can be used to make hot apple cider or rice pudding or other desserts that call for it like baked apples or apple pie. It is also good for tea. I prefer Ceylon cinnamon because it is less toxic but one must realize it is not as flavorful.

The below information is based on my research and is as accurate as possible from the Internet information I found. I am by no means a cinnamon expert, although I am very well aware that Ceylon (this one) is considered true cinnamon and is safer to consume than the rest.

Cinnamon is a spice created from the inner bark of the Cinnamomum tree. The powder is just the ground up bark (meaning ground up cinnamon sticks). There are four different types of those trees that vary by region and they are:

- Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon Cinnamon) - This is considered true cinnamon and has a milder aroma and flavor than what most people are used to. It is tan-brown in color and contains many tight soft layers. It is large and thick. This cinnamon is not known to be toxic in large quantities like the typical cinnamon on the market. Ceylon cinnamon is also less common and is prized as a cooking spice. It is expensive compared to the more common variety. This Cinnamon is of this variety.

- Cinnamomum cassia (a.k.a. Cinnamomum aromaticum ) (Saigon Cinnamon) - is the typical small, thinly rolled, dark brown, and hard cinnamon sticks that most people are familiar with and which is relatively cheap compared to Ceylon. This is also good in desserts. However, this contains a toxin (i.e.Coumarin) that is harmful if too much is consumed. It can cause kidney, liver, and lung damage and may even cause cancer. How much is too much is unknown to me. Note that Ceylon Cinnamon also contains Coumarin too but based on my Internet research, it contains 250 times less than this version.

- Cinnamomum burmannii (Korintje Cinnamon) - This is also known as Indonesian cinnamon, Padang cassia, Batavia cassia and has higher amounts of coumarin than the Saigon Cinnamon above. It is apparently the most common and cheapest type of cinnamon in the US sold in powdered form.

- Cinnamomum loureiroi (Royal Cinnamon) - This is an heirloom variety that is very rarely exported from the mountains around Vietnam.

In summary, this is excellent cinnamon but do realize the fragrance and taste is less pronounced and aromatic than cassia cinnamon and also less toxic but usually more expensive. If I want more flavor, I mix the two cinnamons. I feel the current price of $16.95 for one pound is reasonable for this.

UPDATE Sept 22 - I made two trays of cinnamon baked apples using a 50/50 combination of this Ceylon cinnamon with their Cassia cinnamon. It came out great. See the BEFORE and AFTER photos.

title: Does not taste like Yerba Mate but it’s a nice tasting good "tea"
text:
I consider this a tisane because it is not a true tea. Nevertheless, the flavor is good. There are 20 teabags in this package. 

I brewed a teabag in one cup of boiling water for 4 minutes. The brew came out a clear golden yellow and the flavor tasted like a mix of mint and lemon. 

The ingredients are green mate, lemongrass leaves, spearmint leaves, gingko pieces, ginseng pieces, nettle pieces, calendula petals, cornflower petals, and sunflower petals.

In summary, for the current price of $14.95, this comes to $.75 cents per cup. The flavor is mild and pleasant. However, if one is used to the strong bitter brew of Yerba Mate sipped in a gourd, this tastes nothing like that.

title: Exceptionally flavorful cinnamon powder from Vietnam
text:
This is one pound of organic cassia cinnamon powder sourced from Vietnam. When I opened the package I was struck by the freshness of this powder and the aroma. The taste is exceptionally flavorful.

This cinnamon can be used to make hot apple cider or rice pudding or other desserts that call for it like baked apples or apple pie. It is also great in tea. I personally prefer Ceylon cinnamon.

The below information is based on my research and is as accurate as possible from the Internet information I found. I am by no means a cinnamon expert, although I am very well aware that Ceylon (not this one) is considered true cinnamon and is safer to consume than the rest.

Cinnamon is a spice created from the inner bark of the Cinnamomum tree. The powder is just the ground up bark (meaning ground up cinnamon sticks). There are four different types of those trees that vary by region and they are:

- Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon Cinnamon) - This is considered true cinnamon and has a milder aroma and flavor than what most people are used to. It is tan-brown in color and contains many tight soft layers. It is large and thick. This cinnamon is not known to be toxic in large quantities like the typical cinnamon on the market. Ceylon cinnamon is also less common and is prized as a cooking spice. It is expensive compared to the more common variety.

- Cinnamomum cassia (a.k.a. Cinnamomum aromaticum ) (Saigon Cinnamon) - is the typical small, thinly rolled, dark brown, and hard cinnamon sticks that most people are familiar with and which is relatively cheap compared to Ceylon. This is also good in desserts. However, this contains a toxin (i.e.Coumarin) that is harmful if too much is consumed. It can cause kidney, liver, and lung damage and may even cause cancer. How much is too much is unknown to me. Note that Ceylon Cinnamon also contains Coumarin too but based on my Internet research, it contains 250 times less than this version. I believe this Cinnamon is of this variety.

- Cinnamomum burmannii (Korintje Cinnamon) - This is also known as Indonesian cinnamon, Padang cassia, Batavia cassia and has higher amounts of coumarin than the Saigon Cinnamon above. It is apparently the most common and cheapest type of cinnamon in the US sold in powdered form.

- Cinnamomum loureiroi (Royal Cinnamon) - This is an heirloom variety that is very rarely exported from the mountains around Vietnam.

In summary, this is excellent cinnamon and the fragrance and taste is fresh and aromatic. I feel the current price of $14.95 for one pound is reasonable.

UPDATE Sept 22 - I made two trays of cinnamon baked apples using a 50/50 combination of this Cassia cinnamon with their Ceylon cinnamon. It came out great. See the BEFORE and AFTER photos.

title: No one would ever know you spent so much; it’s mostly packaging – 2.5 stars
text:
This gift box is mostly packaging. I chose the "sending hugs" box. This is what the receiver gets:

- A beautiful magnetic closure box with ribbon with the "unboxme" label on front.

- The contents and nesting material (also packaging) are wrapped in one sheet of tissue paper (see photo).

- A 1.6 ounce box of raspberry jam petites mini butter cookies with jam. There are only four cookies in the box in two separate plastic packages. They were good but I ate them in one sitting.

- A spray of pressed flowers; I am not sure what to do with this as it is useless to me.

- A tiny 3-ounce sea salt and sage candle. The scent is unpleasant to me.

- One skin peel mask.

- One box of 10 herbal sweet rose "tea" (actually it is a tisane). The "tea" is excellent and tastes like a rose and basil combination. I brewed it at 185 degrees Fahrenheit for 4 minutes (even though the box says 1 minute). The brew was a light amber (see photos). Supposedly, each cup is 5 calories and I always thought teas and tisanes were zero calories. The ingredients are organic tulsi and organic rose.

- A "sending hugs" message written on card stock but not actually a card.

The usable gifts to me are the 10 tea bags and 4 cookies. Everything else I would not touch. Even if I liked all these items, the current price of $38 is expensive for such a few items. The box it came in is very nice and certainly reusable but not everyone will reuse a box with the "unboxme" name on it. I would rather make my own basket to give a friend with items I know he or she will use.

title: Tastes like candied fish with leathery texture; VERY PRICEY – 3.5 stars
text:
There is one serving of sweet smoked wild mackerel in this package that is 230 calories drained of the sunflower oil. There are 600 mg of sodium per serving and 15 grams of protein (see nutrition label photo).

The fish is tasty and sweet but also leathery like a jerky. I like it enough to buy it occasionally except that the current price of $6.47 is very expensive for a 3.88 ounce package. This is a product of Scotland and is imported by Culinary Collective in Lynnwood Washington.

title: My boyfriend & I love this; 120 calories per can w/150mg of caffeine; all sugar
text:
There are four 15.5-fluid-ounce cans of mate mate drink in this package. It contains 150mg of caffeine which is almost double the caffeine in a cup of coffee. Each can is a serving at 120 calories each. The ingredients are purified water, organic cane sugar, organic yerba mate extract, organic brewed yerba mate and peppermint (purified water, organic yerba mate, and organic peppermint leaf), organic honey, citric acid, organic mint natural flavor, and organic caffeine.

My boyfriend and I love this. It has a light sweet tea taste. Note that it is all sugar and caffeine. There is none of the yerba mate smokey and bitter flavors that I am familiar with.

At the current price of $10.99 ($11.99 minus $1) this comes to a pricey $3 per can.

title: Fresh, fragrant, & flavorful tisane with predominantly spearmint notes; brew is an orange amber
text:
There is one pound of loose leaf organic whole leaf herbal tisane in this resealable bag. Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this loose leaf herbal tisane is very good. Upon opening the pouch I was hit with a super fragrant spearmint aroma.

The flavor is fresh and tastes as I expected. I brewed one teaspoon in a cup of almost boiling water for 4 minutes. I do use my own tea bags and infusers. The resulting brew is a clear orange amber and tastes subtly like spearmint with other notes. I love it.

The ingredients are organic rosehip, organic verbena, organic spearmint, organic anise, organic lavender, orange lemon peel, and organic rose petals. None of the herbal ingredients are unsafe to me. I've tried other teas before that have licorice and such, which affects blood pressure.

This is distributed out of Great Neck, New York but I have no idea where the ingredients are sourced from.

In summary, I am a tea purist and 99.9% of the time only drink teas (meaning those that are made from the camellia sinensis evergreen leaves). I rarely like tisanes and usually try them and then give them away. However, this herbal tisanes is excellent and very flavorful; it's a keeper. There is no current price listed for this tea but I suspect they will price it in the $25 to $35 range.

The current price of $9.99 is pricey for this small 0.75 ounce amount but this is a quality tisane and fresh too.

title: Faint vegetable flavor w/toasty overtones; fragrant tea; brews to a cloudy green
text:
This tea looks like powdered matcha with tiny blades of grass that have been cut short with roasted brown rice included. The ingredients are actually green tea and brown rice.

This tea has a fragrant aroma that is slightly vegetal but mostly toasty. I brewed one teaspoon at 185 degrees for 2 minutes in 10 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a very cloudy green and the flavor and aroma was toasty with subtle sweet vegetal overtones. I really like this tea.

I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this. I also love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

The current price of $35 for a 7-ounce bag comes to $5 per ounce which I consider expensive but competitively priced. This comes in a resealable pouch.

title: Intensely bold brew; decent coffee for those who love dark roast
text: My boyfriend and I tried this coffee. There is one single serving good enough for a pot of coffee. It does not say on the package whether this should be used to brew 12 cups or 8 cups of coffee. I used it to brew 8 cups because that was the pot size I had. We both felt this made an intense brew; a bit too intense but still very good. It is strong and not as bitter as a Starbucks brew. Overall, we think this is good coffee. The current price of $5.99 is expensive for this, especially for one pot of coffee. I took off 1 star for the high price and 1 star for the taste.

title: Spice doesn’t dispense through small holes well; tastes mostly of garlic/onion w/some heat
text:
This spice is good on fish. It also is for pork, chicken and vegetables. The ingredients are garlic, onion, salt, spices (it doesn't exactly say what spices), dehydrated orange, citric acid, and cayenne.  I can definitely taste the garlic and onion, and the heat from the cayenne. The current price of $12.99 is competitively priced.

Note that the spice does not dispense through the small holes easily. I like this blend but do not love it as it is too one dimensional for me.

title: Subtly flavored green tea with ginger & lemon; has a light golden brew
text:
This is one pound of loose leaf organic green tea, organic ginger, organic lemongrass and organic lemon myrtle in this resealable bag. Upon opening the pouch I was hit with a lemony ginger aroma. I brewed one teaspoon to about 8 ounces of 170 degree Fahrenheit water for 2 minutes just as the instructions indicate.

This aroma is of lemon ginger and it has a subtle lemon ginger flavor. The brew is a light golden. I really like this tea. It is distributed out of Great Neck, New York but I have no idea where the ingredients are sourced from.

The current price of $43.47 comes to $2.72 per ounce, which is pricey.

The reason I rated this 3 stars instead of 4 is because this tea contains myrtle. According to my Internet search Myrtle "contains a chemical that can cause asthma-like attacks and lung failure. Myrtle can also cause nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, low blood pressure, blood circulation disorders, and other problems. There isn't enough information to know if using the leaf and branch of myrtle is safe." With regard to whether it is safe as a tea, my Internet search says that "In traditional herbal medicine Myrtle essential oil was used to combat acne and other skin conditions, as a herbal tea it has been used to treat diabetes by lowering blood glucose levels. Myrtle tea has also been traditionally used to treat urinary and bladder infections."

title: Highly fragrant cardamom; tastes great brewed in coffee, tea, or sweet & savory recipes
text: This cardamom is highly fragrant and tastes great. I use it with the grinds to brew coffee but it can be used in teas or sweet and savory recipes. I am impressed at how fresh this tastes. The current price of $7.99 for 4 ounces is very reasonable.

title: These black cumin seeds can be used like pepper for a multitude of dishes; has a mild flavor
text:
Black cumin seeds supposedly have health benefits. These are fresh and whole and can be used like pepper. I have to grind them or crush them in a mortar and pestle first. I used them on pasta and the flavor is mild.

At the current price of $9.99 for a 16-ounce bag this is reasonably priced.

title: I like the chewy texture but it is otherwise tasteless; cooks in 3 minutes - 2.5 stars
text: This plant-based pasta is made of 100% organic soybean. It cooks in three minutes in boiling water. The aroma while cooking is weird and hard to describe. The texture is chewy but the "pasta" is pretty much tasteless. Also, it seems that whatever I added to it was just soaked up by the fettuccine. Adding butter, cheese, salt, etc. did not heighten the dish in any way like it does with actual pasta. This is not something I would order again. The current price of $7.79 for 8 ounces is also pricey.

title: Muddy looking matcha green tea that tastes healthy & vegetal
text:
There are 20 tea bags inside a resealable pouch. The matcha powder inside gets all over everything. It has a strong vegetal aroma. I brewed one tea bag at 170 degrees (package says 160) for 1 minute in 8 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a muddy green and the flavor sweet and vegetal and I love it.

This is a product of Japan. The ingredients are green tea and matcha green tea powder.

In summary, I really like this tea. At $14.99 for 20 tea bags, it comes to an expensive $.75 cents per cup.

title: Crystal clear amber orange brew with an earthy & smokey flavor profile that is a bit complex
text:
I love all things tea and have many different teas. This tea is a raw Pu-erh tea (they spelled it Puerh on the bag).

Based on my Internet research, the healthline site says "Raw pu-erh is made from non-fermented green tea leaves that were picked, quickly roasted, sun-dried and then steamed to be compressed into round disks called cakes. After which the cakes are aged until the tea's taste is properly transformed." The Mansatea site says, "Raw pu-erh can be aged upwards of 50 years or more. Younger raw pu-erh is aged anywhere from a few months to a few years, whereas aged pu-erh is generally aged anywhere from a decade to upwards of 50 years or more. Unlike raw pu-erh, ripe pu-erh goes through an accelerated aging process."

I brewed one tea bag at 212 degrees Fahrenheit for 3 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The amber orange brew was crystal clear and the aroma and flavor was definitely a slightly smoky, yet earthy pu-erh with a complex flavor profile. This tastes like I expected it too.

There are 33 triangular tea bags in this puch and this comes from Lincang, Yunna, China. The current price of $14.99 comes to almost $.46 cents per tea bag.

title: 60 servings over 10 8.2 ounce boxes; delicious but more of a snack than a healthy breakfast
text:
These types of cereals are more of a snack food than a healthy breakfast. There are 10 8.2 ounce boxes in this order. Each box contains 6 servings of 150 calories each or 210 calories with the addition of 3/4 cup of milk (I assume that is whole milk).

I personally consider this a snack food even though it is fortified with vitamins, especially since the first ingredient is corn meal and the second is sugar. Nevertheless, these taste very good and my rating is based on that.

The ingredients are degerminated yellow corn meal, sugar, whole grain oat flour, modified food starch, contains 2% or less of hydrogenated vegetable oil (coconut, soybean and/or cottonseed), salt, maltodextrin, wheat flour, natural flavor, red 40, yellow 5, blue 1, yellow 6, BHT for freshness.

The vitamins and minerals are vitamin C (ascorbic acid), reduced iron, niacinamide, vitamin B6 (pyridoxine hydrochloride), vitamin B2 (riboflavin), vitamin Bl (thiamin hydrochloride), folic acid, vitamin D3, vitamin 812.

At the current price of $55.17, this comes to $5.52 per box which is competitively priced.

UPDATE: I gave two boxes to my neighbor and he loves this. He almost ate an entire box in one night and rates this 10 out of 10 stars.

UPDATE Aug 18, 2022 - I finally got feedback from my Post Lady and my boyfriend. They both ate a box of this and wanted more. The general consensus is that this is absolutely delicious! Everyone but me rates this 5 stars or more!

title: Typical gunpowder green tea from China; brew is a slightly cloudy golden amber
text:
This is four ounces of loose leaf gunpowder tea in this resealable bag. Upon opening the pouch I was hit with the typical green tea aroma of Gunpowder tea. I brewed one teaspoon to about 8-10 ounces of 185 degree Fahrenheit water for 4 minutes. The instructions say to brew two teaspoons from 1-3 minutes in 175 degree water. I prefer less tea during my brew.

This has a strong flavor that is slightly bitter even though I used warm water. The bitterness would be less pronounced if I brewed with the 175 degree water. The brew is a slightly cloudy golden amber. I like this tea but do not love it.

The current price of $8.99 comes to $2.25 per ounce. Note that this just contains organic green tea from the Zhejiang Province of China.

title: Almost bland green tea; brews to faint cloudy & pale amber
text:
This tea comes in round tea bags inside a resealable pouch that is inside a box. It has barely any aroma. I brewed one tea bag at 185 degrees for 4 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a faint cloudy and pale amber and the flavor was almost bland.

This is a product of India. The ingredients are 100% pure green tea.

In summary, I am not impressed with this tea and it is not one I would order again. At $14.99 for 100 tea bags, it comes to a reasonable $.15 cents per cup. This tea is neither bad nor is it great. It reminds me of Lipton.

title: Good in soup; interesting texture; requires at least 8 hours of soaking before use
text:
I like vermicelli in soups instead of noodles and also glass noodles made of sweet potato starch like this one is made of. However, these noodles are super wide and thick and require at least 8 hours of soaking in lukewarm water before being cooked for 10 minutes.

I used these to make soup. They are chewy and slippery like they are supposed to be with a texture that is much more pronounced than a thinner noodle made of sweet potato starch. These will be tasteless unless someone knows how to prepare them. Since I do not like Asian spices, I use them for texture and not taste and they are perfect. I love the chewy bite. However, I would not order these again simply because I do not care to soak my noodles for 8 hours before use. Otherwise, I really like these.

There are about 4 servings per package and a serving is 55 grams and the package is 300 grams. Each serving is 190 calories. The ingredients of the noodles are sweet potato starch and potato starch.

I suspect I can get these cheaper than $9.99 per package at my local Asian store as I consider that price expensive.

PS I attached photos of them dry, with water soaking, what they look like after being soaked but before cooking, and in the prepared soup.

title: Reminds me of Annie’s Mac and Cheese; tastes good but very pricey
text:
Kraft Deluxe is my favorite boxed macaroni and cheese and this is a close second. It reminds me of Annie's mac and cheese.

There are four 6 ounce boxes of tiny elbows with one package of powdered cheese per box. I just cooked the pasta for 9 minutes, drained it, threw it back in the pot, and then added 1/4 cup of milk,  1 tablespoon of butter (optional), and the cheese packet. In order to make this more healthy, I also added in some steamed broccoli and some parsley. I tried it both plain and with the addition and it was very good.

The calories as prepared come to 280 per serving and there are 2.5 servings per container. Note that the butter calories are not included since that is optional.

Overall, I like this. However, the current price of $24.29 comes to $6.07 per box which I consider outrageously expensive. I can purchase an equivalent amount of shells for about $2 and then add Velvita cheese and get a similar result. This is the only reason I rated this 3 stars. Otherwise, based on taste, this is 4 stars to me.

title: Delicious; tastes a little bit like lemon pepper; great with fish; salt 1st ingredient
text:
To me this rub is delicious. I love this spice on fish. However, the first ingredient is salt even though the name seems to indicate it is made of pepper and herbs.

The ingredients are salt, organic dehydrated garlic, organic cane sugar, organic dehydrated onion, organic black pepper, organic spices, organic paprika, organic rice concentrate, organic sunflower oil, citric acid, organic chili pepper, organic lemon oil, and organic paprika extract.

I really love this spice and the current price of $6.99 is competitively priced. Just realize the first ingredient is salt.

title: Delicious tiny jawbreakers just to eat or as cupcake topper; powdery finish like Smarties candies
text:
There are two pounds of psychedelic jawbreakers the size of the tip of a pencil eraser for the current price of $14.99. These jawbreakers are hard and have a subtle taste to start with a stronger powdery finish that reminds me of Smarties candies from yesteryear.

These are goods as party favors or just to eat in a bowl. I feel they can also be used as a topper for cupcakes but it would be important to let everyone know they are jawbreakers.

title: Not much black tea taste; only a hint of rose; brews to a dark amber
text:
If it were not for the hint of rose, this tea would be flavorless. I brewed one teaspoon to 8 ounces of boiling water for 4 minutes and the resulting brew was a dark amber. It has a hint of rose flavor but without that, there is no "what I call" black tea taste.

Overall, this is just an okay tea. The current price of $27.99 for 16 ounces comes to a reasonable $1.75 per ounce.

title: I use in place of vermicelli or pasta for texture in my soup
text:
Sometimes I want to use vermicelli, soba noodles, or sweet potato noodles like this in soups instead of pasta. They are chewy like they are supposed to be and have the same texture I am used to. I boiled them for 10 minutes and they were ready. They will be tasteless unless someone knows how to prepare them. Since I do not like Asian spices, I use them for texture and not taste and they are perfect. I love the chewy bite. Also, they pick up the spices I use in my dish.

There are 13 servings per package but the package is NOT resealable. However, I do not need that since I used both 10.58 ounce packages in what I was making. I would definitely order these again although I can get them cheaper than $10.95 per two-pack at my local Asian store.
