I can buy a 3.05 ounce package of Japanese noodles for $1.09 at my local Shop Rite, which means three packages would be the equivalent size and would cost me less than $7 for the amount in these two packages combined.

title: Flavorful & fresh cumin seeds; tastes great in my meatloaf
text:
These cumin seeds are flavorful and fresh. I add cumin seeds to my meatloaf and it makes it taste great and gives it a little more flavor.

There are six 0.95 ounce containers of seeds with a fully open hole dispenser from which to dispense the seeds. I would have preferred pencil holes to dispense them slowly. The current price of $8.99 comes to $1.50 per container.

title: Just beautiful but will only work with dense cakes
text:
This topper is just beautiful and it is very heavy for a cake topper. It weighs a total of 2.3 ounces. This will only stay on a dense cake like cheesecake, fruitcake, zucchini nut bread, etc. due to the weight. I got this for a friend who is turning 95 and I plan on making a cake for him but it cannot be light and airy due to the size and weight of this. It is 8" from tip to tip and 4.5" from side to side.

The current price of $14.99 is expected for this topper.

UPDATE Nov 4, 2022 - I finally got to use this for my friend's 95th birthday on Nov 3. I baked a cheesecake and used this as a topper. It looked fabulous on top--see photo.

title: Delicious candied jellies that have a hint of pistachio; messy with powdered sugar
text: These are exactly like candied jellies except they have powdered sugar on the outside. They are gummy-like but not so sticky that they pull out a filling. They are delicious and have a hint of pistachio flavor. The ingredients are sugar, corn starch, acidity regulator (citric acid-E-330) and pistachio. They are also messy due to all the powdered sugar. Be sure to open these over an area (such as a table) that can be cleaned easily. The current price of $13.89 is competitively priced.

title: Fresh w/good flavor & a bit of heat; ingredients are ground cayenne pepper; product of Turkey
text: This cayenne pepper is made of ground cayenne peppers. It smells and tastes fresh and has a nice flavor with a bit of heat. This is great in soups or on eggs or any other food item it pairs with. The current price of $24.99 comes to $0.45 cents per ounce since this is 3.5 pounds (56 ounces) or what I call cafeteria size. This is a product of Turkey.

title: Delicious candied jellies that taste like tangerine and orange
text: These are exactly like candied jellies except they have powdered sugar on the outside. They are gummy-like but not so sticky that they pull out a filling. They are delicious. However, at the current price of $19.35, they are also expensive.

title: Sweet and flavorful with a little bit of a kick
text:
This barbecue rub is delicious. It is sweet and very flavorful with just a little bit of kick. The ingredients are brown sugar, sea salt, spices (including chili pepper, black pepper, cinnamon, celery seed), garlic, onion, sugar, tomato, corn maltodextrin, silicon dioxide (to make free flowing), apple cider vinegar solids, extractives of paprika, natural flavor (including mesquite smoke) and disodium inosinate and guanylate (flavor enhancers).

This is 15 calories per two teaspoons with 380 mg of sodium and 2g of sugars.

I really love this rub and feel the current price of $4.44 is reasonable for this.

title: Delicious conversation candy hearts for pastries, cupcakes, or just to eat
text:
There are two pounds of conversation candy hearts for the current price of $12.99. These hearts smell great and they taste absolutely delicious. The writing on the hearts is not as perfect as I expected. It is on the light side, uncentered, and sometimes cryptic. Other than this, I love these. They are perfect for decorating cupcakes or dessert or just eating them out of the bag. These are the best candy hearts I ever tasted.

Some sayings are the front are:DM ME
SWEET
PEACE
SMILE
ROCK STAR
BAE (not sure what that means)

title: Very spicy kimchi; I had to drain the liquid to eat it
text:
I never had kimchi before but always wanted to try it. Well it is all cabbage and spice and very hot. I ate a can over some plain rice but I had to drain the liquid because it was just too hot for me. The cabbage has a nice crunch and if it were a little less spicy, I would have liked this.

There are three 4.23-ounce cans in this shipment. At the current price of $15, that comes to $5 per can of stir-fried kimchi. Each can is about 120 calories as there are about 6 - 20 calorie servings per can. The ingredients are napa cabbage, water, red chili pepper, garlic, salt, high fructose sugar, ginger, MSG, green onion oil, radish, and soybean oil. There is an allergy notice for corn and soybean on the can.

title: Nothing like typical basmati rice; not as fragrant; rice is long when cooked - 3.5 stars
text:
There are 10 pounds of basmati rice in this resealable package. When I opened the bag, I was not presented with that wonderful basmati aroma I am so used to. There was absolutely no "fragrance" other than that of the plastic bag it came in.

I made one cup in my rice cooker. I was hit with a nice but subtle fragrance after the cooking cycle completed but it did not have that taste. The "grains" came out long like fingers. I have never seen such long rice except for wild rice (which isn't rice at all but a grass). I ate this plain and with some kimchi. It was good and I liked the texture and the length. I also liked the mouthfeel of this. However, this is nothing like typical basmati rice.

When they say it is the world's longest basmati rice, they are not kidding. However, it seems some of that length was traded for the flavor. It tastes like super long Carolina white rice to me. I like it a lot but I was looking for the basmati flavor.

The current price of $21.99 ($23.99 minus $2) is expensive. I can get 25 pounds of highly fragrant (but less long) basmati rice for $26.99 at my local BJs.

title: Made of 100% brown rice; tastes so much better than it looks; extremely crunchy
text:
I never heard of Nurungji before but I have had rice cakes. These are like a thin wafer of a rice cake; it is all crunchy. There are about four or five flat "rice crackers" with lots of rice crumbs in each of the 15 packages. Each package is one 70-calorie serving that contains 0.5g (1%) fat, 16g (6%) carbas, 1g protein, and 1% potassium. The only ingredient is 100% brown rice.

I ate an entire package and even though they look horrible, it was delicious with peanut butter and jelly or just jelly alone. I absolutely love the crunch. This can be used with cream cheese and salmon or a spread of avocado. It can even be broken up over yogurt, ice cream, or another dessert to add a bit of crunch without taking away from the flavor of a dessert. Note that the cracker itself does not have much flavor at all but it is what it is paired with that elevates the snack.

title: Excellent elbows but I wish both the amount and elbow was larger
text:
There are 20 seven-ounce packages of very tiny elbows in this shipment. The elbows take about seven minutes to cook in boiling water. They are like any other standard pasta and are as flavorful as other pastas. These are perfect for soups or noodle salads. Unfortunately, the elbows themselves are about 3/4 the size as expected and also the package is only seven ounces. When I buy elbows in the store, I get a one-pound package. I had to use two packages to make my noodle salad recipe and even then I was short two ounces.

My only complaint is the shrinkage of the size in ounces and the pasta itself. Otherwise, this is great. The current price of $15.20 comes to $.76 cents per package or about $.11 cents per ounce.

title: These lavender flowers are fresh & fragrant; perfect 4 tea, potpourri, bath bombs, etc.
text:
These lavender buds (ingredients are dried lavender flowers) are fresh and fragrant. It comes in a 5 ounce resealable bag that is best before Nov 22, 2023 and it's current June 16, 2022, so that is almost 1.5 years for use.

I made the lavender tea (it's acutally a tisane) with one teaspoon of the buds in 8 ounces of boiling water and steeped for 10 minutes. The resulting brew was a pale purplish gray. The tea had a faint taste of lavender and it was very relaxing to drink.

Note that the package says these are edible lavender buds, which means they can be used in recipes such as lavender brownies and lavender ice cream, etc. They can also be used for other do-it-yourself (DIY) projects such as making bath salts, bath bombs, potpourri, fragrance sachets, etc. These are food-grade, caffeine free, 100% natural, and have no additives.

The current price of $10.99 is very reasonable for this bag of lavender flowers (a.k.a. buds).

title: Highly fragrant & flavorful but way overpriced; the tisane is not for those who take BP meds
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this set of two cans is made of both tea in one can and tisane in the other. 

The cleanse loose leaf tea contains green tea (the only actual tea), orange peel, cinnamon, ginger, senna, cacao nibs, fennel, and pomegranate arils, and natural flavor. The only ingredient not organic is the natural flavor.

The soothe loose leaf tisane contains peppermint, chamomile, lemon balm, fennel and licorice root. All these ingredients are organic.

I brewed the cleanse blend in a cup of 200 degree Fahrenheit water with a teaspoon of the tea for 4 minutes. The resulting brew was a clear yellow/orange and it tasted delicious. The flavors are fresh and balanced. 

Unfortunately, I really should not be consuming the other tea since I have high blood pressure. Licorice should be avoided for those with high BP. There are also some other side effects listed on the web. For those who do not have this worry, I am sure it is as flavorful as the cleanse blend as it smells just as fantastic.

The tins this tea / tisane comes in are airtight and very nice at preserving the blend inside. However, the current price of $47 is expensive. The soothe blend has 1.5 ounces of herbal ingredients and the cleanse blend has 3.5 ounces of tea mixed with herbal ingredients.

title: Coffee has a smooth, deep taste for being a dark roast with a hint of bitterness
text:
This is a 124 K-cup package of high caffeine dark roast coffee made from a blend of coffee from Brazil and India.

The resulting brew has a hint of bitterness and it is strong but still smooth as is to be expected of a dark roast. I personally prefer this with half and half whipped up with a whisk as can be seen in my last picture. This coffee with cream tastes very good and has depth and I usually do not like dark roasts. I am not crazy about it black.

The current price of $15 comes to almost $1.25 per cup of coffee which is a bit pricey. Overally, I think this is very good coffee that will appeal to those who like the flavor between dark and medium roast.

title: Quick on-to-go snacks; I find it impossible to eat one pack; they're delicious but pricey
text:
There are 12 one-ounce packages of little mini keebler cookies in this package. Each package has about 12-13 cookies. Each package is also one 140 calorie serving with 7g fat, 95 mg of sodium, and 19g of carbohydrates. There is also 1g of protein per package.

These minis are incredibly flavorful despite being so small. I actually ate two packages in one sitting--so these are dangerous snacks from that perspective.

At the current price of $10.34 ($13.79 minus 25%), this comes to $.86 cents per package.

UPDATE - I gave my neighbor two packages and he LOVED them. He rated this 5 stars.

title: Tastes like the tea given out at a Chinese restaurant; brew is a clear golden amber – 3.5 stars
text:
This tea is harvested in India.

There are four ounces of organic imperial green loose leaf tea in this resealable bag.  I brewed one teaspoon to 8 ounces of 185 degree Fahrenheit water for 4 minutes. This tea has a mild flavor typical of the type of the tea served in Chinese restaurants.The brew is a clear golden amber. I like this tea but do not love it.

The current price of $10.99 comes to $2.75 per ounce. Note that the leaf pictured through the bag on the Amazon site is not exactly what is received.The leaf is more chopped up than pictured on the Amazon site which is more of a half leaf. See my photo.

title: Very good black tea; brews to a beautiful amber
text:
This is Ceylon Black Supreme Pekoe Loose Leaf Tea. I brewed one teaspoon to 8 ounces of boiling water for 4 minutes and the resulting brew was a clear amber. It has a very smooth and balanced flavor.

Overall, this is very good tea. The current price of $10.99 for 3 ounces is on the high side of competitively priced for this.

title: Smooth flavored coffee w/a hint of spice and an orange/yellow tinge
text:
Per the instructions, I used two grams of coffee to 7 ounces of boiling water. Note that two grams came out to be one level teaspoon. The resulting brew had that orange/yellow tinge of turmeric and it even was visible after I added half and half.

I do not care for this black but with cream it tastes good. There is just a hint of spice. Overall, I like this. The current price of $14.99 comes to about $2.12 per ounce. There are about 100 servings per bag.

title: 15 are flavored salts; only 5 with no salt; perfect for gifting
text:
There are 20 small jars of "spices" in this package. I did assume, however, that these were spice blends. Practically all of these have the first ingredient as salt. In total there are 15 jars that contain salt and 5 that do not have any salt.

I tried the Italian spice rub and it was absolutely delicious! In my opinion, this is a five-star product for the current price of $34.99, which comes to $1.75 per bottle. The only reason I rated this 3 stars is because there is nothing on the Amazon site that says there are mostly flavored salts. If someone is watching their salt intake, they will be disappointed to find that this is mostly made of flavored salts.

I've listed the "spices" below. Note that salt is the first ingredient in all of them except where I noted otherwise.

- mexican spice rub
- memphis spice rub (contains NO salt)
- cajun spice rub
- southwest spice rub
- caribbean (salt is the 2nd ingredient)
- chili onion spice rub (contains NO salt)
- chili rosemary spice rub (contains NO salt)
- chili parsley spice rub (contains NO salt)
- chili garlic spice rub (contains NO salt)
- garlic salt
- Italian spice rub
- curry salt (salt is the 3rd ingredient)
- thyme salt
- jalapeno spice rub
- chili salt
- jamaican jerk spice rub
- black cajun spice rub
- montreal spice rub
- chipotle lime spice rub
- rosemary & herb salt

title: Tastes like ordinary black tea; I do not taste the bergamot
text:
There is one box of 100 round earl grey tea bags in this package. Earl grey is one of my favorite teas. However, the fragrance of this earl grey smells more like St. Joseph's baby aspirin than the bergamot aroma I am used to. 

I brewed the tea with boiling water and let it steep for four minutes. It brewed to a deep auburn brown hue of earl grey black tea. The taste of the bergamot is non-existent and tastes almost like regular black tea that is not much better than a Lipton tea bag.

The current price of $14.99 for 100 bags comes to a very reasonable $.15 cents per cup but it is not one I would order again.This comes from India.

title: These seeds have hardly any flavor and what there is I do not care for
text:
These are Ethiopian Mustard Seeds. They are usually used in bean type salads. I tried this but they are almost flavorless to me. I also do not care for the flavor that comes through. This is very different from the flavor of the yellow mustard powder I like that is made from tumeric and white mustard seed.

They are imported from Modjo, Ethiopia and distributed from Oakland, California. It contains a single origin seed.

The current $10.95 price for 2 ounces is on the high side of competitively priced.

title: Delicious, fragrant, & fresh Ethiopian Ajwain seeds; similar to caraway but tastes like thyme
text:
I believe these are Ajwain seeds and NOT caraway seeds based on my photo research and taste tests. These type seeds belong to the same family as caraway but taste more like thyme than the licorice flavor of caraway.

I at first thought these were caraway seeds as advertised; however, after tasting them, they do not have that typical flavor. These are perfect to use in soups and stews, on potatoes, meats, breads, etc. These are fragrant and fresh and taste delicious. They are imported from Modjo, Ethiopia and distributed from Oakland, California. It contains a single origin seed.

This has a 5-star taste to me. However, I rated this 3 stars for two reasons. First, the jar label is currently different from the one pictured on the Amazon site (compare my photo with theirs). The jar label does not say wild caraway seed like their photo and description does. Second, this is NOT caraway at all. The label does not correctly identify the seeds inside, which I believe are Ajwain seeds. Of course, they could be some other seed but it does have a slight flavor to thyme.

title: Highly fragrant; great for recipes, ham, in a potpourri mix, or as a tea
text:
These cloves are excellent and highly fragrant. They are great in recipes, ground, for use when baking ham, in potpourri, or as a tea. Note these are made in India and are organic.

I used this to make "tea." I ground a teaspoonful in a mortar and pestle and then boiled it in a cup of water for 10 minutes. I then strained the sediment and drank the liquid. It was very flavorful.

The serving size is 6 grams at 20 calories per serving. There are 38 servings in this 1-pound package. The current price of $19.99 is competitively priced in my opinion. This is a product of India and the expiration is 2.5 years from now (December 2024).

title: The size of a 10-pound bag of cat food; onion flakes are fresh and flavorful
text: I must say when I received this, I thought it was a 10-pound bag of cat food. This 2-pound bag of onion flakes is huge. The only ingredient is organic onion flakes. They look like dehydrated onions to me. However, the flakes are flavorful without being too potent and they are perfect to add to hamburger meat, meatballs, soups, etc. As long as one can use this much or has someone to share it with, this is perfect. The serving size is 7  grams at 25 calories per serving. There are 129 servings in this 2-pound package. The current price of $14.99 is reasonable in my opinion. This is a product of India and the expiration is 2.5 years from now (December 2024).

title: Pasta has a very chewy texture and flavor is subtle
text:
There are three 10-ounce bags of pasta in this set. The three flavors are egg, lemon pepper, and roasted garlic fettuccine. I made the roasted garlic one and tasted the pasta after three minutes of boiling. It was very, very chewy and pretty much flavorless. It just tasted like plain pasta. I boiled it in total for almost five minutes but that did not change the chewy texture much. I just find it odd that it is so chewy.

Nevertheless, this still tastes like pasta but plain pasta and not with flavor.

The current price of $20.89 ($21.99 minus 5%) comes to $6.96 per 10-ounce bag. This pasta is okay but not something I would order again due to the chewy texture. Note that the ingredients in the roasted garlic pasta is durum wheat semolina, wheat gluten, digestive resistant corn starch, egg, granulated garlic, and roasted garlic powder.

title: Smells fragrant but does not taste as good as my West Indies nutmeg
text:
The first time I ever tried whole nutmeg was when I was given a gift of nutmeg from the West Indies. This nutmeg smells the same but it does not have the intense flavor profile of my other nutmeg.

I use a grater to grind it down. Nutmeg is great in desserts (e.g. rice pudding) or coffee or any drinks or dishes that call for it. What I love about whole nutmeg is that it stays fresh for longer because it is ground when needed.

This whole nutmeg from India is just okay because its flavor is super subtle.

The current price of $9.71 ($10.79 minus 10%) is very reasonable for this.

title: Coffee is not worth the high price for the lousy design but the taste is good – 2.5 stars
text:
I tried a lot of Fabula coffee lately and both my boyfriend and I think their coffee is very good.

This is a 24 K-cup package of coffee in a HUGE box each individually packaged in its own vacuum sealed foil wrap. The foil wrap should not be opened until one is ready to brew the coffee.

The coffee brews exactly like any other K-cup and the resulting brew is a bit bitter and strong as is to be expected of a dark roast. I personally prefer this with half and half whipped up with a whisk as can be seen in my last picture. This coffee with cream tastes very good and smooth. I am not crazy about it black.

NEGATIVES

- This is outrageously over packaged

- K-cups cannot fit in any K-cup carousel

- This is way too expensive

The current price of $44.99 comes to almost $1.88 per cup of coffee. This is way too expensive and the packaging alone would cause me not to buy this. They may be proud that the K-cups are not plastic but they are wasting other natural resources including the need for a large box, which results in more gas being used to deliver this, more storage required for the messy size, etc., etc.

title: Extremely strong bergamot flavor and taste; brews to a lovely auburn
text:
This is a 3.5 ounce package of earl grey tea. Earl grey is one of my favorite teas and this one really delivers. The fragrance is intense as is the flavor. This comes from Sri Lanka. Lately, I have been very disappointed with several Earl Grey teas from Sri Lanka but this is absolutely wonderful.

I brewed one teaspoon of this tea with boiling water and let it steep for four minutes. It brewed to a clear and intense auburn hue. The taste of the bergamot is strong and delicious.

The current price of $9.99 is reasonable for this package. I think this tea is fabulous and am pleasantly surprised because I was not expecting it to be this good.

UPDATE - I love this tea so much that I ordered another package. The reason that is so unusual is that I easily have enough tea in my house to last for years--over 30 pounds worth, so I don't need more tea. This tea is so incredible because of its strong bergamot flavor. In fact, it makes an absolutely flavorful iced tea. Another reviewer who rated this 3 stars said this is like drinking perfume due to the strong flavor. Well it is what makes this tea so wonderful to me.

title: Has an intense rosemary flavor; made of ground up twigs and leaves
text:
The current price of this 3.5 ounce bag of rosemary is $11.99 ($12.99 minus $1), which is competitively priced. However, what I do not like is that this is a pulverized version of rosemary leaves mixed in with chopped up twigs. I prefer my rosemary to be an unchopped leaf (needle).

Rosemary like this is perfect for rosemary popovers or rosemary bread. This has an intense flavor. However, the fact that it is ground down is a disappointment for my needs and I would have preferred the twigs be removed and not ground with it.

title: Fresh tasting organic rosemary leaves w/a subtle flavor but it must be picked thru to remove debris
text:
This is a tremendous amount of rosemary--a full one pound. Also, the current price of $14.99 is reasonable for that amount. However, what I do not like is that there is a lot of foreign debris in this rosemary and it has to be picked through before use. It tastes fresh and has a subtle rosemary flavor but I personally do not like picking through my rosemary to remove bugs, pebbles, tomato seeds, etc.

Rosemary like this is perfect for rosemary popovers or rosemary bread. If this were cleaner, I would have no qualms recommending this. However, this is not for me due to the debris.

title: My boyfriend likes these; I just think they are okay
text:
There are three .07 ounce bags in this shipment for the current price of $14.99, which comes to $5 per bag. Each pouch is one 75-calorie serving with 2g of fat, 15g of carbohydrate, 2 g fiber, 8 g sugars, and 2 g protein.

These are light and airy and taste slightly bitter to me with not much flavor. However, my boyfriend really likes these. Neither one of us have ever tried boysenberries before, so we have nothing to compare this to. We ate these plain. These are good to use in oatmeal during the wintertime when good fruits are less available.

These are New Zealand Boysenberries. These expire within about three months of receipt. The expiration on my bags say September 10, 2022.

title: Tastes mostly like coffee with a little pepper thrown in and not much else
text:
When I ordered this, I saw that this was seasoning only. I did not see that this was a coffee-based product. This tastes mostly like coffee with a little bit of pepper. It is not my favorite spice blend for steaks but if coffee flavor is what is wanted, then this delivers. The ingredients are brown sugar, salt, roasted coffee beans, spices (not sure what kind), chili pepper, and silicon dioxide (anti-caking agent).

The current price of $7.99 for this 5 ounce jar is competitively priced if one likes this type of flavoring that is predominantly coffee based.

title: Delicious bacon but way too sweet; more like bacon candy; one serving only
text:
I would not call this thick cut nor would I call this bacon jerky. It is more like bacon candy. It is delicious but my boyfriend and I ate this and found it way too sweet.

This one two-ounce bag is one 180 calorie serving and it includes 44% of added sugars (22g) with 16% (8g) of protein.

The current price of $18.97 ($19.97 minus $1) is super expensive for one serving. The exorbitant price and cloying sweetness has us passing on this.

title: Haul appears to be hit or miss; I got 73 pieces of candy
text:
Based on what the other reviewers wrote, what one gets seems to differ by who gets it. Therefore, the haul is hit or miss insofar as the candy one gets.

I got 73 pieces in total as follows:

1 Tootsie Pops
12 Tootsie Fruit Chews
26 Tootsie Rolls in various sizes
8 Hard Candy (4 lifesavers of various flavors and 4 peppermint)
5 Blow Pops
1 Sour Apple Pop
4 Welch's Fruit Snacks
7 Laffy Taffy
4 DOTS
3 Mike and Ike Candies
1 CherryHead
1 Jaw Busters

Although this candy tastes good (I did try a few pieces), I feel the value is not there. The current price of $19.99 for this type of candy is expensive. My recommendation is to buy individual packages of what is liked and make your own box. The candy is fine but what is received for the price is not the value I expected. If one does not mind overpaying, then this offers a decent variety of miscellaneous pure sugar sweets.

title: Not my favorite ceremonial grade matcha; the powder is bitter not sweet & there are clumps
text:
My goto matcha is [[ASIN:B00ZYAVO6K PANATEA ceremonial grade from Japan]], which is no longer available on Amazon. This is also from Japan and I wanted to see how it compares.

It is important that matcha be made with quality leaves from a trusted source in Japan that has their leaves regularly tested for lead and other contaminants. Selectively sourced matcha not only tastes good but is supposed to be healthy too with lots of antioxidants and other components. A good ceremonial grade matcha should be tested, taste sweet, and froth when whisked.

This matcha is average. I used one heaping matcha scoop's (i.e. chashaku) worth with 3 ounces of 180 degree water and then whisked it to a froth. This “electric green tea powder” makes a slightly frothy, vegetable, and slightly bitter brew. It does not dissolve as good as expected and I had some hard clumps in the brew (see two photos).

Unfortunately, I cannot find, either on the package or on the Amazon site, whether this matcha has been tested for contaminants. This is average ceremonial matcha and the current price of $9.99 for 1.06 ounces is cheaper than expected, which is why I am not surprised this is not the greatest.

For those who consume matcha regularly, I recommend getting the following items or the equivalent.
- [[ASIN:B07JDFK1LT chawan (match bowl)]]
- [[ASIN:B07J6PLBXJ chasen (matcha whisk) with chashaku (matcha scoop) and matcha spoon]]
- [[ASIN:B00OEKNMPC chasen naoshi (matcha whisk keeper / stand)]]

Instead of a matcha whisk, I sometimes use an electric whisk like the one that comes with this [[ASIN:B09DYYGXQG matcha kit]].

title: Not as flavorful as mayo to me; tastes sweet; expires quickly
text:
This is supposedly a substitute for mayonnaise. I tried this on my BLT and do not find it as flavorful as mayo. I also ate some plain off my finger and still found it blander than mayo and more sweet.

This is 40 calories per tablespoon. The ingredients are water, vinegar, sugar, olive oil, canola oil, soybean oil, modified food starch, egg yolks, salt, less than 2% mustard flour, paprika, spice, natural flavor, dried garlic, potassium sorbate, and calcium disodium EDTA (to protect flavor).

The current price of $4.48 for this 30 ounce jar is reasonable but what is not reasonable is that this expires within three months. Note that this requires refrigeration after opening just like mayo does.

title: This candy dissolves in my mouth with a nice fizz; can't tell the flavor but it's good
text:
I heard of pop candy but never tried it. Apparently, hot candy syrup is mixed with carbon dioxide gas under high pressure so that after the candy is shattered and turned into what looks like a powder, tiny bubbles of CO2 that make it pop in one's mouth.

There are 26 packages of strawberry-flavored candy in this tub. Each package is one serving of 40 calories each that is pure sugar. I ate a whole package and love the fizz and "pop"  in my mouth. The candy does not have a strong strawberry flavor to me. It just tasted like good candy but if someone were to blindfold me, I would not be able to describe the flavor.

The current price of $12.99 brings this to $.50 cents per package.

title: My boyfriend barely tastes any flavor but the flavor comes through for me; a bit too sweet
text:
I ordered this for my boyfriend because he likes energy drinks. The first thing he said is that he liked the design of the can. However, when he drank this, he said it barely had any flavor. Of course I had to taste it and I think it tastes exactly like pina colada. The flavor to me is good but the drink is way too sweet tasting to me even though it is zero calories. Each 11.2 ounce can contains 100% DV of vitamin C, Niacin, Pantothenic Acid, Vitamin B6, Folate, Biotin, Vitamin B12 and 25% DV of Zinc.

The ingredients are filtered sparkling water, malic acid, guarana extract, acidity regulator [trisodium citrate], natural flavors [pineapple, coconut], vitamin C, sweetener [sucralose L-theanine, niacin, vitamin B5, zinc citrate, vitamin B6, vitamin B12, folic acid, and biotin.

Apparently the guarana is the caffeine but it doesn't say there is any caffeine in the ingredients list.

At the current price of $19.09 this comes to $1.59 per 11.2 ounce can since there are 12 cans in this package.

title: An absolutely delicious mushroom blend ruined by way too much sodium and spice
text:
There are six 10 ounce pouches in this shipment. Each pouch is 2.5 servings at 60 calories per serving or 150 calories per package. However, per serving the sodium is 480mg and per package it is 1230mg--which is outrageous.

This can be microwaved or cooked on the stove. I actually steamed mine for 17 minutes because I cook with steam. This is absolutely delicious but after my first spoonful, my lips were burning from the salt. I actually drained all the liquid and blended it with rice. It was still way too salty and spicy for me and it tastes like the first ingredients are all salt and spice.

 The ingredients are mushrooms, roasted tomatoes, chipotle in adobo (chipotle peppers, water, tomato paste, vinegar, brown sugar, safflower oil, sea salt, tapioca starch, garlic powder, ground bay leaves, onion powder, ground thyme, ground cumin, ground black pepper), dehydrated onion flakes, water, sea salt, non-GMS vegetable oil (safflower and/or sunflower Oil), brown sugar, garlic powder, ground bay leaves, and yeast extract.

I would never buy this because the sodium ruins this dish entirely. I also do not feel this is mild even though it says it is mild on the package.

The current price of $26.95 comes to $4.49 per pouch, which I consider competitively priced. This is certainly a delicious side that is over spiced and over salted.

title: Saffron smells great and tastes fresh and flavorful
text:
I could smell this immediately as I opened the package. I used this in my rice. First, I soaked about a teaspoon of Saffron in boiling water for 10 minutes and then I added it to the rice before cooking. The rice came out great and the taste of the saffron was flavorful as it should be.

I do wish this came in a dark colored container to protect the Saffron from light.

The current price of $14.24 ($14.99 minus 5%) for 2 grams is competitively priced.

title: Nice herbal tisane w good flavor; brews to a cloudy peach color but not for those who take BP meds
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this set of three boxes of tisanes is good. Each box has 20 tea bags with herbal ingredients (organic chamomile flower, organic spearmint leaf, organic licorice root, organic lavender flower, organic ashwagandha root, and organic hemp seed oil granules.)

The flavor of this tisane is fresh and tastes good and balanced. I brewed one tea bag in a cup of boiling water for 5 minutes. The resulting brew was a cloudy peach.

Unfortunately, after drinking one cup I discovered that I should not be consuming this tea since I have high blood pressure. Licorice root and ashwagandha should be avoided for those with high BP. There are also some other side effects listed on the web. For those who do not have this worry, this is a flavorful tea.

title: Absolutely tasty; like Cheez-It Trail Mix but no cracker; high calorie
text:
I tried the [[ASIN:B09L1N28M3 Kaze cheese bites]] and they are incredibly tasty as is this new concoction. This is also the new way to get FAT!

These are cheddar cheese with a crunch (like Cheez-It crackers but without the cracker) with almonds added.

The ingredients are dry roasted salted almonds (almonds, salt), cheddar cheese (milk, bacterial culture, salt, calcium chloride, vegetable color (annatto), microbial enzymes), and cellulose.

The serving size is one ounce, which fits in the palm of my hand. Each serving is 180 calories with 180 mg of sodium, 15 mg of cholesterol, and 8 g of protein. Since there are three 7-ounce packages in this shipment, that means there are 7 servings in the bag. Note that it is hard to stop eating just one serving and easy to eat the entire 7-ounce bag in the blink of an eye.

The current price of $29.95 brings this to almost $10 per bag (specifically $9.98 per bag). If one has control, then these are a great snack compared to other snack foods on the market. However, this provides a good dose of salt and a tremendous amount of calories for that high price. It also reduces the cash flow in your wallet because these are costly. Since they taste so good, I did not mark them down for those issues. On taste alone, these are 10 stars.

title: Absolutely tasty; like Cheez-It Trail Mix but no cracker; high calorie
text:
I tried the [[ASIN:B09L1N28M3 Kaze cheese bites]] and they are incredibly tasty as is this new concoction. This is also the new way to get FAT!

These are cheddar cheese with a crunch (like Cheez-It crackers but without the cracker) with almonds added.

The ingredients are dry roasted salted almonds (almonds, salt), cheddar cheese (milk, bacterial culture, salt, calcium chloride, vegetable color (annatto), microbial enzymes), and cellulose.

The serving size is one ounce, which fits in the palm of my hand. Each serving is 180 calories with 180 mg of sodium, 15 mg of cholesterol, and 8 g of protein. Since this is a 7-ounce package, that means there are 7 servings in the bag. Note that it is hard to stop eating just one serving and easy to eat the entire 7-ounce bag in the blink of an eye.

The current price of $12.95 is pricey. If one has control, then these are a great snack compared to other snack foods on the market. However, this provides a good dose of salt and a tremendous amount of calories. This also reduces the cash flow in your wallet. Since they taste so good, I did not mark them down for those issues. On taste alone, these are 10 stars. Note that on the package I received, I had to use a scissor to cut the bag open as it was sealed too low when I ripped off the top tab.

title: Absolutely incredible tasting; like Cheez-It Trail Mix but no cracker; high calorie
text:
I tried the [[ASIN:B09L1N28M3 Kaze cheese bites]] and they are incredibly tasty as is this new concoction. This is also the new way to get FAT!

These are cheddar cheese with a crunch (like Cheez-It crackers but without the cracker) with cashews and pecans added.

The ingredients are cheddar cheese (milk, bacterial culture, salt, calcium chloride, vegetable color (annatto), microbial enzymes), cellulose, oil roasted salted pecans (pecan halves, canola oil, salt), oil roasted salted cashews (cashews, canola oil, salt).

A serving size is one ounce, which is almost less than what fits in the palm of my hand. Each serving is 190 calories with 160 mg of sodium, 15 mg of cholesterol, and 6 g of protein. There is no current price on this but I suspect they will price this within the range of $10 to $11 per bag since their three-pack is $29.95. If one has control, then these are a great snack compared to other snack foods on the market. However, this provides a good dose of salt and a tremendous amount of calories. Since I know this is going to be expensive, this also reduces the cash flow in your wallet. Since they taste so good, I did not mark them down for those issues. On taste alone, these are 10 stars.

title: These tisanes interfere with certain medications; not everyone can safely use this detox package
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. I did not realize this set was full of tisanes and ingredients that will interfere with medications I am taking (like blood pressure meds). Therefore, I cannot use this at all. In fact, the day tea is the only box that has two ingredients that contain actual tea.

It does come in a presentable box. There are six items enclosed. The morning kick-start is a .42 ounce  powdered green tea supplement with brown rice syrup. It even says to consult a physician before using. The night tea contains 14 tea bags with lotus leaves, peppermint, fenugreek, licorice, burdock root, and hawthorn fruit. The day tea contains 14 tea bags with Chinese oolong (a true tea), ginseng, Pu-erh tea (also a true tea), ginger, dandelion, lemongrass, and fennel. The daily wellbeing tea contains butterfly pea flower.

Had I known this actually was not tea and full of unusual herbal ingredients, I would not have ordered this. When they say tea, I think they mean tea and not herbal ingredients - my mistake.

This is certainly a beautiful set and those who are not allergic to the above ingredients or who are not taking certain meds (like blood pressure meds) can probably drink this safely. However, I cannot use anything from the set other than the tea bag squeezer.

The current price of $19.95 ($29.95 minus $10) is competitively priced for this set. It is certainly giftable.

title: Tastes and cooks exactly as mung beans should; nice large size
text:
There are four pounds of mung beans in this package. They are clean and taste fresh and cook well. I steamed them in one part mung beans to four parts water--meaning 1/4 cup of beans to 1 cup of water for about 1hour 30 minutes. They came out as they should (see photo). They have a starchy taste and are wholesome. I add them to my salads.

The only negative about this product is that there are no cooking instructions or recipe on the bag. I consider that a mistake.

At the current price of $14.99 these are much cheaper than the mung beans from Bob's Red Mill which sells for $10.29 for almost two pounds. These mung beans come to $3.75 per pound which is a good deal.

title: This oatmeal is delicious and very hearty; it’s fortified with adaptogens if that’s your thing
text:
I will address the package labeling below but first the basics.

There are five servings of individually packaged cups of oatmeal in this shipment for the current cost of $14.99, which comes to almost $3 per serving. This is incredibly simple to make. I just poured boiling water into the cup enough to cover the oatmeal (that was around the halfway mark). I stirred it and after letting it steep for four minutes, I had a very delicious and hearty cup of oatmeal.

Each serving is 230 calories with 45 mg of sodium, 6g of dietary fiber, and 9g of added sugars (10g of sugars overall). It also contains calcium, iron, and potassium.

The ingredients are organic rolled oats, organic flax seed, organic maple sugar, brown sugar, organic cane sugar, organic cordyceps, maca root powder, natural flavor and sea salt. Since the flaxseed is whole, it must be chewed to get the benefit, otherwise it just passes through the digestive system. For those who have certain allergies, note that it is made in a facility that processes wheat, dairy, and nuts.

When I ordered this I thought I was getting just oatmeal and did not realize it contained adaptogens in the ingredients list. That was my mistake for not reading the description correctly. The adaptogens in this oatmeal are cordyceps and maca. I have never eaten either and since I am allergic to a lot of over the counter supplements, I would not have ordered this had I known. Nevertheless, I had no ill effect from eating this oatmeal and will continue to eat all the servings.

Cordyceps is actually a fungus grown on a caterpillar. I believe on a dead caterpillar but am not sure. I actually called adaptofoods and spoke to Jared and he said that they source this in the US from Aloha Medicinals. With regard to maca, that is a root plant that is usually grown in northern Peru or Bolivia. Both ingredients are adaptogens.

According to WebMD, "The theory behind adaptogens says they help your body adjust to physical, chemical, or biological stress. They're thought to stimulate your body's stress-protection response and help its systems return to a balanced state called "homeostasis." As promising as that may sound, we need more research on adaptogens and their possible health benefits."

As for cordyceps WebMD says, "Cordyceps might improve immunity by stimulating cells and specific chemicals in the immune system. It might also help fight cancer cells and shrink tumor size, particularly with lung or skin cancers." ... "People most commonly use cordyceps for athletic performance, kidney disorders, liver problems, and sexual problems, but there is no good scientific evidence to support these uses."

With regard to maca, Medical News Today says, "Maca is a common ingredient in Peruvian cooking that gives dishes an earthy flavor. Maca root plant can be ground up into a powder and added to meals or smoothies. Aside from its culinary uses, maca may also have several health benefits." The health benefits it mentions are:

- Increasing libido
- Reducing erectile dysfunction
- Boosting energy and endurance
- Increasing fertility
- Improving mood
- Reducing blood pressure
- Reducing sun damage
- Fighting free radicals
- Reducing menopause symptoms
- Improving learning and memory

I personally cannot attest to any health claims expected from these two adaptogen ingredients. It's possible that cordyceps can cause mild side effects of "diarrhea, constipation, and stomach discomfort." However, I would think one would have to take it in its supplement form to have that reaction. I did not have any ill effects from eating this.

This product does not make any claims. In fact, there is a warning that says "This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease." It also contains California's proposition 65 warning that says, "This product can expose you to chemicals, including lead, which are known to the State of California to cause cancer and birth defects or other reproductive harm." Since they sell this in California, they have to have that warning if they do not want to get sued like some other great companies with wholesome products (e.g. Bob's Red Mill).

At first I was horrified to see the warning because I have never noticed it on the food products I eat. But these trace minerals are in many of our foods like oats, root vegetables, etc. Although this warning is alarming, it is really there to just satisfy the law and not to get sued. I feel it unnecessarily instills fear when most likely there should not be any fear. Therefore, I am not worrying about it.

In summary, I love this oatmeal for an occassional snack when I do not want to steam my buckwheat groats or steel cut oats. It tastes wholesome and is flavorful. The price per serving is high but competitive. If one is looking for a breakfast food with adatogens, then this is one to consider.

title: Sweet vegetable fragrance/taste; brews to cloudy yellow/green
text:
This tea comes in opaque pyramid tea bags so it is hard to see the contents. It has a sweet vegetable aroma. I brewed one tea bag at 175 degrees for 2 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The resulting brew was cloudy yellow/green and the flavor was all vegetal and slightly sweet. I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this. I also love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

Note that the only English words or instructions on the package are a label that is slapped on in the back. The ingredients are green tea--specifically high grade crude green tea. Note that gokuzyo means highest grade and aracha means crude or rough tea, so this is gokuzyo aracha.

In summary, I really like this tea. At $39 for 100 tea bags, it comes to a reasonable $.39 cents per cup.

title: Can I have some food with my salt; only noodles, flavorings, salt, & microscope rubbery pieces
text:
There are six cups of seafood flavored noodle soup in this package. The cups are large because they include the vermicelli, the square block of dried flakes with the rubbery pieces, and the powdered soup base.

All that has to be done is to combine the ingredients and pour boiling water up to the line in the cup, then cover, and wait four minutes. I stirred after that. The result was a cup of very, very salty noodles with a tiny bit of microscopic rubbery pieces. Obviously, the end product is nothing like the picture on the cup.

Each cup is one serving of 310 calories, with 820 mg of sodium, and 6 grams of protein. I actually had to pour the soup in a strainer, run water over it, and then eat what was left because I found this so incredibly salty. Please do understand that I eat absolutely no salt normally other than what occurs naturally in the foods or what is added by a restaurant. Therefore, I am more sensitive to a salty flavor than others may be. However, this is not for me.

The current price of $22.99 ($27.99 minus $5) comes to $3.83 per serving.

title: Flavorful spice but subtle unless I use a lot; overall a balanced flavor
text:
I used this all over my veggies but the flavor was subtle. I have to use more than a shake to taste this. I thought this was supposed to be spicey but it is not at all spicey. I like it as the flavor is balanced but not strong. Umami is the correct word as it is more on the savory side.

The ingredients are spices, salt, sichuan pepper, onion, yeast extract, citric acid, kelp, tamari (water, organic soybeans, salt, organic alcohol), mushroom, and seaweed.

The current price of $14.99 is slightly pricey for this 3.4 ounce jar.

title: Decent but small garbanzo (chickpeas) beans but very pricey
text: These chickpeas were clean and I did not find any debris in them as others have mentioned. They are a bit smaller than usual. Nevertheless, I made hummus with them and it came out well. However, the current price of $8.54 for one pound is very pricey for this. These garbanzo beans are no better than the one pound package that I can buy at my local grocery store for $2.99.

title: Excellent Yerba mate; wish this large bag was resealable & came with directions
text:
When I traveled to Argentina the first time in 1995, I was introduced to mate. There was something about the ritual of drinking this "tea" as it is such a pure pastime but looks so illicit. The mate cup (Yerba mate gourd) is artistic and small and everyone sits around in a group passing the mate cup to drink the tea. I loved the camaraderie of the experience. The gourd pictured is the one I purchased back in 1995 in Argentina.

This is a large bag of mate and it is excellent. It tastes exactly like I remember it. Mate is high in caffeine and tastes slightly bitter because the cup is almost filled to the brim with mate. Then tepid water (158 to 176 degrees Fahrenheit) is poured over this and the straw acts as a sieve through which the tea passes but the grinds do not. The tea can be reused several times in the same day until the flavor is gone. I had a co-worker from Argentina tell me that he adds a small rind of lemon to the brew to cut down on the bitterness. Less mate can be used to reduce the bitterness as well. This can be brewed like any other tea or even in a French press. I only use a mate gourd because I want that authentic experience.

Although this is an excellent mate, there are three negatives with this product. First of all, it DOES NOT come in a resealable bag despite what the Amazon site says. Second, nowhere on the bag does it say how to brew this. Not everyone who buys this tea will know how and purchasers should not have to research this or go to the Amazon site to figure it out. Third, I do not believe the claims on the bag that this supports weight loss and lowers cholesterol. Those claims should not be put on this bag. These three issues are the only reason I took off one star. Otherwise, this is a 5-star mate. Also, the current price of $13.99 is reasonable and competitively priced.

title: Light/sweet vegetable fragrance/taste; brews to a slightly cloudy fluorescent yellow
text:
This tea looks like finely mowed long-blades of grass. It has a light and sweet vegetable aroma. Per the instructions, I brewed two tablespoons at 176 degrees Fahrenheit for 1 minute in 8 ounces of water--note package says 7 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a slightly cloudy fluorescent yellow and the flavor was lightly sweet and vegetal. I almost always feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking Sencha tea. I love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

Note that the instructions on the package were easy for me to understand, which is also a plus. That is not always the case with teas that come from Japan or China.

In summary, I like this tea. At $25.74 for a 2.82-ounce bag, it is not cheap and rightfully so considering the quality. However, it is competitively priced. Note that I do brew the tea a couple of times before throwing it out. Tea like this is possible to brew more than once and still have the same flavor.

title: Coffee is mild, smooth, and overall well-balanced but pouch is not resealable
text:
This coffee smells and tastes good. I brewed several tablespoons in my French press for 4 minutes with boiling water. The flavor is mild, smooth, and overall well-balanced. For Starbucks lovers, it is not.

Unfortunately, the pouch is not resealable. Once the tab is removed to open it, the coffee is exposed. I think the tab on the pouch was put in the wrong place because it looks like there is a zip-lock mechanism inside but the tab rips the package below that point.

The current price of $29 for this 12-ounce bag is pricey. I took off one star each for the price and problem with the bag.

title: This is a flavorful tisane with predominantly spearmint notes in a pale yellow brew
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this "refresh" loose leaf herbal tisane is good.

The flavor is fresh and tastes as I expected. I brewed one teaspoon in a cup of boiling water for 4 minutes. I do use my own tea bags and infusers. The resulting brew is a clear pale yellow and tastes subtly like spearmint with other notes. I love it.

The ingredients are organic apple pieces, organic ginger, organic orange peel, organic spearmint, organic eucalyptus, organic sage leaves, organic elderflower, natural orange flavoring, and natural spearmint flavoring.

In summary, I am a tea purist and 99.9% of the time only drink teas (meaning those that are made from the camellia sinensis evergreen leaves). I rarely like tisanes and usually try them and then give them away. However, this herbal tisanes is excellent and very flavorful; it's a keeper. I tried their 4-pack [[ASIN:B08BTP4766 here]] and they are excellent as well.

The current price of $9.99 is pricey for this small 0.75 ounce amount but this is a quality tisane and fresh too.

title: Tastes like an ordinary green tea; brew is muddy fluorescent yellow
text:
I never heard of Benifuuki Japanese Green Tea but I drink green tea all the time. I also never heard of a tea that is supposed to help allergies. I personally do not have allergies to pollen or the outdoors but I love green tea.

Except for a sticker on the back, the package is in Japanese. There are 30 tea bags in this package. I brewed this with 8 ounces of 175F degree water for 2 minutes (package says 60 to 120 seconds). The tea has a very faint vegetal flavor but otherwise it is tasteless. It brews to a muddy fluorescent yellow. There is nothing about this tea that would have me coming back for more.

The current price of $30 is pricey for this but probably the going rate for this type.

title: Sour melon fragrance/taste; brews to a clear rich auburn w/tiny sediment
text:
There are 10 tea bags in this package. The tea has an earthy and sour melon fragrance and tastes like that too.

I brewed one tea bag at 175 degrees for 4 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The bag recommends 2 minutes but I prefer longer. The resulting brew was a rich auburn and the flavor tastes exactly as it smells and I do not care for it. I am sure this is good tea but since it is pu-erh tea it is fermented. It is probably this fermentation that ruins the flavor profile for me. I do love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

In summary, I wanted to love this tea but I do not. At $14.99 for 10 tea bags, it comes to $1.50 cents per cup which is pricey but in line with a good pu-erh tea.

title: Good sea salt that tastes as it should; grinder or mortar and pestle needed
text:
This is a good sea salt with a typical salty taste. The coloration is grayish. The ingredients are grey sea salt PGI from Guerande France. If this is not going to be used in boiling water or hot soups, then a grinder or mortar and pestle is needed to finely break down the salt.

The current price of $4.99 for 3 ounces comes to about $1.66 per ounce. I like the salt although it is pricey. The gray color does turn me off a little bit.

title: Fresh with good flavor and a bit of heat; ingredients are dried arbol chiles only
text: This chile (not chili) powder is good and is made of only dried arbol chiles. It smells and tastes fresh and has a nice flavor with a bit of heat. This is great in soups or on eggs or any other food item it pairs with. The current price of $14.99 is about three times the price I can buy the same one pound size of McCormick's chili powder for but this one only has dried arbol chiles as an ingredient, whereas chili powders have other spices added. I guess that is the difference between chile (this one) and chili (the others).

title: 5 coffee flavors from light to dark roast; various caffeine levels; super $$$$$
text:
There are 10 single-serve, individually wrapped light to dark roast coffee tea bags in five varieties with two of each variety. The varieties are:

- Early Bird Blend - light roast that is bright and sweet; level 5 in caffeine; countries of origin Guatemala  and  Colombia

- Social Dog Blend - medium roast that is velvety sweet; level 4 in caffeine; countries of origin Papua New Guinea, Nicaragua, and  Columbia

- Careless Cat Blend - medium roast that is smooth and warm; level 2.5 in caffeine; countries of origin Guatemala, Mexico, and  Peru

- Night Owl Blend - dark roast that is creamy and rich; level 3 in caffeine; countries of origin Colombia, Sumatra, and Peru

- Family Blend - medium roast that is smooth and rich; it is unclear what the caffeine level is; countries of origin Colombia, Guatemala, and  Nicaragua

In the photo is the Carless Cat Blend brew. I brewed this with 8 ounces of boiling water and steeped the bag for about 4 minutes.The flavor was full-bodied, smooth, and sweet. It tasted exactly as a medium roast should. I tried this both without half-and-half and with it. I prefer it with half-and-half. I decided to use an electric matcha whisk to mix it into the coffee and it came out frothy. That takes this up a notch.

The current price of $19 comes to $3.80 per cup of coffee which is extremely pricey but one is paying for the convenience and variety. The only reason I rated this 3 stars is because the price is so outrageous. Otherwise, this variety pack is excellent and I love that the blends come from different countries.

UPDATE: I gave my boyfriend half of these and he says they are all too light flavored no matter how long he steeps them. To him this is 3-star coffee and a 1-star price.

title: Green tea with excellent flavor but it has stevia in it
text:
There are 36 K-cups in this box. The ingredients in this apple green tea are green tea, apple, hibiscus, natural flavors, orange peel, and stevia. I love the flavor and so does my boyfriend. However, I am very disappointed there is stevia in it as that makes the tea sweet and I do not like sweet teas and definitely hate artificial sweeteners most of all. Nevertheless, this tea is so flavorful, that I only took off one star for that.

The current price of $19.87 comes to almost $.55 cents per K-cup.

title: Black tea with excellent flavor but it has stevia in it
text:
There are 36 K-cups in this box. The ingredients in this apple black tea are black tea, apple, hibiscus, natural flavors, orange peel, and stevia. I love the flavor and so does my boyfriend. It almost tastes like peach tea. However, I am very disappointed there is stevia in it as that makes the tea sweet and I do not like sweet teas and definitely hate artificial sweeteners most of all. Nevertheless, this tea is so flavorful, that I only took off one star for that.

The current price of $19.87 comes to almost $.55 cents per K-cup.

title: Superbly flavored guac mix; love the tin; garlic first ingredient
text:
There is one 1.7 ounce tin of "everything but the all natural guacamole seasoning" in this package.

I tried this on my avocado salad and it gave it a spectacular flavor. This does not only have to be used on guacamole. It will go on eggs, chicken, pork, and fish. The flavor has depth and is savory.

The ingredients are garlic, onion, salt, jalapeno, cilantro, red pepper flakes, lime juice, and red cayenne.

The current price of $8.49 is expensive for this but I did not take off any stars for the price because I love this so much. The flavor is incredible and a perfect combination of ingredients. I also love the tin this comes in. It is magnetic and will stick to the side of the fridge. Also along the rim, it has a large hole to dispense a lot or small holes to dispense a little.

title: Intensely fragrant and spicy whole peppercorns; grinder or mortar and pestle needed
text:
This pepper is intensely fragrant and spicy and it is from Cape Town, South Africa. A grinder or mortar and pestle is needed to finely break down the pepper. It smells and tastes fresh. It also grinds well.

Overall this is a great black (really brown) pepper. The ingredients are whole black peppercorns. The current price of $7.95 for 3.52 ounces comes to about $2.26 per ounce.

title: Good sea salt that is subtly salty; grinder or mortar and pestle needed
text:
This is a good sea salt with a lighter than typical salt taste. This is non-iodized (spelling on package says non-iodated) coarse salt from the Kalahari. If this is not going to be used in boiling water or hot soups, then a grinder or mortar and pestle is needed to finely break down the salt.

The current price of $9.99 for 8.8 ounces comes to about $1.14 per ounce.

title: Mildy flavorful cinnamon; rec’d 12 sticks & several broken pieces
text:
There are 12 sticks in this package along with several tiny broken pieces. The sticks are short and not the long ones I am used to. I used this in a cup of tea made with boiling water and the resulting brew was lackluster. I could barely taste the cinnamon. In addition, there must have been a loose piece in the stick because there was a tiny piece of bark in my cup.

Cinnamon can be used to make hot apple cider or rice pudding or other desserts that call for it. It is also great in tea like I tried it; just use another brand. I personally prefer Ceylon cinnamon.

The below information is based on my research and is as accurate as possible from the Internet information I found. I am by no means a cinnamon expert, although I am very well aware that Ceylon (not this one) is considered true cinnamon and is safer to consume than the rest.

Cinnamon is a spice created from the inner bark of the Cinnamomum tree. There are four different types of those trees that vary by region and they are:

- Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon Cinnamon) - This is considered true cinnamon and has a milder aroma and flavor than what most people are used to. It is tan-brown in color and contains many tight soft layers. It is large and thick. This cinnamon is not known to be toxic in large quantities like the typical cinnamon on the market. Ceylon cinnamon is also less common and is prized as a cooking spice. It is expensive compared to the more common variety.

- Cinnamomum cassia (a.k.a. Cinnamomum aromaticum ) (Saigon Cinnamon) - is the typical small, thinly rolled, dark brown, and hard cinnamon sticks that most people are familiar with and which is relatively cheap compared to Ceylon. This is also good in desserts. However, this contains a toxin (i.e.Coumarin) that is harmful if too much is consumed. It can cause kidney, liver, and lung damage and may even cause cancer. How much is too much is unknown to me. Note that Ceylon Cinnamon also contains Coumarin too but based on my Internet research, it contains 250 times less than this version.

- Cinnamomum burmannii (Korintje Cinnamon) - This is also known as Indonesian cinnamon, Padang cassia, Batavia cassia and has higher amounts of coumarin than the Saigon Cinnamon above. It is apparently the most common and cheapest type of cinnamon in the US sold in powdered form.

- Cinnamomum loureiroi (Royal Cinnamon) - This is an heirloom variety that is very rarely exported from the mountains around Vietnam.

In summary, these sticks are disappointing. I assume it is the cheapest form of cinnamon and it is definitely not Ceylon. Their fragrance is barely released with heat so it barely spiced up my tea.

At the current price of $5.95, it comes to about $.50 per stick as there were 12 sticks in my package. My recommendation is to pass on this one and purchase another brand like this [[ASIN:B095XBDTPM one]].

title: Typical gunpowder green tea from China; brew is a clear golden amber
text:
This is one pound of loose leaf gunpowder tea in this resealable bag. Upon opening the pouch I was hit with the typical green tea aroma of Gunpowder tea. I brewed one teaspoon to about 8-10 ounces of 185 degree Fahrenheit water for 4 minutes. This tea has a strong flavor that is slightly bitter even though I used warm water. The brew is a clear golden amber. I like this tea but do not love it.

The current price of $14.99 comes to a reasonably priced $.94 cents per ounce. Note that this just contains organic green tea from China.

title: Jasmine aroma before brewing is intoxicating; tea is flavorful with a golden brew
text:
There are 30 tea bags in this resealable bag. Upon opening the pouch I was hit with an intoxicating aroma of Jasmine. I brewed one tea bag to about 8-10 ounces of 185 degree Fahrenheit water for 4 minutes. This tea has a wonderful jasmine flavor to it and is a clear golden amber brew. It is one of two of the most flavorful Jasmine teas I tried in a long time.

The current price of $14.99 comes to a competitively priced $.50 cents per cup. Note that this is a blend of green tea and jasmine flower fragrance. It is hand-rolled in the Fujian province of China.

UPDATE: I've been enjoy this for a couple of weeks now and it is by far the best Jasmine tea I have tasted in a long time. I love it. When I run out of tea, I'm ordering this. I wish they had the loose variety as it would be cheaper.

title: Not extra-spicy but it is excellent; very flavorful & savory; love the dispensing tin
text:
There is one 7.7 ounce tin of "extra spicy bayou boil BBQ seasoning" in this package. They actually over package this wrapped in paper inside a box. This makes it perfect for gifting but also adds to a lot of packaging waste.

I tried this on my broccoli and it was delicious. The flavor is savory and very good. However, I would not call this extra spicy so I took off one star for that. Nevertheless, to me this is a 5-star seasoning mix because I personally do not like anything too spicy. This will go great on eggs, chicken, pork, and beef and anything else one desires.

The ingredients are oregano, paprika, granulated garlic, minced onion, coarse salt,sugar, thyme, black pepper, bay leaves, ground celery seed, red cayenne, chili pepper, chipotle pepper and red pepper.

The current price of $16.99 is a bit pricey for this. In summary, it has a great flavor that is savory and definitely tastes like BBQ. It is less spicy than I expected. I do love the tin this comes in. Along the rim, it has a large hole to dispense a lot or small holes to dispense a little. There are some spice flakes that do have some trouble being dispensed out of the small holes.

title: Kernels are lightweight, so they shoot out of the air popper otherwise this is good popcorn but $$$
text:
I used about 1/4 cup of kernels in my air popper. Since the kernels are lightweight, they kept flying out of the popper from the start. I had to hold the popper backwards with a towel so that they wouldn't fly out. However, once I did that and they popped, then all but about ten kernels were fully popped.

This popcorn is fresh and tastes as it should. The current price of $9.34 ($10.99 minus 15%) is a bit pricey for this.

title: Adds a nice crunch & flavor to my salad; tastes fresh & exactly as expected
text:
There is one 9.3 ounce jar of bagel seasoning in this package. It comes in a nice dispenser spice jar. This is not only for bagels as it can be used on burgers, eggs, poultry, and other dishes. I used this on my salad and it gave it a nice crunch and elevated the flavor.

The ingredients are mostly organic except for the sea salt. The ingredients are sesame seeds, garlic, onion, coarse sea salt, poppy seeds, and canola oil.

The current price of $6.99 is reasonable for this. In summary, this has a very good flavor and nice crunch and it tastes fresh. It is everything I expect in a good bagel seasoning.

title: Five Latin American coffee varieties; great for travel; smooth flavor profile
text:
There are 20 single-serve, individually wrapped medium roast coffee tea bags in five varieties with four of each variety from Guatemala, Honduras, Costa Rica, Brazil, and Colombia.

In the photo is the coffee brew from Brazil. I brewed this with 8 ounces of boiling water and steeped the bag for about 4 minutes.The flavor was full-bodied but smooth and tasted exactly as medium roast should. I tried this both without half-and-half and with it. I prefer it with half-and-half. I decided to use an electric matcha whisk to mix it into the coffee and it came out frothy. That takes this up a notch.

The current price of $21.99 comes to $1.10 per cup of coffee which is slightly pricey but one is paying for the convenience. I really like this selection from Latin America.

title: Fresh with good flavor and a bit of heat but pricey
text: This chili powder is good. It smells and tastes fresh and has a nice flavor with a bit of heat. This is great in soups or on eggs or any other food item it pairs with. However, the current price of $13.99 is almost three times the price I can buy the same one pound size of McCormick's chili powder for and it is not much different in flavor. Hence I took off one star for the high price.

title: Mildly flavored spice mix; love the tin; salt first ingredient
text:
There is one 1.7 ounce tin of "everything but the Sunday roast seasoning" in this package.

I tried this on my avocado salad and it had a nice light flavor to it. This will go on eggs, chicken, pork, and fish. However, the flavor is very mild.

The ingredients are salt, celery seed, sugar, onion, black pepper, rosemary, caraway seed, bay leaves, thyme, savory, marjoram, basil, paprika, garlic, arrowroot powder, bay leaves, parsely, and dill tips.

The current price of $7.74 ($8.49 minus 10%) is also a bit pricey for this. In summary, it has a good flavor but the flavor is very delicate compared to what I expected and salt is the first ingredient.

I do love the tin this comes in. It is magnetic and will stick to the side of the fridge. Also along the rim, it has a large hole to dispense a lot or small holes to dispense a little.

title: Slightly bitter medium roast pour over coffee; just okay & very $$$
text:
At $15 for seven coffee "tea bags," this is very expensive coffee as it comes to $2.14 per 6-8 ounce cup. Each disc is individually wrapped, so it is perfect for travel. I just removed the top and poured boiling water slowly over the grinds. The water drains slowly, so I had to keep adding a few ounces at a time.

The result brew is just okay. It is medium roast but is slightly bitter. To me this is way too expensive and there is a lot of packaging. If it were under $1 per cup, then I would say it is worth the splurge only for traveling. Note that it is best to use this with a glass cup so you can see the level of the coffee.

UPDATE: My boyfriend gives this two stars. He said, peeling the top off can spill the coffee everywhere. This didn’t happen to him but it has a lot of potential to. The coffee took too long to seep through--even though this is typical of these type of travel coffee options--except for the coffee tea bag. He had to keep lifting filter out of the cup which was annoying and the resulting brew was weak to him.

title: Absolutely incredible tasting; like Cheez-It crackers with the crunch but without the cracker
text:
These are absolutely incredible tasting. They taste like Cheez-It crackers and have the crunch of a cracker and the flavor of a full cheese cracker like Cheez-It. However, these are just cheese and nothing else. The moisture in the cheese is removed through a process and these nuggets are produced.

The ingredients are gouda cheese (milk, salt, bacterial culture, microbial enzyme, vegetable color (annatto))and cellulose. A serving size is one 1-ounce package. Each package has 25 to 31 pieces in it and is 150 calories with 340 mg of sodium, 35 mg of cholesterol, and 11 g of protein.

There are 12 1-ounce packages in this shipment. I have no idea how much these will cost, so factor that in before buying. However, this is a healthy snack compared to most others on the market. I actually find it extremely difficult to just eat one package; these are that good. In fact, I ate four packages in one day.

UPDATE: I gave two packages to my boyfriend and he LOVES these. He feels the same way I do. He said these tastes like Cheez-It crackers and he loves the crunch and flavor.

title: Intense and fragrant pepper with slightly sweet taste; grinder required
text:
The color of this pepper is more brown than black and the flavor is sweet and fragrant. It smells and tastes fresh. It also grinds well.

Overall this is a great black (really brown) pepper. The ingredients are biodynamic organic whole black peppercorns. This is currently not priced but I would expect to pay around $8 for this 8 ounce bag as an online price.

title: Intensely bitter & astringent; I threw the brew out after the first sip
text:
There is a 15-ounce can of ground coffee/chicory and two long gold spoons in this set. The grinds do not smell much like coffee but I suspect that has to do with the addition of chicory.

Per the instructions, I used one heaping tablespoon per cup. For my small pot of coffee, one heaping coffee scoop which is the equivalent of two slightly heaping tablespoons of coffee.  

The resulting brew looked like coffee; however, it tasted terrible. The brew was intensely bitter and astringent and I threw it out immediately. This is definitely not for me. My understanding is that chicory added to coffee is supposed to make it milder and reduce the bitterness. I did not find this the case.

The current price of $9.99 is competitively priced if one likes this brew.

title: Light roast coffee and not dark roast; cannot be stored in a K-cup holder
text:
There are 10 K-cup coffee pods in this box. They are wrapped in a foil bag because the coffee is in a mesh pod. This means that they cannot be stored in a K-cup holder because the coffee is exposed and not contained in a plastic K-cup.

My boyfriend and I tried this. I think it is okay coffee but my boyfriend loves it. Note that we both love MILD coffee and NOT DARK ROAST. This is supposed to be a dark roast and it is not. So from our perspective, I give this 4 stars for taste and my boyfriend gives it 5 stars. However, from the perspective of what this is supposed to be, it is 1 or 2 stars.

The current price of $11.99 is expensive for this as it comes to $1.20 per cup, which is expensive.

title: My boyfriend loves this and I hate it; 60 calories per can w/70mg of caffeine; all sugar
text:
There are six 8.4-fluid-ounce cans of mate mate drink in this package. It contains 70mg of caffeine which is almost as much caffeine as a cup of coffee. Each can is a serving at 60 calories each. The ingredients are water, sugar, agave syrup, carbon dioxide, citric acid, natural flavors, yerba mate extract, caffeine, and colour (caramel 150d).

To me this tastes like liquid smoke. Also, it is all sugar. I am familiar with yerba mate and it has a smokey and bitter flavor. I actually hate this drink and give it 1 star but since I got it for him, it is his rating I am posting. My boyfriend loves this drink and gives it 5 stars for taste but 3 stars overall due to the high price. To him it tastes like ginger ale but he would never buy this because it costs too much per serving. At the current price of $14.25 ($15 minus 5%) comes to $2.38 per can.

title: Absolutely fresh and flavorful tarragon
text:
There are six .2 ounce jars of tarragon in this package. The jars are sealed and have a dispenser top. The tarragon is incredibly fresh and flavorful. I use this on fish, in pasta sauce, in noodle salads, soups, etc.

The current price of $12.70 ($14.94 minus 15%) comes to $2.12 per jar, which is pricey but still competitive.

title: Whole bean decaf espresso blend; tastes a bit bitter to me but otherwise okay
text:
This is a 12-ounce bag of whole bean decaf espresso. The beans look and smell great and the beans are fresh. The resulting brew, however, is a tiny bit bitter and I drink it with half and half. Note that I brew espresso coffee as regular coffee after grinding. This is okay to my palate but not great. The current price of $16.99 for 8 ounces is also on the high side of competitively priced.

Note that mine arrived with a best buy date of 2/15/2023, which is 11 months from now.

title: Very flavorful sauce but doesn’t taste like Bolognese and it is too pricey
text:
This sauce is very flavorful to me and from that perspective, I give it 5 stars. However, it tastes nothing like bolognese and for that I take off 1 star. Also, I took off another star for the $12.41 price tag for 19.4 ounces, which is very expensive.

There is nothing but good ingredients in this sauce and they are chopped tomatoes, extra virgin olive oil, kabocha squash, carrot puree, walnuts, shiitake mushrooms, dehydrated carrots, and sea salt. There's 170 calories per half cup serving and there are 4.5 servings per jar. Each serving has 5g of sugars and 430 mg of sodium.

title: Great selection of herbal tisanes; love the lemon ginger
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this set of four tisanes is good. There are four tins of loose leaf herbal ingredients (organic sleep, organic lemon ginger, organic peppermint, and herbal cleanse).

The flavor of these tisanes is fresh and tastes as they should. I brew one teaspoon in a cup of water for 4 minutes. I do use my own tea bags and infusers. The photo of the cup of tea attached to this review is of the lemon ginger brew which is a clear pale yellow and tastes subtly like both lemongrass and ginger. I love it.

Below are the ingredients of all the tisanes.

ORGANIC LEMON GINGER - organic ginger, organic lemongrass, organic liquorice root, organic lemon peel, and organic spearmint.

HERBAL CLEANSE - organic rosehip, organic verbena, organic spearmint, organic anise, organic lavender, organic lemon peel, and organic rosepetals [SIC].

ORGANIC SLEEP - organic peppermint, organic lemongrass, organic rosehips, organic hibiscus, organic chamomile, organic valerian root, and natural flavoring.

ORGANIC PEPPERMINT - organic peppermint.

In summary, I am a tea purist and 99.9% of the time only drink teas (meaning those that are made from the camellia sinensis evergreen leaves). I rarely like tisanes and usually try them and then give them away. However, this variety of herbal tisanes is excellent and very flavorful.  I love them and plan to keep these for myself.

title: Tea smells fresh and fragrant; brews to a deep amber hue; tastes bold
text:
At 2lbs 3.3 ounces, this is a large bag of loose leaf black tea from India. The bag is resealable, which is a plus. The current price of $14.99 is also very reasonable for this. The ingredients are black tea (51% Indian tea).

I steeped one teaspoon of tea in a 10-ounce mug of boiling water for four minutes. The resulting brew was a clear but deep amber hue. The taste is bold and much better than the typical Lipton black tea sold in the US. My boyfriend liked it as well. It smells fresh and tastes good enough for me to drink plain. I still like this [[ASIN:B09BDM93SK version]] of their tea slightly better, but this is good as well.
