Overall, I like this tea and the price is reasonable. It also tastes good with milk and sugar if that is what is desired.

title: Nice balanced flavor profile; brews to a clear golden amber
text:
There are 15 tea bags in this package. The ingredients are apple pomace, black tea (20%), rosehip seed, rooibos (10%), sweet blackberry leaves, elderberry flowers (6%), peppermint (3%), natural flavouring, rhubarb pieces (1%), and mullein flowers (1%).

I steeped one tea bag in boiling water for 5 minutes and the resulting brew was a clear golden amber. My boyfriend and I drank this and really enjoyed it. The blackberry comes through the most in this brew.

The current price of $7.99 comes to $.53 cents per cup.

title: Too sweet for our taste; a relatively healthy snack ruined w/sprinkles and glaze
text:
The current price of $11.50 for one 7.5 ounce bag of popcorn is outrageously overpriced in our opinion. My boyfriend and I tried this and feel this is way too sweet. I thought there was going to be birthday flavoring on this, but this has a glaze and sprinkles on them. We could not eat much of this at all without feeling sick.

In this 7.5 ounce bag there are supposed to be 7-1/2 servings at 140 calories for each 1 ounce serving. Each serving contains 2g protein and 22g carbs with 12g of them added sugars.

After having tried this, neither my boyfriend or I would ever buy this. The kettle corn itself is great but the cloyingly sweet flavoring on top (sprinkles and glaze) ruins this for us. We disliked this so much, we threw it out.

title: Very fresh; great taste; good compliment to fish dishes; not overpowering
text: I use turmeric periodically on my fish dishes. This powder is very fresh and has a great taste that is subtle and not overpowering, yet very flavorful. I am very pleased with the quality. Note that this is organic turmeric and is repackaged in a facility that packages dairy, eggs, sesame, and mustard. The current price is $5.70 for 6 ounces comes to a reasonable $.95 cents per ounce.

title: Tastes like sour grapes; not the way I want to eat my fruit and veges - 2.5 stars
text:
These candy chews are okay. There are 6 packs of 12 chews per package for a total of 72 chews or 12 servings since there are 2 servings per pack. I thought these would be sticky and like caramel but are not. The chews are similar but do not stick to the teeth, which is great. They have an intense grape flavor to me; more like sour grapes.

Each serving of six chews is supposed to contain one serving of fruits and vegetables. Each serving is equivalent to having half a beet, one carrot, and a slice of pumpkin, sweet potato, and squash. Each serving is also 110 calories. There are 6g of added sugars that are part of the 26g carbohydrates and 60 mg of sodium.

Personally, I am not crazy about these as either a candy or as a health treat. They have an intense grape aftertaste to me that is bordering on sour. These are okay but not great. However, my boyfriend really liked these and rated them 5 stars.

The current price of 21.99 comes to $3.67 per package or $1.83 per serving, which I consider expensive.

title: Very fresh ground star anise and has the intense licorice flavor I have come to expect
text:
This star anise powder tastes very fresh and has the intense licorice flavor I come to expect. This can be used in many dishes (soup, stews, tea, etc.) including fresh toast.

The only negative is that the $9.99 price tag is a bit steep for 1.3 ounces. I can get 1.75 ounces for $2.49 at my local store. Of course, it doesn't taste as good as this one but four times the price is a bit much. The price is the only reason I took off one star. Otherwise, this is excellent.

title: Very lemony and peppery; strong but good lemon pepper seasoning
text:
I actually love the flavor of this spice on fish or chicken. It can also be used on pasta. The lemon and pepper really comes through and is very balanced.

The ingredients are salt, lemon peel, garlic, onion, back pepper, sugar, and tumeric.

This is manufactured in California but I have no idea where the ingredients are sourced from.

In summary, I love the flavor of this spice on certain dishes and it tastes very fresh with a balanced flavor profile where both the lemon and pepper seasoning are the predominant notes. The current price of $6.99 for 8 ounces is very reasonable for this.

title: This coffee is superb; both my boyfriend and I rate this 5 stars
text: At the current price of $17.99, this coffee is pricey for one pound of beans. However, it is absolutely excellent. I ground the beans and made a pot of coffee. My boyfriend and I were amazed at how full-bodied but mild this coffee is. Since it is Espresso, someone might expect a stronger brew but we prefer a milder brew. This is milder than expected, which makes it five stars for us. It has a very balanced flavor profile.

title: This seasoning is superb; it tastes fantastic on rainbow trout
text:
This Fajita seasoning is superb. It goes with meat, fish, chicken, pork, etc. I used this on my rainbow trout and it tasted fantastic. My boyfriend even commented how he loved the flavor.

The ingredients are sea salt, spices (including paprika), sugar, dehydrated onion and garlic, citric acid, and less than 2% silicon dioxide added to prevent caking.

The only negative is that the top is very difficult to pry up. I have to use a cake spatula to pop it up to expose the dispensing holes. The seasoning, however, is great.

The current price of $14.95 is on the high side of competitively priced for this 30-ounce container.

UPDATE: I shared some of this with a friend and they loved it. One of her son's ate five pieces of fish because of this spice and the other took some down to Florida for his cooking. This is definitely a hit.

title: Tastes orangey but terrible like it is full of saccharin; I threw it out
text:
I dissolved two teaspoons of this in hot water and the resulting brew is a cloudy and frothy green like a good matcha. However, the taste is terrible even though it tasted a lot like orange. It also tasted like it was made of fake sugar (like saccharin).

Each serving is 39.3 calories and the ingredients are green tea, citrus unshu, sugar, dextrin, sucralose, and natural flavor.

I had my boyfriend try this tea and he said " I do not like this at all." I threw this out immediately.

title: Disgusting – tastes like it is full of saccharin; I threw it out
text:
I dissolved two teaspoons of this in hot water and the resulting brew is a cloudy green like a good matcha. However, the taste is disgusting. It tastes like it is made of fake sugar (like saccharin).

Each serving is 39.3 calories and the ingredients are green tea, citrus unshu, sugar, dextrin, sucralose, and natural flavor.

I had my boyfriend try this tea and he said "this is 'expletive' horrible." I threw this out immediately. Yuck.

title: Very fresh; great taste; good compliment to fish dishes; not overpowering
text: I use turmeric periodically on my fish dishes. This powder is very fresh and has a great taste that is subtle and not overpowering, yet very flavorful. I am very pleased with the quality. The current price is $10.55 for 8 ounces comes to a slightly pricey $1.32 per ounce. Since this is single-origin, I expect the price will be higher than commercial grade.

title: I use this like coconut oil for cooking; it has a deep full-bodied flavor that I like
text:
I have a friend who is Asian Indian and she cooks a lot with ghee (clarified butter). I had never heard of ghee before that. I know that ghee can be used like butter and actually spread on toast and other foods. However, since this is raw, I am uncomfortable consuming this without cooking with it to destroy any bacteria. There are so many warnings out on the Internet about consuming any raw milk product, that I take the safer route. 

This is made from camel milk and is a product of India. Desert farms distributes it and their website has some more information. Once opened, this can be stored at cold or room temperature. It can be eaten raw (I don't eat it this way for safety concerns) or fried (which is the way I eat it). It has a very high smoke point of 450 degrees Fahrenheit. It actually is hard like lard or coconut oil and melts like those oils too.

I fried my eggs and potatoes with this and it gave them such a great flavor. It was full-bodied and deep, not necessarily nutty but delicious in a way I cannot describe. One tablespoon of this is 120 calories, so it is high calorie as are all oils and butters. It contains 45mg of cholesterol per tablespoon and there are no trans fats but it is obviously made up of saturated fat. I do not subscribe to the health benefits of frying or oils, except olive oil. Therefore, despite all the benefits touted on the Internet about camel ghee or ghee in general, I just dismiss them.  

Note that this has a nine month shelf life and since this was packaged in December 2021 and I received it March 14, 2022, it is only good until August 22. This means by the time this is ordered and received, most will only have about 5 months to consume this nine-ounce jar in full.

The current price of $35 is expensive but it does add a lot of flavor to fried dishes; at least in my opinion. If one can splurge, I highly recommend giving this a try.

title: Strong peppermint taste and smell; very expensive at $1.25 per cup
text: I ordered the peppermint tea and it  has a strong peppermint aroma and a great minty flavor. However, at its current price of $14.99 for a 12-K cup pack, it comes to a very expensive $1.25 per cup. This is the only reason I took off one star. The taste is great to me and it brews to a deep amber.

title: These seasoning is very good & tastes great on potatoes but there are thick pods & ….
text:
I actually love the flavor of this spice on my potatoes; they made them taste great. The flavor really comes through and the ingredients seem fresh. However, this does not dispense well out of the container--the side with the holes. Also, there are pods that are inedible that are peppered throughout this spice (see photos). That is disappointing as I was going to rate this 4 stars. It would have been 5 stars if there were no pods and it dispensed easily.

The ingredients are all organic EXCEPT for the seal salt and they are basil, garlic, oregano, onion, sea salt, coriander, rosemary, black pepper, parsley, thyme, marjoram, and red pepper.

This is made in the USA but I have no idea where the ingredients are sourced from. This brand also supports several non-profits.

In summary, I love the flavor but not the clump. The current price of $8.99 is competitively priced for this.

title: Very lemony and also very clumpy; super mild flavor, no heat
text:
I actually love the flavor of this spice on fish. The lemon comes through but no other flavor is detectible. Also, this does not dispense well out of the container. It is totally clumpy and that, along with the less than mild heat, is the only reason I rated this 3 stars.

The ingredients are organic lemon powder (Organic Concentrate, Organic Lemon Oil), Organic Coriander, Organic Honey Powder (Organic Maltodextrin, Organic Honey), Sea Salt, Organic Lemon Peel, Organic Thyme, Organic Garlic, Organic Onion, Organic Black Pepper, Organic Birdseye Chile, Organic Ghost Chile.

This is made in the USA but I have no idea where the ingredients are sourced from. This brand also supports several non-profits.

In summary, I love the flavor but not the clump. The current price of $8.99 is competitively priced for this.

title: Has nice crunch & good texture; tastes as chocolate sprinkles should; cocoa powder is 3rd ingredient
text: Chocolate sprinkles on ice cream are hard to stop eating and this one is no different. They are 50 calories a tablespoon and the first three ingredients are sugar, hydrogenated palm kernel oil, and cocoa powder, etc. These are definitely not good for you but they taste great. The current price of $14.54 for two pounds comes to $7.27 per pound.

title: A nice black tea with a hint of bergamot; it's almost Earl Grey tea; smells wonderful; mild taste
text:
I was pleasantly surprised when I opened this tin. The tea smells heavenly and exactly like Earl Grey. In fact, the aroma is intoxicating to me. I expected plain black tea. This tin comes with 5.3 ounces of loose leaf blak tea with .05% of bergamot. Also included are 40 tea bags (I counted them). Even though the Amazon site says this makes 50 cups of tea, only enough tea bags for 40 cups is included.

I steeped one teaspoon of tea in a 10-ounce cup of boiling water for four minutes. The resulting brew was a clear and deep reddish amber. The taste is mild and much better than the typical Lipton black tea sold in the US. It smells fresh and tastes good enough for me to drink plain. The bergamot is not noticeable once brewed but the flavor is still very good.

Overall, I like this tea. The current price of $18.96 is slightly pricey for 5.3 ounces of black tea but then one is paying for the tea bags included.

title: Good cayenne pepper with the right amount of heat
text:
Cayenne pepper has many uses including as a spice for soups or rice, or meat, etc. I used it on my meat and vegetables.

There are twelve 1.62-ounce containers in this package. This has just the right amount of heat and adds a good flavor to the dish, providing that is what is desired.

I tried this in my corned beef and cabbage dish (pictured) and it was very good.

The current price of $14.70 ($17.29 minus 15%) comes to almost $1.23 per container, which I consider very reasonable.

title: Good vanilla beans that are moist and fragrant; 32 beans in my 4 ounce pkg
text:
In the 4-ounce package of beans I received there were 32 vanilla beans. The beans are fragrant and moist as a good vanilla bean should be. I made vanilla sugar with these and it came out well. I also make my own vanilla extract.

For vanilla sugar, I cut the bean down the center and scrape out the seeds and add that to the top of one cup of sugar. I then cut the bean in tiny pieces and add that on top and then pulse until it is evenly distributed into the mix. Note that most people use one bean to two cups of sugar but I prefer a more fragrant sugar. Note that since the bean is moist, the sugar will clump and has to be shaken once in a while.

To make vanilla extract, I take about six vanilla beans, split them down the center, and insert them into about 10 ounces of Vodka. I then put the bottle in a cool place and shake it weekly. After six months, I have a wonderful pure vanilla extract; there's no more of that store bought junk.

In summary, these are good beans. The current price of $46.99 is a little pricey but most good beans sell for about $13 or more for 10 beans, which would be the equivalent to about $41.60 for the 32 beans I received.

title: A very mild tasting spice; did not pack the flavor I expected; main ingredient is salt
text:
I rarely use spices on my food, so when I do, I tend to have a more sensitive palate. I tried this on my vegetables and meat both before and after cooking. It added a very subtle flavor to the food but it was so mild. I did have an aftertaste of a tingly sensation on my tongue but that is pretty much it.

It is almost impossible to read the ingredients list on this jar. The ingredients are season salt (salt, sugar, paprika, garlic, spices, microcrystalline cellulose for anti-caking), onion, garlic, paprika, yeast extract, red and black pepper, and other herbs and spices.

I wish the label were clearer and would have loved had they listed exactly what herbs and spices were used.

The current price of $8.76 ($10.95 minus 20%) is reasonable for this. Just realize the main ingredient is salt.

title: Sweet vegetable fragrance/taste; brews to an almost clear fluorescent yellow – no English instruc
text:
This tea comes in opaque pyramid tea bags so it is hard to see the contents. It has a sweet vegetable aroma. I brewed one tea bag at 185 degrees for 4 minutes in 8 ounces of water. The resulting brew was an almost clear fluorescent yellow and the flavor was all vegetal and sweet. I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this. I also love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

Note that there are absolutely no English words or instructions on the package, which is the only reason I rated this 4 instead of 5 stars.

In summary, I love this tea but hate that there is nothing written on the packaging that I can understand. At $28 ($35 minus 20%) for 50 tea bags, it comes to $.56 cents per cup.

title: Flavorful seasoning that goes great on pasta; love the magnetic canister with dispensing holes
text:
This seasoning comes in a small plastic package that has to be cut open and dispensed into the tin. The amount is very small. The tin has weep holes and a larger hole to dispense the spice along the rim. I really like this design. The spice dispenses easily through both holes.

I used this on my pasta and it really kicked the flavor up a notch. The flavor is bold and very Italian. The ingredients are garlic, salt, red pepper flakes, green bell pepper, butter powder, basil, oregano, black pepper, parsley, fennel, and rosemary.

The tin has a magnetic bottom, so that I can affix it to the side of my fridge and grab it whenever I want to douse a dish. Overall, I love this seasoning and the tin it comes in.

title: Seasoning is subtle & mildly flavorful; love the magnetic canister with dispensing holes
text:
This seasoning comes in a small plastic package that has to be cut open and dispensed into the tin. The amount is very small. The tin has weep holes and a larger hole to dispense the spice along the rim. I really like this design. The spice does not dispense easily through the weep holes but it dispenses fine through the larger hole.

I used this on my regular food and it goes well on pasta and not only pizza. The flavor, however, is more muted than I expected. There is a subtleness to the spice. I expected bolder flavors.

The tin has a magnetic bottom, so that I can affix it to the side of my fridge and grab it whenever I want to douse a dish. The ingredients are garlic, salt, basil, green bell peppers, sesame, black pepper, fennel, and rosemary.

Overall, I like this seasoning but I love the tin.

title: Grinds fine and is full of flavor and it claims to be 37% less sodium
text:
This grinder grinds the salt finely, which I love. The flavor is excellent and tastes like very good salt. Since it contains 37% less sodium, this type of salt is better for those with high blood pressure. Per the package, the ingredients are from an underground brine salt mine from the Atacama Desert and Silicone Dioxide is used as an anti-caking agent.

This is a product of Chile. The reason this product contains 37% less salt is because the saline groundwater is allowed to evaporate in the desert and during the process, a natural chemical reaction takes place to unite sodium and potassium in a unique salt grain containing 37% less sodium than regular salt.

title: A flavorful & healthier version of a CCC; tiny & very crunchy; the box is a novel - see UPDATES
text:
There are six 6-ounce boxes of cookies in this package with two 3-ounce (I assume they are 3 ounces) packages of cookies in foil wrapped bags. Supposedly, there are about 5 servings per bag or 10 servings per box. Each bag to me was one serving and not 5. In fact, although the box says there are 10 cookies per serving, that is totally not true. There were only about 18 cookies per bag I tried, which means 36 cookies per entire box and not the 100 cookies claimed (5 cookies x 10 per serving x 2 bags = 100 cookies). This gets one star for misrepresentation because it is really impossible for me to know how many cookies are really in the 105 calories per serving?

These cookies taste like a healthier version of the Famous Amos cookies. I love the crunch and texture and the taste is flavorful but not sinful. On taste alone, I rate this 4 stars. In fact, I ate an entire box in two sittings (not recommended). I do like that they do not use high fructose corn syrup or palm oil or preservatives. However, it is still a cookie with organic cane sugar. See photo of nutrition label.

Now for the box, that contains warnings and meaningful poems. There is so much writing on the inside and outside of the box that it is like a novel. I personally find this gimmicky and most people will not read all this. The box is entirely too busy with writing for me. One even gets to meet their dog Cariton--a King Charles Spaniel. I feel they should save the money on printing and pass the savings along to the customer.

The current price of $35.50 for 6 boxes comes to a pricey $5.92 per box since to me there are really only two or three servings per box.

If the box was less busy, the price a bit lower, and the nutrition facts correctly indicated, this would have received 4 stars on taste alone. It's a nice, healthier version of a CCC (chocolate chip cookie).

1st UPDATE 3/3/22 - I always give away the cookies when I get more than one box because I want to get other opinions. I gave the remaining five boxes away hoping to get feedback. My downstairs neighbor ate these and below is her assessment verbatim. Based on her sentiment, I'd say she rates these 5 stars.

OMG!!!
My new favorite cookie! - Tastes just like homemade...I love the crispiness, that's how I make mine (and not too sweet!) Darrin likes chewier cookies, but said these are really good!!

I also like how they package 2 bags in the box...some to have now and some for another day without getting stale! Also....on the individual bags, it tells you how to open the bag an wait 10 minutes to allow the cookies to "breathe" - somehow it makes a difference...Yum!!

- HIGHLY RECOMMEND 🙂

thanks for sharing the sweetness!

- Traci

2ND UPDATE 3/3/22 - My upstairs neighbors tried these and both rated them 4 stars. They really liked them.

3RD UPDATE 3/3/22 - My other neighbor in my complex tried these and her assessment is that these are 2 stars based on her review below verbatim.

Her message to me BEFORE eating:
Just read box....interesting....opened 1 bag....it smells different....like coco mixed with something else....not like a chocolate chip cookie smell...not saying it's a yucky smell....just not sure I would eat w just smell alone...im waiting the 10 minutes before I taste
Stay tuned

Her message to me AFTER eating:
Okay...
1. Initial bite...crunchy..not a fan
2. It sorta disintegrates rather than a nice chew with a great taste on tongue
3. After taste is funky after cookie is gone
4. Not tasting chocolate not sure exactly what I'm tasting
Letting it soak in mouth
6. Cookie tastes like dull chocolate. Chip batter...
7. Almost a burnt taste
I prefer other brands over this one...its like the cookie dissolves and chips are after thought

Hope this helps...what else can I tell you...

The aftertaste...not good...I feel like it's in my teeth....gums...not a fan...

ANOTHER UPDATE - My neighbor above who didn't like the cookies gave them to her upstairs neighbor, Tara and she really liked them. Tara rated them 4 stars.

4TH UPDATE 3/5/22 - My boyfriend just tried these and loves them. He gives them 5 stars. He says they taste like Chips Ahoy! cookies. He did say all the writing in the box is gimmicky and uttered the sentence “I am not going to read all that.”

5TH UPDATE 3/10/22 - This is the final update. I gave the last box to a friend. She ate them right away and paid no attention to any writing on the box. She said she really likes these cookies and would rate them between 4 and 5 stars and would buy them if given a chance. Therefore, her ranking is 4.5 stars.

If I were to average all the rankings, this is a 4-star cookie based on taste and how others told me they liked them. They were certainly 4 stars to me from a taste standpoint.

title: Delicious salt with intensely salty flavor & nice crunch when not used in cooking
text:
At $29.95, this salt is expensive; however, it is very good. When used as seasoning on food, it has a nice crunch and an intensely salty flavor that is full bodied. It dissolves easily in cooking and is just as great there. This salt is from Vancouver Island, Canada (from the clear waters of the Canadian Pacific). I love it.

I do wish the tub had a handle on it; that is the only negative.

title: This honey is thick and delicious and its MGO 45+ means it has antibacterial properties as well
text:
This honey is absolutely delicious. I never had manuka honey before. It is from New Zealand and supposedly, with an 45+ MGO is supposed to have antibacterial properties. The higher the rating the better. According to the manukadoctor website, “The strength of Manuka Doctor Manuka Honey is measured using the MGO (Methylglyoxal) grading system from 30 MGO to 840 MGO. …When deciding on which Manuka Honey to buy, the greater the number, the stronger the antimicrobial activity.”

I tried some of this plain and it has a very nice flavor that is intense, full-bodied, and delicious. I also put some in my tea and it gave it a great flavor.

The current price of $16.90 is expensive but competitively priced.

title: This curry powder adds a warm, toasty flavor to my dishes
text:
Curry powder has many uses including as a spice for soups or rice, which is what I use it for.

There are twelve 1.75-ounce containers in this package. The ingredients in the blend are spices (including turmeric, cumin, and ginger) and onion. What I particularly like is that this is not as strong as some curry powder as I tried previously but others might prefer more flavor.

I tried this in my rice dish (pictured) and it was very good.

The current price of $14.82 ($17.44 minus 15%) comes to $1.24 per container, which I consider a reasonable price.

title: Faint vegetable flavor w/toasty overtones; very fragrant tea; brews slightly cloudy pale yellow
text:
This tea almost looks like blades of grass that have been cut short with organic roasted rice included. The ingredients are actually green tea and organic roasted rice

This tea has a wonderfully fragrant aroma that is slightly vegetal but mostly toasty. I brewed one teaspoon at 185 degrees for 3 minutes in 10 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a slightly cloudy pale yellow and the flavor and aroma was toasty with subtle sweet vegetal overtones. I really love this tea.

I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this. I also love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

The current price of $35.95 for a 12.8 ounce bag comes to about $2.81 per ounce which I consider reasonable considering the quality and flavor. I also like that this comes in a resealable pouch.

title: Delicious olive spread made from kalamata olives; thick but spreadable
text:
For the current price of $12.99, one gets one 4.8 ounce jar of kalamata (a.k.a. kalamon) olive spread that is equal to 4.5 servings. There are two tablespoons per serving at 70 calories each with 350mg of sodium.. The spread is thicker than I expected but it is delicious by itself or on crackers with cream cheese. It would be great on French bread, omelets, etc. I really love the spread but it is pricey. This must be refrigerated and eaten 20 days after opening. That should not be a problem because I had to stop myself from eating the entire jar in one sitting.

Despite what other reviewers said about this not being sealed, I actually consider it safe. The top opens with a pop and is easy to open but the jar I got was sealed until I twisted off the top. Jars like this, unopened, only last about three to six months. Once opened, it must be consumed almost immediately, which is why it is best when it comes in a small jar.

title: Delicious olive spread made from green olives; slightly oily
text:
For the current price of $12.99, one gets one 4.8 ounce jar of green olive spread that is equal to 4.5 servings. There are two tablespoons per serving at 40 calories each with 380mg of sodium. The spread is a little oilier than I expected but it is delicious by itself or on crackers with cream cheese. It would be great on French bread, omelets, etc. I really love the spread but it is pricey. This must be refrigerated and eaten 15 days after opening. That should not be a problem because I had to stop myself from eating the entire jar in one sitting.

The top opens with a pop and is easy to open but the jar I got was sealed until I twisted off the top. Jars like this, unopened, only last about three to six months. Once opened, it must be consumed almost immediately, which is why it is best when it comes in a small jar.

title: Good tisane; tastes minty like it should; brews to a golden amber
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this tisane is good. There are three boxes of 16 tea bags each box. The ingredients are organic peppermint leaf, organic sage leaf, organic lemon peel, organic spearmint leaf, organic eucalyptus leaf, organic licorice root, organic lavender flower, and organic lavender extract.

I brewed this with boiling water and steeped it for five minutes. The tea smells are fresh and fragrant and brews to a golden auburn hue. The flavor is minty, balanced, and light.

UPDATE - I gave this to my boyfriend because I am a tea purist and usually do not drink herbal tisanes even though this is absolutely delicious. This is what he said. "I was quite surprised how good it is. The mint aroma flies out of the cup once it is steeped. An excellent choice. An added bonus is the tea doesn’t stain the cup, therefore it shouldn’t stain my teeth."

title: This honey is thick and delicious and its MGO 80+ means it has antibacterial properties as well
text:
Although this is great honey, the jar is less than half the 17.6 ounce size specified. They sent the 8.75 ounce jar.

However, this honey is absolutely delicious. I never had manuka honey before. It is from New Zealand and supposedly, with an 80+ MGO is supposed to have antibacterial properties. I tried some plain and it has a very nice flavor that is intense, full-bodied, and delicious. I also put some in my tea and it gave it a great flavor.

The current price of $44.99 is super expensive, however. I did not take off any stars for the price as I am aware that honey from New Zealand is super expensive and this is no different. The reason I rated this 2 stars is because I ordered a17.6 ounce jar of honey but only received an 8.75 ounce. Therefore, beware that what is ordered is not received and one is paying for a larger amount than one gets.

title: Four pounds of sugary bliss perfect for a pinata where variety is key
text:
I am a chocolate fan myself but these candies are perfect for a pinata. There are four pounds of candy that include several tootsie pops, smarties, starburst, now and later, top pops, vidal soft fruit, green apple chews, peach chews,  Swedish fish, etc. There was only one Bon Bon Bum Bubble Gum pop in the bag but I suspect that  is because there seems to be no system as to how this is filled up with candy.

Nevertheless, this is the perfect fill for pinatas or for a candy bowl at a party or even Halloween. The current price of $24.99 is $6.25 per pound, which is pricey for sweetness that is not chocolate.

UPDATE Feb 16 - I had ordered this to give to a teacher who schools young children. She gave these out a few days after Valentine's and the kids went ballistic. She said they particularly loved the Dubble Bubble and Now and Later candies that were in the bag. Both the teacher and the children gave this bag of candy 5 stars.

title: Tea smells fresh and fragrant; brews to a deep reddish amber; tastes bold
text:
At 2lbs 3.3 ounces, this is a large bag of loose leaf black tea from India. The bag is resealable, which is a plus. The current price of $18.62 ($21.91 minus 15%) is also very reasonable for this.

I steeped one teaspoon of tea in a 10-ounce mug of boiling water for four minutes. The resulting brew was a clear and deep reddish amber. The taste is bold and much better than the typical Lipton black tea sold in the US. It smells fresh and tastes good enough for me to drink plain.

Overall, I like this tea and the price is reasonable. It also tastes good with milk and sugar if that is what is desired.

title: Bold breakfast tea with a hint of smokiness
text: There are 15 sachets in this package. This is typical English Breakfast Tea. I used one tea bag to one 8 ounce cup of boiling water and steeped for 4 minutes. The brew is a deep amber color and the flavor is clean and slightly bold with a hint of smokiness. The current price of $11.50 is pricey as it comes to $.77 cents per cup.

title: Smells like peanut butter; looks like wallpaper paste; tastes absolutely disgusting
text:
There are three 10.7 ounce bags in this package. It says the package is supposed to last five servings but if one follows the directions on the package, there are only three servings per package. The ingredients are gluten free rolled oats, chia seeds, peanut flour, olotillo corn, raw cane sugar, sea salt, organic cocoa powder, cinnamon, and a trace of lime.

Per serving, I used a half cup of the oat powder (and it is powder) to three-quarter cup of water. The results looked like paste. Both my boyfriend and I tried it and hated it. We both threw it out. Note that each serving is 250 calories (not counting the fruit I added on top or the milk) and has 7g of sugar with 170 mg of sodium.

This is definitely not for us. The current price of $24.95 comes to $8.32 per bag which seems to be 3 - 1/2cup servings even though they say 5 servings. We'll pass.

title: Only for Starbucks lovers; coffee is so strong I can't stand it & it's pricey; good for travel
text:
[[VIDEOID:3679544f7343f966d1dc3340faee224e]] I tried this [[ASIN:B08DRHQC9H coffee]] and loved it so much that I figured I would order these. However, the current price of $10.99 for 6 servings is insanely expensive at $1.83 per cup. The other brand is only $.40 cents per cup.

There are six individually wrapped coffee tea bags in the pouch good enough for six cups of coffee. When I opened the box, a wonderful fragrant aroma greeted me. I just removed the "coffee tea bag" from its package and spread its wings over my mug. I then ripped the top seam off so it looked like a drip coffee maker's open filter. Next I slowly poured boiling water over the pouch. I did not let it steep at all because the water went through the filter so slowly and the coffee was so black. I also had to be careful to only pour about an ounce of water at a time or it would overflow. I used about 8-10 ounces of water.

It brewed to a dark black color and the taste was very strong like Starbucks coffee. It is definitely not to my liking. Even after I loaded it with cream, I found this almost undrinkable.

I absolutely love this invention but not this coffee. However, for those who like a full flavored, deep and strong brew then this fits the bill. These portable pouches are a traveler's dream.

Note that sometimes the paper wings over the cup come undone and spring back to the coffee tea bag. I just press down on the rim where the wings are hanging over and that seems to help. This does not happen after the water is poured.

Also, this type of pod will not always work with someone who is disabled. My boyfriend with Parkinson's found this too complicated to use.

title: Golden amber brew with a nicely balanced flavor
text:
This is a very nice oolong tea with a balanced flavor profile. I brewed this with 195 degree water for 4 minutes. The brew is golden amber and the flavor is as it should be for an oolong tea; slightly flavorful and balanced.

Overall, I really like this tea. The current price of $15.95 for 20 teabags comes to $.80 cents per cup.

title: Scentless and bland oolong tea that brews to a clear light amber
text: This oolong tea is bland. I brewed this with 190 degree water for 4 minutes. The brew is a clear light amber. The resulting flavor profile is what I would call dishwater bland. There is nothing compelling about this tea and I would never order it again.

title: The peppermint is incredibly fragrant; brews to slightly cloudy yellow; tastes minty as it should
text: The peppermint is incredibly fragrant and it tastes minty when steeped. It brews to slightly cloudy yellow. I used 185 degree water and brewed one teaspoon for 4 minutes. This is a very nice tea and the "mint" is fresh. The current price of $14.99 for 3.52 ounces is competitively priced.

title: Tastes like salt infused with NOTHING
text:
This is a grinder full of 96% salt with 2% each of basil and garlic and some chlorophyll used as a coloring agent. The salt granules are large but grind out small as shown in my photo. I personally taste nothing but salt on my dish. I tried it plain as well and there is no other flavor that comes through.

The current price of $11.99 is expensive for just plain salt. Another thing I do not like is that the cardboard label has to be removed to successfully grind this. Once removed, there is no label on the container saying what this is. I would have preferred a stick on label on front instead.

title: Smells like aspirin w/no bergamot flavor; tastes like plain tea
text:
There are three boxes of 20 earl grey tea bags each in this package. Earl grey is one of my favorite teas. However, the fragrance of this earl grey smells more like St. Joseph's baby aspirin than the bergamot aroma I am used to in the US. This comes from Sri Lanka. Had I known it came from there, I would not have ordered it because I tried earl grey from Sri Lanka before and it had the same taste and smell.

I brewed the tea with boiling water and let it steep for four minutes. It brewed to a deep auburn brown hue of earl grey black tea. The taste of the bergamot is non-existent and tastes almost like regular Lipton tea.

The current price of $20.99 for 60 bags comes to $.35 cents per cup, which is a reasonable price had I really liked this tea--BUT, I don't.

title: Delicious low calorie biscotti that has a crunch like toast
text:
These are surprisingly delicious. It looks like small and long slices of Melba toast and crunches like it too, yet it tastes like pistachio biscotti. I finished the entire container in three days. Note that there are a lot of crumbs on the bottom of the container.

What I do not understand is the serving size. It says the serving is one piece at 25 calories; however, there are both long and short pieces, so I assume it is the short piece that is 25 calories.

The ingredients are egg whites, organic evaporated cane juice, all natural unbleached wheat flour, pistachios, pure vanilla extract, and lemon powder.

In summary, although I love the taste, I would not buy these because the current price of $21.99 is way too expensive. I normally would lower the rating one more star to 3 stars because of this but I liked the way they tasted so much that my rating is for the flavor and crunch only based on how I would have rated this in a blind taste test.

title: These lentils make a delicious creamy but less hearty lentil soup
text:
I used these to make a red lentil with bacon and spinach lentil soup and it came out savory and delicious. The only thing that was different from some other lentils I used previously is that these lentils are very small. As a result, the soup is not as hearty, meaning it does not have the "bite" of larger-sized lentils because they soften easily making the soup more creamy than more hearty.

In summary, if I want a creamier texture to my lentil soup, then this is the perfect buy. If I prefer a soup where the lentils hold up and have more texture, then I use a larger lentil bean. The current price of $12.74 is about double the price of what I can buy lentils for at my local market.

title: Light/sweet vegetable fragrance when dry/all vegetal as a tea; brews a cloudy fluorescent green
text:
This tea looks like finely mowed grass. It has a light and sweet vegetable aroma. I brewed one teaspoon at 185 degrees for 1 minute in 10 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a cloudy fluorescent green and the flavor was all vegetal and not as sweet as I expected. However, I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this. I also love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

Note that the instructions on the package are well-written, which is also a plus. That is not always the case with teas that come from Japan or China.

In summary, I like this tea. At $33 ($35 minus $2) per 3.5 ounce bag, it is not cheap and rightfully so considering the quality.

UPDATE July 31, 2022: I also tried this in their tiny one-package version of gokuzyo aracha loose leaf tea. I brewed the entire package to 8 ounces of 175 degree Fahrenheit water for 45 seconds. This tea has a mildly sweet flavor with vegetal overtones. The brew is a cloudy green with tea sediment in the cub. I really like this tea but at the current price of $4.99 for one serving, that is expensive. The same tea can be brewed about three times before it is tasteless.

title: I could make my own but this tastes good; perfect on hot pretzels
text:
This is just sugar and cinnamon with some additives. The ingredients are sugar, cinnamon, and natural flavor (maltodextrin, organic sunflower oil, natural flavors, organic rice starch).

It does taste very good and is perfect on one of those King Size Super Pretzels that are sold in the big box in the freezer at BJs. I just bake the pretzel and when done, brush on some melted butter, and a little bit of this and it tastes like Auntie Anne's cinnamon sugar pretzels. Of course, I can just mix my own cinnamon and sugar and get the same result without all the additives and the current $9.95 cost for 7 ounces.

These are perfect on pancakes, waffles, french toast, and other savory items. It also turns these items into more of a desert than a healthy breakfast.

title: This almost all "alliums" spice mix is not as flavorful as I expected; onion is a bit crunchy
text:
I tried this plain and with rice and it is not as flavorful as I expected. Also, the onion in the mix is a bit crunchy. Note that the spice does not dispense easily through the section with the holes.

The ingredients are spices (they do not spell out the spices), dehydrated onion, onion powder, shallots, and garlic oil. I wish they spelled out what they mean by spices, which is the first ingredient. Everything else is an allium (shallots, onion, garlic). There are some green bits in the spice but herbs are not listed in the ingredients which I find so weird.

title: I love the hot pepperoni but the aftertaste of the other two, not so much
text:
For the current price of $12.97 ($14.97 minus $2), there are nine pork sticks included in this package which comes to $1.44 each stick. There are three each of hot pepperoni, chorizo, and original.

I tried all three and the hot pepperoni is great; I really love that one. However, the original is too bland and has a strange aftertaste. The chorizo has a nice flavor but the aftertaste is not to my liking either. However, I also gave a set of three to my Postman and he really liked them and rated them 4 stars.

Each stick is a 110 calorie serving with 6g of protein each. There are no nitrates, hormones, or antibiotics added. The original and hot pepperoni each have 365mg of sodium with the chorizo only 290mg. Pork is the first ingredient in each one.

Overall, these are not my favorite meat sticks. Perhaps it is because they are made of pork instead of beef. The hot pepperoni, however, is just wonderful to my palate.

UPDATE 1/23/2022 - I had my boyfriend try these. He liked the original flavor and hated to pepperoni. He hasn't tried the other flavor but so far he rates this 3 stars.

title: Flavorful rub but cumin flavor shines through; sugar is main ingredient
text:
This rub is flavorful. The ingredients are sugar, brown sugar, salt, paprika, garlic, chili powder (chili pepper, cumin, salt, oregano, garlic, contains less than 2%: silicon dioxide), salt, spices (whatever that means), tomato, onion, and rice concentrate. This is flavorful on pork although the cumin flavor shines through more than the barbeque flavoring. Despite sugar being the main ingredient, it does not taste sweet.

The current price of $13.95 is a bit high for this 9.5 ounce container.

title: Flavorful rub but very salty
text:
This rub is flavorful. The ingredients are salt, pepper, garlic, spices (whatever that means), onion, chipotle, paprika, and tomato. This does take beef up a notch but it is a bit salty since salt is the main ingredient. I would have preferred less salt and more of the other spices.

The current price of $14.95 is a competitive online price for this 12 ounce container.

title: Brews to a cloudy fluorescent yellow; tastes vegetal & sweet but not roasted; very nice
text:
This tea looks like finely mowed grass. It has a wonderfully sweet vegetable aroma. I brewed one teaspoon at 185 degrees for 1.5 minutes in 10 ounces of water. The resulting brew was cloudy and was sort of fluorescent yellow and the flavor was sweet and vegetable.

I actually love this tea; however, I did not taste any roasted flavor profile but to me this is so good, I consider this a 5-star tea. I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this. This is grown on a tea farm in Jeju, South Korea. All instructions are in Korean except for brewing instructions. It says to brew at 70C which is 158F but I usually brew green teas at 185, which is what I did.

Since this is expensive tea, I brewed this twice and the second cup was as good as the first. I brewed the second cup at 175 degrees just to try it at a lower temperature. I usually do not brew the same tea leaves more than once but a really good tea, I brew twice. Some people do more.

In summary, I really like this tea. At $25.99 per bag, which is 1.76 ounces, this is pricey but it tastes like quality. Note that it comes in a resealable pouch.

title: Fragrance-free dry but mildly vegetal once brewed; brew a clear amber/yellow
text:
This is a subtle but nice oolong tea with vegetal notes. There are 150 grams (5.291 ounces) in the box. The tea is pearl shaped meaning the leaves are rolled into balls. I could detect no fragrance when I opened the vacuumed sealed container; however, the flavor comes out if the brew.

There are no brewing instructions on the tin; therefore, I brewed this the way I usually brew oolong teas. I brewed one teaspoon of tea with 195 degree water for 4 minutes. The tea expanded fully in the infuser--see photo. The brew is a clear golden amber/yellow. The resulting flavor profile is subtly vegetal.

According to the Amazon site, the same one teaspoon of tea can be brewed 5-7 times. I almost never brew my tea more than once. I brewed this twice but the flavor profile disintegrates after that.

Note that the label on the tea recommends storing at room temperature in the refrigerator and to keep it dry. Obviously that is ridiculous advice as it needs to be one or the other. I store it at a cool room temperture as I do all my teas.

Overall, I really like this tea. The current price of $27.89 comes to $5.27 per ounce which is expensive but on par with the pricing of higher quality teas.

title: Highly aromatic decaf beans; resulting brew is smooth and light
text: There are three cups of whole bean decaf coffee in this package that is highly aromatic. I absolutely love the fragrance of the beans. After grinding, I brewed a small amount and the resulting brew was smooth and light, just the way I like it. To me this is very good coffee. The current price of $18 is pricey for this, but then again, this tastes better than most decaf coffees I tried previously.

title: Highly fragrant; I used to make a star anise tisane (a.k.a. tea)
text:
I broke these apart but could not find the seeds. I was going to use the seeds for eating but instead decided to make a tisane. I boiled about 8 pods in 2 cups of water for 10 minutes, strained, and drank the liquid. It is very good.

These pods are fresh and fragrant.

title: Mild mustard with a sweet taste
text: I expected this mustard to be hot but it's not. It has a mild flavor and is sweet to me. My boyfriend likes it but I just think it is okay. My favorite mustard is actually the Batampte Deli Mustard; however, if I want something more savory, then this fits the bill.

title: Tastes exactly like chocolate milk; my boyfriend said it tasted better; some cartons leaked
text:
For the current price of $19.99, there are 12 8-fluid ounce containers of NotMilk plant-based chocolate milk in this package, which comes to $1.67 per container.

Each 8-fluid ounce container is one serving of 130 calories with 5g total fat, 180mg sodium, 16g carbohydrates of which 13g is added sugars. Each serving also contains 25% DV of Vit D & Calcium, 10% DV of Iron, 4% DV of Potassium, and 35% DV of Vitamin B12.

Both my boyfriend and I tried this and it looks and tastes exactly like chocolate milk. My boyfriend even said it tastes better.

Note that some of the containers were stained with the product which meant there was some leakage in shipment.

Since this is plant based this is vegan and it is shelf stable too. All that has to be done is to shake it and drink.

BTW, please do not consider this drink healthy as the second ingredient is sugar.

title: Not much product for price; dissolves instantaneously
text:
There are three vials of powdered saffron in this set. I personally do not like that the vials are not marked because once they are separated from the package, you cannot tell what they are unless one smells them.

The powder dissolves immediately in food. I used this in my rice and one third of the container was gone in a small amount of rice. To me this only contains nine servings (about three per vial).

This is best for those who want to add this to food at the last minute. There is no need to soak the Saffron in hot water for 10 minutes before adding it to food as is usually done with the strands.

This saffron is fresh and has the same taste and smell as the strands I use. I do wish the vials were labeled in case the packaging is lost.

title: These are an acquired taste; healthy but more like medicine to me & wasabi is too intense
text:
Salmon skins have more Omega 3's than the flesh of the salmon, so the skin is very healthy. I also love wild caught salmon. In fact it is all I purchase, mostly from Vital Choice. Therefore, when I saw these, I was eager to try them.

The current cost of $23.75 ($25 minus 5%) comes to $2.97 per bag which is pricey. There are about seven salmon skins per bag--see photo attached of one salmon skin next to the bag. I was only able to eat two skins out of the bag in one sitting. I love the crunch but the wasabi is too intense for me and I do not taste any ginger. Also, there is a fishy aftertaste. To me this is more like forcing myself to eat my vitamins rather than enjoying a snack. I know the skins are good for me, especially since these are from wild caught salmon, but the aftertaste and intensity of the spice is just too much for my palate.

Each bag is one 80 calorie serving with 4g of total fat, 105mg of sodium, 50mg of cholesterol, and 10g of protein.

The ingredients are wild salmon skin, organic & sustainable palm oil, allulose, salt, natural flavors, horseradish powder, creamer powder (medium chain triglycerides acacia gum, spices, and citric acid. Note that it is manufactured in a facility that also processes milk, egg, peanuts, soy, shrimp, almond, cashew, walnut, pecan, and coconut.

UPDATE: I had my Postman try a bag and he said these were okay but it has a fishy aftertaste. He gives these 3 stars.

UPDATE: I also had my boyfriend try these and he did not like them at all. He said the wasabi was good but he can't get past the idea of eating fish skins. He gives these 1 star.

title: Nice assortment of 3 green teas from China
text:
This box contains 30 teabags with 10 of each of 3 full leaf green tea varieties from China. There are 10 of FEZ (grown in the Pacific Northwest), 10 of Jasmine Silver Tip (grown in Fujian Province), and Mao Feng Shui (grown in Zhejiang Province).

These teas are flavorful. I particularly like the Jasmine Silver Tip (see photo). It brews to a clear golden amber. I brewed it with 185 degree water for 4 minutes.

The current price of $29.99 comes to $1 per cup. This is a nice gift set for a green tea lover.

title: Each pkg is equivalent to one cup of flavorful garlic or pork broth for a ramen soup base—no ENGLISH
text:
When I ordered this, I actually thought I was getting Japanese noodles and not soup base. Nevertheless, this soup base is excellent. The only problem is that I would not be able to tell what to do with it without reading the Amazon instructions or looking at the two photos that explain what is inside.

The white packets are the Tonkotsu Shoyu Base Shio broth which most likely has salt, with soy sauce and miso paste, and is made with pork hock (first joint of the pig's leg) and trotters (pig's feet). Both trotters and hocks are full of flavor and gelatine. I say most likely because I had to do a search on the Internet to find out what the typical ingredients were.

The red packets are the Kumamoto Garlic Base which most likely is made of miso or a broth and garlic or perhaps black garlic. Once again, I did an Internet search on what should be in this type of soup base.

I attached a photo of the garlic base and the end result. The Amazon instructions say to use 270ml (9.13 fluid ounces of boiling water) with the base. I used a little more liquid because I am not used to salt. I actually boiled it with the ramen and the broccoli. The soup was very flavorful even though I watered down the base.

There are a total of 10 packets in this set; 5 pork and 5 galic. At the current price of $13 ($14 minus $1), it comes to $1.30 per cup. Overall, this is excellent but it needs to have some information in English on the package, which it does not. I was almost ready to throw this out without trying it because I thought I was receiving noodles and not base and I had no clue what the ingredients were until I went back on the Amazon site. Had English instructions and ingredients been provided, this would have received 5 stars.

title: I love the chewy texture but soup base is flavorless & this is insanely pricey
text:
There are six 2.43 ounce individually wrapped packages of Ramen in this box each with its own spice packet. For the current price of $39, this comes to $6.50 per package of single-serve noodles.

Per the instructions, I boiled two cups of water and cooked the ramen for 7 minutes. It said to reserve one cup of cooking liquid but the water is reduced to one cup after all that boiling. I put this in a bowl and then added the full packet of seasoning. It was flavorless to me. I tasted no garlic chicken flavor or salt or anything at all. I did love the chewy texture of the ramen, however. I think it's best if I throw away the spice packet and use the ramen for my own soup base.

Each serving is 310 calories with 22 grams of protein and 880 mg of sodium. The ingredients are Pumpkin Seed Protein, Wheat Gluten, Food Starch Modified, Sustainable Palm Oil, Wheat, Contains less than 2% of the following: Yeast Extract, Soybean Powder, Black Garlic Powder, Garlic Powder, Sea Salt, Natural Flavors, Onion Powder, Coconut Milk Powder, Salt, Sesame Oil, Spices, Turmeric (For Color), Safflower Oil, Sunflower Oil, Gum Acacia, Tricalcium Phosphate, Potassium Chloride, Calcium Carbonate,
Maltodextrin, and Tapioca Maltodextrin.

Contains: Wheat, Soy, Tree Nub (Coconut), Sesame.

After having tried this, I would not order this again.

title: Just like steeping tea but coffee is very weak even after a 5-minute steep - 2.5 stars
text:
There are 5 individually wrapped coffee tea bags in the bag good enough for 5 cups of coffee. I just put 8 ounces of boiling water into a cup and let it steep for 5 minutes. The directions do not say how long but mention to cover and steep for a few minutes if stronger coffee is desired.

The brew looks full bodied but the flavor is weak. I tried it both without half and half and with and was not impressed as it is too weak.

The current price of $9.95 ($12.95 minus $3) for 5 cups of coffee is insanely expensive as it comes to $1.99 per cup.

UPDATE Jan 23, 2022 - I just had my boyfriend try this and he is very disappointed. He loves mild coffee but said this was too weak.

title: Two lightly flavored teas, one tisane, & one narrow infuser
text:
This gift set comes with two teas, one tisane, and one narrow infuser. Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen.

- Lemon Green Tea
- Blood Orange Black Tea
- Red Berry Delight (this is the tisane)

I found the teas and tisane to be almost tasteless. Also, the infuser twists open and there is no room for expansion in the chamber. In addition, sediment escapes (see photo). To me this set is just okay and not very flavorful. I also do not like the infuser.

After having tried this, this is not a set I would order again.

title: Light vegetable fragrance when dry/light vegetable overtones as a tea; brews a cloudy yellow
text:
This tea looks like finely mowed grass. It has a light vegetable aroma. I brewed one teaspoon at 185 degrees for 1 minute in 10 ounces of water. The resulting brew was cloudy and was sort of fluorescent yellow and the flavor was light and vegetable and a little bit bland. However, I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this. I also love that this comes from Japan instead of China even though it does not say so on the package. It's just that Fukamushi Sencha Shizuoka is grown in Japan.

This can be brewed at least twice. I usually do not brew the same tea leaves more than once but a really good tea, I brew twice. Some people do more.

Note that the instructions on the package are well-written, which is also a plus. That is not always the case with teas that come from Japan or China.

In summary, I like this tea. At $18.99 per bag, it is not cheap and rightfully so considering the quality. I would have liked this to come in a resealable pouch, instead I have to use a clip to keep the package closed.

title: Saffron smells fresh and tastes pretty good
text:
I used this in my rice. First, I soaked about a teaspoon of Saffron in hot water for 10 minutes and then I added it to the rice before cooking. The rice came out great and the taste of the saffron was mild but still flavorful.

I do wish this came in a dark colored container to protect the Saffron from light.

title: Sweet smelling vegetal tea; brews to a light cloudy green
text:
This tea almost looks like mowed grass mixed with matcha powder. It has a strong but sweet vegetable aroma. I brewed 1.5 teaspoons at 185 degrees for 1 minute in 10 ounces of water. The resulting brew was cloudy, had some sediment, and was sort of fluorescent green. The flavor is deliciously sweet and vegetal - almost like drinking sweet spinach water. This tea really tastes excellent to me.

I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this with a hint of dessert (the sweetness) blended in. I also love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

Note that the instructions in the package are well-written, which is also a plus. That is not always the case with teas that come from other countries.

What is interesting about this tea is that for the hot version, it says to strain the tea. However, for brewing the cold version it says to dissolve the tea in the water. This is another reason that I feel it is very "matcha-like."

In summary, I really love this tea. At $65 per 3.5 ounces, this is expensive tea but it is a cut above most of the other greens teas I tried previously. A very good green tea is typically expensive and I can taste the difference with this one. It is absolutely excellent to my palate!

title: Extremely light bergamot flavor; an okay Earl Grey
text:
There are 25 earl grey tea bags in this package. Earl grey is one of my favorite teas. However, the fragrance of this earl grey smells more like St. Joseph's baby aspirin than the bergamot aroma I am used to in the US. This comes from Sri Lanka.

I brewed the tea with boiling water and let it steep for four minutes. It brewed to the typical auburn brown hue of earl grey black tea. The taste of the bergamot is very light; I consider this an okay brew but it is not my favorite flavor of earl grey.

The current price of $7.99 for 25 bags comes to $.32 cents per cup, which is a reasonable price

title: Scrumptious mango “potato chips;” not totally healthy but oh so tasty; they are pricey
text:
For the current price of $42, there are 12 individually packaged 1.41 ounce bags of crunchy mango fruit chips, which comes to $3.50 per bag.  The ingredients are mango, rice bran oil, and sea salt. Each bag is one serving at 170 calories with 4.5 grams of fat, 0mg cholesterol, 240mg sodium, 33g dietary fiber, 1g protein, 0.4mg iron, 224mg potassium, and 16mg Vitamin C.

To me these crunch and have the mouthfeel of potato chips and they taste great with the sweetness of the mango. I personally love the taste of these. I do not feel these are healthy with the rice bran oil and sodium, but they are very good. Unfortunately, these are too expensive even if the mango is "all fried up." I took off 1 star for the exorbitant price. If it were not for the price, I would have rated this 5 stars.

UPDATE: I had both my Postman and my boyfriend tried these and they both say these chips are delicous. They absolutely love them but they are astounded at the high price.

title: Decent coffee for cappuccino/espresso but it kept clogging my grinder
text:
The beans are oily and smell fresh. I spent several hours grinding this into a fine grind because it kept clogging my burr grinder. I never had coffee clog my grinder before and I have grounded 5 pounds of coffee several times. This is only 2.2 pounds. Nevertheless, once everything was ground, I made myself a cappuccino. The resulting brew was good, although not as strong as other espresso I tried previously.

Overall, I like this coffee but still prefer lavazza blue caffe espresso much better.

title: Delicious, fresh, moist, dense, heavy, and calorie laden fruitcake I love
text:
I've tried over eight different fruit cakes since I was a child yet I always keep coming back to Jane Parker, the old A&P brand. I absolutely love their fruit cake and am glad it is now being reproduced from their recipe. This cake is delicious, fresh, moist, dense, heavy, and calorie laden. It is also sweet, so a little is very satisfying but also leaves me wanting more.

I love this classic light fruit cake but $79.97 is expensive. Every few years I buy myself one as a treat and this year it was time and I am not disappointed. The tin it comes in is also wonderful and holiday festive.

title: Surprisingly delicious extra virgin olive oil in “a shampoo bottle” - product of Turkey - 4.5 stars
text:
Most olive oil is adulterated with canola and other oils even though they are not labeled as such. Over the past 30 years I have tried over 30 different olive oil brands and have been disappointed by most. The only brand I ever buy is Paesano olive oil from Sicily. However, this organic extra virgin olive oil is a close second.

It is surprisingly delicious and has peppery overtones as a good olive oil should. It is a product of Turkey, is organic, cold pressed, and extra virgin. I can only assume this one is not watered down with other oils as it tastes authentic. However, one never really knows unless the olive oil is tested.

Nevertheless, I really love this oil. It comes in a 16.9 fluid ounce glass bottle that looks like a shampoo bottle. In addition, they threw in a free sample of three .34 fluid ounce single serve packets. This oil is in second place next to my favorite, which is still Paesano. The current price of $23.39 ($25.99 minus 10%) comes to $1.38 per ounce.

title: Versatile selection of tea and tisane blends to please most palates
text:
There are 16 Different Blends of tea handcrafted in Boulder Colorado in pyramid sachets and they are:

- Japanese Orchid (whole leaf green tea) - ingredients high-grade Japanese green tea 

- Peppermint (organic herbal tea) - ingredients 100% organic peppermint leaves

- Morning Moja (organic pu'erh tea) - ingredients organic black tea, organic pu'erh tea, organic orange peel, natural vanilla flavor

- Tumeric Tonic (organic triple root chai) - ingredients organic ginger root, organic cinnamon bar, organic fennel, organic peppercorns, organic sarsaparilla root, natural flavors, organic turmeric, organic nettle leaf

- Decaf Lemon Ginger (organic green tea) - ingredients organic decaffeinated green tea, organic ginger, organic lemongrass, organic rose hips, organic lemon peel, organic lemon verbena, natural lemon flavor

- Hibiscus Cucumber (organic herbal tea) - ingredients organic hibiscus flower, organic licorice root, organic lemongrass, organic sage, organic rose hips, natural cucumber flavoring,  natural lime flavoring, organic fennel

- Wild Harvest Pu'erh (whole leaf green pu'erh) - ingredients high-mountain green pu'erh

- Blood orange (whole leaf herbal tea) - ingredients red rooibos, orange peel,  hibiscus, rosehips, rose petals, safflowers, natural orange and vanilla flavors

- Pink Rose Lemonade (whole leaf herbal tea) - ingredients organic lemongrass, orange peel, rose petals, lemon balm, spearmint, organic lemon verbena, chamomile, lavender, natural lemon flavor

- Meditative Mind (organic white tea) -  ingredients organic white tea, organic jasmine pearls, organic rose petals

- Earl of Grey (whole leaf black tea) -  ingredients black tea, citrus rind, licorice root, mallow flowers, bergamot, citrus and vanilla flavoring

- Sweet Magnolia (whole leaf green tea) - ingredients magnolia scented green tea, jasmine flowers

- 8 Immortals (whole leaf oolong tea) -  ingredients high-mountain dancong oolong

- English Breakfast (organic black tea) - ingredients 100% organic black tea

- Bolder Breakfast (whole leaf pu'erh tea) - ingredients black tea, pu'erh tea, calendula flowers, sunflower petals, dark chocolate flavoring

- Clouds and Mist (organic green tea) - ingredients 100% organic green tea

I tried the Japanese Orchid. It is not whole leaf but may have started out as such. The tea is pulverized in the bag which makes the brew cloudy. I brewed it at 175 degrees Fahrenheit for three minutes. The resulting brew was cloudy fluorescent yellow  and the taste is vegetal as it should be.

I also tried the Earl of Grey. I brewed it at 212 degrees Fahrenheit for four minutes. The resulting brew was a vibrant caramel and the taste was devine--Earl Grey all the way with a hint of citrus.

I love the variety in this package as there is a combination of tea and tisanes (herbal ingredients only) to suit most palates. The current price of $19.99 is an expensive $1.25 per cup.

title: Roasted Camelina Seeds is 1st ingredient, then sugar; delicious
text:
I never heard of camelina seeds; apparently they are like flax seeds. This is the first ingredient followed by sugar and then quinoa and cranberries. I really like the taste of these. Each serving (one package) is 230 calories with 50mg of potassium, 40mg of calcium, and 3mg of iron. There is also 8g of protein, 5g of fiber and 10g of sugar.

These tiny squares actually look like bird suet to me. Nevertheless, I really like them but I do not like all the sugar.

The current price of $19.95 for nine 1.66 ounce packages is an expensive $2.22 per package. I would never buy these because of the high price and the sugar. However, they are delicious to me and are vegan. I know that others do not care for the taste but I do.

title: Tasty but full of sugar & citron only; contains pits
text:
This is delicious. I used two tablespoons to one glass of cold water. It does not dissolve well at all but the resulting drink was delicious. I did not finish the entire glass because it has sugar in it and I do not drink drinks loaded with sugar. I am going to pass this on to a friend who loves smoothies as it would be better in that type of drink. With a smoothie the ingredients are pulverized and this has a lot of rind and some pits in it that requires that.

Although I find this very tasty, the first ingredient is sugar, which is a negative in my book. Had I known that, I would not have ordered this. Each tablespoon is 45 calories (15mg) and two to three tablespoons are needed to make a drink or smoothie. It can also be made with hot water. The ingredients are citroned sugar (citron, sugar), fructose, oligosaccharide, water, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, citric acid, carrageenan, and Vitamin C.

In summary, there is a lot of rind in this and just a few slivered pits as can be seen through the jar in the first photo. It does make a wonderful and tasty drink but the sugar and the pits are not for me, so I'll pass.

title: Really nice fruity flavored green tea
text:
There are 12individually wrapped pyramid shaped tea bags in this set.I brewed one tea bag in 8 ounces of 185 degree Fahrenheit water for 4 minutes. The resulting brew is light rosy amber and the flavor is balanced and fruity.

The ingredients are all organic except for the natural flavors. The ingredients are guayusa, rosehips, hibiscus, jasmine green tea, green rooibos, apple bits, raspberry bits, and natural flavors.

At the current price of  $16.80 ($21 minus 20%), this comes to an expensive $1.40 per cup.

title: Nice vegetable tea; brews light cloudy fluorescent yellow
text:
This tea almost looks like blades of grass that have been cut short. It has a strong vegetable aroma to me. I brewed two teaspoons at 175 degrees for 2 minutes (bag says 90 seconds at 167) in 10 ounces of water. The resulting brew was cloudy, had some sediment, and was sort of fluorescent yellow and the flavor was light and vegetable with sweet overtones - almost like drinking spinach water. This tea really tastes good to me.

I actually feel like I am drinking something very healthy when drinking this. I also love that this comes from Japan instead of China.

Note that the instructions on the package are well-written, which is also a plus. That is not always the case with teas that come from Japan or China.

In summary, I really like this tea. At $17.50 per3.5 ounce bag, it is competitively priced.

title: Takes pork up a notch; salt is first ingredient
text: This would have received 5 stars if it were not so salty. The ingredients are salt, sugar, spices, granulated onion, and granulated garlic. I used this on my pork loin and let it marinate for about 1.5 hours and then baked it. It came out delicious but the predominant flavor was salt. I have more pork marinating 24 hours and will cook that nice. This spice rub is delicious but salty.

title: These are healthy, low in calories, slightly salty, & have a wonderful bite; VEGAN by default
text:
I never heard of sea grapes before. This is a one-serving package of 100 grams (3.5 ounces). It is only 18.5 calories for the entire package and has small amounts of Vitamin A and protein and fiber.

Per the package directions, I soaked these in water for 5-7 minutes. Since I do not like a salty taste, I rinsed them several times to reduce the sodium content. These "grapes" expand and are ready to eat after that. They are attached on the stems and the entire stem with grapes (the size of caviar) can be eaten.

They are practically tasteless except for a slight saltiness; however, I love the mouth feel. These pop in my mouth when eaten. This would be perfect in a salad.

These are expensive at $10.39 ($12.99 minus 20%) per serving but that serving can be eaten over several days if stored in the refrigerator. This is a product of Vietnam and by default, these are vegan.

title: Fresh, clean, deep burgundy red brew; good tisane but not for me
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, this tisane is strong, like an intense punch with a slight tartness.

Per the instructions, I brewed 1.5 teaspoons with 208 degree Fahrenheit water and steeped between five to seven minutes. The only ingredient is cut and sifted hibiscus flowers. The flowers have an interesting aroma that it probably can be used as potpourri as well.

This is a large 12-ounce package. The current price of $15.61 comes to $1.30 per ounce. Note that drinking hibiscus tea in moderation is generally considered safe. Fortunately for me, this is not a hit for my taste buds.

title: Utterly delicious and decadent chocolate; it is hard not to eat all three packages at once
text:
These are so decadent that I do not know which ones to eat first. I tried all of them and they are so delicious that I cannot pick a favorite. In this package are three 4.25 ounce boxes of chocolate.

- The milk chocolate cherries are 140 calories per serving. A serving size is approximately four pieces and there are about four servings per pack.

- The milk chocolate sea salt almonds are 160 calories per serving. A serving size is approximately seven pieces and there are about four servings per pack.

- The dark chocolate sea salt caramels are 130 calories per serving. A serving size is approximately eight pieces and there are about four servings per pack.

I have no idea how much this very small pack of three chocolates will cost but I bet it will be expensive. This chocolate is smooth and the combinations are flavorful. They are utterly superb, a rich and mouth watering delight, and some of the best chocolate I have ever had the pleasure of tasting!

title: Average quality Jasmine tea; lightly fragrant & light tasting; brews to a clear amber
text:
This is a five ounce tin of Jasmine pearls. Upon opening the tin, there was only a faint aroma of Jasmine. I brewed 1.5 teaspoons to about 10 ounces of 185 degree Fahrenheit water for 4 minutes. The tin says to do 175 for 3 minutes but I usually brew green tea hotter and for 1 minute more. This tea has a faint jasmine flavor to it and is a clear amber brew. It is not the most flavorful Jasmine tea I ever tried.

The current price of $22.95 is pricey but typical of Jasmine pearl tea. This is from Fujian, China and only contains the ingredients of green tea and Jasmine flowers.

title: Small blooms but tasty tea; smells and tastes like fruit
text:
Any drink that is made from herbal ingredients is a tisane and not a tea. Tea only comes from two main varieties of the camellia sinensis evergreen. Nevertheless, these flowering bulbs are actually white tea with other "tisane" ingredients and the resulting brew is fruity tasting and has a wonderful aroma.

Per the instructions, I brewed one ball with 16 ounces of 195 degree Fahrenheit water and steeped for three to five minutes. The only ingredients are white tea, amaranth flowers, jasmine flowers, and natural flavors. By natural flavors I assume they mean the blueberry and white peach essence.

There are 10 bulbs in this package and they only lightly flower with a few leaves that fall off. The current price of $39, this comes to $3.90 per 16 ounce pot of tea. I've tried several flowering teas before and never liked any of them. Most are beautiful but tasteless. This one is subtly pretty but very flavorful, which is wonderful.

title: True cinnamon; has a lovely mild & aromatic flavor in hot drinks like tea; great in rice pudding
text:
I heard about Ceylon Cinnamon but never tried it before. My first impression was disappointing because there was almost no cinnamon aroma when I sniffed the sticks. However, its aroma is released when used in cooking or with heat. I used this in a cup of tea made with boiling water and the resulting brew was absolutely delicious. It can be used to make hot apple cider or rice pudding or other desserts that call for cinnamon. Since I have never used this type before, I have nothing to compare it to. Nevertheless, I love it. It is also safer to use in large quantities than the three other types.

The below information is based on my research and is as accurate as possible from the Internet information I found. I am by no means a cinnamon expert, although I am very well aware that Ceylon (this one) is considered true cinnamon and is safer to consume than the rest.

Cinnamon is a spice created from the inner bark of the Cinnamomum tree. There are four different types of those trees that vary by region and they are:

- Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon Cinnamon) - That is this one. This is considered true cinnamon and has a milder aroma and flavor than what most people are used to. It is tan-brown in color and contains many tight soft layers. It is large and thick. This cinnamon is not known to be toxic in large quantities like the typical cinnamon on the market. Ceylon cinnamon is also less common and is prized as a cooking spice. It is expensive compared to the more common variety.

- Cinnamomum cassia (a.k.a. Cinnamomum aromaticum ) (Saigon Cinnamon) - is the typical small, thinly rolled, dark brown, and hard cinnamon sticks that most people are familiar with and which is relatively cheap compared to Ceylon. This is also good in desserts. However, this contains a toxin (i.e.Coumarin) that is harmful if too much is consumed. It can cause kidney, liver, and lung damage and may even cause cancer. How much is too much is unknown to me. Note that Ceylon Cinnamon also contains Coumarin too but based on my Internet research, it contains 250 times less than this one.

- Cinnamomum burmannii (Korintje Cinnamon) - This is also known as Indonesian cinnamon, Padang cassia, Batavia cassia and has higher amounts of coumarin than the Saigon Cinnamon above. It is apparently the most common and cheapest type of cinnamon in the US sold in powdered form.

- Cinnamomum loureiroi (Royal Cinnamon) - This is an heirloom variety that is very rarely exported from the mountains around Vietnam.

In summary, these Ceylon sticks are excellent. Their fragrance is released with heat and it spiced up my tea so well that no sugar was needed. In fact, I have been using the same stick for days. I just lay it out to dry and use it the next day with another cup of tea. I can't wait to try several new sticks with rice pudding. At the current price of $6.99, it comes to $1 per stick as there were 7 sticks in my package.

UPDATE - I finally made rice pudding with three of these Ceylon sticks. It came out great. The only issue I encountered is that pieces of the sticks break off and have to be fished out of the pudding as can be seen in my photo. I do not have that issue with the cassia variety of sticks. After using this as flavoring in my pudding, the sticks are soft and I throw them out. When using a stick for tea, I can use it repeatedly about five times but I cannot do that when cooking with rice pudding.

title: Light vegetable fragrance w/vegetable overtones as a tea; brews light cloudy fluorescent yellow
text:
This tea almost looks like blades of grass that have been cut short. It has a very light vegetable aroma to me. I brewed one teaspoon at 185 degrees for 3 minutes in 10 ounces of water. The resulting brew was a light fluorescent yellow and the flavor was very faintly vegetable with lightly sweet overtones.
