title: Extremely fragrant and fruity with a subtle taste when ground; I love it
text:
I never heard of long pepper before. This package of whole dried long pepper spikes is very fragrant and smells fruity. I decided to grate it and put it in soup. It has a subtle flavor. It is nothing like the bold, spicy taste of regular black pepper most people are familiar with.

Based on my research this can be used in cooking the same way one would use a bay leaf. Another option is to break up the stalks into pieces and grind it in a coffee grinder to be used similar to the way regular pepper would be used. Others say these can be steeped with tea.

After having tried this, I love them. The current price of $4.76 for 3.5 ounces seems very reasonable for this; however, I am unfamiliar with the price range of this spice.

title: Very fresh; great taste; good compliment to fish dishes
text:
I noticed Amazon is combining reviews for various spices. Note that this review is for Pride of India's turmeric powder.

I use turmeric periodically on my fish dishes. This powder is very fresh and has a great taste. I am very pleased with the quality. The current price is $10.99 for around one pound give or take a few ounces. The reason I say this is because the Amazon site says this is 22 ounces. The container says 1.25 lbs (20 ounces). I am going to assume it's 20 ounces which comes to $.55 cents per ounce.

title: Excellent and flavorful peppermint leaf which makes good peppermint tea with BUGS
text:
There are two 4 ounce bags of cut peppermint leaf in this package. I steeped one teaspoon of leaf in boiling water for five minutes and it came out great. It brews to a golden hue and tastes very fresh. Both my boyfriend and I love this. 

The current price of $8.95 for two bags is reasonable for this as it comes to $4.48 per bag.

UPDATE I went to use this tea again and there were bugs in the sealed package. It went right in the garbage and this review went from 5 to 1 star.

title: Delicate lavender flavor with a beautiful amber hue
text:
I ordered the Lavender Bloom Black Tea. It is loose leaf and packaged inside another black foil bag. Also, included are one-serving samples for three other teas.

This tea brews to a beautiful amber hue. The flavor is delicate and smooth but definitely predominantly lavender. Note that I brewed it in boiling water for four minutes. Both my boyfriend and I feel this tea is very good. I told him I was giving it 5 stars because I loved it and he said, "is there 6 stars." In fact, I tried their [[ASIN:B07Q4D8MKR Darjeeling Summer Glory Black Tea]] and loved it and have been drinking it almost daily since I got that one.  

The ingredients are black tea, lavender, and rose petals. Usually tea is zero calories or close to that but it says each cup is 7.11 calories. I wonder how they determined it is this many calories per cup.

The current price of $13.99  is reasonable for 3.5 ounces of loose leaf tea which supposedly makes up to 40 cups.

title: Not my favorite meat stick; wish it were smaller and more flavorful
text:
There are supposed to be 12 meat sticks in this box but I received 13--that is to my benefit. Unfortunately, these are not my favorite. First of all, I feel these are too big and fat. I would prefer a stick that is half the size as a snack. Second, it is not as flavorful as I expected. They are just okay.

Each stick is one serving, so there are 12 servings in the package. Each serving is 170 calories with 60 from fat, 2g saturated fat, 45 mg cholesterol, 870mg sodium, 2g carbohydrate, and 24g protein.

INGREDIENTS: PORK, CONTAINS 2% OR LESS OF SEA SALT, SPICES, VINEGAR, SUGAR, VEGETABLE POWDER (CELERY POWDER, SEA SALT), WATER, NATURAL FLAVORINGS, LACTIC ACID STARTER CULTURE. STUFFED IN BEEF COLLAGEN CASING. 

The current price of $34.99 comes to almost $3 (specifically $2.92) per stick which is competitively priced. After having tried these, I would not order them again. Also note that a scissor is needed to open the wrapping covering the stick.

UPDATE - I finally got my postman to try these and he thought they were very good and rates them 4 stars. He did say, however, that they look like cigars and it was sausage-sized.

title: Good meat snack and the jalapeno is not too spicy; only 100 calories per 1-ounce stick
text:
I was not sure if I would like these because they are jalapeno and I do not like spicy foods. Nevertheless, these are not too spicy at all. Each stick is one serving at 100 calories each with 8g of total fat (3 g / 15% is saturated fat), 240mg of sodium, 4g of protein, etc.

There are no nitrates or nitrites added except for what naturally occurs in sea salt and celery juice powder. The ingredients are 100% pasture-raised pork, water, sea salt, organic dextrose from tapioca (used to initiate fermentation), celery juice powder, organic jalapeno powder, organic jalapeno pieces, organic blanck pepper, lactic acid starter culture, and stuffed in a dye-free collagen casing.

There are 12 sticks in this package. At the current price of $22.39 ($27.99 minus 20%) comes to $1.87 per stick.

In summary, I like these and find them tasty and satisfying. I love that this comes from pasture-raised pork. I do feel the ingredients are good considering all the junk on the market today.

title: Delicious - 10 stars for taste; 2 stars for the health benefits
text:
Fava beans are broad beans and these are fava beans smothered in a delicious combination of spices and oils. Therefore even though the beans are healthy, the addition of the palm oil, sodium, and other stuff makes this an unhealthy snack, in my opinion. Nevertheless, it is much better than candy and the taste is wonderful. My boyfriend and I could not stop eating these because they were so good.

There are six bags in three flavors (spicey, seaweed, and crab). Each bag is 71g (or 2.5 ounces) each. The sodium per bag ranges from 170 to 360 mg depending on the flavor. The second ingredient in all of them is palm oil which leaves a greasy residue on our fingers.

The current price of $11.39 comes to $1.90 per bag.

title: These are good eaten raw as a breath freshener
text:
Years ago I attended a party that my friend gave and she and everyone there except for me was Asian Indian. They served fennel seeds at the end of the meal and it was the first time I ate the seeds. I thought they were delicious. These seeds taste the same. They used them as a breath freshener and this is what I use them for. I do not use this for cooking as I have no idea how to cook with these. Nevertheless, these are very good raw.

The current price of $6.29 ($6.99 minus 10%) is reasonable for this.

title: Smooth flavor with a beautiful amber hue
text:
I ordered the Darjeeling Summer Glory Black Tea. It is loose leaf and packaged inside another black foil bag. Also, included are samples for several other teas.

This tea brews to a beautiful amber hue. The flavor is subtle and smooth. Note that I brewed it in boiling water for four minutes. Both my boyfriend and I feel this tea is very good.

What I do not like is that the ingredients are not fully listed on the bag. It says Darjeeling Black Tea Blend but I want to know what's in the blend, especially when it says each cup is 7.11 calories. Tea is usually zero calories. This is the only reason I rated this 4 stars as it would have received 5 stars from me otherwise. We have been drinking this since we got it and love the tea.

The current price of $12.59 ($13.99 minus 10%) is reasonable for 3.5 ounces of loose leaf tea which supposedly makes up to 40 cups.

title: Smells divine and brews to the color of red wine; tastes great too but BUYER BEWARE
text:
BUYER BEWARE - Note that I was supposed to receive a six-pack and only received one box. I can only assume that it is because I am a reviewer even though the order said six boxes. Usually when reviewers receive one of an item, it is stated on the order and this was not. Therefore, my rating of 4 stars is for the tea itself. I am going to assume purchasers will get all six boxes.

There are 16 tea bags in one box. I brewed this as per the directions which was to steep a tea bag in 8 ounces of boiling water for 10 minutes and cover. Normally, I steep my teas for 4 minutes only but I made an exception in this case. The tea smells great and it tastes great too. Since there is spearmint included in this blend, it has minty overtones. It also brews to the color of a fine red wine.

The ingredients are darjeeling black tea, rose hips, and spearmint. There may be other herbs in this mixture but it does not say on the box. It only says "our herbalists blended this tea with cooling and supportive herbs...."

Had I received all six boxes, this comes to $4.17 per box at the current price of $25.50. This means each cup of tea costs $.26 cents ($4.17 divided by 16 tea bags per box), which I consider reasonable.

title: Smooth, light coffee that has a great taste to me; hazelnut is barely detectable
text:
I really like this coffee. It is mild and tastes like a medium coffee with a smooth finish. It is made from 100% Arabica coffee beans with natural and artificial flavoring. I barely detect the hazelnut, however, but I still like this.

At the current price of $16.99 this comes to an expensive $.77 cents per k-cup.

title: This is a tisane not a tea; the taste is subtle but nice
text:
This is actually a tisane and not a tea because there are no leaves from the evergreen bush known as camellia sinensis which is where all tea comes from (at least from a tea purist perspective). A tisane is made from herbs and other stuff.

The all organic ingredients this contains are hawthorn fruit, ginger root, orange peel, peppermint, fennel seeds, coriander seeds, and momordica fruit. There is a warning on the package that says:

"WARNING: Recommended for adults only. Consult with your physician prior to use if you are pregnant, nursing or taking medications."

I am not sure what that is because all the ingredients are pretty standard except for the hawthorn and momordica fruit (the first and last ingredients). Nevertheless, these are supposed to be good too.

I steeped one teaspoon of this tea for about 4 minutes in boiling water. The resulting brew was a slightly cloudy golden amber color. The taste is subtle with the predominant flavor of fennel and coriander. Overall, the brew is pleasant.

The current price of $13.49 ($14.99 minus 10%) is reasonable for this 4 ounce package.

title: I like this tisane but my boyfriend does not; it has a great licorice flavor
text:
I like this loose leaf tisane; it is not a tea because it does not have leaves from the camellia sinensis evergreen plant. Nevertheless, it is very good. It is made with all organic ingredients of ginger root (24.9%), licorice root (15.3%), rooibos leaf, orange peel, astragalus root (9.8%), lemon-grass leaf, anise seed, fennel seed, cardamom, and star anise.

I made this with boiling water and steeped it for almost 15 minutes as the instructions say. The predominant flavor profile is licorice. I love licorice, so this is pleasant tasting to me. My boyfriend did not care for it; he gave it 2 stars because he cannot get used to the aftertaste. I give it 4 stars.

There is a warning on the container to "consult your healthcare practitioner before use if pregnant or breastfeeding, suffering from an autoimmune disorder or taking medication."  I know that astragalus is an immune booster, so I can see why those with autoimmune have to be careful because they already have an overactive immune system. It is the same with eating garlic. However, I am not concerned with these warnings based on the ingredients.

In summary, this is not for everyone. Those who love licorice will probably like this. Those who do not, will probably find this strange tasting. The current price of $30 is a bit pricey for this; however, this should make 40 cups which comes to $.75 cents per cup.

title: Mild, smooth coffee; aroma when open the bag is just intoxicating; very pricey
text:
For the current price of $22.50 ($25 minus 10%), this is very expensive coffee as it comes to $2.25 per ounce. However, this coffee is very mild and has a nice smooth flavor. This is definitely not for Starbucks lovers or those that love dark deeply-flavored almost burnt coffee.

I love the aroma when just opening the package. The beans are pale as can be seen from my photo.

Overall, I like it. As for the price, I will let the consumer decide if it is worth the purchase.

title: Tastes like the tea they give for free at a Chinese restaurant; teabags may break open when wet
text:
There are 20 tea bags in this box. Although there is only green tea made with Jasmine Flower, there is no flavor of Jasmine that comes through. This tastes like the tea they give for free at a Chinese restaurant. It is a little bit smokey and bland. Also, I had a teabag break open, so the bags are thin and all of them may not hold the tea when wet. I had a few pieces of leaf sediment in my cup.

There are no good brewing instructions on the box. I brewed this at 185 degrees Farhenheidt which is the typical brewing temperature for green tea (unless one likes their green tea very bitter). I also do not like that they are promoting this as a detoxifier and body fat reducer.

The current price of 5.29 is reasonable as it comes to $.26 cents per cup if using one tea bag per serving.

PS I had a few more cups of this tea and every time the tag and string fell off the tea bag and there was a hole in the bag where they were. The teabags are of inferior quality.

title: Intoxicating delicious and I do not care for turkey; this 2-pack is 4 one-ounce servings
text:
For the current price of $10.99, one gets two two-ounce packets of turkey jerky. Each two-ounce pack is two one-ounce servings. Honestly, I ate the entire two ounces immediately, it was SOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO GOOD. The only thing I do not like is that I find it hard to open the package without a scissor.

The turkey is moist and extremely flavorful. The flavor is also extremely balanced with all its ingredients as not one particular ingredient stands out to me. I do not taste the basil, or pepper, or any ingredient but they meld into a super tasty jerky. Each one-ounce serving is 70 calories with zero calories from fat but the total fat listed is .05g, so there is fat but not rounded up in a serving. There is 20mg of cholesterol, 180mg of sodium, 7g of carbohydrates (6g of them are sugar), and 8g of protein. There's also 2% of the daily value of Vitamin A & C, calcium, and iron per serving.

INGREDIENTS:
- Turkey
- Brown Sugar
- Tamari (Water, Soybeans,Salt, Sugar)
- Natural Smoke Flavoring (Water, Natural Hick Smoke Flavor,Vinegar, Molasses, and Salt)
- Apple Cider Vinegar (Apple Cider Vinegar, Filtered Water)
- Black Pepper
- Lemon Juice (Water, Concentrated Lemon Juice)
- Lemon Oil
- Tomato Paste (Tomatoes, Citric Acid)
- Garlic
- Italian Seasoning
- Red And Green Bell Peppers
- Paprika 
- Celery Juice Powder (Celery Juice, Seal Salt),
- Cayenne Pepper
- Organic Crushed Red Pepper
- Contains Soy

This is a powerhouse of flavor and full of protein. Each two ounce package is $5.50 each or $2.25 per serving if using their calculations. However, it is IMPOSSIBLE to stop at just one ounce. I had to stop at an entire package or I would have eaten the entire four ounces five minutes after I opened the box. This is the best jerky I have ever had in my life. The only reason I rated this 4 stars is because the price is so expensive.

title: Smells absolutely delicious but not very tasty; looks like water; only 10 tea bags not 60
text:
There is one box of 10 tea bags and not 6 boxes of 10 tea bags each as advertised. It is possible they only gave Vine reviewers one box but usually that is mentioned on the order and it was not. Therefore, buyer beware.

This is a green tea blend with blueberry and lavender. I brewed it just off the boil for 3 minutes per the instructions. The tea smells absolutely delicious but it brews so light that it looks like water. The brew also smells wonderful but the taste is lackluster.  

The tea taste is 2.5 stars to me. The deceptive advertising of 60 tea bags caused me to rate this 1 star. At the current price of $31.85 it comes to $3.19 per tea bag for a box of 10. If this were six boxes, at that price it would come to $5.31 per box instead of $31.85 for one box; in that case it would be $.53 cents per tea bag which is still expensive but competitively priced.

title: Lovely green tea with vegetal notes; it’s Grade B which indicates broken tea
text:
Included in this package is an airtight 3.5 ounce bag of Grade B BiLuoChun Green Tea loose leaf tea. Also included is a bag clip and a small sample of Osmantus & Oolong Loose Leaf Tea. Grade B is apparently a broken leaf blend typically used in a breakfast style tea.

I personally brew one teaspoon of these tea leaves per cup at 185 degrees and steep for four minutes. Green tea should always be brewed at less than boiling to avoid a bitter taste. The brew is a pale yellow and the aroma and taste is fresh and vegetal. It definitely tastes healthy. I have not tried the sample tea yet but I look forward to it.

This should be stored in a cool dry place at 18 degree Celsius (equivalent to 64.4 degrees Fahrenheit). Since I keep my house at 57 degrees, I can store this at room temperature. Although they say this can be steeped three times, I never do that. Typically this would be brewed in a gaiwan such as [[ASIN:B08D8KX1DY this one]], which is why it would be steeped three times because this holds only a few ounces.

I personally like this tea and its subtle vegetal flavor, so I rate this 4 stars. My boyfriend loves this tea and rates it 5 stars. I feel the current price of $13.49 is typical for tea of this grade.

PS I also tried the one teabag sample of the Oolong tea. I steeped it 3-4 minutes at 212 Fahrenheit. The brew is amber and the flavor is vegetal and very good.

title: Crackers arrive in pieces & I only received one box of six; rosemary flavor predominant
text:
I ordered the six-pack but received only one box. When I get a multipack of a food item, I share it with as many people as the product number so that I can get their assessment. Unfortunately, I could not do that in this case.

These crackers arrived smashed and came in one five ounce box. They are somewhat tasty to my palate with rosemary being the predominant flavor followed by tomato, basil, and then pepper. The crackers are very dry, so I had to drink milk with them. There are nine crackers per serving (providing one can figure out how many small pieces equals nine). Each serving is 140 calories. Ten percent comes in the form of saturated fat, 4% in the form of added sugars, and 10% in the form of sodium. The ingredient are a flour blend of brown rice flour, tapioca starch, quinoa and millet flour followed by palm fruit shortening, tapioca syrup, chia seeds, cane sugar, sunflower oil, tomato powder, sea salt, leavening, garlic, basil, xanthan gum, paprika, rosemary, black pepper, and rosemary extract.

This is definitely not healthy food. Also the current price of $22.45 is an expensive $3.74 per 5 ounce box (i.e. $.75 cents per ounce) providing one receives the full six boxes. If only one box is received, the price is stupendously outrageous. The taste, cost, and ingredients are not enticing enough to me to ever want to buy these.

title: Six varieties of pure bitterness; I do not like any of these teas
text: There are 36 K-cups in this package, which amounts to 6 K-cups of 6 different varieties (oolong, jasmine, English breakfast, earl grey, and black tea). I personally do not like any of them as they come out all bitter. My favorite K-cup tea is the Twinnings Earl Grey. I will not be ordering this set again.

title: Flavorful tisane with subtle turmeric flavor; brew is a very pale yellow
text:
This is a tisane (all herbal ingredients) because there are no leaves from the tea plant (camellia sinensis; an evergreen species). The ingredients are ginger, turmeric, licorice root, lemon bits, galangal, natural flavors, and black peppers.

It comes in a 2.5 ounce package that supposedly makes about 20 to 25 cups of tea. I like the tab to open and the lock strip to close. I used one teaspoon per one cup of water even though 15 was recommended. I steeped it for 5 minutes at 208 degrees as specified. The brew is a very pale yellow. The predominant taste is tumeric and only a hint of ginger even though that is the first ingredient. I personally feel it is a very good tisane and so does my boyfriend. We both rate it 4 stars.

The current price of $6.98 is reasonable.

title: Good dark roast coffee but pods get stuck in the Keurig & pods shouldn't be put in carousel
text:
This dark roast coffee is good. The flavor is bold and strong, a little bit bitter, but not as super strong as Starbucks. I personally love medium brew coffee but I think the flavor of this brew is 4 stars. Nevertheless, this gets 2 stars from me because of the pods. The lid of the pod gets stuck in the top of the Keurig as can be seen from my photo. I have to wrestle with the machine to remove it. Since it has a mesh bottom, the Keurig does not have to punch a hole through the bottom; however, the rim of the pod is too big. It is for this reason only that I suggest a pass on these. Another thing to note is that these pods should not be put in a carousel that holds K-cups because that is open to the air. The coffee is exposed in a mesh, which is why they come in sealed foil bags that need to be resealed after each pod is removed for brewing.

The currrent price of $16.99 comes to $.57 cents per pod for 30 pods.

UPDATE 9/26/20 - My boyfriend tried this and gives it 4 stars. He says it is good but strong. Also, the pods did not get stuck in his Keurig.

title: I do not like this tisane (not a tea) at all
text: I do not care at all for this tisane. First of all, there are no ingredients listed on the label. Secondly, this is not a tea because it does not contain any leaves from the camellia sinensis plant. Since only herbs are used (I suspect just chamomile and lavender), it is a tisane. Nevertheless, if it tasted good to me I would have rated this highly. I personally do not like the smell or the flavor of this light amber brew. The combination of chamomile mixed with lavender does not excite my taste buds and the aroma reminds me of dirty socks. Obviously, I am the odd one out here as everyone else seems to love this brew. I could not even drink one cup of this. There are 30 teabags (cups) in this bag.

title: Bar size is smaller than advertised & only six bars are in the package
text:
This review is for one box of the chocolate peppermint stick bar. There are six 1.69 ounce bars in the box.

There is nothing nutritional about this bar but the chocolate peppermint stick is very good. I like the crispy mouth feel and the subtle peppermint taste. I had my boyfriend try it also, and he loved it. It was 5 stars for him and 4 stars for me.

Each bar is 200 calories with 7g of added sugar (15%) and 8g of protein and 90mg of sodium (4%). The first few ingredients are a protein grain blend [crisps (soy protein isolate, rice flour alkalized cocoa)], rolled oats, roasted soybeans, brown rice syrup, cane sugar, etc.

Note that the Amazon site says there are 36 bars at 2.4 ounces each bar. The bars are 1.69 ounces. It is possible Vine reviewers only got a 6-pack instead of 36 bars but I cannot be certain of that. If one truly gets 36 bars, then it comes to a little over $1 per ba which is competitive. To me I would rate this four stars for taste but the bar size is not as advertised.

UPDATE 9/21/20 - My upstairs neighbors each tried one bar and on a scale of 1-5, they said this was 7.5 and the best thing I ever gave them to try. My Postman also tried this and gives it 4 stars for taste like I do.

UPDATE 9/27/20 - The last bar I gave to another friend and unfortunately he rated it 2 stars because he does not like peppermint and only likes chocolate.

title: Tastes like red wine vinegar should; acidity is 6% per Amazon site
text:
I am not a person who uses vinegar a lot but this tastes exactly the way red wine vinegar should taste. I used this in my steamed kale dish and it gave it flavor. This is great for salads, marinades, or just in cooking when red wine vinegar is required. Per the Amazon site, the acidity level is 6% but nowhere on the bottle does it say this. I would have preferred it be denoted on the bottle.

This is good ORGANIC red wine vinegar that is gluten free, non GMO, and unfiltered (which provides better flavor).

title: This coffee is absolutely fabulous but not typical espresso
text:
My boyfriend and I ABSOLUTELY LOVE this coffee. It has a smooth taste and is not bitter or strong. I had to grind all the beans first. I ground it into a fine grind. The beans were not oily which is fine. I was always told oily beans indicate freshness but found out over the years that this is not true.

We used four tablespoons of coffee to six cups of water in our automatic drip coffee maker. We loved this both black and with half and half.

Note that we did not use this as espresso in an espresso machine. We brewed this like regular coffee. It is a medium roast and that is what we like. Espresso is normally strong and this is not strong. We do not like strong coffee, so this is perfect for us. My boyfriend and I consider this coffee OUTSTANDING!

title: Smell is super fishy; brew is almost black; taste is smooth but plain
text:
I tried their [[ASIN:B08933QGH3 raw Jasmine Pu-erh tea]] and it is excellent. I absolutely love it. I also decided to try their other teas, including this one.

There are three boxes of 12 sachets each box for a total of 36 tea bags of Classic Ripe Pu'er Tea.  Note Pu'er is normally spelled Pu-erh. Ripe Pu-erh goes through an accelerated aging process. As to how long this was aged, it is unclear.

Everything is in Chinese on the box except for one of the box bottoms. There are images on the side to brew the tea at 100 degrees Celsius which is 212 Fahrenheit for 2-3 minutes. I brewed it at 212 and steeped the tea for 2 minutes.

This tea brews almost black. My boyfriend thinks this very good but I personally feel the taste is smooth but very plain.

The ingredients are Yunnan large-leaf sun-dried green tea. 

The box it comes in is supposed to open like a drawer but the bottom flap gets caught making it almost impossible to get to the tea without performing delicate surgery.  There are 12 high-end triangular tea bags in this box. The current price of $36.90 for this tea comes to $12.30 per box or $1.03 per cup (i.e. tea bag).

title: Smells like a field of roses; brews to a deep reddish amber
text:
I tried their [[ASIN:B08933QGH3 raw Jasmine Pu-erh tea]] and it is excellent. I absolutely love it. I also decided to try their other teas, including this one.

The box says this is Rose Ripe Pu'er Tea.  Note Pu'er is normally spelled Pu-erh. Everything is in Chinese on the box except for the box bottom. There are images on the side to brew the tea at 100 degrees Celsius which is 212 Fahrenheit for 2-3 minutes. I brewed it at 212 and steeped the tea for 4 minutes.

This tea brews to a deep reddish amber. My boyfriend and I liked the taste. It tasted like a little bit of rose (flowery) with an earthy flavor.

The ingredients are Yunnan large-leaf sun-dried green tea and rose. 

The box it comes in is supposed to open like a drawer but the bottom flap gets caught making it almost impossible to get to the tea without performing delicate surgery.  There are 12 high-end triangular tea bags in this box. The current price of $16.90 is expensive for this tea as it comes to $1.41 per cup (i.e. tea bag).

title: Medium Amber Brew with the earthy flavor typical of Pu-erh tea
text:
I tried their [[ASIN:B08933QGH3 raw Jasmine Pu-erh tea]] and it is excellent. I absolutely love it so I decided to try this one.

The box says this is Classic Raw Pu'er Tea.  Note Pu'er is normally spelled Pu-erh. Everything is in Chinese on the box except for the box bottom. There are images on the side to brew the tea at 100 degrees Celsius which is 212 Fahrenheit for 2-3 minutes. I brewed it at 212 and steeped the tea for 4 minutes. 

The amber brew is clear and to me it tastes very good with the typical earthy flavor of Pu-erh tea. My boyfriend said it tastes wonderful.

The ingredients are Yunnan large-leaf sun-dried green tea. I have no idea how long this was aged or if it was aged like Pu-erh.

The box it comes in is supposed to open like a drawer but the bottom flap gets caught making it almost impossible to get to the tea without performing delicate surgery.  There are 12 high-end triangular tea bags in this box. The current price of $15.99 is expensive for this tea as it comes to $1.35 per cup (i.e. tea bag).

title: What is it? Tangerine Peel or Orange Peel; this tea tastes like Lipton; not much flavor - 2.5 stars
text:
I love all things tea and have many different teas. I tried their [[ASIN:B08933QGH3 raw Jasmine Pu-erh tea]] and it is excellent. I absolutely love it.

The box says this is Tangerine Peel Ripe Pu'er Tea but it should say Orange Peel. Note Pu'er is normally spelled Pu-erh. Ripe Pu-erh goes though an accelerated aging process. As to how long this was aged, it is unclear.

Everything is in Chinese on the box except for the box bottom. There are images on the side to brew the tea at 100 degrees Celsius which is 212 Fahrenheit for 2-3 minutes. I brewed it at 212 and steeped the tea for 3 minutes. I usually steep my teas at 4 minutes but the brew was so dark, that I took their advice. I was actually surprised that they say to boil the water because green tea is usually brewed with 185 degree water to keep it from being bitter.

The dark brew was clear with a slight citrus aroma. However, no orange (or tangerine) came through in flavor. It tasted mostly like a higher end Lipton tea bag. This is definitely not a favorite of mine as it is plain tasting. The ingredients are Yunnan large-leaf sun-dried green tea and Dried Orange peel.

The box it comes in is supposed to open like a drawer but the bottom flap gets caught making it almost impossible to get to the tea without performing delicate surgery.  There are 12 high-end triangular tea bags in this box. The current price of $16.90 is way too expensive for this tea. This gets 2.5 stars from me rounded up to 3.

title: Crystal clear amber brew with nice Jasmine flavor & aroma & subtle earthy undertone
text:
I love all things tea and have many different teas. This flavored tea is a raw Jasmine Pu-erh tea (they spelled it pu'er on the box). I heard of Pu-erh tea (the ripe one) but never the raw variety.

Based on my Internet research, the healthline site says "Raw pu-erh is made from non-fermented green tea leaves that were picked, quickly roasted, sun-dried and then steamed to be compressed into round disks called cakes. After which the cakes are aged until the tea's taste is properly transformed." The Mansatea site says, "Raw pu-erh can be aged upwards of 50 years or more. Younger raw pu-erh is aged anywhere from a few months to a few years, whereas aged pu-erh is generally aged anywhere from a decade to upwards of 50 years or more. Unlike raw pu-erh, ripe pu-erh goes through an accelerated aging process."

Everything is in Chinese on the box except for the box bottom. There are images on the side to brew the tea at 90 degrees Celsius which is 194 Fahrenheit for 2-3 minutes. I brewed it at 195 and steeped the tea for 4 minutes. The amber brew was crystal clear and the aroma and flavor was definitely Jasmine with a subtle earthy undertone. This tea is really pleasant and satisfying. I definitely love it. The ingredients are Yunnan large-leaf sun-dried green tea and Jasmine.

The box it comes in is supposed to open like a drawer but the bottom flap gets caught making it almost impossible to get to the tea without performing delicate surgery.  There are 12 high-end triangular tea bags in this box. Although there is no price shown on Amazon, I would expect they will be selling this for between $12 and $14 as an online price which is expensive but still competitive.

title: My favorite variety of flavored K-Cup coffee; taste is excellent and blend has a smooth finish
text:
I discovered this coffee when I recently purchased the [[ASIN:B078SZS7C6 Keurig Flavored Coffee Collection Variety Pack]]. Of all the coffees in that package, Khalua Original blend was the absolute best. This coffee is supposedly flavored with rum, vanilla, and caramel but I can not taste that. However, both my boyfried and I find this to be excellent coffee with a very smooth finish. I would definitely purchase this again and again and again.

There are six 12 packs of K-cups in this box for a total of 72 K-cups.

title: This tea has a nice smooth bergamot flavor considering it comes in a k-cup
text:
I am mostly a tea purist and good tea does not come in a k-cup. I prefer loose leaf. However, since I purchased the Keurig, mostly for my boyfriend's coffee, I figured I would buy tea to brew in a pinch. This tea has a very nice bergamot flavor and is definitely one I would order again.

I have only two dislikes. First, it is almost impossible to see how many k-cups come in the box as written on the box. That is written in very small white letters on the bottom along with the ounces of each k-cup. Second, it was very hard to remove the perforated section of the box to access the contents. Other than these two small details, I have no complaints and this tea will stay on my subscribe and save.

title: I purchased both the chicken & beef baby food for my sick cat; the price is right
text:
I purchase both the chicken and beef baby food for my sick cat because I could no longer find lamb baby food which is really the best to try for cats. However, the price of $10 for each is only twenty cents more than what my local store sells it for. Unfortunately, my sick cat would not eat this but my other two cats ate it. This is also great for starting kittens on solid food but should not be part of a regular diet; only as a treat or to start them eating or to get a sick cat to eat.

Lamb is always the best but I do not believe Gerber has this anymore. The next best is turkey or chicken with beef as a last resort. Usually cats that refuse to eat will eat Gerber meats but, obviously, not always.

title: Tastes a bit too artificially sweet but otherwise it’s good coffee; it is NOT CAPPUCCINO
text:
There are 24 French Vanilla Cappuccino K-cup pods in this package.  Note that this is a flavored and sweetened coffee and not actually Cappuccino like I orginally thought.

I made a medium cup of this and the end brew tasted artifically sweet. The coffee itself is good, however. I personally would never buy this for two reasons. First, it is too sweet. Second, a small cup of water has to be run through the Keurig after every use to ensure the nozzles do not get clogged.

Each serving contains 70 calories, 4g (20%) saturated fat, 170mg sodium, and 5g of sugars all of which are added sugars.

INGREDIENTS:
- sugar
- coconut oil
- corn syrup solids
- instant coffee
- salt
- sodium caseinate
- contains less than 2% mono- and diglycerides, of maltodextrin, cocoa, dipotassium phosphate, surcralose, natural and artifical flavors, ,

In summary, I do not like the sweetness of this brew. I have had this and their hot cocoa pods for a couple of months and could not review them because I did not have access to the Keurig I got my boyfriend since COVID-19 has kept us out of each other's household. I actually had to purchase a Keurig in order to review this product and am upset that this is not as good as I anticipated. Once I get together again with my boyfriend, I will have him try these pods and will update my review with his assessment. However, I cannot recommend this unless one likes very sweet instant coffee made in a Keurig that is trying to market itself as Cappuccino.

UPDATE May 21, 2020 - I gave this to my boyfriend to try and he said, "It’s drinkable if there is nothing else available, but it is too sweet for my taste."

title: Synthetically and overbearingly sweet; I could barely drink a half a cup
text:
There are 24 salted caramel hot cocoa K-cup pods in this package.

I made a medium cup of this and the consistency was fine but the end brew was cloyingly sweet. I actually could not drink the entire cup. I tried doctoring it up with half and half and it was still bad. I eventually had to throw it out. I noticed when I dumped it that there was "sediment" that settled to the bottom of the cup. It does not say that I have to keep stirring this. The interesting thing about this is that the sweetness tastes very synthetic as if it were made with Sweet and Low even though artificial sugar is not in the ingredients list.

Each serving contains 60 calories, 2g (9%) saturated fat, 180mg sodium, 6g of sugars of which 5g (10%) is added sugars, and 1g protein.

INGREDIENTS:
- sugar
- corn syrup solids
- cocoa (processed with alkali),
- coconut oil
- nonfat dry milk
- salt
- contains less than 2% of maltodextrin, sodium caseinate, natural and artifical flavors, mono- and diglycerides, dipotassium phosphate, surcralose, acesulfame potassium

In summary, I personally would never buy this for two reasons. First, I absolutely hate this cocoa and its sweetness. Second, a small cup of water has to be run through the Keurig after every use to ensure the nozzles do not get clogged. Note that I have had this for a couple of months and could not review it because I did not have access to the Keurig I got my boyfriend since COVID-19 has kept us out of each other's household. I eventually just gave in and had to purchase a Keuring in order to review this product in my house and am beyond upset that this tastes so bad to me. Once I get together again with my boyfriend, I will have him try these pods and will update my review with his assessment. However, I cannot recommend this unless one likes super sweet cocoa.

UPDATE May 16, 2020 - My boyfriend tried this and he says this hot cocoa STINKS. He hates it.

title: Coffee has the strong and bitter Starbucks taste of their dark brew; it is too bold for me
text:
There are eight cans of coffee good enough for a half a pot or more depending on how one likes the strength of their coffee. Since I know Starbucks coffee is strong, I used six cups of water instead of four. The resulting brew is still very bold and a bit bitter. It would have been undrinkable to me if I did not use two extra cups of water instead of the four cups recommended.

At the current price of $49.99 it comes to $6.25 per half pot of coffee or $12.50 per pot. This is super expensive for such strong and bitter coffee. However, if one loves Starbucks coffee, then this is the real deal.

CAVEAT - I recieved a box of eight cans. Normally reviewers will be told if they receive only part of a shipment. There is no indication one box is a partial shipment. However, I suspect it is but not mentioned. If that is the case, then those who order this will get 4 boxes of 8 cans for 32 cans in total which makes this a reasonable $1.56 per half pot or $3.12 per pot.

title: Not as good as 2 other ceremonial grade matchas Iuse to buy but price is reasonable - 3.5 stars
text:
This matcha is good but not great. It has a hint of bitterness. I like to taste the powder plain first before using it so I noticed it lacks the sweetness of the two other brands I have used previously. However, it is still good enough and the price is reasonable.

Several years back I got into matcha for a while and ordered Panatea Matcha (no longer in existence under that name), which was ceremonial grade and high quality. It has an excellent sweet taste to it. I also ordered matcha directly from Hibiki-an in Japan which is good.

To make matcha it is good to have a:
- chawan (cup) 
- chasen (whisk)
- chashaku (scoop)
- chasen naoshi (whisk keeper) - nice to have only as a place to store the whisk

I filled one heaping serving on my chashaku and then used my chasen to mix with about 3 ounces of 180 degree water. I was never very good at getting it to foam like it is supposed to. Neverthless. the resulting beverage was okay but definitely not as good as the Panatea I have tried before which I always felt was excellent. 

On the other hand, I am glad this is from Japan. It is very important that the soil this is grown on be tested for impurities like arsenic, lead, pestisides, etc. I have no idea if this matcha was tested or not but Japanese matcha tends to be safer than Chinese matcha. Matcha is made from the ground leaves of the camellia sinensis plant, which is an evergreen shrub/tree. It is the same tea that many teas come from, except that matcha is the actual "powdered" leaves that are being consumed. This is why it is so important to know its source if consuming regularly. I have no idea if Matcha Moments periodically tests the soil it gets its matcha from. 

In summary, this matcha is not bad but I it does not taste like the highest grade ceremonial matcha I am use to. Nevertheless, the current price of $17.95 is very reasonable but their same [[ASIN:B082RCYQWJ  larger sized matcha]] in a pouch is $.90 cents cheaper and is the better deal but without the can.

title: Love the large size and this worked perfectly in my angel food cake recipe
text:
Cream of tartar is a byproduct of winemaking. I never heard of it before I started making angel food cakes.

I only use cream of tartar for my angel food cake recipe but I understand it has use in other recipes where whipped egg whites are needed. It definitely increased the stability and volume of my egg whites as can be seen in the photos. My angel food cake came out fantastic and tasted as great as always.

At the current price of $3.99 this is steal. I paid $3.59 for the 1.5 ounce size in the McCormick brand and this size is over three times larger at 5 ounces.

I love the results, the size, and the price. Based on my Internet search, it appears it has other uses as well such as as a non-toxic cleaner and stain remover, to deter ants, etc.

title: Delicious collection of rice and pasta
text:
This is an excellent variety of rice and pasta. Note that only the rice is 32 ounces, so the Amazon site is misleading on the ounces. Included in this collection are:

- 32 ounces Texmati (rice)
- 32 ounces Arborio (rice)
- 26.5 ounces Couscous (pasta)
- 26.5 ounces Orzo (pasta)

I cooked all four varieties using my steamer and they came out excellent. Each one was fresh and delicious.

title: Out-of-this-world taste; these sugar cookies are flavorful at 70 calories per cookie
text:
Oh boy, it is hard to eat just one of these per day. These are highly flavorful and have a hint of vanilla taste to me even though vanilla is not in the ingredients list.  They have a nice bite feel and are a bit crispy. According to the package one serving is two cookies at 140 calories or 70 calories per cookie.

UPDATE March 14, 2020 - I had my boyfriend try these and he thinks they are great. He rates these cookies five stars.

title: Tastes better than Hershey’s chocolate syrup & 10 more calories too
text: Both my boyfriend and I tried this in whole milk and compared the same amount to a glass of Hershey's chocolate syrup.  Hershey's is five fewer calories per tablespoon but the ingredients on both are relatively similar starting with high frutose corn syrup--no one said this was healthy.  This brand tastes better to us by a small margin than Hershey's.  Attached are photos of the labels of both syrups and a picture of the chocolate milk with this syrup. We did get it to dissolve by stiring a few times. Overall, this is a winner to us.

title: Not packaged well for shipping but this tastes as good as any other quick cooking oats I tried
text:
First of all, this is shipped in a padded paper envelope which increases risk of damage. My package arrived in perfect condition but I suspect that will not be the case for most.

Second, this tastes as good and cooks as quick as other quick cooking oatmeal I tried before. I personally prefer groats (which is the uncut oats) that takes 37 minutes to cook unattended in my steamer. I cooked this in my steamer (1/4 cup of oats to 1/4 cup of water) for 7 minutes. The results are in the picture.

In summary, if one wants the quick cooking variety, then this it perfect and it all comes down to price. I personally would not buy this because I eat groats only. However, if I ate quick cooking oats or needed rolled oats for a recipe, I would purchase this as it is just whole grain rolled oats.

title: Watery apple sauce with a hint of bitterness;  just okay
text:
My boyfriend and I tried this six-pack and the apple sauce is watery and has a hint of bitterness. It is certainly not our favorite. I personally prefer Mussellman's chunky style apple sauce. 

The ingredients are apples, water, and ascorbic acid to maintain color. One serving (one of the six pack) is 60 calories.

title: Tomato Soup tastes a bit too sweet and salty for me
text:
I decided to cook this with half a cup of skim milk instead of one cup of water. The reason is that I do not like watery soup. Previously, I tried their cream of chicken and added one can of water and it was way too watery.

Nevertheless, this soup is way too sweet and salty for me. I ate most of it but threw a little bit out. I am not a canned soup eater in general, but a full can is 23g of sugars and1170mg of sodium. If it were less salty and less sweet, it would be better to me. Now I could have added more water or milk, but then it would have been less thick and I do not like thin soups.

title: Flavorful soup; does not taste salty but has a lot of sodium; I’d use less water
text:
I made this per the instructions which was to add one can of water to one can of soup. To me the soup came out watery. I prefer a thicker soup.

This can is suppose to be 2.5 servings at 280 calories for the can. Although it did not taste salty, there are 2080mg of sodium per can which I do not like.

The soup was flavorful and had a few microscopic chicken pieces in it. I actually added rice to thicken it up because I did not like it plain. To me this soup is a close equivalent to Campbell's Soup.

title: Good coffee flavor but a bit over-spiced with pepper & too expensive
text:
At the current price of $17.99 for 12 K-pods, this is very expensive coffee. I took off one star for that alone.

Both my boyfriend and I tried this black and with cream. The coffee itself is good but the spice comes through and mostly it is pepper that is the predominant flavor. I actually liked the coffee with cream better than black because it made the flavor more balanced. My boyfriend thought it was okay but would not buy it because he said he tasted too much pepper.

The ingredients are arabica coffee, turmeric root extract, black pepper. With regard to flavor, my boyfriend rates this three stars and I rate it four stars. Personally, we would rather eat our turmeric than drink it.

title: Utterly sinful and salty chocolates but way overpackaged for delivery
text:
These chocolates are incredible and sinful. They are definitely overpackaged when shipped as I got a box in a box for the six single serve packs. They are all made with fair trade cocoa.

IN THE BOX
Two 2.3 ounce packages of milk chocolate toffee almonds - 340 calories
Two 2.3 ounce packages of dark chocolate sea salt cashews - 330 calories
Two 2.1 ounce packages of dark chocolate sea salt caramels - 270 calories

FLAVOR
The toffee almonds have a rich toffee taste with a hint of chocolate and the crunch, but not the taste, of almonds.
The sea salt cashews are richly salted and have an intense dark chocolate taste with the soft crunch of the cashew.
The sea salt caramels pair a richly salted intense dark chocolate with the sweet taste of caramel.

The chocolates are lovely. I have no idea what these will be priced at but these are definitely highly salted intense chocolate treats.

title: Delicious if used sparingly; extremely salty with a hint of garlic
text:
I used this on some sprouted brown rice with olive oil and mint and it was extremely flavorful but very salty. Salt is the first ingredient and there is only a hint of garlic in this. It is good for potatoes and steaks.

The top has a shaker side and a pouring side. The garlic sort of sticks together and does not dispense easily on the shaker side. I had to shake vigorously to get this out.

In summary, I love the flavor but the salt is a bit overpowering. It is important to use this sparingingly if watching one's salt intake.

title: Rice is incredibly delicious--much sweeter and more sticky; not really quick cooking – 4.5 stars
text:
I never heard of sprouted brown rice before. However, this rice is sweeter than normal rice and is more "sticky" and less chewy that regular brown rice. I used my rice cooker to cook this. First, I washed the rice. Per the instructions, I cooked it on the white rice setting with a 1/3 cup more water. Three cups was done in about one hour which is typical of when I cook white rice. Therefore, it is not quick cooking unless the package means it cooks quicker than standard brown rice. If that is what is meant, then it does cook quicker. I took off 1/2 a star because I felt the words quick cooking are misleading and because the current price of $8.25 is expensive for two pounds.

I had this with olive oil and black garlic powder (see photo). I also added mint in the rice (not shown). I could not stop eating this. It is incredibly delicious!

title: As good as any other poopy seeds I’ve tried before; it all comes down to price
text: These are typical poppy seeds and taste the same as any other poppy seeds I have tried before. The container has a flip top for easy dispensing but I unscrewed the top (after removing the seal) to pour out enough for my recipe. I made poppy seed popovers (see photos) and they came out good. To me, purchasing this will all come down to price for a 3.5 ounce container.

title: Excellent medium brew coffee; my boyfriend and I enjoy this
text: This comes in 6 boxes of 10 K-cups. The brew is medium roast. We brew our K-cups on the medium cup setting which is about 10 ounces. It brewed a flavorful cup of coffee that was not too bold or too bitter. Although it was good black, it was best with half and half. We do not like very strong coffee, so this is a five star brew to us.

title: Comes in 3 packs of 24 or 25 pieces of white, milk, and dark chocolate
text:
There are three triangular boxes in this pack. Each box contains 24 or 25 pieces. I counted mine in all the boxes and each box contained 10 milk chocolate pieces and 7 white chocolate pieces. However, two boxes contained 8 pieces of dark chocolate and one box contained 7, which means I received 74 instead of 75 individually wrapped chocolates in total.

It is 210 calories for 5 pieces. I tried all three flavors and they were all typical Toblerone flavor and were relatively good and creamy with a bit of mouth feel (crunch) and a honey finish. I personally did not care for the crunch in the white chocolate, but the "chocolate" itself (white chocolate is not actually chocolate) was good. This is not my favorite candy as I prefer Lindt over this any day. However, for those who like this brand, the flavor is typical of their larger triangular bars in a single serving size.

My only negative is that two of my three triangular boxes were shipped partially unsealed (see photo) which is undesirable if someone is giving this as a gift.

UPDATE 1/20/20 - I had my Postman try all 3 flavors and he loved these. He rates them 5 stars.

UPDATE 3/2/20 - I had my upstairs neighbors try all 3 flavors and both of them loved them. They rated all of them 5 stars.

title: Tasty salsa with a little bit of a kick to it but not too hot; it's great on fish
text:
I actually love this salsa simply because it is not too hot and it is tasty. It goes perfect with white fish such as flounder, tilapia, etc. I personally do not like very hot foods and this has a little bit of a kick to it but not what I would consider very hot.

The ingredients are:
- diced tomatoes
- tomato puree
- green bell peppers
- onions
- jalapeno peppers
- salt
- distilled vinegar
- garlic
- coriander
- cilantro
- paprika
- natural flavor
- cumin

It has 10 calories for 2 tablespoons and 190mg of sodium.

Overall, at the current price of $2.99, this is excellent. However, if you really want HOT, then look elsewhere because it is only mildly hot.

NOTE - It came packaged in bubble wrap in a plastic jar inside a padded envelope. There is some risk of breakage if a carrier throws the package this comes in. My carrier did not do this but beware of this possibility.

title: Tastes like chocolate peanut butter w/o the nuts; it’s VEGAN
text:
My boyfriend and I love the taste, texture, and spreadability of this spread. It is also vegan. The only thing is that it is not as easy to handwash the spread off the knife when done.

INGREDIENTS
- Dry Roasted Soybeans
- Organic Soybean Oil
- Organic Powdered Sugar
- Organic Cocoa
- Organic Palm Oil
- Organic Vanilla Flavor
- Sea Salt

One two tablespoon serving is 180 calories with 120 of it fat calories. There are only 3 grams of saturated fat, 0 of cholesterol, and 95 mg of sodium.

Note that refrigeration is not required after opening but it may extend the freshness.

I personally would not make a steady diet of this but tastewise it is great. The current price of $15.59 for two 16 ounce jars is competitive at $7.80 per jar.

My boyfriend and I both felt this spread was excellent.

title: Strong, good, but expensive coffee that both my boyfriend and I love
text:
This coffee is very good and strong. My boyfriend and I brewed this on a coarse grind in the KRUPS Personal Café Grind Drip Coffee Maker and the brew came out fantastic. It is strong when drunk black but would have been much stronger had we chosen to do a find grind. It is superb once we used cream in the brew. Both of us love this coffee when brewed with a coarse grind. Neither one of us can drink this black due to its bold taste.

At the current price of $49.48 plus the 15% off  coupon offer ($7.42), the final price is $42.06 which comes to $10.52 per pound. Without the coupon it comes to $12.37 per pound. This is definitely expensive coffee with or without a coupon. Its expensive but very good coffee--at least to our palate.

title: This coffee has a funny, almost sour taste to me; I personally do not like it but my friend loves it
text:
There are 42-K cups in the box. When brewing this coffee, there is no "coffee aroma" that I am use to. Also, I tried this black, with heavy cream, and with whole milk and there is a hint of sourness to the flavor of this brew, in my opinion. The sourness is not from the milk or cream either. Even though my boyfriend refuses to drink decaf coffee, I had him take several sips from each brew and he said he liked the coffee and that it tasted fine but weak. I found it almost to be tastesless except for that hint of sourness.

I am going to see if I can get a couple of decaf lovers I know to try this and will report back on their assessment. However, this coffee is not to my liking, hence the 2-star rating.

UPDATE - My girlfriend tried this and she loved it. Here are her exact words verbatim - "The decaf coffee bullets were very good (for a decaf standard)….and when half and half was added, it enhanced the coffee experience. The coffee had a very good consistency, and was delicious. I would purchase this--and or recommend to others...I'm looking forward to enjoying another cup tomorrow." Therefore, she gives this 5 stars.

FINAL UPDATE - I had two other friends try this. One said she found the coffee to be bitter and did not like it. Her boyfriend, however, found it to be just okay. She rates it 2 stars and he rates it 3 stars.

title: My boyfriend loves the strong flavor of this coffee and I like it
text:
There are 96 Keurig pods spread over four boxes of 24 count each. This coffee smells intoxicating when it is being brewed and my boyfriend really loves the flavor. I only like the flavor because I find it to be a bit strong for a light coffee. My boyfriend agrees that it is strong but he likes it that way.

I do feel it is a bit strong tasting for a coffee that is described as light. Nevertheless, my boyfriend rates this 5 stars and I rate this 3.5, hence the 4-star rating.

title: Exquisitely smooth & creamy - decadent, scrumptious, sinfully delicious
text:
I must confess; I never felt milk chocolate was worth eating but I love dark chocolate until I tried this. 
 
There is "heaven on earth" indeed and it arrives in the Coca K'Choo Merry-Go_Mousse bar. Yes, the center mousse filling is microscopically thin but the taste makes me utter "I'll have what she's having" from the Harry Met Sally movie. WOW - this bar truly is delicious!

There are four servings per four ounce bar at 160 calories (that is two squares). There are six bars per package.

I shared these bars with several others and below are their opinions, which are all 5 stars.

TASTE TESTER UPDATES as of 11/9/19:

- My POSTMAN tried a bar and he loved it. He says "it was very good" with a large smile on his face. He gives this chocolate 5 stars.

- I also gave this to my upstairs neighbors and they both loved it and rated this 5 stars.

- I gave a bar to my boyfriend. His first reaction before even trying the bar was to say "I don't want any candy." I asked him to try one piece and he say "WOW, this is verrrrrrry verrrrrry goood." He then asked for another piece and probably would not have stopped there had I not said it was time to eat lunch. He gives this 5 stars as well.

UPDATE 11/12/19: I gave my next to last bar to my substitute mailman and he LOVED it. He also said it was 5 stars. I then gave my last bar to my chocolate-loving friend who said it was delicious. It was so delicious that he convinced his mother, who actually hates chocolate, to try it and she also said this chocolate is delicious. She said she believes the "barely noticeable" mousse center makes it so creamy. Both of them also gave this 5 stars.

This means 8 people (includes me) found this chocolate to be excellent. Even though there were six bars my upstairs neighbors and my friend both shared their bars.

title: Good for pairing recipes with specific spices; too gimmicky and expensive for me
text:
This review is for the Italian seasoning recipe series. NOTE THAT THE IMAGE ATTACHED IS RELEVANT TO THIS PRODUCT. IT SHOWS THE APP THEY DIRECT YOU TO ON THE PACKAGE DOES NOT EXIST!

To me this microscopic Italian seasoning set is only good if one wants a portable set of spices to carry around ( in which case you can buy them separately for cheaper) or needs some recipe help. Other than this, these are not worth the money to me.

The spices are fresh but are nothing spectacular nor are they any better than some other brands. In fact the brand I love the best in spices is Jackie's kitchen, which are super fresh.

In the package are three teeny weeny Italian spices:
- 0.62 ounce of crushed red pepper
- 0.2 ounce of whole Egyptian majoram leaves (they look crushed)
- 0.35 ounce of dried whole rosemary leaves

Two recipes are printed on the back of the box to get you started. These recipes use all three spices but I was not interested in making them (i.e. baked butternut squash risotto and roasted chicken and vegetables). That recipe card with the two recipes can be removed along the perforation. There is also a reference to ten or more additional recipes if one downloands the flavor maker app. Unfortunately, I could not find that app (see screen print). I also could not find any series on YouTube as the box says. Perhaps these features are not yet available.

This set is definitely not worth the ridiculous price of $13.28. It is the price and the lacklusterness of the spices that has me rating this two stars.

The majoram is tasteless to me, the rosemary is good but not as pungent as it could be, and the crushed pepper is only moderately hot.

title: Tasty but salty snacks with a nice crunch
text:
This single-serving 12-pack is really over packaged with a box in a box. However, they arrived in perfect condition. There are two flavors in the box; six are aged white cheddar and six are sour cream and onion. Both flavors taste as I expect them to taste. They also have a nice crunch; however, they are salty to my taste.

AGED WHITE CHEDDAR
- 110 calories
- 6% (5g) total fat, 5% (1g) of which is saturated
- 2% (5mg) cholesterol  
- 10% (230mg) sodium
- 4% (12g) total carbs, of which 2g dietary fiber and 1g is sugar
- 4g of protein

Ingredients - organic chickpea flour, organic lentil flour, organic white rice flour, organic corn flour, organic cheddar cheese, etc. (see 2nd and 3rd photos)

SOUR CREAM & ONION
- 110 calories- 6% (5g) total fat, 5% (1g) of which is saturated
- 2% (5mg) cholesterol  
- 9% (210mg) sodium
- 5% (13g) total carbs, of which 2g dietary fiber and 1g is sugar
- 3g of protein

Ingredients - organic chickpea flour, organic lentil flour, organic white rice flour, organic corn flour, organic sour cream powder, etc. (see 4th and 5th photos)

In summary, the saltiness and shipment overpackaging are my only dislikes. Otherwise, I really like the taste and the crunch of these snacks. I usually do not eat highly-processed products that come out of a bag or box. However, if one wants a snack, I think these taste good and have a good mouth feel.

PS I gave some to two other people and will update my review with their star rating when they report back. However, myy rating is 4 stars and their rating will not change that.

UPDATE: My boyfriend tried these and loves them. He gives this snack 5 stars although he does feel this is way too expensive at $1.75 per bag at the current price of $20.98. However, with the 25% off that is currently on the Amazon site, this would be $15.74 which is $1.31 per bag. My boyfriend said even $1 per bag is too expensive for the single serving and I agree. Nevertheless, he rates it 5 stars for the taste.

UPDATE 2: I gave two bags (one of each flavor) to my POSTMAN and he really liked these. He rates them 4 stars.

FINAL UPDATE: I gave two bags (one of each flavor) to my upstairs neighbors. They shared them and both loved the white cheddar (5 stars) and only she loved the sour cream and onion (5 stars) but he did not (2 stars).

title: Almost flavorless herbal tea (a.k.a. tisane)
text:
There are two 3-ounce packages of Hibiscus Fruit tea in this package. Although, this is really not a tea made from the camellia sinesis plant, but is a tisane made from other herbs, fruits, etc.  This means there is no caffeine. A 1-1/2 teaspoon serving gets brewed at 208 degrees for 5-7 minutes. 

Ingredients - apple, papaya (papaya, can sugar), hibiscus, orange slices, natural flavors, pomegranate bits, safflowers

The liquor is a ruby rose color. The taste is practically flavorless. Attached are the before and after photos.
 
Unfortunately, I do not like this at all. If it had flavor, then I could enjoy it as a sort of "nightcap."

title: Good source of protein, vitamins, and minerals and has a great mouthfeel
text:
My boyfriend and I really love this shelled hemp. It is vegan with 10g of plant-based protein per serving of three tablespoons at 180 calories.

We use this in oatmeal and smoothies. It has a nice mouthfeel in oatmeal adding just a bit of crunch. Overall, we both find hemp flavorless but it has a lot of vitamins and minerals such as phosphorus, zinc, and iron. It is also a good source of good fats with 11g polyunsaturated fat and 1.5g monosaturated fat per serving.

In summary, we have tried two other brands of hemp (but forget which ones they were) and they are all good. For us, it really comes down to price.

title: Seven (7) refreshing loose leaf tisane (a.k.a. herbal teas) samplers
text:
There are seven refreshing herbal teas. These are not teas, which are made from the camellia sinesis plant, but are tisanes made from other herbs, spices, fruits, etc. There is a good variety of tisanes in this box. Note that this box not really giftable as it is just an unattractive cardboard box.

Over a couple of weeks I tried each one of these teas and based on the brew, this is my assessment.

Ginger Sweet Peach - Brew 208 degrees 5-7 min; no caffeine
Ingredients - ginger, apple bits, hibiscus, papaya bits, rose hip, pink peppercorns, natural flavors, orange blossoms, rose petals
Liquor is a cloudy rose color with a hint of spice and hibiscus but no peach.
Overall 3 stars.

Cocoa Mint Chill - Brew 208 degrees 5-7 min; no caffeine
Ingredients - rooibos tea, peppermint, cocoa shell, chocolate chips, natural flavors, cornflowers (contains MILK)
Liquor is a clear amber rose color with a strong minty fragrance and flavor. Infuser needs to be a  tight mesh because particles are small and will leak through to the brew.
Overall 5 stars.

Lavender Chamomile - Brew 208 degrees 5-7 min; no caffeine
Ingredients - chamomile, lemongrass, blue mallow blossoms, lavender
Liquor is a clear pale yellow color. Taste is typical chamomile with no hint of lavender. 
Overall 4 stars.

Berry Pear-Adise - Brew 208 degrees 5-7 min; no caffeine
Ingredients - apple pieces, papaya bits, prunes, pineapple bit, blackberries, natural flavors, hibiscus, cornflowers
Liquor is a light cloudy apricot color. "Leaf" is chunky due to all the dried fruit pieces. Taste is sweet but not particularly distinctive of any one flavor. 
Overall 3 stars.

Palm Beach Punch - Brew 208 degrees 5-7 min; no caffeine
Ingredients - apple pieces, hibiscus, carrots, orange slices, lemongrass, eucalyptus, beetroot, natural flavors, sweet blackberry leaves
Liquor is a light mauve color. "Leaf" is variable with some small lemongrass and other cuttings and larger dried fruit pieces. Taste is like punch and aroma is orange. 
Overall 4 stars.

Nutty Almond Cream - Brew 208 degrees 5-7 min; no caffeine
Ingredients - apple pieces, almonds, cinnamon pieces, beetroot, and artificial flavors (contains NUTS)
Liquor is a clear red color. "Leaf" is chunky due to all the nut pieces. Taste is like Christmas in a cup; absolutely wonderful with a hint of spice and apple, etc. 
Overall 5 stars.  

Mango DreamZZZ - Brew 208 degrees 5-7 min; no caffeine
Ingredients - chamomile, mango pieces, lemongrass, orange peel, orange blossoms, naturals flavors, cornflowers
Liquor is a faint yellow color. "Leaf" is mostly chunky due to all the dried fruit and flour pieces. Tastes like a hint of chamomile and nothing else. 
Overall 2 stars.

Based on the average of my ratings above, this is almost a 4-star tisane collection. I would, however, expect it to come in a better box at the current price of $34.22. None of the teas put me to sleep as expected and none have caffeine. Attached are photos of the before and after for each brew.

title: This tastes like cinnamon pretzels to me; not my favorite flavor
text:
I use to hate caramel until I tried Tara's caramels about a year ago.  Since then, I ordered every flavor except the [[ASIN:B079V19NL5 Bourbon]] since I do not like alcohol. Below are the caramels I ordered. My absolute favorites are the apple and the sea salt; they are the only five-star caramels that appeal to me. This snickerdoodle flavor is mediocre to me with a cinnamon overtone and a hint of buttery pretzels. I suspect for those who love that taste, this will be a hit but I never knew what Snickerdoodle was until I tried these caramels.

[[ASIN:B079V1FYZ9 Snickerdoodle]] - This one
[[ASIN:B079V32T1R Pecan Flavored]] 
[[ASIN:B079TSJ1TF Madagascar Vanilla]]
[[ASIN:B079TSHR6B Vanilla]]
[[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Sea Salt]] - 2nd favorite
[[ASIN:B079TZMFDH Apple]] - My favorite
[[ASIN:B079V1FYZB Mocha]]
[[ASIN:B079V154JV Licorice]]

These caramels are soft, smooth, buttery, and creamy without being cloyingly sweet. Unfortunately, I am not a fan of the flavor of pretzels and cinnamon combined.

Three pieces are one serving and equal 130 calories. There are 3.5g of saturated fat, 15mg cholesterol, 45mg sodium, 15g sugar, and 21g of carbohydrate. However, if you care about the nutrition label, then do not eat these because it is nothing but corn syrup, sweetened condensed milk, heavy cream, invert sugar, butter, brown sugar, sugar, natural flavor, cinnamon, sea salt, and soy lecithin. Note that most of the flavors above have all these same ingredients except for the flavoring and spices.

I definitely will not buy this flavor again and have since canceled my subscribe and save.

title: Enhances fish, vegetables, potatoes, and pasta with lemon butter sauce
text:
There are only two 1.76 ounce pouches. Each pouch seasons one to two meals depending on how much one wants to use. I used one pouch for two meals. My boyfriend and I liked the flavor as it spiced up our fish, kale, and pasta.

There are only about 2 tablespoons of lemon butter sauce in each pouch. It comes out like a cream (see second picture) and has to be heated up for a few minutes.

Overall, my boyfriend and I like this sauce but I doubt that it is economical to buy even though no price is currently listed. One full box of two pouches will only serve two to four people maximum.

Per the nutrition label (first photo), there are 4 servings per container and each pouch has 2 tablespoons at 70 calories each with 22% saturated fat.

title: There's no PECAN in the ingredient's list; only PECAN flavor which I cannot taste
text:
I use to hate caramel until I tried Tara's caramels about a year ago. Since then, I ordered every flavor except the [[ASIN:B079V19NL5 Bourbon]] since I do not like alcohol. Below are the caramels I ordered. The only ones I really like so far are the apple and the sea salt. Those are my two absolute favorites. This pecan caramel is definitely not one of my favorites.

[[ASIN:B079V32T1R Pecan Flavored]] - This one
[[ASIN:B079TSJ1TF Madagascar Vanilla]]
[[ASIN:B079TSHR6B Vanilla]]
[[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Sea Salt]] - 2nd favorite
[[ASIN:B079TZMFDH Apple]] - My favorite
[[ASIN:B079V1FYZB Mocha]]
[[ASIN:B079V154JV Licorice]]
[[ASIN:B07CZHDQVW Snickerdoodle]] - I have not tried these yet as they are still on order

They pecan caramels are soft, smooth, buttery, and creamy without being cloyingly sweet. Unfortunately, I am not impressed because the pecan flavoring leaves a bit of a burnt overtone to the caramel which makes me dislike these.

Three pieces are one serving and equal 130 calories. There are 3.5g of saturated fat, 15mg cholesterol, 45mg sodium, 15g sugar. However, if you care about the nutrition label, then do not eat these because it is nothing but corn syrup, sweetened condensed milk, heavy cream, invert sugar, butter, brown sugar, sugar, pecan natural flavor, sea salt, and soy lecithin.

I definitely will not buy this flavor again and have since canceled my subscribe and save.

title: Decent flavor with slightly gritty texture; not sweet; not bad for gluten free
text:
I am not a fan of gluten free products and I do not have to be because I am not allergic to gluten. However, I am impressed with this three box set of cupcakes, chocolate chip cookies, and bread. For something that is certified gluten-free; free of grains, soy, corn, dairy and gums/emulsifiers, this is pretty darn good. The main ingredient in every recipe is almond flour (see pictures 3, 8, and 13 for ingredients label). This almond flour is what I believe causes the slightly gritty texture which is not offensive to me at all.

I made all three boxes. I attached five pictures of the boxes, directions, ingredients list, and before and after photos of the finished product in the order of cupcakes, cookies, and bread.

The first box can be turned into muffins, cake, or cupcakes. I made 12 cupcakes. The second box can be turned into 12 chocolate chip cookies. The third box produces one small loaf of artisan bread.

I actually liked each of the resulting items with the bread being my least favorite. If one has a gluten allergy, this is an excellent option. I also love that the finished dessert, in the case of the cookies or cake, is not sweet. Overall, well done.

At the current price of $22.77, this comes to $7.59 per box which is a tad expensive but not by much.

title: Almost tasteless and what I can taste is not enjoyable to me
text:
This is five calories of carbonated water with 3% juice for flavor. Ginger and lemon are practically the last ingredients in the list. There is a faint ginger and lemon flavor but this water is mostly tasteless. Also, that faint flavor I do taste is not pleasurable to drink.

The ingredients are (see photo 2):
- carbonated water
- organic apple juice
- organic erythritol (Per my Internet search this is a sweet substance extracted from certain lichens and algae and is used medicinally to dilate blood vessels.)
- organic ginger juice
- organic natural flavors
- citric acid
- organic lemon juice concentrate
- organic stevia extract Reb A

I would never buy this after having tried it.

UPDATE: My boyfriend tried this and rated this 3 stars. He says it is just okay. Therefore, he can drink these.

title: Elevates a salad to the next level; has lots of sugar & nuts are greasy
text:
This review is for the Honey Roasted Sliced Almonds & Berries salad toppers.

These toppers elevate the flavor profile of a salad. They are delicious to me. The blueberries and cranberries in the batch I received were sweet with a medium to soft density and the roasted almonds were also sweet.

The entire package looks greasy (see glistening of clear part of package in first photo) and that is from the sunflower oil during processing. There is also a lot of sugar (9g or 13%) in each package from the honey roasted almonds and sweetened blueberries and cranberries. Each package is 130 calories, no cholesterol, 50mg of sodium, 4g protein, etc. (see third picture). The ingredients are honey roasted almonds, blueberries, and cranberries but read the ingredients list in second photo which details the ingredients used in processing such as sugar and sunflower oil.

Due to all the sugar and oil, I personally would not make this a daily salad topper habit. However, for an occasional treat these are wonderful.

title: Utterly delicious "candy bars" in disguise; crunchy, nutty, & flavorful
text:
These bars are incredibly delicious to me but they are "candy bars" in disguise. They are crunchy, nutty, and flavorful.

There are 12 bars per box broken down into the following four flavors in the order that I like them.

1- salted caramel flow - is crunchy, sweet, intensely caramel flavored, nice nutty taste
2- finger pickin BBQ - is crunchy with overwhelming barbecue flavor and slightly nutty
3- peanut butter pretzel - is crunchy, slightly sweet and salty, has a nice peanut butter taste
4- honey srirocka - is crunchy with a kick to it and slightly nutty; paprika and chili pepper comes through in the flavor

I attached a picture of all the bars and what each one looks like. I positioned the photo so that the wrapper is showing what bar it is. The ingredients are also in each associated photo. However, below is a list of the first three ingredients and the sodium and sugar listed on the nutrition label. All bars are 210 calories each and have zero cholesterol. Each bar is one serving size.

1- salted caramel flow - 280mg sodium, 9g of sugar (peanuts, brown rice syrup, pea protein, etc.)
2- finger pickin BBQ - 310mg sodium, 9g of sugar (peanuts, brown rice syrup, wheat flour, etc.)
3- peanut butter pretzel - 250mg sodium, 8g of sugar (peanuts, brown rice syrup, wheat flour, etc.)
4- honey srirocka - 280mg sodium, 8g of sugar (peanuts, brown rice syrup, pea protein, etc.)

In summary, these are super scrumptious to me. I am not fooling myself that these are healthy. To me these are a good choice for a candy bar. The current price of $16.88 comes to $1.41 per bar, which I feel is reasonable for this.

UPDATE 5/15/2019 - My boyfriend likes these nut bars a lot and rates them 4 stars.

UPDATE 5/17/2019 - My postman said he liked the bars as well and rates them 4 stars.

title: Popcorn is way too salty for my taste; butter flavor is subtle
text:
There are six boxes of four packages each for a total of 24 packages of microwavable popcorn. Each package is 2.5 servings. Of course, I ate the entire package as one serving. Each serving is 170 calories with 5g (25%) saturated fat , 10mg (3%) of cholesterol, 320mg (14%) of Sodium, 19g (7%) of carbs, 3g (11%) of dietary fiber, and 3g of protein.

Each bag takes 1-1/2 to 2-1/2 minutes to pop. Mine took 2 minutes and I was afraid to leave it longer even though there were still some unpopped kernels.

The resulting popcorn is just okay to me. I find it is way too salty and the butter flavor is subtle. However, it will satisfy a craving. If it was not so salty and the butter flavor was more intense, I would have rated this 5 stars.

At the current price of $35.15, it comes to about 1.46 per bag which is reasonable.

UPDATE - I gave two boxes to my postman and he really likes this. I got the impression this was 4.5 stars for him. Also, he did not find them too salty.

UPDATE 5/15/19 - I gave one box to my upstairs neighbors and they both said it was 4.5 stars; just the right amount of salt and butter. I also gave a box to my boyfriend and he does not care for the popcorn at all and rates it 2 stars. In addition, I gave a box to my other neighbor Sarah and she really liked it (another 4 stars).

title: Makes a strong cup of coffee black that tastes better with cream
text: My boyfriend and I love this coffee. Since it is a dark roast, we used the highest water setting on the Keurig. It is very strong black. Where it really shines with us is when creamer is added. This makes a delicious cup of coffee. Also, the current price of $17.48 for 80 K-cups comes only to $.22 cents per cup of joe.

title: Taste and texture is similar to Pirates Booty; first ingredient is corn meal
text:
This review is for Angie's BOOMCHICKAPOP White Cheddar Puffs, 6 Oz. (Pack of 12).

These taste like and the texture looks a lot like Pirates Booty. The first ingredient is corn meal. I like Pirates Booty as I usually get a craving for that once per year. However, these are not as satisfying as Pirates Booty to me because they do not have as strong a cheese flavor but they are still addictive. Obviously this is junk food. However, when I want to eat junk food, it usually has to knock my socks off and this is just okay to my palate. Even Pirates Booty does not knock my socks off; it just satisfies a craving.

There are 12 6-ounce bags in this shipment. The bags have an expiration date four months out, which means one has to eat a maximum of three bags per month before they go stale. However, that should be no problem because these can become addictive. It feels like each bag is one serving--which is not a good thing for the waistline or health.

The package claims it is non-GMO, gluten free, baked and not fried, made with real cheddar cheese, kosher, contains dairy (milk), and 0g trans fat per serving.

There are six servings in each bag and each serving is one ounce at 140 calories, 5% saturated fat (11% overall fat), 240mg Sodium, 85mg Potassium, 18g of carbohydrates with 2g coming from sugar, and 2g of protein.

The ingredients are corn meal, sunflower oil, whey, cheddar cheese, sea salt, natural flavor, lactic acid, yeast extract, and butter.
