This gets about 3.5 stars from me for taste and 3 stars for price. The current price of $43.33 comes to about $3.61 per 6 ounce bag. I ate two bags myself and gave away the rest.

- My postman said he thought they were alright and felt the same way I did - about 3 stars.

- My neighbor Debbie tried them and shared them with another neighbor and both said they were a little salty. Debbie said the bag was hard to open. I felt the same way about the bag being hard to open but did not feel it was worth mentioning until she also said it. They both said it was 3 stars.

- My boyfriend thinks this snack taste excellent. He says they do not stain your hands like the regular cheese covered snacks do. He did say the bag on this was impossible to open and he had to use a scissor. Overall, he rates this 5 stars.

- My other neighbor Sarah loves these puffs. She said it is nice and crunchy and satisfies her salty cravings. She rates it 5 stars.

- My other neighbor Marc and his girlfriend thought this was okay. They gave it 3 stars.

- My second postman's daughter also was given a bag and she LOVED this. She gives it 5 stars.

title: Mildly spiced, tasty, & a bit chewy; ate one entire two-serving pack as one serving
text:
There are eight packages in this box (first photo). Each package is two servings of 70 calories each with 11g of protein (last photo).

I expected this to be very spicy but is was very mild. I did not have to drink water even after eating an entire two-serving package and an additional piece from a second package. The jerky does sometimes have a fat vein running through it and some pieces are a bit chewy. Overall, however, it is very good (see second photo).

I do feel that the current price of $45.25 is a bit expensive because that comes to $5.68 per package. The sodium is 110mg per serving which is not too bad considering it is jerky.

The ingredients are:
- grass-fed and grass-finished beef (which is what makes this so expensive)
- organic coconut aminos
- pineapple juice
- honey
- water
- sea salt
- garlic powder
- onion powder
- white pepper
- paprika
- cayenne pepper
- jalapeno powder
- ground ginger
- black pepper

The flavor is very balanced considering the spicy ingredients. I usually eat my food bland, so it is actually surprising that I like the flavor.

UPDATE: I gave three bags to my POSTMAN and he loved it. He says this jerky is very, very good. When I first offered him the first package, he was not thrilled because he usually does not like jerky. Looks like this product converted him.

UPDATE 4/29/2019 - I gave three bags to my boyfriend and he also loved it. He gives this 5 stars.

title: Very spicy but very good, especially when combined with other food to tone down the spice
text:
When I first saw the price of $25.15 for this 5.7 ounce box of salsa verde with red beans, I considered that price insanely outrageous. However, although the website does not currently say this, there are eight 5.7 ounce boxes for this price. UNFORTUNATELY, I had to remove the photo showing you this because AMAZON REFUSED to post my review if it contained the picture that showed all eight boxes. Receiving eight boxes makes this a very reasonable purchase. I asked Amazon over a week ago to correct the website but am not sure when or if that will be done. I did the math and the cost per ounce is correct for eight boxes.

Each box contains one pouch (see fourth photo) that is equivalent to two servings when cooked. I just brought 1-1/2 cups of water and 1 tablespoon of butter to a boil. Once boiled, I added the box contents and then covered and simmered for 10 minutes. The resulting meal was al dente and very good but very spicy.

Ingredients:
- Red beans
- Parboiled brown rice
- Sugar
- Garlic
- Spices (including parsley, jalapeno pepper, black pepper, cumin, turmeric)
- Salt
- Onion
- Yeast extract
- Citric acid
- Lime juice concentrate
- Brown sugar
- Natural flavor

Each serving is 280 calories (550 calories if eating the entire container). There is 600mg of sodium (28%), 10g of protein, and 4g sugars per serving. See the third photo for the full nutrition label.

Photo five shows the full two servings in the pot and photo six shows the full two servings plated on one plate. In photo seven I added my own chickpeas and peas to the one serving of the mix to tone down the spice. I actually ate the entire two servings with the addition of the chickpeas and peas as my dinner. It was very good. This dish would pair well with chicken or fish.

I would definitely order this if I wanted a quick meal side. Although it is a bit spicy for my taste, I know how to easily tone that down with the addition of other items.

title: Extremely flavorful beverage with refreshing peach overtones & only 50 calories per can
text:
There are 24 - 8.45 fluid ounce cans in total. Each of the four boxes contains 6 cans. I attached a picture of one of the four boxes and one of the cans.

I do not drink soda or carbonated beverages. My beverage of choice is water most of the time and then skim milk or tea. However, I absolutely love the flavor of this drink. Each 8.45 fluid ounce can is 50 calories each all coming from the 12g of sugar. I wish the cans were larger. This is delicious to my palate. It tastes like peach Snapple on steroids (meaning with carbonation).

At the current price of $31.53, this comes to about $1.32 per can so it is a bit expensive. The taste, however, is fruity, peachy, and very flavorful to my palate.

UPDATE: I had both my POSTMAN and boyfriend try this and they loved this drink.

title: Adds subtle flavor to fish, eggs, soups, etc.
text:
This adds subtle flavor to fish, eggs, soups, and other dishes. I added it to my fish and it enhanced the dish.

The ingredients in order are:
- sea salt
- garlic
- spices (including red pepper, oregano, fennel)
- red bell pepper
- lemon peel
- sunflower oil

I tasted the salt, red pepper, and oregano but the other spices were muted.

title: Definitely cannot be a 1-1 substitute in baked goods as per the bag's instructions; tastes okay
text:
I am not allergic to gluten and have never knowingly eaten gluten-free items where there is supposed to be gluten. As far as this substance is concerned, I am totally illiterate on the subject. Nevertheless, I wanted to try this flour to see what all the rage is about. It is vegan, GMO free, corn free, soya free, and peanut free.

I was hoping the bag would point me to recipes but it did not. Instead it did even better than that. It says this flour can be substituted 1 to 1 with their all purpose mix which I assume is any all purpose flour. To me this means, whenever a recipe calls for 1 cup of all purpose flour, I can use 1 cup of this flour instead. Therefore, that is what I did.

This bag is a full 3 cups and my chocolate chip recipe needed a full 3 cups of flour. This was my only substitution; this flour for my ordinary flour. Well, attached are the before and after picture. The final product of 25 to 30 chocolate chip cookies came out as one full cookie on each baking sheet as can be seen in the last photo.

What I learned is that this flour cannot be substituted out without some sort of adjustment. What adjustment that is, I have no idea as it would require research.

In the end, the cookies were okay tasting. I did not like that they were flat squares but I personally would not buy this flour because I did not like the results. However, if someone has a gluten allergy, this is pretty good flour if one can figure out how to bypass all that flatness that results from using this.

Note that there are 10 servings in this package (whatever that means because it was a full 3 cups of flour). Each serving is 130 calories with 32g of carbs and 1g of protein.

There is a warning on the package not to eat the raw batter.

Ingredients are:
- Organic tapioca starch
- Organic sorghum flour
- Organic millet flour
- Organic potato startch
- Organic green buckwheat flour
- Emulsifier (xanthan gum)

title: Utterly disgusting; has a super burnt taste to me but my postman thinks it tastes okay
text:
This would have received 1 star from me. However, I gave a bar to my postman to try and he actually thought it was okay but did say (after eating half a bar in less than two minutes), that it has an aftertaste. He gives it 3 stars.

I tried a piece the size of a fingernail and could not stomach this. It smells awful and tastes even worst to my palate. It smells and tastes of extra burnt caramel; as if someone left caramel in the pot too long.

There are four 2.8 ounce bars in this box and each bar is 160 calories with 130 calories from fat. The ingredients are unsweetened chocolate, cocoa butter, erythritol, inulin, dextrin, whole milk power, milk fat, natural flavors, stevia extract, organic soy lecithin, vanilla, and sea salt. See second photo for nutrition label.

title: Too intense black but great light with half and half
text:
I ordered these for my boyfriend and he loved the brew but he did say he did not taste the French Vanilla Flavor. We use the medium-cup size on the Keurig. I tried this black (see photo) and felt it was too strong / almost burnt flavor. However, when I made it light with half and half it tasted great and I could detect the French Vanilla.

There are 100 pods to the box. My boyfriend gave this 5 stars and I gave this 3.5, hence the 4-star rating.

title: Flavor is exceptionally balanced with just the right amount of "kick"
text:
Since this is such a large jar, I brought this over my friend's house so three of us can try this. I was afraid I would not like it because it has pepper in it. However, I loved the flavor. It enhanced the flavor of the pork chops my friend made (see last photo) and I kept coming back for more sauce. My friend loved it too. We both gave it five stars. Her son thought it was very good and gave it 4 stars.

There are 30 calories in 2 tablespoons with 250mg sodium and 5g sugars.

The first eight ingredients are:
- tomato puree
- distilled vinegar
- sugar
- molasses
- salt
- spices (including black pepper, paprika, chili pepper)
- corn starch
- brown sugar
- etc.

title: Cookies are soft, sweet, & tasty but are too costly & packaging is overdone
text:
These cookies are moist, soft, sweet, and tasty. Although they are not my favorite type of cookie, they do taste freshly baked. My problem is with the overdone packaging and the cost.

There are 3 boxes of 6 cookies each for a total of 18 cookies for the current price of $18.01. These cookies are small, so that is a $1 per cookie which is not cheap. Also, each box, contains two plastic inserts inside the wrapped cookies. As can be seen from the second photo, there are three cookies in the bottom plastic bray and four cookies on top. I assume this is to keep them from being broken but it seems overkill.

Each box contains six servings. One cookie is one serving. Each serving is 100 calories with 4% cholesterol, 115mg sodium 17g of carbs due to the 23% sugar. It is a cookie so this is to be expected. If these were about 60 cents per cookie (no more than $11) and there was less packaging, then I might have rated these 5 stars.

PS - I gave a box to my Postman and he said they were soft (he expected hard cookies) and too sweet and he really did not care for them. The impression I got is that these are 2.5 to 3 stars to him with regard to taste.

title: Super high glycemic; easy to overindulge; just like candy but does not taste as sweet
text:
I am glad I only received one of the twelve 3.5 ounce packages for review because if I had not, I probably would have eaten myself sick. These are delicious to me and do not taste overly sweet but they are somewhat sweet. This is NOT a health food and one package is 2.5 servings at 185 calories per serving. This means I ate almost 600 calories by eating the entire bag in less than 5 minutes.

Each serving has 4g (21%) of saturated fat, 0 cholesterol, 85mg of sodium, 14g of sugar; 2g of protein, 5% of potassium 240mg, etc. See first photo for full nutritional label and ingredients list.

The ingredients in order are:

- Peanut butter cup coating (made of dried cane syrup, cacao beans, certified sustainable palm kernel oil, peanut flour, coacao butter, sea salt, sunflower lecithin; & soy lecitin)

- bananas

- peanut flour

In summary, these are incredibly delicious and I am glad I have no more as they are addictive and unhealthy like candy. The label states these are Kosher, vegetarian, grain free, and made with fair trade chocolate. As long as these do not melt in shipment, they should look like my second photo with dehydrated bananas on the inside. The texture is chewy with a subtly sweet taste.

title: Almost 7 pounds of cafeteria food that is a bit bland but has "heat" to it; best over pasta or rice
text:
NOTE: Since Amazon is combining reviews, this review is for the canned chorizo skillet.

First, this is not my kind of food. I prefer fresh, whole ingredients as grown in nature. However, this is my boyfriend's kind of meal. He is someone that prefers food in boxes, cans, and packages. Despite our differences in food, we both felt the same about this meal.

I heated this up over his place. We both agreed this tasted bland with a little heat but it was okay over pasta and would probably be even better over rice or with eggs. If we had parsley, we would have put that on top to give it some color.

As can be seen from the pictures, this reminds me of canned hash and some other animal food I prefer not to mention. It looked like there was corn in the can but it is not in the ingredients list, so I guess we were wrong. All I saw, other than the pulverized meat, was the potatoes. There are some microscopic green and red flakes throughout but the entire meal is pretty much tasteless and it was the pasta that enhanced it a bit. It was okay enough that we both had two servings but I left the rest at my boyfriend's house because it is more his thing. We both agreed this was three stars--just okay.

There are 12 one-cup servings per container at 510 calories. Each serving has 60% saturated fat yet zero trans fats. There are 14 grams of protein, 1050mg (46%) sodium, and 75mg (25%) cholesterol.

The ingredients are:
- cooked chorizo
- rehydrated potatoes
- potatoes
- water
- 2% or less of dried green bell peppers, dried red bell peppers, sugar, dried onions, salt, natural flavors, soybean oil, sulfiting agents

It is distributed by Nestle and is for the food or cafeteria industry and not the consumer market. This is why this comes in an almost 7-pound heavy-duty can.

title: My container had eight sticks and they were fresh & mildly flavored
text:
There are only eight sticks in the container I received which is a very small quantity. I usually buy cinnamon sticks by the pound at the Indian stores which is the best way to purchase them because there is a large quantity for a cheap price of $3, $4, $5, or $6.

I used one of these sticks for my hot tea and it proved to be a mild flavor enhancer. I usually use about 4 whole cinnamon sticks when cooking rice pudding. However, they are great in tea as well if one wants a hint of spice.

title: Flakes are too small to act as a flavor enhancer; it's more of a colorant
text:
So far I have tried seven Jackie's Kitchen spices in the past several weeks (includes this one) and am impressed with how flavorful and fresh these are. However, this particular one does not meet up with my expectations. I find the parsley flakes to be too small to act as a good flavor enhancer. I tried this several times already and can barely taste the parsley in the dish. It smells nice and it tastes fresh when I lick it plain out of my hand but for recipes, this enhances the colors in my dish more than the flavor.

If the flakes were bigger, this would be great in my macaroni salad or meatloaf. Parsley flakes are also good for stews, rice dishes, eggs, on fish, etc.

title: Rich, delicious, crunchy espresso beans covered in luscious dark chocolate
text:
Yum! I absolutely love these chocolate covered espresso beans. They are rich, delicious, and crunchy. There are 5-1/2 servings in this 8 ounce bag at 170 calories per serving with 17g of sugar and 2g of protein. It does not say how much caffeine is in each serving. I never thought I would ever be eating a coffee bean but these are great.

See the second photo for the full nutrition label.

UPDATE - I had my POSTMAN try these and he LOVED them.

title: Tastes way too sweet for me
text:
I love dark chocolate so I thought I would love this. There is a combination of dark chocolate coated cranberries and yogurt (white) coated blueberries in one 8 ounce package. However, I find this to be cloyingly sweet even though these are actual covered blueberries and cranberries.

There are 5-1/2 servings per package at 180 calories per serving with 25g of sugar. See the second photo for the full nutrition label.

If these were not so sweet, i would have rated them four stars.

UPDATE - I had my POSTMAN try these and he said these are WAY TOO SWEET, especially the yogurt (white) ones.

title: I added this to a bean and tomato dish and it added another flavor dimension
text:
Over the past months I have tried several Jackie's Kitchen spices and they are very fresh and flavorful. I have yet to find one I did not like. This pimento add another flavor dimension to my bean and tomato dish. I am not one to use pimento often but I have added it to any fish that is white, such as tilapia, or on hard boiled eggs.

Although this is a good addition to soups, stews, casseroles, and vegetables if a recipe calls for it, I tend to use it mostly on fish and eggs.

title: These dried basil leaves enhanced the flavor of my rice dish
text:
Over the past months I have tried several Jackie's Kitchen spices and they are very fresh and flavorful. I have yet to find one I did not like. These dried basil leaves enhanced the flavor of my rice dish.

This is perfect in rice dishes, on pasta or potato salad, egg salad, soups, marinara sauce, stews, gravies, and soups. I plan to use this in a cold macaroni salad dish one day soon.

title: Portable, sweet, thick yogurt but without any live & beneficial cultures
text:
There are 60 portable packages of yogurt in six boxes (10 per box) of two flavors (blueberry and berry). There is no need to refrigerate this unless it is opened. Once it is opened it either needs to be consumed right away or put in the fridge for no longer than 24 hours after which time it should thrown away.

To consume, just screw off the top and suck up like a straw while squeezing the package into your mouth. It was easiest if I titled my head back to "eat" it. The blueberry does not taste like blueberry to me but just a conglomerate of berries. The berry flavor tastes mostly like strawberry to me.

Each pouch is one serving of 90 calories with 15 from fat, 5% saturated fat, 3% cholesterol, 12g of sugar, and 4g of protein, etc. See photo two for the full nutrition label and ingredients. Cultured reduced fat milk and can sugar are the first two ingredients in each.

In summary, this tastes like a sugary milk snack to me and it lacks the beneficial cultures of yogurt. I think this is better than candy but I do not believe this is as healthy as real yogurt. It does taste pretty good and it is smooth and creamy. Also the fruit comes from either blueberry or strawberry/raspberry puree. After having tried this, I would not buy it unless I needed a portable source of milk protein. Also, at the current price of $49.88, it is expensive as it comes to a little more than .83 cents per 3 ounce serving.

UPDATE March 9, 2019 - I gave some to my Postman and he really likes these. He said they taste very good to him.

UPDATE March 11, 2019 - I gave these to my boyfriend and he said they are "not bad really" but it was "difficult to get past the serving method" (meaning having to sip it up like a straw).

title: I would not call these granola bars but they taste GREAT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
text:
To me granola is hard, dry, and crumbly. This are chewy, of medium softness, and OH SO DELICIOUS. Each bar is 170 calories with 9g of total fat (3g or 15% saturated fat, 125mg sodium, and 0 cholesterol. There is 11g of total sugars and 4g protein per bar. See fourth photo for full nutrition facts.

The first seven ingredients in order are:
- roasted peanuts
- sugar
- corn syrup
- whole grain oats
- palm kernel oil
- whole grain wheat
- rice flour
- etc.

See the third photo for the full ingredients list. The first photo is what the bar looks like on top and the second picture shows the "glaze" on the bottom.

There are 48 bars in the box (mine had 49 and I counted it twice). At the current price of $16.88 that comes to .35 cents per bar.

Obviously, these are not as healthy as plain roasted unsalted peanuts. Nevertheless, these are much better than many other bars I tried in the past. I really love the taste and texture. It was hard to stop eating them. The only reason I lowered the rating one star is because there is nothing granola about these in my opinion.

I actually got these for my boyfriend but almost did not want to let them go. He loves them as well and I made sure he took them out of my sight.

UPDATE March 4, 2019 - I gave two bars to my Postman to try and he said they were great. He loves them.

title: The "powdered sauce" is only mildly reminiscent of marinara; lower in sodium than other "flavors"
text:
There are four 9.45 ounce boxes and at the current price of $18.99 it comes to $4.75 per box which makes this extremely expensive. I personally would not pay that high price. Nevertheless, the price is up to how much the purchaser wants to pay.

Each box supposedly makes three servings but I feel it is more like two servings or even one serving as it is easy to overeat this. In fact, I turned this into one serving for me.

All I had to do is dump the rotini into two quarts of boiling water and cook for nine minutes. Once the pasta is cooked and drained, I stirred in two tablespoons of butter, a half cup of milk, and the one seasoning packet. I then blended it and let it stand for two minutes before serving.

The last picture shows it plated and what is equivalent to one serving which is 410 calories, with 450 mg of sodium, and 30% saturated fat. The first photo shows the nutrition facts and ingredients list.

Overall, this is very expensive and the powdered sauce is only mildly reminiscent of marinara. Thank God for [[ASIN:B07LCNDNPH Jackie's Kitchen Italian Seasoning]] which helped "doctor it up." I would not buy this blend. The only "flavor" of theirs I like is their [[ASIN:B07C9SS9FD Alfredo flavored version"]].

title: Great for adding a little bit of "heat" to homemade dishes
text:
I have tried several Jackie's Kitchen spices recently and they are excellent. I used this to spice up a pasta dish for some "heat" and it enhanced the flavor.

This is also good on eggs, seafood, meats, veges, soups, etc.

Overall, I would buy this brand.

title: Changed the flavor of my pasta dish for the better
text:
I have tried several Jackie's Kitchen spices recently and they are excellent. I used this to spice up a pasta dish and it changed the flavor for the better. I plan on using it when making Moroccan food; like their stews that call for this spice.

Overall, I would buy this brand.

title: The powdered cheese sauce for this BROKEN to pieces lentil rotini is chemical tasting
text:
There are four 10.3 ounce boxes and at the current price of $18.99 it comes to $4.75 per box which makes this extremely expensive. I personally would not pay that high price. Nevertheless, the price is up to how much the purchaser wants to pay.

Each box supposedly makes three servings but I feel it is more like two servings or even one serving as it is easy to overeat this. In fact, I turned this into one serving for me.

All I had to do is dump the rotini into two quarts of boiling water and cook for nine minutes. At first, I thought I got the wrong pasta because the raw noodles are red in color; however, it changes to white after it is cooked and looks like what is on the front of the box. Once the pasta is cooked and drained, I stirred in two tablespoons of butter, a half cup of milk, and the two seasoning packets. I then blended it and let it stand for two minutes before serving.

The second picture shows it plated and what is equivalent to one serving which is 430 calories, with 820 mg of sodium, and 30% saturated fat. The first photo shows the nutrition facts and ingredients list.

Overall, this is very expensive and I do not care at all for the flavor of the powdered cheese sauce. I would not buy this blend. In addition, there were a lot of broken pieces of pasta in the boxes.

title: Full bodied, flavorful arabica roast; we love it
text: These beans brew into a full bodied and flavorful cup of coffee. I love the smell of the oily beans before they are ground. After they are ground, the fragrance is intoxicating to me. This did have a hint of bitterness to it black but with half-and-half it is excellent. We both love this coffee.

title: Extremely fresh; adds a nice flavor to coffee and is perfect for baked goods
text:
Recently I have been trying Jackie's Kitchen spices and they are excellent. This is a 12-pack of pumpkin pie spice which is a mixture of cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, and cloves. My boyfriend and I used it in our coffee and it added another dimension to the brew including toning down any bitterness.

I plan on using this in cupcakes or muffins and with french toast. I can also be used in pies, cakes, and cookies and to top off the foam on a cappuccino.

UPDATE Feb 18, 2019 - I used this to make cranberry chutney and it came out great (see photo).

title: Fresh crushed red pepper; it spiced up my scrambled eggs nicely
text: I new to trying this brand of spice and am so far impressed. This crushed red pepper is extremely fresh. I used it in my scrambled eggs this morning and it added a nice flavor to the meal. I plan on using this in soups or pasta sauces. This appears to be a great brand. The one I received is good until 2023.

title: This really enhances the flavor of many dishes; also tastes very fresh
text:
As per the label, this is all organic with a combination of the four following ingredients.

- Basil
- Oregano
- Marjoram
- Rosemary

I used this in a pasta dish I made and it really enhanced the flavor and the ingredients tasted so fresh. I plan to use this on fish and in pasta sauces and soups. The container I got is good until 2023.

I never heard of this brand, but so far I am very pleased. At the current price of .72 cents this is an absolute steal.

title: The powdered cheese sauce for this lentil rotini is chemical tasting
text:
There are four 11 ounce boxes and at the current price of $18.99 it comes to $4.75 per box which makes this extremely expensive. I personally would not pay that high price. Nevertheless, the price is up to how much the purchaser wants to pay.

Each box supposedly makes three servings but I feel it is more like two servings or even one serving as it is easy to overeat this. In fact, I turned this into two servings for me.

All I had to do is dump the rotini into two quarts of boiling water and cook for nine minutes. At first, I thought I got the wrong pasta because the raw noodles are red in color; however, it changes to white after it is cooked and looks like what is on the front of the box. Once the pasta is cooked and drained, I stirred in two tablespoons of butter, a half cup of milk, and the two seasoning packets. I then blended it and let it stand for two minutes before serving.

The last picture shows it plated and what is equivalent to one serving which is 450 calories, with 840 mg of sodium, and 30% saturated fat. The second photo shows the nutrition facts and ingredients list.

Overall, this is very expensive and I do not care at all for the flavor of the powdered cheese sauce. I would not buy this blend.

title: This lentil rotini is incredibly starchy / pasty but I really like that and the flavor
text:
There are four 9.52 ounce boxes and at the current price of $18.99 it comes to $4.75 per box which makes this extremely expensive. I personally would not pay that high price. Nevertheless, the price is up to how much the purchaser wants to pay.

Each box supposedly makes three servings but I feel it is more like two servings or even one serving as it is easy to overeat this. In fact, I turned this into two servings for me.

All I had to do is dump the rotini into two quarts of boiling water and cook for nine minutes. At first, I thought I got the wrong pasta because the raw noodles are red in color; however, it changes to white after it is cooked and looks like what is on the front of the box. Once the pasta is cooked and drained, I stirred in two tablespoons of butter, a half cup of milk, and the seasoning packet. I then blended it and let it stand for two minutes before serving.

The last picture shows it plated and what is equivalent to one serving which is 400 calories, with 520 mg of sodium, and 30% saturated fat. The second photo shows the nutrition facts and ingredients list.

Overall, this is fine for a once-in-awhile treat. However, it is very expensive and not everyone will like the starchy or pasty texture/taste of this pasta. I just happen to like that "stick to your ribs" kind of meal once in a while.

title: Balanced flavor of coffee with sweetener; do not taste the caramel but we do not care about that
text:
There are 12 8.1 fluid ounce cans in the package. Each can is 80 calories with 1.5g saturated fat, 125mg sodium, 13g sugars, 1g protein. There 134mg caffeine per serving. This is a product of Japan. It can be served chilled or over ice.

INGREDIENTS are coffee (water, coffee), milk, sugar, cream, natural flavor, sodium bicarbonate, potassium phosphate, pectin.

I noticed another reviewer had mentioned that there are small specks of dirt or some black debris in the rim of the can. Sure enough he is correct. I rinsed the top of each can before use to remove them.

As recommended, I shook the can well. However, it fizzes over when opening it, so be prepared. Both my boyfriend and I drank this and we agree that it tastes good. Neither one of us detect any caramel. However, we think an 8.1 ounce serving is too small.

Overall, this is a tasty caffeinated beverage that we wish were at least 12 to 16 ounces. It would also be nice if the can tops were clean.

title: Tastes as delicious as other pastas so it all comes down to price for me
text:
This pasta is very good as is most store bought pasta I have ever tried. The ingredients are semolina (wheat), durum flour (wheat), niacin, ferrous sulfate (iron), thiamin mononitrate, riboflavin, and folic acid. There are six 2-ounce servings in the package at 200 calories each. I boiled it for 10 minutes for an al dente pasta. Attached is a picture of the finished product. My boyfriend and I found it absolutely delicious.

Even though this tastes great, I would never pay $11.07 for one 12-ounce serving of pasta. It is for that reason only that I rate this 5-star pasta 4 stars.

title: Delicious well-balanced flavor in a medium amber brew; not sure about its detox effects
text:
This green tea is very good. It brews to a medium amber. The ingredients are nettle leaves, ginger, green mate, green tea, spearmint, turmeric, sunflower petals, rose petals, mallow flowers, and natural citrus flavors.

I brewed it at 185 degrees instead of 175 and steeped it for 4 minutes as this is the way I like to brew anything with green tea in it. The spearmint flavor comes through with other flavors that are very well balanced.

All the ingredients are supposedly good for me; however, I can never attest to the detox claims. My only disappointment is with the packaging. Tea should always be stored in a container that light does not penetrate. The front of this package is silver but the back is completely clear exposing the tea to light. I would have preferred it to be covered completely or to at least have only a slight peek-a-boo hole there.

Overall, this is an enjoyable brew.

title: Convenient and very tasty & somewhat chunky but expensive clam chowder; did not taste bacon
text:
For the current price of $21.99, one gets four servings of easily prepared clam chowder that costs $5.50 per serving. I just boiled the pouch in boiling water for five minutes and then served it. I would have preferred my chowder hotter. Next time, I will put the soup in a dish in my steamer and heat it up that way so that I can be sure it is hot.

Each serving is 340 calories with 210 fat calories. There are 580 mg of sodium in each serving. I attached a picture of both the nutrition label and ingredients list. The first several ingredients in order are water, cream, onions, potatoes, sea clams & sea clam juice, razor clams, carrots, celery, etc. I did not taste the bacon at all, which is the tenth ingredient in the list. Nevertheless, I found this to be tasty.

Overall, I like the way this is seasoned and it tastes good to me. It is great for convenience purposes or for eating on the run. It can be eaten out of the pouch because the bottom sits firmly on a table. I also like that it is chunky even if the chunks are mostly potatoes and celery.

title: This makes a golden brew of flavored green tea
text:
This smells like a cake or sweet praline to me. I do not taste the chocolate or hazelnut but there is some nutty flavor. It has an overall sweet taste and, unfortunately, I hate it. However, my boyfriend loves it--hence the 3-star rating. This really got 2.5 stars since I give it 1 star and he gives it 5 stars.

I brewed this at 175 degrees (box recommends 176) and steeped it for almost 4 minutes (box recommends 2-3). The resulting brew is a golden hue as can be seen in the photos.

The ingredients are gen mai cha (roasted rice, green tea, & popped rice), brittle pieces (sugar, hazelnut), cocoa husks, sweet blackberry leaves, chicory root, almonds, carob, and natural and artificial flavor.

title: Excellent selection of 10 green teas; infuser is needed
text:
This is an excellent selection of 10 green teas. Each packet contains one serving of loose leaf tea. I have to use my own infuser to brew each cup.

The 10 teas included are:
- China Jasmine (light brew is pictured in my photo)
- Gorgeous Geisha
- Geisha Getaway
- Green Rose
- Jade Moutain
- Japanese GMC Sencha
- Japanese Sencha
- Lemon Sorbet
- Marrakech
- Sencha Peach

All are brewed at 176 degrees Fahrenheit and steeped for two to three minutes for a wonderfully delicious and varied brew.

There are only three disappointments I have. First, the Lemon Sorbet label needs to be redesigned. The label is a bright yellow with white lettering and it is IMPOSSIBLE to read. Second, I wish these came in their own "silk" tea bag. Since these are so expensive, that would elevate the experience. Instead, each tea is $1.50 per cup and one needs their own infuser. Third, this does not exactly come in a gift box. Mine came in a squashed box similar to store bought brands in a box. Nevertheless, these teas are EXCELLENT!

title: Nice balance of flavors for a K-cup tea; it is a strong organic oolong brew
text:
NOTE - Amazon combines reviews for several tea varieties. My review is for the Metabolism Oolong Tea.

There are 12 single serve K-cups in the box and at the current price of $15.69 that is $1.31 per cup. I consider that to be very expensive. I am always skeptical of claims that a tea boots metabolism due to the superfoods and probiotics it is infused that I suspect also drives up the cost.

TEA INGREDIENTS:
- Organic oolong tea leaves
- Organic garcinia cambogia powder
- Organic orange peel powder
- Organic yerba mate powder
- Organic rose hip extract
- Bacillus Coagulans at 1 billion CFU at time of manufacturing
- Organic morenga leaf powder

As can be seen from the photo, one K-cup makes a strong brew. I actually like the taste considering that it is pulverized tea and other ingredients in a K-cup. It has a nice balance of orange and grassy overtones that is pleasing to my palate. As for the metabolism claims, I am skeptical. One would have to drink a lot of this tea to see if those claims are true. There are no warnings on the package as to how many cups to drink in a day, so I assume this is safe to consume several times a day if desired.

PS This is considered non-GMO and vegan.

title: Each fruit strip is 25 calories with 8g natural sugar; not necessarily healthy but better than candy
text: My boyfriend and I tried these and they taste pretty good. There are 30 individually wrapped flat fruit strips per box. The ingredients are organic apple puree and organic grape puree concentrate. There's less than 1g of protein and 8g of sugars. It is made from whole fruit and non-GMO. It is not much better than a candy bar in my opinion; however, I would rather choose this snack than a candy bar.

title: This coffee is bold and bitter without cream but with cream it is good
text: My boyfriend and I tried this coffee both plain and with cream. Plain it is very bitter. With cream it is not as bitter and tastes pretty good considering it is a very bold brew.

title: This mustard has a grainy texture and lighter flavor than a smooth mustard & is perfect for recipes
text:
I purchased this mustard several times before and have used it in recipes. They changed the label and serving size on it, but this relabeled version delivers the same good flavor.

My favorite mustard is Batampte Deli Style (which is smooth), but I only use that on deli meats or the rare hot dog I might eat. However, that is not the type of mustard I would use for recipes. For recipes, I prefer whole grain. I find that whole grain mustard permits me to make a topping for a fish disk without overpowering the fish. For instance I made this walnut-sage crusted rainbow trout (see picture) with this Maille Mustard and it complemented the dish perfectly. For me it is a matter of using the right mustard for the right dish.

There are six 7.3 ounce jars in the package.

title: These are delicious for both man and woman
text:
I eats nuts daily and after trying [[ASIN:B07322XVRK their other variety]], I decided to purchase these. I love them. They are unsalted but wholesome. There are 22 one-ounce serving size packages. Yes it is expensive but for the high price I get variety and no salt as well as wholesome fresh nuts. Each serving is 163 calories with 130 fat calories.

I am perplexed as to why this is marketed to men; to me that is ridiculous. These are good enough for everyone.

The attached photo shows the breakdown of nuts in each package. I will definitely purchase these again along with their other variety I like.

title: These elbows cook to al dente in 8 minutes in boiling water and taste great
text:
There are 12 one-pound bags and for the current price of $15.84, it comes to a very reasonable $1.32 per bag (i.e. per pound). I usually use elbows for cold macaroni salad or soups as they are the perfect size for those dishes. Today I just cooked it for 8 minutes until al dente and added olive oil, grated cheese, broccoli, prosciutto, and sun-dried tomatoes.

The first photo shows the ingredient list, second photo is plain with no toppings, and third photo is with all the trimmings.

Ingredients:
- Durum Wheat Semolina
- Niacin
- Iron Lactate
- Thiamin Mononitrate
- Riboflavin
- Folic Acid
- Water
(It contains gulten and wheat and may contain soy.)

This is one of many good tasting pastas on the market. I would definitely order this again as I would many other brands.

title: Fragrant delicious rice in about 7 minutes; 5 minutes after the water boils
text:
This rice is fragrant and fluffy just like the box claims. There are about seven (half cup uncooked) servings in the package. I just boiled one cup of water, then stirred in one cup of this precooked jasmine rice, removed the pot from the burner, covered it, and waited five minutes. The picture shows two servings. Each serving is about 1 cup of cooked rice which is equivalent to half a cup of uncooked rice.

This jasmine rice is delicious and fragrant and fluffy. Although I have a rice maker and prefer to make my own rice, this is perfect for the times I want it in a hurry. Also, it is perfect for the elderly person who has trouble cooking because this is quick and easy. I might turn my 91-year old friends on to this since they have trouble cooking rice even with a rice cooker.

title: These hazelnuts are pretty good; however, I like them better with the skin on
text: I bought this bag in either Costco or BJs a while back and I am almost finished with them. They are very good. I never ate hazelnuts without the skin on, so these are less bitter than the skin on hazelnuts. After trying these, I prefer raw hazelnuts and not roasted.  However, these are perfect for recipes because they are already "skinned."

title: I'm nuts over nuts and these hazelnuts taste very good to me--like right out of the shell
text:
I purchased [[ASIN:B0797CV511 Nature's Garden Roasted Hazelnuts]] in the past and they are good but not great. I tend to like the skins on even though it makes the nut taste slightly bitter.

These nuts tasted as fresh as if I cracked the hazelnut myself. As soon as I got them I could not wait to dig in. I would definitely order these again over the roasted ones. However, if these are being used in a recipe, the ones without the skins would be better unless you plan to roast and "de-skin" them yourself.

title: This is made with red lentil flour but it tastes too starchy for my tastebuds
text:
Note that I received one box of red lentil penne as a reviewer and not the entire variety pack. However, purchasers should receive the entire four-box set.

The only ingredient in this pasta is red lentil flour. It cooks in 7 minutes to "al dente." Cook for 1 to 2 more minutes if a softer pasta is desired.

I cooked it 7 minutes and it was firm. This pasta is firmer than the regular or whole wheat pastas I love. It also has a starchy taste to me and sort of just "breaks" in my mouth as opposed to that smooth chewy texture of other pastas. I love red lentil "beans" in a soup but this pasta is not to my liking. I tried it plain, with olive oil, and with pasta sauce and sausage. Nothing helped make this palatable to me.

The third picture shows the nutrition label. It is 180 calories for a 2 ounce serving or 330 calories for a 3.5 ounce serving.

Since it is red in color, it plates very well as you can see from the last picture.

Unfortunately, I would not purchase red lentil pasta after having tried it.

title: Each packet makes one pot of delicious fiesta chicken in a slow cooker
text:
There are 12 packets to the box. There are easy directions on the back of each packet. The aroma emanating from my slow cooker during the cooking process infused my condo with an enticing "scent." The resulting dish is savory.

DIRECTIONS:
- Add two pounds of chicken breast to the empty slow cooker
- Combine one package of seasoning mix with two 14.5 ounce cans of undrained diced tomatoes, one 15.75 ounce can of whole kernel corn, and one 15 ounce can of drained/rinsed black beans.
- Add that mixture to the slower cooker and cook on high for four hours or low for eight hours.
- When cooked, break up the chicken into pieces and return to the pot, mix in, and serve over a bed of rice.
- Add shredded cheese, sour cream, chipped cilantro, or green onions as a topping if desired.

I added an additional pound of chicken breast and an additional can of diced tomatoes to the recipe. In addition, I cut the kernels off of eight ears of fresh corn. I should have also added an extra can of beans to get more of the black beans showing throughout the disk but I did not have another can.

The resulting meal was delicious. I plated it over a bed of steamed brown rice and added some grated Parmesan on top. Each seasoning packet is eight servings. Since I usually like less spice, I increased the ingredients for the same packet. Nevertheless, it was wonderfully tasty and so easy to make. I would definitely order this product again.

UPDATE - I gave some to my boyfriend and he said "Wow, this is really delicious" and then ate two servings.

title: A powerhouse of vitamins & minerals that can be added to cereal, smoothies, yogurt, etc.
text:
I tried whole hemp seeds and I did not like them. However, my boyfriend and I tried these hulled hemp seeds in [[ASIN:B07D89SP8L organic]] and loved them that we also decided to try them in the non-organic variety. Both varieties taste the same and pack the same nutriional punch except that at the current price of $14.99 for 24 ounces it is a lot more and a lot cheaper than their organic variety.

We both had this in our cereal (oatmeal and regular cereal) and it did not distort the flavor of what we were eating. There is a nice rice-like mouth feel to eating this and there is not much taste to it but it is nutrient dense. This reminds me of chia seeds which have no flavor but add nutritional value to a meal.

The only ingredient is hulled hemp seed. Each serving of 3 tablespoons is 180 calories. I personally only use 1 tablespoon.
