title: This pasta tastes no different to me than regular pasta; it is delicious and apparently "diabetic friendly"
text: I purchased two 12 ounce boxes of Aliveris "diabetic friendly" elbows at Home Goods simply because I feel it is healthier and lower on the glycemic index than regular pasta. Each box was priced at $2.29 which is a lot cheaper than Amazon. This tastes like regular pasta and is delicious. The ingredients are organically grown durum wheat semolina and soy germ. I absolutely love it and would purchase this again at an equivalent price. Cooking time is also the same as regular pasta.

title: Oat bars that are definitely not a health food and contain lots of sugar but they are better than candy
text:
When I ordered these, I did not realize this is a mix that has to be prepared and baked. I thought these were already prepared oat bars. Nevertheless, this is super easy to make. In less than five minutes I mixed together one bag of mix, 1/4 cup of vegetable oil, and two tablespoons of water. I spread the mix into a lightly greased pan and patted it firmly down. Then I baked on 325 degrees for 25 minutes. Once it was cooled, I cut them into bars.

The bars are chewy and delicious but they are definitely not healthy. They are also a bit crumbly. Cinnamon, raisins, and sugar are the predominant flavors that shine through. They are also a bit greasy; perhaps the 1/4 cup oil recommended is a bit too much. However, for a light snack that is one step up from candy, they are acceptable as a once in a while treat. After the oats in the ingredients list, most of the remaining ingredients are sugar and oil. I count the raisins as a form of "hidden" sugar.

There are six bags. Each bag makes 16 double-postage-stamp-sized bars (see picture). Each bar would be 100 calories as prepared with 15 fat calories. There's 40 mg of sodium, 1g of fiber, 11g of sugar, and 2g of protein in each serving.

The mix ingredients in order (not counting the oil and water added) are:
- Organic oats
- Organic raisins (organic raisins, organic sunflower oil)
- Organic biodynamic cane sugar
- Organic brown sugar
- Organic identity preserved wheat flour
- Organic sunflower oil
- Organic cornstarch
- Organic cinnamon
- Organic vanilla extract (organic sugar, organic vanilla extractives)
- Sea salt

PS I have no clue what biodynamic cane sugar and identity preserved wheat flour are.

UPDATE: I had my boyfriend try one of the bars and he said he could really taste the sugar in these. He did say they were good but that sugar was the predominate taste to him.

title: Makes 8 pitchers of smooth, refreshing cold brew coffee without any bitterness so it is easy to drink black
text:
This cold brew coffee is superb. There are four boxes which are enough to produce eight pitchers of cold brew coffee. At the current price of $30.86 that comes to $3.86 per brewed pitcher which is not bad.

Each box contains two packages and inside each package are two mesh filter packs filled with coffee. I opened one package, put four cups of cold water into a pitcher, inserted the two mesh coffee filters, and refrigerated overnight (recommended 8-12 hours). The next morning, I removed the coffee filters and added three more cups of water per the instructions. The cold brewed coffee came out fantastic. I never was able to drink black coffee in my life, yet this is so smooth and refreshing that I had to drink it black.

Not only do I love the coffee but opening the unbrewed coffee package releases an intoxicating coffee aroma that I just wanted to smell all day.

This is convenient, easy to make, and refreshing.

UPDATE: I made a pitcher of this for my boyfriend. He just sent me an email that said "The cold brew coffee is excellent. Drinkable without cream."

title: These caramels taste like licorice but they lack the deliciousness of the typical buttery caramel flavor
text:
I use to hate caramel until I tried [[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Tara's Sea Salt Caramels]] about a month ago. Since then I ordered over $100 worth of caramels in various flavors in the 20 ounce size--not this 11.5 ounce size.

In my batch of mocha caramels, there were two licorice ones so I was able to taste test these without buying a batch. They taste mildly like licorice but they lack the creaminess and typical buttery caramel flavor I expected. I suspect these have the same calories and sugar as all the other caramels and that three pieces are one serving of 130 calories.

Obviously, no caramel is healthy but once in a while one must cheat. At least I love two of Tara's caramel varieties that I will continue to purchase. Below is a list of the ones I tried in the 20 ounce size and my rating. For these licorice ones, I give them 3 stars. By the way, I had my friend try every one but the licorice and she loved them in the order I do. She said she never tasted caramels like these.

[[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Sea Salt Caramels]] - 5 stars (I ordered 2 more batches)
[[ASIN:B079TZMFDH Apple Caramels]] - 4.5 - 5 stars (I ordered 2 batches)
[[ASIN:B079V154JV Licorice Caramels]] - 3 stars (Two of these caramels were in my Mocha batch)
[[ASIN:B079TSHR6B Vanilla Caramels]] - 2.5 stars (I ordered 1 batch)
[[ASIN:B079V1FYZB Mocha Caramels]] - 2 stars (I ordered 2 batches and wish I did not)

title: Delicious granola that is high calorie and expensive; also the granola bits are big
text:
I actually think this Granola is delicious; although the pieces are very big. As is typical of all granola packages, "purely elizabeth" claims there are nine servings per one 10 ounce bag. I disagree. To me there are no more than 4-5 servings, at least if I want to eat more than one spoonful. The attached picture shows 2-1/2 servings.

Each one ounce serving is 140 calories with 50 calories from fat, 3g saturated fat, 0 cholesterol, 135mg sodium, 3g dietary fiber, 6g sugars, and 4g of protein.

The ingredients in order are:
- Organic Gluten-Free Oats
- Organic Coconut Sugar
- Organic Raw Virgin Coconut Oil
- Peanut Butter
- Organic Fair-Trade Dark Chocolate Chunks (Organic Cane Sugar, Organic Chocolate Liquor, Organic Cocoa Butter, Organic Vanilla Extract)
- Sunflower Seeds
- Peanuts
- Organic Quinoa Flakes
- Organic Amaranth Puffs
- Cacao Powder
- Organic Chia Seeds
- Cinnamon
- Sea Salt

Overall, I think this is a good quality product but like all granola, it contains sugar, is high calorie, more than one serving is needed, and it is expensive at the current price of $28.03 for 3 bags which is about $9.35 per bag.

UPDATE July 12, 2018 - I had my boyfriend try this granola and his exact words were "This granola is excellent."

title: Utterly tasteless powdered creamer; makes me feel like I am drinking my coffee black
text:
This is a powdered creamer and there are over two pounds of it in this bottle. Personally, I prefer liquid creamers for my coffee such as the CoffeeMate or the International brands.

The directions say to use one teaspoon per cup of coffee. I tried that but my coffee tasted like I had no creamer in it. Also, even though the first ingredient is corn syrup solids, I taste no sweet taste in my coffee. My coffee was only lightly colored and I suspect that is because it is a non-dairy product.

Note that each teaspoon is 10 calories with only 1% fat calories and no sugars (which I find strange since corn syrup is a sugar). The first three ingredients are corn syrup solids, vegetable oil, dipotassium phosphate, etc. The ingredients are obviously not healthy but that is typical of all powdered creamers. Personally, I do not feel this adds anything to my coffee and is utterly tasteless. Even though I do not like powered creamers, I would choose the [[ASIN:B00SV118WO Coffee-mate Original Coffee Creamer Powder]] over this.

UPDATE - I had my boyfriend try this just in case he would like it and he hates. Unfortunately, the entire two pounds went in the garbage. This stuff is horrible.

title: These caramels do not taste like vanilla to me & they lack the deliciousness of the typical buttery caramel flavor
text:
I use to hate caramel until I tried [[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Tara's Sea Salt Caramels]] a few weeks ago. Since then, I ordered several more batches of those caramels in addition to two batches her [[ASIN:B079V1FYZB Mocha Caramels]], one batch of her [[ASIN:B079TZMFDH Apple Caramels]], and one batch of these [[ASIN:B079TSHR6B Vanilla Caramels]].

Surprisingly, I am not impressed with these vanilla caramels. These stick to the wrapper, unlike the sea salt and apple ones I tried. They also do not taste much like vanilla and they lack the creaminess I expected. Only two of her varieties that I tried are fantastic but these I will not be ordering again. My favorite one is her [[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Sea Salt Caramels]] with the [[ASIN:B079TZMFDH Apple Caramels]] a close second. I'm actually sorry I ordered these.

Three pieces are one serving and equal 130 calories. There are 3.5g of saturated fat, 15mg cholesterol, 45mg sodium, 15g sugar. The ingredients are nothing but corn syrup, sweetened condensed milk, heavy cream, invert sugar, butter, brown sugar, sugar, natural flavor, sea salt, and soy lecithin. I assume the Madagascar vanilla is hidden in the "natural flavor" as nothing on the label references this.

Overall, I give these 2.5 stars.

title: Too strong and too bitter for both me and my boyfriend
text: Neither my boyfriend or I liked this. Besides being very strong (even using over 8 ounces of boiling water), it is very bitter. I had to load it with cream to be able to drink this. My boyfriend was kinder and rated this 3 stars but I rate it 2.

title: Great variety of nuts and fruit in each packet and very fresh tasting with no salt; LOVE THEM!
text:
There are 24 single-serve pouches in the box. I love the variety in each once ounce packet. Each serving consists of 30% almonds, 20% walnuts, 15% macadamia nuts, 10% raisins, and 10% cranberries for a good variety of protein. Note that the nuts are whole and not in pieces so they are hard on the teeth. They are unsalted but still taste great and fresh. Each serving is 176 calories with 144 calories from fat and only 1g of sugar from the fruit. Overall these are excellent.

The box says this is the product of the U.S.A. but some nut ingredients are sourced globally. The cashews are from Brazil, India, Vietnam, Indonesia, and / or Ghana. The macadamia nuts are from Australia, Guatemala, Kenya, Malawi, and / or South Africa. The walnuts are from Chile.

UPDATE Dec 2, 2018: I just ordered more of these nuts on November 24. I love them and they are wonderful. I have also tried their other varieties [[ASIN:B07CZC5GT7 here]] and [[ASIN:R3O0VDPDWRQOFT here]] and they are all equally good.

title: These caramels do not taste like mocha or caramel to me; they lack the deliciousness of the typical buttery caramel flavor
text:
I use to hate caramel until I tried [[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Tara's Sea Salt Caramels]] a few weeks ago. Since then, I ordered several more batches of those caramels in addition to two batches these mocha ones and her [[ASIN:B079TZMFDH Apple Caramels]].

Surprisingly, I do not like these at all. These stick to the wrapper, unlike the other two I tried. They also do not taste much like mocha and definitely do not taste like caramel. Her other varieties are fantastic but these I will not be ordering again. I miss the buttery creaminess of the other two flavors. My favorite one is her [[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Tara's Sea Salt Caramels]] with the [[ASIN:B079TZMFDH Apple Caramels]] a close second. I'm actually sorry I ordered two of these based on the other five-star reviews.

Three pieces are one serving and equal 130 calories. There are 3.5g of saturated fat, 15mg cholesterol, 45mg sodium, 15g sugar. The ingredients are nothing but corn syrup, sweetened condensed milk, heavy cream, invert sugar, butter, brown sugar, sugar, natural flavor, sea salt, and soy lecithin.

title: There's no APPLE in the ingredient's list but the flavor profile is amazing; they are sinful; decadent, smooth, creamy caramels
text:
I use to hate caramel until I tried [[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Tara's Sea Salt Caramels]] a few weeks ago. Since then, I ordered several more batches of those caramels in addition to these apple ones and her [[ASIN:B079V1FYZB mocha caramels]].

These apple caramels are delicious and have a unique flavor profile unlike her other sea salt variety. I cannot describe the flavor but I would not call it apple; nevertheless, it is incredibly delicious. There is no apple in the ingredients--just flavoring. So far all of her caramels are the best I ever ate. They are soft, smooth, buttery, and creamy without being cloyingly sweet.

Three pieces are one serving and equal 130 calories. There are 3.5g of saturated fat, 15mg cholesterol, 45mg sodium, 15g sugar. However, if you care about the nutrition label, then do not eat these because it is nothing but corn syrup, sweetened condensed milk, heavy cream, invert sugar, butter, brown sugar, sugar, natural flavor, sea salt, and soy lecithin.

These are so sinfully delicious and have such a unique flavor profile that I do not care that there is no APPLE in the ingredients list.

title: Tastes like dessert but I nearly broke a tooth on the first bite
text:
The one positive is that this tastes like dessert in the form of oatmeal cookies. And for those who care, the oats are gluten free.

NEGATIVES
- In my first spoonful, I nearly broke a tooth as there was a cherry pit in the serving. Lucky I did not bite down hard. See first picture. This is such a bad first impression that I would never buy this.

- Next, the first picture is actually of three servings. To me there are only about four or five servings per bag; however, the bag says 10 servings - if one only eats one ounce.

- This is high calorie with 8 grams of sugar. See the second picture for the full ingredients list.

- At the current price of $20.85 this is extremely expensive for three 10-ounce bags. They say 30 servings among the bags, I say 12-15 servings max.

Had there not been a cherry pit in my first spoonful, I would have rated this 3 stars because it tastes good. However, in my opinion it is not a healthful cereal and it is high calorie and expensive.

title: Delicious almonds and cashews with a touch of sea salt and roasted without oil; easy on the teeth
text: These are absolutely delicious roasted almonds and cashews without all the extra oil and a touch of sea salt. What I also love is that the nuts are softer than when they are raw so they are easy on the teeth. Each serving is approximately 160 calories with about nine one ounce servings per container.

title: This makes a great cup of smooth and bold coffee that has an exceptionally balanced flavor profile to me
text:
This coffee is smooth and bold without being overpowering. I doubted I would like it because it has a deep aroma, but it is very good. It is also from Folgers.

I ground the beans on medium with my burr grinder and made this in an automatic drip coffeemaker.

UPDATE May 20, 2018: My boyfriend tried this and said "WOW is that good." He loves this coffee just like I do. Even though it looks very dark, it is not super strong and not bitter--just perfect!

title: Sinful; decadent, smooth, creamy caramels without being cloyingly sweet; the most delicious caramels I ever ate
text:
First, I must confess that I dislike caramel. I keep trying different kinds over and over again and then give them away. I always feel that they are too sweet or too sticky and chewy. Therefore, my original intention was to try one and then pass the rest along to a girl friend who loves caramel.

These are the best caramels I ever ate. They arrived on an 83-degree day, so they were soft. I like caramels better when they are soft rather than hard. Each "tiny treasure" peeled easily away from its wax paper wrap. The caramel is smooth, buttery, and creamy with just a hint (and I mean a tiny hint--like a granule or two) of sea salt. The sea salt enhanced the flavor a bit.

I ate three servings in one sitting. I forced myself to brush my teeth just so I would not eat anymore. Three pieces are one serving and equal 130 calories. There are 3.5g of saturated fat, 15mg cholesterol, 85mg sodium, 15g sugar. However, if you care about the nutrition label, then do not eat these.

The 11.5 ounce container is about the same size as a coffee cup (as you can see from the picture), but a lot of goodness comes in this small package.

These are so sinful that if the price is right I may buy a few containers for my two caramel-loving friends and perhaps one or two for me.

UPDATE: Unfortunately, I selfishly ate all but six of these in two days. It is taking a lot of restraint on my part to continue to save three each for my girl friend and my boyfriend to try. Tina's caramels are definitely the new "chocolate."

UPDATE August 8, 2018: Since I loved these so much, I ordered seven 20-ounce jars of her various caramels (see below). The sea salt is by far the best with the Caramel Apple a close second. In addition, I gave four friends samples of each of three caramels (sea salt, caramel apple, and mocha) and three of the friends LOVED the sea salt, with one friend hating the sea salt because they do no like salt. This "flavor" is by far the favorite based on mine and my other friends' taste tests.

[[ASIN:B079V3BX9N Sea Salt Caramels]] - 5 stars (I ordered 2 more batches)
[[ASIN:B079TZMFDH Apple Caramels]] - 4.5 - 5 stars (I ordered 2 batches)
[[ASIN:B079V154JV Licorice Caramels]] - 3 stars (Two of these caramels were in my Mocha batch)
[[ASIN:B079TSHR6B Vanilla Caramels]] - 2.5 stars (I ordered 1 batch)
[[ASIN:B079V1FYZB Mocha Caramels]] - 2 stars (I ordered 2 batches and wish I did not)

title: Loved the quinoa but not the spice mix; also it's lacking in vegetables
text:
There are eight five ounce boxes for the current price of $19.99 and each box is two servings, but for me each box is one serving as I eat it as a meal.

I love quinoa but I have always purchased the kind I had to wash off to remove the saponins that leave a soapy residue that causes the grain to be bitter. This quinoa is already pre-washed and is very easy to make. Just bring one cup of water with the entire package contents and spice mix to a boil and then simmer for 12-15 minutes. I added 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter, which was optional.

Cooked as prepared is 310 calories with 90 fat calories. I assume without the butter the calories would be as packaged at 260 calories with 35 fat calories. There are 360mg of salt but it did not taste too salty to me.

Unfortunately, I just found the taste to be okay. I liked the nuttiness of the quinoa but really did not like the spice mix, which is why I give this 3 stars. In addition, the only visible vegetables are the flecks of carrots peppered throughout the mix. I did not see the red bell peppers or celery that is listed in the ingredients list.

ONE BIG NEGATIVE is the packaging. Each box is wrapped in plastic. Instead, Tiny Hero needs to think about wrapping the quinoa in a zip lock plastic bag INSIDE the box and leave the box without any plastic wrap. The reason I say this is because working with quinoa are like working with grains of sands. After I unwrapped the plastic from the box, I had quinoa granules all over my counter, sink, stove, etc. simply because they escape through the closed box. Once I open the flap, there is even more that escapes. If the quinoa were sealed inside instead, then I could empty the contents of the zip locked sealed bag over the stove without creating a mess. Nevertheless, my 3-star rating is for the product and not the packaging. I loved the quinoa and disliked the spice and only saw carrot flecks in my dish.

title: This has no resemblance to food I am use to eating
text:
I am not a typical customer of this type product. My diet consists mostly of fresh fruits, vegetables, nuts, beans, eggs, fish, and sometimes meat. Occasionally I will supplement this by frozen foods. On rare occasions I will also eat canned foods such as [[ASIN:B0005ZW3UA HORMEL Dinty Moore Beef Stew with Potatoes & Carrots]] which I consider quite good and tasty.

Therefore, my intention in trying this is to find that once in a while meal where I deviate from the norm. However, after having tried this, this is definitely not it. I tried two of the four varieties. It's a five-pack but one meal is repeated twice. After having tried those two, I'm not sure I can eat any more of this.

Each "meal" microwaves in one minute. The Chicken Breast and Gravy with Mashed Potatoes and the Turkey and Dressing were both like mush. The gravy on the chicken breast made the potatoes look like applesauce. The Turkey and Dressing had perhaps one or two pieces of pulverized celery that was barely recognizable. By no means are these meals complete. They are not even meals or quality food in my opinion. If I closed my eyes, the taste was just okay. If I were starving and stranded in the woods, I would probably eat this like a five-course feast. However, since I have other options, I'll pass.

I attached seven pictures in this order. The last two are of the two cooked meals I ate.

1. The first picture is of the 5 Hormel meals that come in this package. There are two of the roast beef and one each of the others.
2. This is the ingredients and calorie count for the Roast Beef and Gravy with Mashed Potatoes
3. This is the ingredients and calorie count for the Chicken Breast and Gravy with Mashed Potatoes
4. This is the ingredients and calorie count for the Turkey and Dressing
5. This is the ingredients and calorie count for the Chicken Alfredo
6. The sixth picture is of the microwaved Chicken Breast and Gravy with Mashed Potatoes
7. The last picture is of the microwaved Turkey and Dressing microwaved

UPDATE:
I finally ate the Chicken Alfredo and the Roast Beef and Gravy with Mashed Potatoes. The Chicken Alfredo was creamy but did not taste like Alfredo and the noodles were like mush even though the chicken was okay. The Roast Beef and Gravy with Mashed Potatoes was actually my favorite (if you could call it that). It tasted fine. In fact, since there were two of those, I gave the extra one to my boyfriend to try and he said, "I actually enjoyed the roast beef and gravy with mashed potatoes. When I finished I wanted another one." For me, I would never buy any of these after trying them. However, my boyfriend would at least buy the one he tried. However, he eats most of his meals that come from a box and I usually eat freshly created meals, so our tastes are very different. A person who loves TV dinners, canned food, and other boxed meals probably would like to love this.

title: Tasteless tea - no Earl Grey Flavor whatsoever
text:
When I opened the package, I was blown away by the wonderful Earl Grey and citrusy aroma. However, I have had several cups and made sure my palate was clean so that there was no other taste in my mouth. To me this taste like plain black tea. This tea is pretty much tasteless. Overall, this is an incredible disappointment. I drink lots of different teas and my favorite Earl Grey so far is Tippy Earl Grey from Golden Moon Tea - see [[ASIN:B01FUUNFOW here]]. I canceled this subscribe and save item as I am so disappointed. I expected better considering the overwhelmingly positive reviews.

There is also one more negative. The bag is supposed to be resealable but the internal zip lock pulls away from the bag in spots leaving me without the ability to seal this bag.

UPDATE May 10, 2018 - The owner contacted me and was generous enough to sent me some replacement teas to try (see picture and story). The customer service is outstanding and the teas smell great. Unfortunately, after have more than 10 cups of tea on a clean palate, I just could not taste any Bergamot flavor. The brew itself is clean (meaning no sediment) which is very good, but the tea is flavorless or almost flavorless as in the case of the chai I tried. I am always surprised when a tea smells fabulous but it does not carry over into the brew. I wish them well.

title: This Mao Jian Jasmine's flavor is too subtle and has grassy / vegetal overtones
text: I love tea and have tried many flavors and brands. I do not care of this version of Jasmine. It has a light Jasmine flavor with grassy or vegetal overtones. However, I absolutely love [[ASIN:B000SATIMS Davidson's Jasmine Rose]] which is exceptional and has a balanced Jasmine and Rose (floral) flavor. I also love their [[ASIN:B00542YXFW Davidson's Anise Seed]] which is just anise seed that can be brewed into a tea or eaten on its own. That one is delicious; it tastes like strong licorice. Nevertheless, I will no longer order Mao Jian Jasmine.

title: These Brazil nuts as taste as fresh as the ones I buy at my local Wegmans--LOVE THEM
text: I usually get Brazil Nuts from my local Wegmans bulk bins because they taste fresh and I do not have to buy so many.  I tried these [[ASIN:B001PB4LR6 Brazil Nuts]] on Amazon and was disappointed.  However, these Sunbest nuts taste just as fresh as the Wegmans. Since I only eat two per day, because Brazil nuts are toxic when more are eaten, these will last awhile. I will definitely order them again.

title: These nuts taste like they are covered in a sweet dust; I hate them but my boyfriend loves them; unhealthy in my opinion
text:
This review is for the creme brulee covered almonds. I absolutely hate these nuts but my boyfriends loves them. To me they taste like almonds covered in a tasteless dust but to my boyfriend they taste like delicious creme brulee. These are gluten free and vegetarian. There are about three servings in a 3.5 ounce pack. I received one pack but purchasers should receive six. These are just almonds and sugar (lots of sugar). The first three ingredients are almonds, sugar, & corn syrup.

I consider these mostly unhealthy due to all the sugar, salt, and butter in the ingredients list. At the current price of $29.63, these come to $4.94 per pack, so they are expensive too. These are not for me and if my boyfriend did not love them, I would have thrown them out. Therefore, it is hit or miss. If one likes sugar covered almonds, these fit the bill.

He gives these 5 stars, I give them 1 star - hence the 3-star rating.

title: This medium roast makes an very bold cup of coffee that both of us like
text: My boyfriend and I really like this coffee. One K-cup makes an intense cup of coffee almost as strong as espresso. We had to put a lot of cream in the cup. This is definitely stronger than medium to our tastes buds. This tastes like a fresh brewed drip coffee rather than something that came from a K-cup.

title: This has a very subtle musty chipotle flavor w/a lot of ”choke-inducing spice dust” & some nut crunch for 80 cal. per serving
text:
I tried this on eggs and yogurt. It was okay on eggs and horrible on plain yogurt. This breakfast topper has a very subtle musty chipotle flavor with a lot of “choke-inducing spice dust” (if inhaled accidentally) and some nut crunch. Each two-tablespoon serving is 80 calories, 35 of which are fat calories. Most of the calories must come from the sunflower seeds. The first four ingredients are onion, sunflower seeds, quinoa seed, and spices. See attached photo for full list.

I would have preferred for the bottle to have a shaker top with holes in it instead of being fully open. A totally open bottle top introduces the risk of more mix being used than desired.

After having tried this I would never buy this breakfast topper.

title: Protein boost from whey and 2 grams of sugar; only 40 calories a package to enhance a smoothie
text:
There are four small smoothie boost packets in this box. Each serving is 40 calories with 50mg sodium, 2g sugar, and 5g protein and 4% Vitamin C. There's no cholesterol or fat. I made a smoothie with 3/4 cup of frozen blueberries, 1/2 cup of skim milk, and 1 pkg of Smoothie Boost. The end result was refreshing and it tasted good. It probably would have tasted better with frozen bananas instead of blueberries because the mix smells like strawberries.

The mix ingredients are whey protein isolate (milk), sugar, corn maltodextrin, strawberry, malic acid, beet powder, natural flavor & vanilla bean extractives.

Attached are several photos of the ingredients list on the box, the packet, and the end product of my smoothie.

I personally would not buy this because of the sugar and the expense. At $7.60 for 4 drink packets, that is $1.90 per pack for 40 extra calories, some sugar and whey protein. Nevertheless, it does taste pretty good for those who are interested.

title: A spicy and tasty peanut butter that delivers a little bit of heat; it also does not contain sugar
text:
This peanut butter has a little bit of heat and is very tasty. I had it on bread with jelly; plain is too hot for me. I like their idea of putting this on noodles as I might try that. There are 24 servings per this 12 ounce jar at one tablespoon per serving. Each serving is 80 calories with 40 from fat. There's 0 cholesterol, 1g of saturated fat, 190mg of sodium, 2g of dietary fiber, no sugars, and 3g protein. At the current price of $15.89 per 12 ounces, it is expensive but good for a treat myself kind of snack.

The ingredients are few as follows:
- Peanuts
- Kaffir Lime Leaves
- Sugar Lemongrass
- Salt
- Cayenne
- Citric Acid

title: Makes very dense pancakes that are not as sweet or fluffy as regular pancakes but are still tasty
text:
My review is for the three-pack of pancakes that includes one chocolate chip, one six-grain cinnamon, and one classic recipe as shown in the first picture. At least that is what I received and I believe Amazon is combining reviews with their other varieties.

All I had to do was add water. There is about 1-1/4 cups of mix in each bag. I used half the portion that was for 6-7 pancakes which turned out to be four of my size type of pancakes (see last picture). Just mix 3/4 cup of powder with 1/2 cup of water and then make them on a griddle. I did not notice the warning on the chocolate chip pancakes that said all the chocolate chips were on top of the mix, so my first batch had most of the chips in them.

The pancakes were tasty but they are very "meaty" and stiff and not fluffy like typical pancakes. I almost had to use a knife to cut them because they are dense. I prefer regular fluffy pancakes more but these offer a nice change. Also, the regular pancakes tend to be sweeter tasting than these. Each serving has three or four grams of protein depending on which mix is being used.

Attached are the following pictures:
1 - The three packages I received. Good for six breakfasts for me (2 per pack).
2 - The ingredients list for the chocolate chip pancakes.
3 - The ingredients list for the cinnamon pancakes which has only spices listed and not cinnamon as an ingredient.
4 - The ingredients list for the classic (plain) pancakes.
5 - A picture of the four pancakes I made with half the chocolate chip bag (that's 3/4 cup of mix).

title: Delicious fruitcake just like I remember from A&P; I'm glad someone is making this again
text: I am glad this recipe is being made again. The Jane Parker fruit cake (formerly from A&P) is my absolute favorite since childhood. Unfortunately, this is extremely expensive since A&P is out of business but it's delicious and does not compare to any other fruit cake I have every tried. There's lots of fruit and lots of nuts and oh so little cake. LOVE IT!

title: At one teaspoon per cup, this coffee tastes a little weak but the price was right
text:
My boyfriend and I have been on a kick of trying various instant coffees. So far we have tried six. He purchased this one at a discount store. At one teaspoon per cup, this brew tastes a little weak but the price was reasonable. Below are a list of other instant coffees we have purchased.

[[ASIN:B003Y5S0XW Nescafe Taster's Choice Instant Coffee]] - This was the first instant coffee we tried and we fell in love with it. After trying Taster’s Choice we decided to try several other premium brands that were rated highly, which let us to trying this one.

[[ASIN:B01FSVS5UC Lavazza Prontissimo Intenso Tin 95g]] – We did not like this because of the sediment in the cup. The medium intense taste was 5 stars but the “texture” of the 10% of coffee grinds and 90% instant was a turn off with a lots of sediment floating in the brew. It is also expensive.

[[ASIN:B00VWHNPUC Starbucks Via Instant Medium Roast Colombia Coffee]] – this coffee comes in single serve paks. It tastes weak but it is caffeinated and it is expensive, so we would not buy this again.

[[ASIN:B01G2B1GEE illy Instant Coffee - 100g]] – This instant has a smooth bold taste. We like the tin it comes in but it is also expensive.

[[ASIN:B0085H9YHK Nescafe Instant Coffee Gold]] – This brew tastes a little weak and the coffee is more expensive. We really like the glass jar this comes in. Note that Taster's Choice originally came in a glass bottle as well until we started buying larger quantities of it at BJs and Costco where it comes in an opaque plastic jar.

Overall, my boyfriend and I would choose the Nescafe Taster’s Choice Instant Coffee for both price and taste. If price were not an issue, then it is a toss-up for us between the Nescafe Gold and their Taster’s Choice brand or Illy which is also good. In general, the coffee can always be made stronger by adding more than one teaspoon to a cup (note we added one teaspoon in all cases). The only one we would never purchase again is the Lavazza due to the grinds in the cup and the Starbucks.

title: For a Starbucks, this coffee tastes weak and its expensive and highly caffeinated
text:
My boyfriend and I have been on a kick of trying various instant coffees. So far we have tried six. This coffee comes in single serve paks. It tastes weak, but it is highly caffeinated, and it is expensive, so we would not buy this again.

Last year I tried [[ASIN:B003Y5S0XW Nescafe Taster's Choice Instant Coffee]] which both my boyfriend and I fell in love with. After trying Taster’s Choice we decided to try several other premium brands that were rated highly, including this one.

[[ASIN:B01FSVS5UC Lavazza Prontissimo Intenso Tin 95g]] – We did not like this because of the sediment in the cup. The medium intense taste was 5 stars but the “texture” of the 10% of coffee grinds and 90% instant was a turn off with a lots of sediment floating in the brew. It is also expensive.

[[ASIN:B01G2B1GEE illy Instant Coffee - 100g]] – This instant has a smooth bold taste. We like the tin it comes in but it is also expensive.

[[ASIN:B0085H9YHK Nescafe Instant Coffee Gold]] – This brew tastes a little weak and the coffee is more expensive. We really like the glass jar this comes in. Note that Taster's Choice originally came in a glass bottle as well until we started buying larger quantities of it at BJs and Costco where it comes in an opaque plastic jar.

[[ASIN:B0052BRVEA Douwe Egberts Aroma Rood Instant Coffee, 200 gram]] – This brew tasted a little weak but the price was good since we purchased it at a discount store.

Overall, my boyfriend and I would choose the Nescafe Taster’s Choice Instant Coffee for both price and taste. If price were not an issue, then it is a toss-up for us between the Nescafe Gold and their Taster’s Choice brand or Illy which is also good. In general, the coffee can always be made stronger by adding more than one teaspoon to a cup (note we added one teaspoon in all cases). The only one we would never purchase again is the Lavazza due to the grinds in the cup and this Starbucks brand.

title: This coffee has a smooth bold taste but it's expensive; the tin is nice
text:
My boyfriend and I have been on a kick of trying various instant coffees. So far we have tried six. This instant has a smooth bold taste. We like the tin it comes in but it is also expensive.

Last year I tried [[ASIN:B003Y5S0XW Nescafe Taster's Choice Instant Coffee]] which both my boyfriend and I fell in love with. After trying Taster’s Choice we decided to try several other premium brands that were rated highly, including this one.

[[ASIN:B00VWHNPUC Starbucks Via Instant Medium Roast Colombia Coffee]] – this coffee comes in single serve paks. It tastes weak, is highly caffeinated, and it is expensive, so we would not buy this again.

[[ASIN:B0085H9YHK Nescafe Instant Coffee Gold]] – This brew tastes a little weak and the coffee is more expensive. We really like the glass jar this comes in. Note that Taster's Choice originally came in a glass bottle as well until we started buying larger quantities of it at BJs and Costco where it comes in an opaque plastic jar.

[[ASIN:B0052BRVEA Douwe Egberts Aroma Rood Instant Coffee, 200 gram]] – This brew tasted a little weak but the price was good since we purchased it at a discount store.

If price were not an issue, then it is a toss-up for us between the Nescafe Gold and their Taster’s Choice brand or Illy which is also good. In general, the coffee can always be made stronger by adding more than one teaspoon to a cup (note we added one teaspoon in all cases). The only one we would never purchase again is the Lavazza due to the grinds in the cup and the Starbucks.

title: Exceptionally silky milk chocolate that melts in the mouth
text:
This chocolate is smooth and silky, melts in your mouth, and is lightly sweet. My boyfriend says this is exceptional chocolate. I personally prefer dark chocolate over milk chocolate but, for milk this is very good.

There are three 3.52 ounce bars. Each bar is 230 calories with 130 fat calories per serving with 2.5 servings per bar.

The packaging is also very attractive and “rich” looking. Overall, this is an excellent choice for milk chocolate lovers.

title: These sliced almonds taste fresh and are perfect for stirring into oatmeal, yogurt, etc. or as a garnish
text: I tried Happy Belly Whole Almonds previously and loved them. These are the sliced version of those almonds and they taste great. This is perfect for stirring into yogurt, oatmeal, etc. It's also great to add to cooked french green beans as a garnish or even to decorate a cake or cookies. These are no different than other sliced almonds I have tried so purchasing this all comes down to the price.

title: I hate the pasty taste of this pasta; it also can only be boiled or it almost turns into a bean paste
text:
I purchased one 8 ounce box of the penne variety of this red lentil pasta for $2.99 at Home Goods. It has a best by date of July 5, 2019. Since I usually steam everything, I steamed some of the penne in water for a few minutes. Every penne was stuck to every other penne and all the water was absorbed (see second picture); it looked and tasted horrible. Not only was the pasta awful, it disintegrated into a paste that looked like a chickpea paste the consistency of hummus. I threw it out and I rarely throw out food.

Nevertheless, I am not one to give up, so the second serving I cooked in boiling water for the recommended 8-10 minute time specified. As it says on the box, the water turned cloudy and foamy. The pasta came out nice looking this time (see first picture) but it tasted almost as awful with pasty overtones. Based on my results from steaming (which does not happen with regular pasta), this is not something that can be put in a casserole or in a crock pot.

In summary, I hate this pasta. If it holds together, it tastes like I'm eating pasty beans in the shape of pasta. I will never purchase this again.

title: Looks like bird suet and half the bars taste bland and the other half are only marginally flavorful to me
text:
These bars remind me of bird suet. I tasted a piece of all four bars and that is as much as I could eat of these because for the most part I found them to be flavorless. Each bar contains 5 grams of protein and is 190 calories, except the banana pecan which is 200 calories. To me these are one step up from candy and have between 9 and 11 grams of sugar per bar. Attached is a picture of the nutrition labels.

There are a total of 16 1.4 ounce bars and I listed them in the order of my taste preference:

- 4 Tangerine Cashew with Vanilla & Mango - This was the most flavorful as the tangerine was detectable and tasty.

- 4 Coconut Curry Cashew with Ginger & Tumeric - I tasted the coconut and curry in this one. I do not like curry but in this bar it boosted its flavor.

- 4 Banana Pecan with Cocoa Nibs & Cinnamon - I could only taste the cinnamon.

- 4 Cranberry Almond with Black Pepper & Orange Zest - This one tasted like wet cardboard.

I tried a piece of all four bars (see attached picture of bars next to their boxes). If they tasted better, this would be a bar I might purchase for an occasional snack. I do not consider these healthy but they are better than candy. However, because they are mostly flavorless and remind me of bird suet, these are not for me.

UPDATE Sept 17, 2017 - My boyfriend tried these and said to me. "You know those bars you have on the counter. I LOVE THEM. They are absolutely delicious. I can taste the cashew butter, although I'm not sure why they would put black pepper in. The bars are soft enough where they won't break your teeth." Note that cashew butter is the first ingredient in every bar and I could not taste that. My boyfriend is use to eating bars and things that come out of boxes where I eat that stuff sparingly. Perhaps it is just a difference in taste preference but my boyfriend votes 5 stars for these and it is 2 stars to me. Based on other reviewers it seems these bars are both a hit and a miss depending on a person's taste preferences.

title: This has a good medium intensity taste but the coffee does not fully dissolve in boiling water
text:
Last year I tried [[ASIN:B003Y5S0XW Nescafe Taster's Choice Instant Coffee]] which both my boyfriend and I fell in love with.

However, we are always willing to try a more premium instant coffee. As a result, I decided to purchase [[ASIN:B0085H9YHK Nescafe Instant Coffee Gold]], which we both find weak, and this Lavazza coffee. This coffee is good but no better than the much cheaper [[ASIN:B003Y5S0XW Nescafe Taster's Choice Instant Coffee]]. Also, both the Nescafe Gold and this Lavazza cost a premium for a tiny amount.

I used one teaspoon to one cup of boiling water. No matter how much I stirred the coffee, it would not fully dissolve and some residue was left at the bottom of the cup. Although, I like the medium intensity taste, I do not like coffee particles floating in my cup (see picture) and it is way too expensive for me to ever purchase again.

Taste - 5 stars
Price - 3 stars
Texture - 2 stars (the sediment in the cup really bothers me)

UPDATE Sept 16, 2017 - My boyfriend noticed the ingredients list includes 10% ground coffee and 90% instant; this is the reason for the floating grinds. He does not mind the grinds in his coffee and he says it is a visual thing. He did say he did not like the look of the sediment on the bottom but the good taste causes him to overlook that. To him this coffee is wonderful and intense. He gives it 5 stars.

title: This instance coffee tastes weaker than their Taster's Choice instant coffee
text: My boyfriend and are are not impressed. From time to time we both use [[ASIN:B003Y5S0XW Nescafe Taster's Choice Instant Coffee]] which we both find very good. This Nescafe Gold tastes much weaker than that one and not as bold. My boyfriend does not notice much difference but I noticed the lighter / weaker taste. To us this is not worth the price.

title: Tastes like dark chocolate Nestle's crunch to me; my boyfriend LOVES these but I only like them
text:
My boyfriend LOVES these. I only like them. They are dark chocolate with quinoa and taste like a dark chocolate Nestle's crunch to me. I bought four bags of these at TJX for $5.99 each which to me is very expensive because there are only 7 pre-wrapped pieces in the bag. It's even more expensive on Amazon at over $1 for a tiny piece of chocolate. Nevertheless, these make relatively healthy snacks.

UPDATE Oct 17, 2017 - I have been buying these regularly at TJX when I can find them; which is not easy as their inventory is hit or miss. Nevertheless, I am now loving these and now my boyfriend only likes them.

title: This tastes like the Bob's Red Mill Muesli I eat but price is too expensive and serving size too small
text:
These cereal packets are great for convenience and portion control but each one cost $2.40 per serving at the current price of $11.99 and one serving is not enough for me and my boyfriend. There are only five servings in this box. We each had to eat 2-1/2 servings to feel like we had a good breakfast, so the whole box was gone in one sitting.

To me this tastes exactly like the [[ASIN:B000EDDSES Bob's Red Mill Old Country Style Muesli Cereal]] that I have been purchasing on Amazon's subscribe and save for years. It is an acquired taste and is very bland. The only difference between this cereal and the one I buy is that the one I buy does not have pumpkin seeds, it cost less, and does not come in single serving packets.

My boyfriend said Evoke tastes like wood pulp and that it needs to be spiced up with some fresh fruit. To me it is an acquired taste and one which I am use to. It is flavorless and "dusty" to my tastes just like the cereal I always buy but I would not buy this because it is way too expensive. For me it would be $6 a serving since I use 2-1/2 servings per breakfast.

In addition to the above, the Amazon site says this ships within one to two months which means one has to order a couple of hundred dollars worth to have a sufficient supply until the next order comes. However, I think that delay will change once this product reaches market.

Each serving size is 160 calories providing one eats only one serving. See attached picture for ingredients. I also attached a picture of one serving in each bowl.

In summary, I personally like it but my boyfriend does not. It is just too expensive for me to ever buy and the serving size is insufficient for me and my boyfriend.

On another note, the flax seeds in this (and all the other cereals I have tried that have flax seeds) are not ground. This means they offer no use to my body other than to pass through it. Flax seed must be ground to get a nutritional benefit from them.

title: This freekeh has a great nutty texture and is delicious to me even though it is unseasoned
text:
Freekeh is a green wheat that is roasted and this unseasoned version is delicious.

I bought it on Amazon a couple of years ago and found it to be a great substitute for rice that is tasty and has a nice nutty texture. I have not had it in years. However, I recently tried [[ASIN:B008OUJGWQ Freekeh Foods Rosemary Sage version]] and did not care for it. I am definitely going back to this brand in the plain version.

title: Chewy, moist, flat cookies that taste kind of greasy
text:
These are simple to make. I had to buy coconut oil and that's it. There are two packages that make about one dozen cookies each. I made one package with 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of water, and one package of mix. Then I mixed it all together and scooped out 12 cookies onto a parchment-lined baking sheet. The cookies look like ping pong balls before they go in the oven. I decided to insert chocolate chips into the tops of half the cookies to add more flavor. I baked them for 14 minutes on 325 degrees. However, when they come out they are flat orbs as can be seen form the picture.

The cookies were moist and chewy and kind of greasy. Both my boyfriend and I felt these tasted okay when warm but this is not something I would buy. I liked the results when I added the chips, although the chips do not melt at the temperature this is baked at. However, once the cookies cooled, we felt they tasted greasier. As a result, we threw them out after eating about two cookies each from the entire batch.

Another thing I didn't like is that there are big chunks in the package that appear to be dried apple. It made the mix hard to distribute evenly.

According to the package each baked cookie is around 110 calories.

The mix itself is made of:
- organic coconut palm sugar
- organic dehydrated apples
- cassava flour
- organic coconut milk (coconut milk and water)
- almond flour
- maple syrup
- arrowroot flour
- vanilla (extractive of vanilla bean, water, and alcohol)
- Himalayan pink sea salt

title: Ground up almonds that can be used like flour or substituted partially for flour
text:
This is nothing more than ground up almonds with a consistency similar to flour. I love the smell of the almond flour and, after reading the product description on Amazon, I discovered 1/4 cup of wheat flour per cup can be substituted with this flour.

I made two batches of Grain Berry Pancakes. With the first batch, I used the mix to make the standard pancakes. With the second batch, I substituted 1/4 cup of the wheat flour mix with with this almond flour. Both batches looked almost identical except the one with the almond flour had a stiffer consistency. The pancakes were also just a tad stiffer and the texture was "stronger" with the mouth feel a tad stiffer. Other than this they both tasted the same. I actually expected better tasting pancakes with this flour but that was not the case.

Of course this can be used to make more than pancakes. There is an "almond butter blondies" recipe on the bag. Barney Butter also has also additional recipes online. For instance, one tablespoon of this flour can be added to oatmeal.

OTHER FACTS
- Flour contains only blanched roasted California almonds
- Peanut allergy safe
- Certified gluten free
- Non-GMO
- Kosher
- Certified Vegan
- Certified Paleo

Personally, I do not have a reason to buy this flour unless a recipe calls for it as I like to get my almond intake through whole almonds. Nevertheless, this is a way for me to slip in more protein into recipes that require flour without sacrificing taste. I do wish the bag said that 1/4 cup of this flour can be substituted for 1/4 cup of wheat flour per cup but it does not say that. This is an important oversight.

title: These taste like a chocolate swirl pound cake
text:
I purchased this at Home Goods for $3.99. They are on the dry side and taste more like a chocolate swirl pound cake than the moist delectable pastry I expected. However, I like them once in a while with tea but I would never pay the high price here.

They are individually wrapped so portion control and freshness are built in.

title: Delicious almonds and nothing but almonds in a large single serving size
text:
This review is for the pack of 14 [[ASIN:B01M74W9I3 whole almonds]]. The package has a hole in the top as if this were going to be sold separately in stores.

These almonds are definitely a 5-star product; except for the cost. I've tried [[ASIN:B01E60RI6S Happy Belly Whole Raw Almonds]] which are just as delicious but do not come in the 1.4 ounce pre-packaged servings.

Each of the 14 pre-packaged 1.4 ounce servings is 230 calories of which there are 180 fat calories. Total fat is 7% with saturated fat at 7%. Protein is 8g, sodium is 0mg, and total carbs is 8g (5g from dietary fiber and 2g from sugars) per serving. There's no cholesterol. The only ingredient in the package is almonds.

I've also tried their [[ASIN:B01M8QF40S Cashew Halves and Pieces with Sea Salt]] in the 14 pack. It's the same as this but only cashews. Each 1.6 ounce serving is 260 calories of which there are 190 fat calories. Total fat is 33% with saturated fat at 19%. Protein is 7g, sodium is 150mg, and total carbs is 13g (1g from dietary fiber and 2g from sugars) per serving. There's no cholesterol. These are also sold in a one ounce package of 8 [[ASIN:B01N2PGQ4Y here]].

The ingredients in each cachew package are:
- cashews from india, brazil, vietnam, indonesia, and ghana
- expeller pressed sunflower oil
- sea salt

In summary, the almonds (and the nuts in their other packages) taste fresh and healthy but the cost is high which is the only reason I took off one star. I have also tried their other combinations below that come in multi-packs.

[[ASIN:B01MTW6VKU Harvest Omega-3 Mix]]
[[ASIN:B01N6CE945 Antioxidant Mix]]
[[ASIN:B01N9D5I2Y Cranberry Almond Cashew Trail Mix]]
[[ASIN:B01N2PGQ4Y Cashew Halves and Pieces with Sea Salt in the 8 Ounce multi-pack]] - this is the same item as the cashews in the 14 count pre-packaged 1.4 ounce servings; only the servings are 1 ounces in this pack.

title: Absolutely delicious crackers plain, with toppings, or as an accompaniment to soup
text: These are absolutely delicious crackers either plain, with toppings, or with soup. They look like plastic squares but they are hard to stop eating. However, I would never pay $24.12 for this box of 2 packages. I purchased the same box at Costco for $8.69 which is their full price when they have them in stock.

title: The anise flavor is my favorite of all their waffle cookies
text:
I've been eating these Pizzelle waffle cookies on and off for years and the Anise flavor is my absolute favorite. I've also tried their lemon, vanilla, dark chocolate, and cranberry flavors. The cranberry is a new flavor and it's horrible; it tastes nothing like cranberry and has a burnt flavor to it. The same with the chocolate. The lemon and vanilla are okay but my absolute favorite is the Anise.

I love that each package contains four pre-packaged servings (it's more like 1-1/4 serving per tiny package). Each waffle cookie is 23 calories each but I always eat an entire tiny package in one sitting. This means each 5.25 pack has 4 servings in it; 5 if you eat according to the label.

I purchased my last package of these at Home Goods for $3.49 for one 5.25 ounce package. One day I may try the chocolate.

title: The only thing I like about this candy is the crunch; it lacks malt flavor and is super sweet; 510 calories per 3.52 ounce bag
text:
I have always loved [[ASIN:B005CV1SPS WHOPPERS Malted Milk Balls]] that come in a milk carton typical of what I remember in the 1960's. Every three or four years I treat myself to a box of WHOPPERS. Therefore, with a name like MALTESERS and a claim that this is a "malt ball candy," I figured these would be similar and I was totally wrong.

The crunch is almost the same as the WHOPPERS but not totally. WHOPPERS are a bit more airy and crackling. The size is also smaller than the WHOPPERS. The worst part is that I detect no malt flavoring whatsoever and the resulting candy is cloyingly sweet.

Each 3.52 ounce bag is 510 calories and contains three servings at 170 calories a serving. If I'm going to eat junk food or what I call "empty calories," it must be worth it for me. After having tried these, I would never buy them. If they tasted malty like WHOPPERS, MARS Chocolate would have had a new periodic customer every few years.

title: I found these to be incredibly delicious and for the right price, I would purchase them again
text:
I paid $2.99 at Home Goods for one 4.1 ounce bag which contains 10 pre-packaged rice cakes at 2 per package. Each package of two is 50 calories. Obviously, this is high glycemic junk food but to me there are delicious. I actually ate 8 servings all at once because they are so light and airy and have a hint of sweetness that makes them delectable.

They pack a large freshener pack in the package the size of a salt package you find with those large baking pretzels. This is supposed to be thrown out. At first I thought it was something that was supposed to go on the treats because it was so large, but it has "do not eat" in small letters on it.

I honestly would not pay more than $4.99 per package for these. For the price I paid, I might purchase them again if I were in the mood for a junk food snack.

title: Same delicious peanut butter cups but not worth the price because the Happy Birthday message is so faded
text:
These are the same delicious and very sweet Reese's peanut butter cups that many people are familiar with EXCEPT that these have a very faint "Happy Birthday" logo on the top.

This 18-ounce box contains 24 small (.75 ounce) peanut butter cups. Two cups equals a serving size of 220 calories (110 fat calories). This means there are 12 servings in this bag. At the current price of $17.45, each piece is $.73 cents or $1.46 per serving. I actually do not feel these are worth the price because the image quality is so faded on the cup and can barely be seen.

I attached two pictures of two different cups I opened. The Happy Birthday message is faded differently on each cup and also there are "chocolate crumbs or dust" on the top surface that would require I brush them off if I were displaying them opened at a party. If I were giving them out unopened, I doubt some people would even notice the message on the chocolate.

title: A sweet tasting highly compressed meat slab that tastes good to me but has a rubbery texture
text:
I really like the taste of these bars but am not too crazy about the texture.

This is a 1-1/2 inch by 4 inch slab of super compressed meat and other substances. I suspect this is manufactured by combining beef, dried wild blueberries, spices, etc. in a blender and then subjecting the extrusion to intense pressure to form a coagulated meat slab.

There are 12 bars to the package. Each bar is 110 calories with 30 fat calories. The first four ingredients are beef, dried sweetened wild blueberries, brown sugar, and quinoa. See the attached photo for the nutrition label and full list of ingredients.

I actually had no trouble eating this and would eat it again in a pinch; however, it is not something I would go out of my way to buy. The texture is like a Slim Jim in a flat form but without the crunch of an exterior casing. There's also 9 grams of sugar in each bar; normally I do not eat sugar with my meat. In addition, to me there is no barbecue flavor in these bars.

title: A trail mix combo of healthy nut and fruit ingredients that capitalizes on Omega-3 (reviews of their other mixes attached)
text:
This Omega-3 Mix is definitely a 5-star product; except for the cost. One multi-pack contains eight pre-packaged one-ounce servings of the Omega-3 Mix. Each one-ounce serving is 150 calories of which there are 100 fat calories. Total fat is 17% with saturated fat at 5%. Protein is 3g, sodium is 15mg, and total carbs is 12 g (2g from dietary fiber and 7g from sugars) per serving. There's no cholesterol.

The ingredients in each one-ounce package are:
- walnuts
- dried sweetened cranberries (cranberries, sugar, expeller pressed sunflower oil)
- almonds
- pistachios
- expeller pressed sunflower oil
- sea salt

In summary, the ingredients taste fresh and healthy and are not sweet at all. There is one BUYER BEWARE that made me take off one star. The product listed is for one multi-pack of 8, which at the current price of $10.56 is $1.32 for each one ounce package. If the cost were more reasonable, I would have rated this five stars.

UPDATE March 2017 - Since this review I tried the follow additional combination.

[[ASIN:B01N6CE945 Antioxidant Mix]] - Each one-ounce serving is 130 calories of which there are 70 fat calories. Total fat is 13% with saturated fat at 5%. Protein is 3g, sodium is 0mg, and total carbs is 14g (2g from dietary fiber and 9g from sugars) per serving. There's no cholesterol.

The ingredients in each one-ounce package are:
- almonds
- dried sweetened cranberries (cranberries, sugar, expeller pressed sunflower oil)
- dried sweetened blueberries blueberries, sugar, expeller pressed sunflower oil)
- cashews
- pepitas (means pumpkin seeds)
- expeller pressed sunflower oil

[[ASIN:B01N9D5I2Y Cranberry Almond Cashew Trail Mix]] - Each one-ounce serving is 140 calories of which there are 80 fat calories. Total fat is 14% with saturated fat at 6%. Protein is 3g, sodium is 30mg, and total carbs is 13g (2g from dietary fiber and 8g from sugars) per serving. There's no cholesterol.

The ingredients in each one-ounce package are:
- dried sweetened cranberries (cranberries, sugar, expeller pressed sunflower oil)
- almonds
- cashews
- expeller pressed sunflower oil
- sea salt

title: I dislike the taste of this coconut water but do not blame the product
text:
I always wanted to try 100% coconut water with no sugar added and this drink fit the bill. There are 90 calories per 16.9 ounce serving. Each serving contains 110mg of sodium, 900mg potassium, and 22g of carbohydrates (19g of which is natural sugar). There's also 4% phosphorus and calcium and 8% magnesium per serving.

Unfortunately, I absolutely hate the taste even if it is subdued. Years ago I purchased unsweetened coconut flakes that had a similar smell and taste. To me this tastes like raw unsweetened coconut which is not very "coco-nutty" and has a kind of "musty bland" flavor profile. It's definitely not for me. I'm going back to plain water or water with a slice of lemon or lime.

title: This tea is a better deal than the Lipton tea bags but produces an average tea brew
text:
NOTE: This review is for the [[ASIN:B01LQ3YGPY Pure Leaf Loose Tea Variety Pack, 10.5 Ounce]] as Amazon has combined reviews from similar "Pure Leaf" products together.

There are two plastic jars of long leaf loose tea in this package totaling 10.5 ounces. There's 4.7 ounces of English Breakfast Black Tea and 5.8 ounces of Gunpowder Green Tea. Although both are listed as products of Morocco, the box says the black is a combination of Assam and Ceylon teas and the green is an Indonesian green tea.

Note that this is distributed by Unilever which makes Lipton tea. This collection is certainly better (from the leaf perspective) than their standard Lipton tea bags which have what I call "tea dust" in them. Those are the fannings or tea leaf specks left behind after processing higher quality tea which, in my opinion, are the lowest quality.

I brewed several cups of this tea. The black tea is full-bodied and no different than ordinary black tea. I would give this one 4 stars. The Gunpowder green tea brew had a nice golden hue to it but was practically tasteless and I used one full teaspoon instead of the 3/4 teaspoon of leaves recommended. I've tasted other Gunpowder teas that have more flavor. This one gets 2 stars from me.

There are two things that I suggest Unilever rethink. First, tea should never be exposed to light. This tea comes in clear plastic containers. Light reduces the efficacy of the tea so it's important to keep them away from light; an opaque container would be better. Second, their instructions for the Gunpowder tea say to use 3/4 teaspoon to one cup of boiling water. The amount is too small to produce any flavor (1 teaspoon is typical) and boiling water with a green tea produces a bitter brew. Green tea is typically brewed at around 185 with some using 175 degree water. I feel the instructions on that jar should be written.

Overall, the quality of this tea is better than their tea bags but nothing especially outstanding. I feel the current $9.98 price (this is $12.98 minus the $3 coupon they are offering on Amazon) is decent for what you are getting especially since many cups of teas can be brewed from these two jars. For those who are not tea connoisseurs, this tea is a step up. However, for those who are specialty tea drinkers, this is a step down.

title: Taste almost like freshly roasted chestnuts; are soft and starchy but the colors may be unappealing to some
text:
I purchased a box of six 3.5 ounce packages at Costco and I believe they were $6.99. However, I saw them recently for $4.99 so either they are now on sale there or I'm not remembering the price I paid. Nevertheless the price was good so I bought them.

I actually like these and to me they are not too much different than fresh chestnuts. I always roasted Chestnuts at Christmas but the results were so hit or miss that I stopped a few years ago. Sometimes the nuts would be hard as a rock and other times they were perfect. Buying these pre-made solves my problem. Since there are no preservatives, they are not light tan as expected. Instead they are a deep brown to black and various shades in between. The color doesn't bother me but it might bother some. The nuts are soft and taste starchy as is typical of the ones I've eaten before. I would definitely buy these again at the price I got them for. In fact I might go back to Costco in a week and buy another box before they run out.

UPDATE Dec 15, 2016 - Once the package is opened, it should be consumed within a couple of days. There are only two servings per envelope, but I didn't consume one package right away and the nuts grew fungus on them and had to be thrown out. I should have stored the open package in the refrigerator as I now see it says on the back.

title: Flat flavor profile with the taste of cinnamon; to me this is a hard biscotti and not soft-baked
text:
I received one of these biscotti to try. It was a hard biscotti to me and did not feel "soft-baked" as described. Also, I tasted a lot of cinnamon even though that is listed as the last ingredient. Other than this the flavor profile was pretty flat. I attached a picture of the ingredients list. Each cookie is 110 calories with 45 from fat--see second image for the nutrition label.

After having tried this, it would not be my first choice. My favorite biscotti has always been homemade. However, I've tried many others over the years and the only two that come close to homemade biscotti to me are the DiBella brand such as [[ASIN:B00P1JWDRI DiBella - Apricot Almond Biscotti]] or Nonni's such as [[ASIN:B00O3I9M4K Nonni's Almond Dark Chocolate Biscotti]]. I think taste is a personal choice, so what one person likes another loves which is why I keep trying different brands of biscotti and only remember the ones I really liked or loved.

title: Mostly flavorless and did not enhance any crackers, fruit, or nuts I tried with it; not worth the calories, sugar, fat and salt
text:
I really wanted to love these but did not.

There are ten 1-1/2 ounce cups separated into two five-count packages. Each 1-1/2 ounce serving is 235 calories with 147 from fat. According to the label, each cup contains:
- 4g saturated fat
- 0g trans fat
- 0mg cholesterol
- 56mg sodium
- 15g total carbohydrates
- 2g dietary fiber
- 10g sugars
- 7g protein
- 0% Vitamin A & Vitamin C
- 2% Calcium
- 4% Iron

Each cup is ready to eat. Just open and dip in crackers, fruit, or nuts. I tried crackers, fruit, and nuts with this and the end result was almost tasteless. The flavor profile coming through was mostly peanut butter and zero chocolate flavor. It also did not enhance anything I put it on. Although there's no high fructose corn syrup or hydrogenated oil in this product, it's not worth the calories, sugar, fat, and sodium to me for such a small serving.

This reminds me of the consistency of Nutella in a tiny disk that's portable and safe at room temperature. Nevertheless, I won't eat this again.

title: Overwhelming bitter with a strong cherry taste
text:
I recently tried the [[ASIN:B01LZGYKUL Init Dark Chocolate Almond and Summer Berries bar]] and loved it so much that I had to buy another box which I found at Walmart for $4.50. While I was buying those bars I noticed these. I wish I could say I love them but I don't. The bar tasted overwhelmingly like bitter cherries. My boyfriend liked both of their bars equally, however.

They come four bars to one box. Each bar is 1.41 ounces, is 180 calories (90 of which are fat calories) but there are only 2.5g of saturated fat. There is 70mg of sodium and 12g of sugar. Each bar also contains 22g carbs and 4g of protein along with 4% DV calcium and 8%DV iron. The first four ingredients are organic almonds, organic dark chocolate coating, infused dried red tart cherries, organic cashews, etc.

I would not buy these again because I didn't like the strong bitter cherry taste.

title: This Planeta extra virgin olive oil is not as deeply flavorful to me as the Paesano unfiltered extra virgin oil I love immensely
text:
I've tried many versions of olive oil (both filtered and unfiltered) and this one is very good filtered oil. Its vegetal / herbaceous overtones really come through but compared to my all time favorite olive oil, this one is not as intense.

My favorite extra virgin olive oil is [[ASIN:B003JCCRI4 Paesano Organic Unfiltered Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 34oz]] which has a deep peppery bite and is very rich and earthy

I rarely cook with olive oil and use it to season bread, pasta, salad, rice, soups, etc. Although this Planeta is indeed a premium tasting oil, Paesano is still my favorite.

title: Unbelievably delicious; tastes better than candy and are a little healthier too but does have a lot of sugar
text:
These bars are unbelievably delicious. They remind me of the KIND bars such as the [[ASIN:B007PE7ANY KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt]] but they taste even better even though KIND bars are delicious too.

These dark chocolate almond and summer berries bar comes in one box of four bars. Each bar is 1.41 ounces, is 180 calories (90 of which are fat calories) but there are only 2g of saturated fat. There is 65mg of sodium and 12g of sugar. Each bar also contains 23g carbs and 4g of protein along with 4% DV calcium and 6%DV iron. The first four ingredients are organic almonds, organic dark chocolate coating, organic tapioca syrup, dried cranberries, etc.

These bars do have a lot of sugar in my opinion, but I think they are a great substitute for a candy bar. They taste so good that they are dangerous to have around. I ate half the package in one night (2 of the 4 bars). LOVE THEM!

UPDATE Nov 3, 2016: I loved these so much that I bought a box for $4.50 at Walmart. I also decided to try their [[ASIN:B01HOX3RIC Init Dark Chocolate Cherry & Cashews Nut & Fruit Bars]] for the same price of $4.50 per 4 bar box. Unfortunately, I did not really care for the Dark Chocolate Cherry & Cashews bar because it tasted like bitter cherry. Nevertheless, these Dark Chocolate Almond bars are great. My boyfriend tried both bars and liked both of the the same.

title: Sticky rice that tastes best when mixed into soup or with beans; quick to make
text:
I did not think I'd like this white rice because of its stickiness; however, it is pretty good.

The ingredients are water, rice, and glucono-delta-lactone (which is a supposedly safe chemical usually used to increase acidity or as a leavening agent in baked goods).

There are three single-serve packages of cooked rice at 260 calories with 20% total carbs, 5g of protein, 2% of calcium, and 15% of iron.

The rice reminds me of a cross between sushi rice and plain white rice. I liked how it tasted by itself but the gelatinous quality of the rice makes it less palatable to me unless it is mixed with beans or put into soup. I had some soup that I purchased which I did not like too much. Once I added the rice to the soup, it made an enjoyable meal.

The instructions provided are for microwaving or cooking in the package in a pot of boiling water. I steamed mine for 17 minutes in the package with the top film pulled back to the dotted line in an electric steamer. It came out wonderful and not as sticky as some other reviewers have mentioned. I also steamed the second package at 14 minutes and that came out the same; so I will do 14 minutes for the last package.

I usually eat brown, red, black, or wild rice instead of high-glycemic white rice. However, if I were in the market for white rice that is quick to make, I would consider purchasing this.

title: The garlic Alfredo tasted awful to me and flavorless to my boyfriend, but we both liked the marinara sauce
text:
My favorite pasta dish in my youth was fettuccine Alfredo. Although I no longer order that due to the saturated fat, I was still excited to try this garlic Alfredo sauce. However, I really did not like it at all. It has an artificial Alfredo and garlic taste and is not very creamy. My boyfriend said it was flavorless. On the other hand, we both found the marinara sauce to be flavorful and very good.

What came in the package were four jars of Barilla spaghetti sauce as follows:
- Two 24 ounce jars of Marinara
- Two 14.5 ounce of Garlic Alfredo

The marinara has five 1/2 cup servings per container at 70 calories per serving with 5 fat calories. There's 9g sugar, 3g fiber, 2g protein, and 400mg sodium. The first four ingredients are tomato puree, sugar, dried onions, and dehydrated garlic….

The garlic Alfredo has seven 1/4 cup servings per container at 60 calories with 5 fat calories of fat. There's 1g saturated fat, 1g sugar, 15 mg cholesterol, 1g protein, and 330 mg sodium. The first four ingredients are water, sunflower oil, cream, and modified corn starch….

In summary, I would not buy this combination. I really, really do not like the garlic Alfredo at all. In fact I felt it was awful. The marinara, however, was very good and I would buy that alone if I were looking for such a sauce. The garlic Alfredo gets 1 star from me and the marinara 4 stars for a 2.5 star rating (rolled up to 3 stars).

title: Each packet seasons one pound of ground beef and is flavorful
text:
Each of the 12 packets will season one pound of ground beef. I made soft tacos with one packet and some lean ground beef and both my boyfriend and I found the meat quite tasty. It can also be used to season beans, rice, etc.

According to the package, about one pound of beef will serve six. Each serving (which they consider 2 tacos) is about 15 calories of spice and each has 320 mg of sodium. The organic spices include chili pepper, paprika, oregano, cumin, onion, salt (not organic), garlic, evaporated cane syrup, and citric acid (not organic) and rice hull fiber.

The only negative is that the directions say to stir in the seasoning mix and water but it doesn't tell you how much water to add to the ground beef. I used about 1/4 cup of water. The only water recommendation is for when making black been tacos, then it says 3/4 cup of water.

Overall, this is a nice seasoning mix.

title: Tastes great, good ingredients made up of predominately rolled outs, but too expensive for me to ever purchase
text:
Each serving is 170 calories, 6g of fat, 4g of sugar, 5g of fiber, and 6g of protein for 1/3 cup. Although, the bag says there are 9 servings in this 12 ounce package, I can't get more than 4 servings out of the bag with the way I eat breakfast. Too little is not satisfying enough for me.

Three ingredients are organic and the rest are not as follows:
- organic oats
- organic cranberries (organic cranberries, organic evaporated cane juice, & organic sunflower oil)
- almonds
- coconut
- chia seeds
- hemp seeds
- organic puffed quinoa

I tried their [[ASIN:B005VFDLN2 Athlete Fuel]] and [[ASIN:B0051S9QTC Classic Swiss]] which were also very good. I also tried their [[ASIN:B006GKBFN4 Morning Zen Gluten Free Muesli]] but that one I did not like. I felt the almonds were too hard and the muesli was tasteless.

At the current price of $12.89 for one 12 ounce bag, I would never buy this as it is way too expensive for what it is. I subscribe and save to [[ASIN:B000EDDSES Bob's Red Mill Old Country Style Muesli Cereal, 18-ounce (Pack of 4)]] that costs me $3.82 per 18 ounce bag. The price, of course is a personal decision. I do think it's just a little better than the Bob's I buy because of the additional nuts but other than this there is not much different from the Bob's muesli I already love.

title: Tastes great, good ingredients made up of predominately rolled outs, but too expensive for me to ever purchase
text:
Each serving is 160 calories, 4g of fat, 5g of sugar, 5g of fiber, and 6g of protein for 1/3 cup. Although, the bag says there are 9 servings in this 12 ounce package, I can't get more than 4 servings out of the bag with the way I eat breakfast. Too little is not satisfying enough for me.

The ingredients are all organic as follows:
- oats
- raisins
- almonds
- flaxseed
- cranberries (cranberries, cane juice, & sunflower oil)
- dates (dates and oat flour)
- rye flakes

I tried their [[ASIN: B005VFDLN2 Athlete Fuel]] and [[ASIN:B00E9Z2LA4 Super Muesli]] which were also good.

At the current price of $10.99 for one 12 ounce bag, I would not buy this as it is way too expensive for what it is. I subscribe and save to [[ASIN:B000EDDSES Bob's Red Mill Old Country Style Muesli Cereal, 18-ounce (Pack of 4)]] that costs me $3.82 per 18 ounce bag. The price, of course is a personal decision. Other than the price, there is not much difference in flavor from the Bob's muesli I already love.

title: Tastes great, good ingredients made up of predominately rolled outs, but too expensive for me to ever purchase
text:
Each serving is 160 calories, 6g of fat, 3g of sugar, 3g of fiber, and 7g of protein for 1/3 cup. Although, the bag says there are 9 servings in this 12 ounce package, I can't get more than 4 servings out of the bag with the way I eat breakfast. Too little is not satisfying enough for me.

The ingredients are all organic as follows:
- rolled oats
- raisins
- almonds
- flaxseed
- cashews
- pumpkins seeds
- rye flakes

The athlete formula has 1 more gram of protein than their [[ASIN:B00E9Z2LA4 Super Muesli]] or [[ASIN:B0051S9QTC Classic Swiss]].

At the current price of $14.19 for one 12 ounce bag, I would never buy this as it is way too expensive for what it is. I subscribe and save to [[ASIN:B000EDDSES Bob's Red Mill Old Country Style Muesli Cereal, 18-ounce (Pack of 4)]] that costs me $3.82 per 18 ounce bag. The price, of course is a personal decision. I do think it's just a little better than the Bob's I buy because of the additional nuts but other than this there is not much different from the Bob's muesli I already love.

title: Almost has the flavor of a dark roast with a smooth finish
text:
My boyfriend and I love Happy Belly's [[ASIN:B015GJ1Q20 Happy Belly French Roast Ground, Organic Fairtrade Coffee, Dark Roast]] which has a bold flavor with smooth finish. This coffee is a little less bold but has that wonderful smooth finish I am use to with this brand.

Even though this is medium roast, the resulting brew was still on the strong side but it was very good. I did not taste the "slight caramel note" as described on the bag. The ground coffee smelled great, however.

I like this coffee and this would be my second choice over their [[ASIN:B015GJ1Q20 Happy Belly French Roast Ground, Organic Fairtrade Coffee, Dark Roast]].

title: Almost has the flavor of a dark roast with a smooth finish
text:
My boyfriend and I love Happy Belly's [[ASIN:B015GJ1Q20 Happy Belly French Roast Ground, Organic Fairtrade Coffee, Dark Roast]] which has a bold flavor with smooth finish. This coffee is a little less bold but has that wonderful smooth finish I am use to with this brand.

I ground the beans and then brewed the coffee. Even though this is medium roast, the resulting brew was still on the strong side but it was very good. I did not taste the "slight caramel note" as described on the bag.

The only negative is that the beans looked dry and did not have that oily sheen on them that is the mark of freshness. The ground coffee smelled great, however. Other than this, I like this coffee.

title: A cup of coffee that is easy to make and mildly satisfying to drink; very convenient as well
text:
There are 18 individual serving packets per box and 8 boxes. One packet (which consists of one heaping teaspoon) will make one 8 ounce cup of coffee. It says there's 50% more per serving but I have no idea what that means because it only makes one cup of coffee.

This lacks the deep flavor of automatic drip coffee but I found it satisfying to drink. It has a smooth and not bitter finish.
