Because it's equivalent to a light to medium brew, the flavor is not strong, which I like. It also provides me with a way to make myself a quick cup of coffee without all the fuss of a machine. In addition, one can stick one of these packets in their purse for a quick cup of coffee on the go (providing there's boiling water and a cup available.) In the summer, when I put a bottle of water in my car, it gets very hot. I could always use the natural heat of a vehicle in the sun to "warm" my water and add the packet of coffee to "flavor" it.

At the current price of $31.24 for 144 cups of coffee that equals about .22 cents per cup, which I consider reasonable. Note that I was only shipped one box for review but I was assured by Amazon that eight boxes are included upon purchase.

title: One heaping teaspoon to 8 oz. of boiling water makes a decent cup of coffee even if it lacks the bold flavor of automatic drip
text:
One heaping teaspoon to 8 ounces boiling water makes a decent cup of coffee in a pinch. This lacks the deep flavor of automatic drip coffee but I find it satisfying to drink and has a smooth and not bitter finish. It also provides me with a way to make myself a quick cup of coffee without all the fuss of a machine.

I love the twist off top. Just turn once to left (counter-clockwise) to open and right (clockwise) to close. It's so incredibly simple and easy.

At the current price of $31.99 for two 7 ounce jars, however, this is very expensive from Amazon. Personally I would look for it in a supermarket.

title: I loved this Matcha so much I licked the “dust” off the can lid
text:
I tried Matcha once a few years ago and was so put off by the bitter brew that I did not pursue this tea until I recently got the opportunity to try the [[ASIN:B01609AD0G Tea-Sere Matcha Tea Maker]]. In my search for tea leaves to grind in this machine, I purchased this PANATEA ready-made Matcha to test in the brewer. Of course, since this is already ground, it almost makes the need for the [[ASIN:B01609AD0G Tea-Sere]] unnecessary.

During my research, I discovered the importance of starting with quality leaves. PANATEA uses Tencha leaves that are granite ground from a trusted source in Japan. They assured me they have the leaves regularly tested for lead and other contaminants. Selectively sourced Matcha, such as this, not only tastes good but is supposed to be healthy too with lots of anti-oxidants and other components.

After trying this tea, I’ve been converted. This 30 gram tin produces 30 3-ounce cups of Matcha. Be sure to refrigerate the remaining powder after opening to keep the tea fresh. Just use 1 gram (about 1/2 teaspoon or what they call one scoop) to 3 ounces of 180 degree water and then whisk to a froth or brew in the [[ASIN:B01609AD0G Tea-Sere Matcha Tea Maker]]. I made this Matcha both ways.

This “electric green tea powder” makes a wonderful frothy, vegetal, and slightly sweet brew with savory overtones. I loved this Matcha so much I licked the powder off the can lid and spoon so not one wisp of “dust” would be wasted.

PS – For those who are interested, in the comments section of this review I attached a link to an interesting article I found on the web about the founders of PANATEA and also a link to my review of the [[ASIN:B01609AD0G Tea-Sere Matcha Tea Maker]].

UPDATE July 16, 2016 - Yesterday, I made a cold Matcha Latte with skim milk using the [[ASIN:B01609AD0G Tea-Sere]] in STIR mode and it came out great with this PANATEA Matcha.

title: Surprisingly scrumptious to me and my boyfriend; each one is also individually wrapped
text:
These are surprisingly delicious. I purchased the Apricot Almond flavor at Home Goods for $2.79 per box. My boyfriend bought the [[ASIN:B00HBBGZGO blueberry lemon]] and the cherry chocolate flavors. The Apricot was my absolute favorite. I make my own pistachio orange biscotti that is excellent and this tastes almost homemade to me. I've tried other store bought brands and never liked them. This is the first store bought biscotti (besides the Nonna chocolate dipped biscotti) that I absolutely love.

My boyfriend loved these too but his favorite was the blueberry lemon.

This biscotti is not rock hard; it's medium hard but still perfect for dunking in tea. Each one is also individually wrapped for freshness and there are six per box. We're definitely going to buy more.

UPDATE July 6, 2016 - I have since tried all their flavors and this is my assessment / taste preference.

My favorite flavors that I LOVE equally:
- Apricot Almond - the apricot and almond flavors are distinct
- Pistachio Cranberry - pistachio flavor is strong and cranberry complements it
- Almond Orange - the orange flavor really comes through but the almond flavor is mild; that was okay with me

Next favorite flavors in order of preference:
- Blueberry Lemon - strong blueberry flavor with a hint of lemon
- Raspberry White Chocolate - rich raspberry flavor; I didn't taste the white chocolate but I didn't mind.
- Coconut Almond Mocha - strong coconut flavor with a hint of chocolate (mocha) flavor

My least favorite flavors:
- Cherry Chocolate - bland, no chocolate flavor and not much cherry
- Double chocolate fudge - strong cocoa powder taste; lacks the deep chocolate flavor I was expecting
- Caramello Macchiato - deep caramel flavor but very sweet; I didn't taste the white chocolate; too sweet for my taste

title: Some nuts in the bag I purchased do not taste fresh
text: These Brazil nuts are a great price but not every nut tastes fresh. I have tasted better Brazil nuts in the past. I would not purchase these again.

title: All five dogs love this grain-free food
text:
I fed this dog food to five dogs in foster care (one large and four small dogs). All five dogs ate this food; four chowed down immediately and one took her time.

I love the size of the kibble which is large and triangular.

The bag claims:
- The food is made in the U.S.A. (not sure where the ingredients are sourced from)
- There's no meat-by-product, fillers, artificial preservatives, or artificial flavors
- This food is totally grain free

I attached a photo of the ingredients list and the claims on the back of the bag. However, below are the first few lines of each.

The first four lines of the guaranteed analysis are:
- Crude Protein (Min) 26.0%
- Crude Fat (Min) 12.0%
- Crude Fiber (Max) 5.25%
- Moisture (Max) 10.0%
- etc.

The first ten ingredients in order are:
- Deboned Chicken
- Potatoes
- Peas
- Chicken Meal
- Dried Ground Potatoes
- Chickpeas
- Tomato Pomace
- Chicken Fat (preserved with mixed Tocopherols)
- Ground Flaxseed
- Tomatoes
- etc.

A cup of this kibble is 386 calories.

Obviously at the current price of $12.99 for 4 pounds this is a little expensive; however, I feel it is a quality food based on the ingredients. I like that this dog food comes in a “resealable” bag. The dogs just LOVE this and cannot get enough; they give it 5 stars.

title: Milk chocolate outer layer with rice crisps, a creamy interior with a tiny toasted and flavorless hazelnut in the center
text:
I purchased these on the recommendation of my boyfriend who loves them. Personally, I think these are just okay. There are 35-medium sized kisses in this gift box. Each kiss is an outer layer of milk chocolate with what tastes like a layer of rice crisps and then a creamy interior with a tiny toasted (and flavorless) hazelnut in the middle. I do not taste the hazelnut at all.

These taste like [[ASIN:B004NPPLFM Nestle Crunch Bar Chocolate Bars]] in kiss-shaped form without any hazelnut flavor. Although, these kisses taste good, I would not purchase these again simply because of the lack of hazelnut flavor. I would buy plain chocolate kisses over these that are less costly. If I want hazelnut flavor I'd purchase [[ASIN:B0029JXG4W Ferrero Rocher Fine Hazelnut Chocolate 5.3oz]] instead.

title: These taste just like plain crackers to me; I barely taste any cheese
text:
These taste just okay to me. They are dime-sized sandwich crackers similar to [[ASIN:B00R7BUHYE Ritz Bits Cheese Big Bag Sandwich Crackers]]. About 30% of the crackers in the box I received were either broken or the cracker sandwich was not aligned. I can chalk up the broken pieces due to shipping; the misaligned crackers are an assembly line issue. Nevertheless, these imperfections do not diminish the taste in any way. Personally, they taste like very bland crackers with barely any cheese flavor. Based on the cute packaging, I say these are being marketed to children and do believe these would appeal to them.

There are 7 servings in one 7.5 ounce package. Each serving is 160 calories, 3% saturated fat, <5mg cholesterol, 280mg sugars, 280mg sodium, 3g of protein. There's also 4% iron and 15% calcium.

The ingredients are

- organic wheat flour
- organic sunflower oil
- organic palm fruit oil
- organic nonfat milk
- organic cane sugar
- organic cheddar cheese (organic cultured pasteurized milk, salt enzymes)
- salt
- organic buttermilk
- calcium carbonate
- organic malt extract
- disodium phosphate
- baking soda
- organic soy lecithin
- organic annatto (for color)
- enzymes

Obviously this is a highly processed snack. For those who eat snacks such as these, I think they are okay for a once-in-awhile treat even if this is not a nutritional powerhouse. However, they are too bland for my taste and have hardly any cheese / cheddar flavor and for that reason I rate these 3 stars.

title: I expected a fruitcake like Jane Parker's brand but this did not taste as good in my opinion
text:
I have been a fan of Jane Parker's fruitcake for over 45 years. However, since A&P and that brand went out of business this year, I wanted to find a replacement. In the past I've tried all sorts of fruitcake including Freihofer's, Entenmann's, and some other brands. None ever compared with Jane Parker.

I wanted to try this fruitcake based on all the positive reviews so my boyfriend purchased one for me as a pre-Christmas gift. I was actually disappointed. The cake does not taste as robust as Jane Parker's and it also tastes a bit undone (under baked). I think it is just okay. To me the quality is between Freihofer's and Entenmann's. With the Jane Parker brand defunct, I now know I will no longer be eating fruitcake. I love fruitcake but in order for me to eat it, it must be exceptional and filled with flavorful fruit and a solid well-baked cake. This cake lacked both qualities to me.

Update Dec 26, 2015 - My boyfriend, on the other hand, liked this cake. He said in order to do a comparison he would have to do a side-by-side taste test with the Jane Parker brand, which is no longer possible to do. However, he's the type of person that will eat almost anything and is more accepting of variation in quality when it comes to food.

title: This tea is so delicious with a highly predominant blackberry fragrance and flavor
text:
I absolutely love this tea. I normally only purchase loose leaf but since The Republic of Tea is a good brand I decided to try this since my boyfriend purchased a can on sale at Bed, Bath, and Beyond and raved about it. After I tried it, we went back and purchased all the cans that were left, which were unfortunately only two.

I steep each bag in boiling water for four minutes and then sit down and enjoy.There's a strong blackberry fragrance and a smooth blackberry taste. I highly recommend this if you like the blackberry flavor; personally I do not taste the sage but the combination works.There are only three ingredients - black tea, natural blackberry flavor, and sage.

title: Nicely packaged and heavenly tasting chocolate assortment although I wish it came without all the salt
text:
The box containing this chocolate is a little longer than a notebook (7-1/8" x 12 - 1/4") but was packaged inside two other boxes each of which were big enough to hold a large pair of boots.

There are eight 3 oz. bars of chocolates in four varieties. Four bars are milk chocolate and four are dark chocolate as described below.

- Two milk chocolate with toasted coconut and macadamia (240 calories per serving)

- Two milk chocolate with sea salted almonds (220 calories per serving)

- Two dark chocolate with raspberries and sea salted almonds (240 calories per serving)

- Two dark chocolate with sea salted pistachios (240 calories per serving)

Each bar contains two servings which is equivalent to 16 servings for the entire 8-bar package. I savored a piece from each bar and this is my assessment in the order of my preference:

- The milk chocolate with toasted coconut is divine and smells and tastes just like a high-end mounds bar.

- The dark chocolate with sea salted pistachios is very smooth and tastes like the nut. There is a salty aftertaste on this one but it is still delicious.

- The milk chocolate with sea salted almonds is smooth and has a little more than a hint of salt.

- The dark chocolate with raspberries and sea salted almonds is very good although I do find the salt slightly overpowers the chocolate and raspberries on this one.

In summary, the presentation is very nice especially since a beige see-through bow is tied on opposing ends of the box. The chocolate is also satisfyingly creamy and slightly salty. The milk chocolate coconut is my favorite due to the heavenly coconut profile I love and because it is the only bar without salt. The dark chocolate pistachio is my second favorite. I think this is a great gift. As long as the recipient does not have to watch their salt intake, this assortment is a chocolate lover's dream. The price is competitive at $35.58 which translates to $4.45 per bar. I would definitely be delighted if I received this Scharffen Berger chocolate as a gift.

title: The pralines are smooth and have a delicate dark chocolate taste but the light chocolate pralines are cloyingly sweet
text:
Since Amazon posts the same review for various assortments of Cailler chocolate, I had to combine my review for all their chocolates into one. Therefore, the review follows the chocolate mentioned in each section below.

FIRST REVIEW

The below review is for the [[ASIN:B014UAGKMM CAILLER Dark Chocolate Selection, Small Box Assortment, 5 Ounce, (16 Pieces)]]. Within this package there are two each of nine separate chocolates totaling 18 pieces. The variety included are:

- Almond Caramel
- Midnight Delight
- Intense Dark
- Cocoa Rush
- Dark Lemonette
- Dark Hazelnut Crush
- Simply Almond
- Intense Cocoa

My favorite is the dark hazelnut crush and my least favorite is the intense cocoa. The chocolate is smooth but not intense. Each piece has a delicate dark chocolate taste.

There are four servings in the box. A serving size is 4 pralines with about 190 calories per serving.

As a side note, these chocolates arrive way over packaged. The small box containing the 16 pieces is encased in a protected brown box.The small box has a slide out compartment holding the chocolates that is wrapped in paper. Once I ripped the paper off, the string tab holding the cellophane was impossible to remove so I had to use a scissor. All 16 pieces could fit in a small snack bag half the size of a sandwich bag. Obviously the packaging is important but I do feel this is a bit over done.

This chocolate is expensive but is competitively priced with other high-end chocolates. I do prefer a more intense dark chocolate flavor than what these pralines provide. My favorite chocolate brands are Lindt and Rausch and some other small batch vendors. Nevertheless, these are very good.

SECOND REVIEW

The below review is for the [[ASIN:B014UAGGZ8 CAILLER Dark Chocolate Selection, Large Box Assortment, 7.9 Ounce, (25 Pieces)]]. Within this package there are two each of nine separate chocolates totaling 18 pieces. The variety included are two of every flavor mentioned above with the addition of three each of the first flavor below and two each of the remaining two flavors listed below:

- Mocca Gianduja (coffee flavored)
- Intense Caramel
- Dark Coffee

One interesting twist with this box is that the serving size is 4 pralines but the calories mentioned are 200 per serving instead of 190 above.

The chocolate flavor profile is the same. The addition of the coffee and caramel flavors complements an already nice selection. The Mocca Gianduja has an intense coffee flavor that is savory; the same with the caramel. The packaging is abundant as well but this time the pull tab on the cellophane worked. Although, no price is currently listed on Amazon for this 25-piece assortment, I expect it will be expensive yet competitively priced.

THIRD REVIEW

The below review is for the [[ASIN:B014UAGKPE CAILLER Praliné Chocolate Selection, Small Box Assortment, 4.8 Ounce, (16 Pieces)]]. Within this package are 16 pralines packaged like all their other boxed chocolates mentioned above. There are two pralines each, mostly milk chocolate, of:

- Velvet Bliss
- Avelotte Caramel
- Gianduja (hazelnut creme)
- Almond Marquise
- Simple Almond (a dark chocolate)
- Milk Hazelnut Crush
- Dark Coffee (a dark chocolate)
- Dark Hazelnut Crush (a dark chocolate)

The serving size is 4 pralines at 200 calories per serving.

The milk chocolate pralines are very sweet. Besides the dark chocolate I like the almond marquise even though I find that one cloyingly sweet. At the current price of $24.90, it is competitively priced with other higher end chocolates. Based on my tastes, I give this one 2.5 stars.

FOURTH REVIEW

The below review is for the [[ASIN:B014UAGKNQ CAILLER Chocolate Signature Selection, Large Box Assortment, 7.9 Ounce, (25 Pieces)]]. Within this package are 25 pralines packaged like all their other boxed chocolates mentioned above. This has a mixture of both light and dark chocolates with light chocolate being the predominant flavor. There are two of each of the flavors below except for the Avelotte Caramel flavor which has three.

- Simply Almond
- Avelotte Caramel
- Velvet Bliss
- Creamy Caramel
- Gianduja (hazelnut creme)
- Milk Hazelnut Crush
- Hazelnut Embrace
- Cocoa Dentelle
- Triangle Crisp
- Intense Dark
- Almond Caramel
- Dark Hazelnut Crush

The serving size is 4 pralines at 200 calories per serving.

The milk chocolate pralines are very sweet and cloyingly so. I prefer the dark pralines in the package. It does have a mixture of of both light and dark chocolates but it contains primarily light chocolate. At the current price of $34.90, I do think this is competitively priced. Because of the sweetness, I give this one 2.5 stars.

UPDATE Nov 2, 2015 - I lowered my review ranking from 4 stars to 3 stars because I averaged out my ratings based on all 4 reviews. For the dark chocolate I rate them about 3.5 to 4 stars; for the light chocolate, I rate them about 2.5 stars; hence the new 3-star rating.

UPDATE November 8, 2015 - I had my boyfriend try all these pralines and he LOVED them. In fact he ate a mixture equivalent to a box and a half of these in one day.

title: My boyfriend LOVES this chocolate but the flavor was too subtle for my taste
text:
My boyfriend purchased this after hearing about this on Bloomberg News as a new addition to Nestles line-up.

The bar is thick and all the nuts and fruit are visible. It certainly looks very appetizing and I could not wait to try it. My boyfriend rated this 5 stars. He said the chocolate was very smooth and was not overpowering. I, on the other hand, prefer Lindt, Rausch, and other dark chocolates by a slight margin over this. I felt the flavor was too subtle for me (almost bland); so I would rate this 3 stars.

This chocolate is expensive but is competitively priced with other high-end chocolates. I am always willing to give new chocolates a try so I am glad my boyfriend purchased this so I could try it. He would certainly buy this again but it is not one I would buy because I prefer a more intense flavor profile.

title: So delicious I found it almost impossible to stop eating these but I had to stop to maintain my healthy diet
text:
These Garlic Parmesan Pretzel Crisps are so absolutely delicious that I found it very difficult to stop eating them. However, since this is a processed snack, I had to resist the temptation to eat all 24 bags I received at once. I stopped at one bag with great difficulty--no kidding. To me these taste like a crisper and baby-sized version of the [[ASIN:B00LOXEH1K Super Pretzel]] with the saltiness of Parmesan cheese and a touch of garlic.

Each 1.5 ounce bag is 170 calories with 25 calories from fat, no saturated fat or cholesterol, 610 mg of salt, 32g of carbohydrates, 1g of dietary fiber, 3g of sugar, and 4g of protein. There's also 3% DV of Calcium and 3% of Iron in each serving (1 bag).

Ingredients in order are:
- wheat flour
- seasoning (whey, salt, Parmesan cheese [part skim milk, cheese cultures, salt, enzymes], reduced lactose whey, maltodextrin, garlic powder, onion powder, torula yeast, modified food starch, dried parsley and natural flavor)
- soybean oil
- sugar
- salt
- malt syrup (corn syrup, malt extract)
- soda

This is made in a facility that processes soy.

Are these healthy? Not in my opinion. I consider these a highly processed snack to be eaten in moderation. However, I find them almost impossible to stop eating. This gets 5 stars for taste. I'll leave it up to the purchaser to decide if they want to make this part of their diet.

UPDATE August 10, 2015 - My boyfriend tried these and he could not stop eating them either. He ate two packages at once. He did say they were salty but loved them nevertheless.

title: Tastes like oatmeal; looks like meatballs, texture is pasty but overall nutritious
text:
After trying these, I prefer hard granola over these soft baked granola blueberry almond bites. I do not taste any almond or blueberry; instead they taste like oatmeal cookies but are a bit pasty in texture. They are also shaped like and look like meatballs.

There are approximately 5 servings per bag with each serving consisting of 180 calories for 3 bites. Total fat is only 10% which is great with 0.5g saturated fat, 0 trans fat, 1.5g of polyunsaturated fat, and 4g of monounsaturated fat. There's no cholesterol. Each serving is 180 mg of sodium, 24g of carbohydrates, 1g of dietary fiber, 11g of sugar, and 7g of protein with 4% calcium and 8% iron.

Ingredients are:
- Whole Grain Oats
- Soy Protein Isolate
- Sugar
- Wheat Flour
- Canola Oil
- Refiner's Syrup
- Water
- Almond Pieces
- Glycerin
- Honey
- Dried Blueberries
- Egg White
- Rice Starch
- Molasses
- Salt
- Soy Lecithin
- Cinnamon
- Baking Sada
- Calcium Carbonate
- Natural Flavor
- Palm Oil
- Blueberry Juice Concentrate
- Mixed Tocopherols Added to Preserve Freshness

Overall, I consider these a healthy snack and since I like oatmeal, the flavor is fine even though it's supposed to taste like blueberry almond. The one negative for me is the pasty texture. Personally, I would choose the hard granola over these because I like them better.

title: These very rich chocolates are absolutely divine
text:
There are 2-pieces each of 8 varieties of European (made in Germany) chocolate in this very small 7 ounce package. The 8 varieties are evenly divided into both dark and milk chocolate. My favorite is the Marzipan!

Dark Chocolate flavors are:
- Marzipan
- Coffee and Cream (has a white chocolate base)
- Dark Cream
- Dark Mousse

Milk Chocolate flavors are:
- Milk Chocolate
- Hazelnut Almond
- Hazelnut Creme
- Praline Creme

This reminds me of the delicious [[ASIN:B00B9JEJVM Galler Gift Box Mini Bar Assortment 24 Ct 10.08 Oz - Belgium Chocolates]] that I've eaten in the past, only better. I'm partial to the dark chocolate flavors but they are all great. Each serving of 3 pieces is 210 calories with 120 from fat. There are five servings in this package of 16 chocolates.

Even though 3 pieces is a serving, one piece is all I need because the chocolate is so intense and smooth. It is absolutely heavenly.

title: Absolutely tasty breakfast biscuit dust
text:
My boyfriend and I tried these and loved the taste; they are absolutely delicious. They are made of whole grain. Even though the dried blueberries are the 9th ingredient in the list, each biscuit has a strong blueberry flavor. My major dislike is that the biscuits are dry, brittle, and most of them arrived in tiny pieces. Just handling them caused them to fall apart if I grasped them too tight. The dryness reminded me of the [[ASIN:B000LTM29M Nature Valley Crunchy Granola Bars Oats 'N Honey bars]]; however, those bars stay together and do not turn into dust.

There are 5 pouches of 4 small biscuits each for a total of 20 biscuits. As per the box, I'm supposed to get long-lasting energy from the 26 grams of whole grain (half of the 48 grams recommended) that is in each 4-biscuit serving. I didn't feel I got the energy specified but the taste wanted me to eat more, more, more.

If all four biscuits are eaten in the one-serving pouch, I get:
230 calories with 80 from fat
2 gram saturated fat
170 mg of sodium
12 gram of sugars

There's 5 grams of protein and 33 grams of carbohydrate and 0 mg cholesterol. There's also 115 mg of potassium.

Ingredients are:
- Whole Grain Oats
- Whole Grain Oat Flour
- Sugar
- Canola Oil
- Whole Grain Barley Flakes
- Honey
- Tapioca Syrup
- Butter
- Dried Blueberries
- Whey Protein Isolate
- Whole Grain Wheat Flakes
- Baking Soda
- Corn Starch
- Salt
- Natural Flavor
- Oil of Rosemary

Although my boyfriend rated these 4 stars, I rated these 3.4 stars based on the following:
- 5 stars for taste
- 4 stars for nutrition
- 3 stars for price (at that current price of $5.99 per box, the cost is $1.19 per tiny pouch)
- 1.5 stars for the texture and its inability to remain in one piece

In summary, these are very tasty and not too bad nutritionally but the biscuits are pre-disposed to turn into dust.

title: A good sesame, nut, and fruit topper for savories like ice cream and perhaps salads
text:
These toppers remind me of sesame with fruit that are cut up into little pieces. I found them tasty on ice cream and okay on a salad. They are also good eaten alone. I believe this will pair well with yogurt also.

The ingredients are almonds, dried blueberries, rice malt, sesame seed, natural flavors, sugar, and sea salt. I received one 4 ounce bag that claims to have 11 servings. I think it's more like 6 servings. The serving size is 10 grams (about 11 pieces) with 50 calories and 35 of those calories are from fat. Saturated fat is 2% and it has 0% cholesterol, 2% each of calcium and iron, and 2 grams of protein. There are only 20mg of Sodium and 1g of fiber and 1g of sugar in each serving.

Overall, I found these to be tasty, chewy, and very heavy on the sesame flavor--which I liked. At the current Amazon price of $20.79 for a pack of 6, each bag is a slightly expensive $3.47 each. These are packaged in California but it appears the ingredients are sourced from China based on the "Product from China" label. The price and China label would stop me from buying these; but taste and ingredients-wise to me these are tasty and nutritious.

title: Gritty, tasteless, and very expensive
text: I purchased three bars of the Taza Stone Ground Chocolate at Home Goods and one bar was 80% dark.  However, after tasting this bar, I'm returning the other two.  The chocolate is gritty (like eating sand) and mostly tasteless to me.  I prefer some taste and a silky feel in my mouth. I just discovered that stone ground chocolate is supposed to be gritty. It is also expensive for such a small bar.  I only paid $5.99 for the 3 oz. bar and Amazon's price of $13.62 is outrageous. Therefore, I would never buy these or other stone ground bars again prticularly because of the gritty feel.

title: I love this better than steel cut oats due to the full-body texture
text:
I've been eating cooked oats all my life and have tried rolled, instant and steel-cut oats (McCann's, Quaker, Silver Palate, Bob's Red Mill, and Country Choice). I fell in love with the McCann's steel-cut oats for the last 12 years. Well, last week I read a book that mentioned groats which I've never heard of before.

Oat groats are the harvested oats in its pre-processed form before they have been steel cut. To me each oat looks like the size of a fat grain of rice. However, they have a nuttier flavor because they are the full kernel and are as good for you nutritionally as steel-cut (if not slightly better) and also lower on the glycemic index since they are unprocessed.

These oats are my new love and once I finish the several pounds of steel-cut still in my possession, I will buy groats instead. The only drawback to groats is that they take longer to cook. I always steam one serving of Oatmeal in my [[ASIN:B00073HLMU Oster 5711 Mechanical Food Steamer, White]]. For the steel-cut oats, I use ¼ cup of oats to ¼ cup of water and steam for 17 minutes. For the groats, I use the same measurements but have to steam them for 22 minutes (5 minutes longer). Since I can set it and forget it in my electric steamer every morning while I'm getting ready for work, the extra 5 minutes do not matter to me. If I were cooking them on the stove in water that would be different because then I would have to attend to them and the cooking time would probably be around 30 minutes. (NOTE - When using this type steamer I remove the drip tray or the oats will take longer to cook. The drip tray is not needed when I am using the rice bowl or another bowl inside the steamer bowl.)

In summary, groats are lower on the glycemic index and have a more robust / nuttier / meaty texture due to the larger size pieces. The only negative is the cooking time. This is definitely my new morning oatmeal and I doubt I'll purchase steel-cut oats again. It's also delicious with ground flax seed, fresh fruit, and skim milk. I gave a bag of these oat groats to the same friend who turned me on to McCann's steel cut oats. He has now become a convert as well and loves the full-body texture.

Don't get me wrong, McCann's, Quaker, and Bob's Red Mill steel-cut oats are delicious and nutty and are five-star products (except for Country Choice) as well; it's just that these groats take the taste and texture further along the taste stratosphere making it a five-star-plus product to me.

title: Lackluster Italian pasta gift basket for an exorbitant price
text:
This gift basket consists of mostly Francis Coppola Mammarella brand products. The "basket" arrived in one big box inside another big box. The gift itself is shrink wrapped in plastic and tied with a very cheap stringy straw-like bow on top.

Included in the gift basket I received was:

- 1 pkg of 3 almond anise biscotti

- 1 12 oz. box of Bumbola Pasta Rustica made of organic ingredients

- 1 12 oz. box of Gemelli Pasta Rustica made of organic ingredients

- 1 25 oz. jar of Tomato Basil Pasta Sauce made of organic ingrediets with first 3 ingredients being organic tomatoes, organic tomato puree, and oganic onions

- 1 recipe card for Farfallone pasta not included in the gift

- 1 "apron towel" 32-1/2 inch wide by 28 inch long with Mammarella name on it. This towel should be used to wrap partially around your waist and be tucked into your pants as you cook.

- 1 5 qt stainless colander made in India. The tag did not say stainless steel only stainless; whatever that means.

PROS:

The pasta sauce tasted good; more like Classico than the cheaper Ragu or Prego. The sauce was good only for one box of pasta, however.

The Gemelli pasta I tried tasted like any store-bought pasta and to me that was good.

The biscotti tasted fine as well, especially dunked in tea.

CONS:

The towel-like apron is made of a thin feed-sack material.

The colandar is very light, very thin and almost felt like tin. In addition, I couldn't get the glue mark from the decal off even after I used the adhesive remover Goof Off. The paper towel I used to wipe on the Goof Off also became stained gray which has not happened before when I've used this remover previously on true stainless steel--which I doubt this is.

The gemelli pasta box I opened filled only half the box, which means the packaging is large to give you the impression you are getting more.

The rest of the gift is all shredded cardboard stuffing.

In summary, at the current price of $44.99 with the cheap colander and apron towel and the lackluster brown boxes of pasta, you could have paid around $27 to make this yourself. For instance, a similar box of the same brand of pasta ([[ASIN:B0031EC0SC Francis Coppola Mammarella Quadrefiore Pasta Rustica]]) is currently listed for $2 on Amazon. A comparable colander is currently listed for $9.99([[ASIN:B00555ETXY ExcelSteel 242 5-Quart Stainless Steel Colander]]). The actual pasta sauce ([[ASIN:B000AQHJFI Francis Coppola Pomodoro-Basilico Sauce]]) is currently listed for $6.49. So if you added all that together plus threw in a few dollars for the cheap towel apron and 3 biscotti, I doubt you would be have paid more than $30. The mark-up is just too high at the current price.

If this gift were priced at $27 or $30 max, then I would consider this an okay buy (3-stars) realizing that at the price you should expect the quality of the non-food items to be cheap, cheap, cheap. The food is fine but are 2 boxes of pasta, 1 jar of sauce and 3 cookies worth this price? In my opinion at the current price, this product isn't even worth considering.

title: These are really tasty jelly sticks
text:
My boyfriend purchased a two-pound bag of these at BJ's and they are very, very good. Do note, however, that the orange slices pictured on the front of the bag implies that these are oranges covered in chocolate which they are not. The packaging is misleading yet very smart marketing for those who don't read the label. My boyfriend thought he was buying chocolate covered oranges.

This is actually an orange flavored jelly stick covered in dark chocolate that is made with real orange juice. It does, however, taste like chocolate covered oranges but without the texture associated with eating oranges. Four pieces are 140 calories with 30 fat calories and 21 grams of sugar. For those with peanut allergies, these are processed in a peanut free plant as per the description on the back of the bag.

This is definitely candy and definitely tasty to both me and my boyfriend.

title: Cheaper than McCannn's but not as flavorful or full-bodied
text:
I've been eating steamed McCann's steel cut oats almost every morning for breakfast for the past six years.

These oats are much cheaper than McCann's steel cut oats but they are not as flavorful, full-bodied, or as nutty as McCann's. The packaging is also very flimsy.

Even though McCann's is double the price of this per ounce, I think I'm going back to them because these oats are not as satisfying to me. In fact I find Quaker Oats steel cut closer to McCann's in taste and body than this one.

title: Delicious almond paste that's just perfect for baking
text: I used this to make pignoli (pine nut) cookies and they came out great.  The paste is very smooth and smells wonderful, just as almond paste should. It was also pretty easy to remove from the can.  I highly recommend this.

title: Very tasty morsels but they are not a health snack in my opinion
text:
I really like the flavor of these Savory Sea Salt Crispy Thins. They definitely have a strong cocoa taste to them. These thins are more like morsels and look like large-size triangular-shaped hard cat food.

Each bag is 6 ounces. There are 6 one-ounce servings per bag at 130 calories (45 from fat) per serving. The first three ingredients are brown rice flour, sunflower seed butter, and sugar which in my opinion makes them closer to dessert than healthy eating. Despite this I really enjoy the instense cocoa flavor with a hint of salt. I find it very hard to stop eating them.

title: Cranberry Walnut goodness that's good with a salad
text:
This is bite-size granola in a package that can be eaten a few at a time as a snack. Each package is 12 oz. and there are ten 1.2 ounce servings in each bag. Each serving is 160 calories with 60 calories from fat. There are 3 grams of protein and 13 grams of sugar in each serving. Each serving also constitutes a daily dietary intake of 12% in fiber and 8% in carbs. The first three ingredients are whole grain rolled oats, brown sugar, and walnuts. Cranberries are 8th on the ingredient list.

I am use to eating my granola with milk so this was a new twist on a what was formally a cerael. I felt these were dry but they tasted good. Drinking a glass of milk took away most of the dryness. There is a high cocoa and cinnamon flavor and the cranberries tasted like raisins to me. I didn't taste the walnuts at all. As a result, I felt the flavor was a bit muted but still good.

Obviously granola is a high calorie snack and it's hard to eat just 1.2 ounces. Nevertheless, I feel it's much better to eat this than candy and cake. After having tried these, this is not a snack I would purchase because of the dryness and the muted flavor. However, I've been eating these for a couple of days and have discovered that putting these clusters in a salad takes away the dryness and gives the salad a nice crunch (like croutons) in addition to adding additional flavor.

I had my boyfriend try these and he likes them.

title: Excellent and tasty preserves
text:
I purchased this a while back from either Home Goods or a speciality store. What made me purchase this jar of preserves is that the contents were not liquidity like some are. I also love cherries. Besides being full-bodied, these Michigan Black Cherry Preserves are delicious and fresh tasting. They also spread easily as I was using this for a peanut butter and jelly sandwich.

What is even more impressive is that I got a call from one of the owners of the company when I wrote them about having trouble opening the jar. This fellow Dan (two days before Christmas) called me back within 30 minutes of my sending the email. The label on the jars from Home Goods (where he thinks I purchased this) don't have the "how to open" instructions on them so I had spent about 15 minutes trying to get the jar open but couldn't. After he directed me to the FAQ on the website and gave me an idea over the phone of how this opened, I was able to reassemble the wires and opened it within seconds. It's a French canning jar that opens differently than what we are use to here in America.

So not only did I get a great jar of preserves but it came with OUTSTANDING customer service!

title: There is definitely healthy vegan food in this package
text:
This gift box is large but only contains 5 items besides some recipe cards. These 5 items are definitely healthy and vegan-based.

In this gift box I received:
12 oz truRoots Organic Quinoa
10 oz truRoots Organic Sprouted Mung Beans (which I can't wait to try)
10 oz truRoots Organic Sprouted Green Lentils
6 oz Bragg Liquid Aminos which is a natural soy sauce alternative (this I'm not too thrilled to try)
1.5 oz Bragg Organic 24 Herbs & Spices Sprinkle Seasoning

There's also a pamphlet on getting started (toward healthy eating), one card describing the products, and 4 recipe cards using the ingredients in the package. I am disappointed that nowhere in any of the instructions (recipe card, package directions, etc.) did it say that quinoa should be washed before cooking. I have a lot of quinoa at home and you always have to wash it first to remove the saponins (soap like film).

I would have rated this package 5 stars because the idea, recipe cards and three grains/beans enclosed are great. However, there are two main reasons that caused me to rate this 4 stars. First, the products supplied can be purchased separately for about $23 retail--of course without the recipe cards which is a nice touch. Second, the box it comes in is too big. I think Whole Foods wants to give the impression that you are getting a lot for the price but it's really a waste of cardboard. If this package were priced between $30 and $32 and came in a smaller box more suitable to the items enclosed, I would have rated this five stars. As of this review, the price on Amazon is $40.

This is certainly an interesting twist on a gift although the recipient might not think you spent so much.

title: This tea's flavor is not what I expected
text:
This China Oolong tea tastes EXACTLY like what you are served for free in almost every Chinese Restaurant. That's not a bad thing because the taste of such a tea is decent. However, I did not expect that it would taste like this since the product description on the site says "... beautiful and full bodied with a fragrant flavor and fruity, sweet aroma." This tea is definitely full-bodied but it is not fruity nor sweet; instead it has a very faint smoky aftertaste similar to Lapsang Souchong tea but definitely not as strong.

On the plus side, when steeped the leaves unfurl beautifully. It is only because the taste was not what I expected that I give it three stars. I only like the tea in Chinese Restaurants; not love it. Although insignificanat with regard to the product; the description on the tin says "TIKUANYIN China Oolong Tea" not "Tie Guan Yin......" Apparently Amazon got more than the flavor wrong.

title: Unfiltered Olive Oil at its best
text: I've tried many versions of unfiltered olive oil and love this one the best. Because unfiltered oil has a premium taste and cost more than regular olive all, I don't cook with it.  Instead I use it after the food is prepared.  It has a very rich and earthy flavor that is perfect for seasoning bread, pasta, salad, etc.  On rare occasions you can find this in Home Goods for the same price (without the shipping, of course).

title: Not bad but not great either
text: This tea has acceptable flavor and tastes a step below Twinings Earl Grey in teabag form.  There is a faint taste of bergamot but it's not strong enough to make this beverage outstanding.  However, this tea is much better than [[ASIN:B002WE2LOU Twinings Green Tea, 8-Count T-Discs for Tassimo Coffeemakers (Pack of 3)]].

title: Excellent Coffee
text: This is excellent medium blend coffee for the [[ASIN:B00451B9FK Tassimo by Bosch T20 Single Serve Coffee Brewer, White]] or any Tassimo that takes T DISCs.  My boyfriend also tried this coffee and was skeptical of Maxwell House (which he ordinarily doesn't like) until he tried it.  He also thought these T DISCs make a great cup of coffee.

title: Good hot chocolate
text: To me, nothing beats homemade hot chocolate created with milk, a molinillo (for example [[ASIN:B001AVREHK Vasconia Molinillo]]) and [[ASIN:B001U5GWWY Callebaut Extra Bitter (70%) - 11lb Block [Item: 7030]]] chocolate in a clay pot on the stove.  However, this hot chocolate is very good compared to the powdered packaged hot chocolates I mixed with water in the past.  A 16 count package really only creates eight 6oz hot chocolates because there are two DISCs; one for the milk and the other for the syrup. My only complaint is that the 6 oz. serving size is too small for the price.

title: Delicious but beware of the calories reported on Amazon
text:
This granola is very tasty as is another variety of Back to Nature Granola I tried - [[ASIN:B001OCKIP0 Back To Nature Chocolate Delight Granola, 12-Ounce Pouches (Pack of 6)]].

The granola is crunchy and has a good balance of sweetness. It is absolutely delicious with milk. However, even though the bag says there are seven servings; there really are only three. The nutrition informaton on Amazon is wrong - you really need to double the calories. I almost rated this product 4 stars for misleading the consumer. Even though Amazon has a disclaimer on their site, the nutrition label (calories) is incorrect for every Back to Nature package when you compare it to the one you receive.

title: An acquired taste
text:
Originally I hated this cereal becaused it tasted so bland and powdery when hot or cold. However, after eating it three times, I have strangely learned to love it and now I've been eating it for one month.

So this was an acquired taste for me that I will definitely order again when I get tired of my steel cut oatmeal. If you are not use to eating healthy foods, however, you might dislike this cereal and never acquire a taste for it.

title: Delicious
text: Delicious and priced well on Amazon.  Also, package says it contains no nuts for those who are allergic.  I love these rice cakes for a quick snack.

title: Look no further for dutch processed cocoa
text: Callebaut is superb dutch processed cocoa for both baking and ice cream.  The taste surpasses other cocoa powder I've tried before.  If you want a great tasting dessert that uses dutch processed cocoa, use this cocoa powder. It is not much more expensive that the store bought variety when you break down the cost per pound.

title: Good Winter Fruit for Oatmeal
text: These dried cherries are great when no fresh fruit is available in the winter.  I use them in my oatmeal with several other dried fruit.

title: Good Winter Fruit for Oatmeal
text: These dried cranberries are great when no fresh fruit is available in the winter.  I use them in my oatmeal with several other dried fruit.

title: Great product!
text: I love the rich flavor and crispness of this cookie my favorite!

title: Great taste !
text: The tastiest caramel ever ! Really gives the sea salt I love them.

title: Homemade old fashion caramels
text: Delicious! They are chewy and taste like old fashioned homemade caramels.

title: Soft , chewy, but not enough sea salt
text: Way to sweet for me, and not much taste of the sea salt, probably won't purchase again, I gifted out the 2nd container I had purchased.

title: Very addictive
text: I love this caramel. It is very soft and flavorful. It is as smooth as butter.  The packaging says you get about 27 pieces but I counted out 32 depending on the size of each individual caramel. I would highly recommend this product to anyone that likes caramels. Be aware though very addictive.

title: OMG this is awesome sauce!
text: Doesn't matter what I'm cooking. Everything that I normally use soy sauce in I have tried Ponzu. Even the Broccoli Beef I made last week was unbelievable. Egg rolls are amazing. I want to buy this by the gallon like I do with Kikkoman's soy sauce. Kikkoman hit it out of the park!  Simply yum

title: Happy belly?  LOL
text: What a fricken awful name for a product. It's better than Kemps but not much better. For cheap milk Walmart tastes about the best

title: Love the pickle juice
text:
I love big pickles and these taste pretty good in fact better than most. What I don't like is mushy pickles with a inner gap and you might get lucky or you might not. Another thing I look for is like no pickles packed all the way to the bottom of the jar. Feel like I'm getting screwed. So I take my chances buying these and throw out the soft mushy ones. The juice is damn tasty.
If you didn't know, Kosher means there is garlic in there

title: I fricken love this stuff when I don't feel good
text: One packet and 3 cups of water. 9 minutes later I'm very happy

title: Not the best SOS but scratched that itch I had for chipped beef on toast
text: It was ok, I  have had better but it tasted good enough for me

title: Tara makes the best!
text: You need to buy these for gift giving, your wife, lover, mother or mother-in-law will love you for this gift. Cheap and very yummy

title: What is not to love about these Truffles
text: OMG do I love these and when I buy them for a gift the person getting them loves them also. If you're reading this review you already know that they are a slice of heaven

title: If you're planning on making Black Bean soup for the first time, try these
text:
A few things I like about these black beans. One, no soaking overnight and two, the flavor.
How to make black bean soup?
1 ham picnic bone
1 gallon of spring water purified ok
1 yellow onion chopped
Simmer your ham bone for 2 hours then remove and cut the rest of the meat off. Put your meat back in the broth and put 4 cans of Bush's black beans
1 can of diced tomatos
simmer 1 more hour
Add 2 tablespoons of cumin
1 tablespoon kosher salt
2 tablespoons of black pepper
For spicy add 1 tablespoon of cayenne pepper or 3 or 4 pickled whole but split jalapenos

title: ! Contains forever chemicals PFOAS
text: Consumer Reports found that Bubly seltzers have higher than recommended levels of PFOAs ("forever chemicals"). We were buying Bubly because it was a good price and a little tastier than Polar. Not anymore. Max of 1 ppt is considered health-protective and Bubly has over 2 ppt per can. This is not a common thing; most bottled waters are well within limits. You vote with your dollar. Bubly, do better! Signed, an ex-customer and concerned consumer

title: Arrived opened, be careful
text:
I am adding this review as a warning to other buyers. This jar arrived opened for me and I wouldn't have been able to tell except for the jam on the inside of the bubble wrap it was wrapped in. The lid appeared like it was on, and lifting the jar from the lid didn't make the jar fall, but the jam clearly had leaked out because the lid was not on tight enough. I am pretty sure opened jam left unrefrigerated for days can harbor bacteria, so, word of caution, check the lid.

Update: After buying this exact same jam from another retailer, I found I really disliked the taste. It was bland and odd.

title: Delicious!

The head chef (my spouse) was surprised by the watery consistency that is produced by following the directions on the bag, but we learned in the second batch that the trick is it let it sit for several minutes to let it thicken up before using it.

title: Yummy, healthy crackers
text: These taste just like Triscuits, even though they are organic. The Whole Foods' 365 brand of organic wheat crackers were absolutely disgusting, flavorless and tasteless. I hope to continue to buy the organic Triscuits through Amazon -- or I hope that Whole Foods starts carrying organic Triscuits! The only disappointment I had was that the 6 pack I received expires less than 6 months after I placed the order. We will probably go through 6 boxes in this amount of time, and I know crackers are fine a while after the best by date; it is just disappointing that a 6-pack would not arrive with better dates.

title: A flavorful cup of coffee from a company that thinks… BiGGeR!!
text:
Kudos for this small business! Nothing is more impressive than those wanting to make a difference with their company and combined quality products with global outreach... this blends both! Nicaraguan gourmet coffee comes in brightly decorative matchbox style dispenser box that is like art itself. It can sit atop countertop/coffee bar with colorful flare from its origin. Included is “postcard” sharing geographical location on a map and an informative snippet about the country and its geography.

Choosing pod for the kurig 1.0- we had a quality fit and pod puncture without issue or tearing. Efforts toward sustainability from packaging to pod are impressive and yet, don’t lack quality. I’ve included photos of inserts you receive including companies QR code and 50% offer as well.

As one who prefers coffee light/sweet this has a stronger presence but, not overbearing. With 20% more grounds per pod it aids to richness of a cup which some may feel has a bitter bite! I found strength but no bitterness.. keep in mind, I’m drinking it light/sweet. (Now, my coffee connoisseur hubby who prefers coffee BLACK- detected smooth and rich with its own notable and distinguished flavor that he favored. One he labeled a solid morning option for a light/med roast.. let’s just say- he is far more particular and in-tune than myself!)

High quality at a higher price- it is one that has merit and I do admire bc, that reaches all the way to the farmers. I respect this enough to be interested in trying future products! Would make a great spoiler or gift option without hesitation. Hoping info. helps decision- stay safe everyone.

title: A splash of zest.. compliments many dishes!!
text:
Very universal flavor that works with a variety of foods. Nothing obnoxious or too spicy- its signature is a sweet and salty flare (sweet onion or pepper?!) as one who has Weber’s “Kickin chicken“ in the spice repertoire.. I find this similar in flavor- with a little more sweet! Added to various dishes this brings a refreshing zest!

-Used while grilling chicken breast- I basted with a thin layer of butter, sprinkle of Newmans seasoning and finished with fresh squeezed lime. This was fantastic.. but, careful- a lot saltier then expected!! Prior to adding table salt I suggest taste test first.

-used with mixed veggies in air fryer: green, red and orange pepper, onion and mushroom- spritzed with virgin oil and sprinkled both sides.

-baked zucchini in oven- with just a spritz of oil I sprinkled with season and fresh grated parmesan.. this was my favorite!!

-Used on outside of potato skin I jacketed with an oil and seasoning- then added butter/sour cream.

-Made beef patties on grill and used seasoning to flavor.. tbh, this was my least favorite- I didn’t care for it with beef. At least not hamburger.

I included QR code in photo above.. scanning provides a plethora of info about product and company.. Such a classic move- all $ from sale of product goes to charity… that is impressive.

title: Cold packing is a must in summer temp.’s..!!!
text:
Being a returning customer with a dzn previous purchases over the past.. I’ve loved these sea salt Carmel’s as a fun spoiler- they’ve never been anything but great and OMG! Such fun packaging and presentation.. very ideal to keep fresh. I’ve even sent x3-4 of these as gifts and not done a single extra anything.. Bc, the arrival in plastic tumbler with jar lid is perfect! I’ve had nothing but kudos/praise from recipients- so, I’ve been happy as can be!

I love the fact these are respectfully priced for the quality received… and trust me there are lot of similar choices out there- but I’ve stuck loyal to one that hit the mark.. why not!

What I didn’t realize- was all my previous orders had been for things like Xmas, valentines, spring bday, Easter.. even Mother’s Day treats.. but not an item ordered during August before! So, it pains me to say.. after all the hype I’ve had for this company- I cant believe they haven’t taken into consideration a need to mail any climate sensitive product appropriately- something controlled during the summer and 100° weather. Certainly I’m not the first one to order during 100° weather.. and this item isn’t even the most vulnerable treat offered. What about chocolates, fudge, etc??

TBH, I’m not sure where the misstep happened- with the company or shipping via Amazon? Only when you spend a handful of dollars during current inflation on something unnecessary except a sweet ”pick me up” after a rough week…you can imagine the let down of disappointment! I received a lidded tumbler full of melted goo with tiny wax papers wrappers mixed throughout!!! I’m just grateful I ordered for myself and not sent directly as a gift to a friend as I have in the past- bc basically this was a bust.. not to mention a fat mess! Not even capable of putting in fridge to bring content to a solid shape- x2-3 pieces that somehow stayed in the wraps- changed consistency.. completely sticky after overnight fridge stay and Grainy separation amongst webbing of sticky caramel.. Who knew!

Seller take note- A solution is needed.. otherwise buyer beware.. These are a great little treat if they can arrive without melting. A cool pack or some other scenario- maybe only available for hand delivery in your local area..?? I realize it might increase the price -maybe an add-on for a few dollars as an option to deliver with necessary precautions..??

until a solution is made… I will not order during hot summer months! Sad but true. Hoping info can help make your decision- stay safe everyone.

title: Perfect for on the go use..!!!
text:
PURE KICK hydration drink is ideal for on the go use- The ease and ability to pack/transport is so helpful! With almost zero footprint you can add to a backpack, travel bag, sports gear etc. No worry about needing excess space or carrying bulky weight like that of premade drinks! Hydration packs are perfect for air travel as well.. as they meet TSA carry-on standards. Easy to store a few extra in your gear and have when needed.. Just provide the water bottle and packet will do the rest..!!

My daughter is a varsity collegiate athlete who travels x2 weekends a month during spring fall season- she loves the take/travel capability of hydration pre-measured pouches.. 72pks at $16 is a smokiń deal.. enough for her season or share with team- these our convenience at its finest! **Hack: Pack pouches in empty water bottle to help travel safely.. then, you have when ready to mix/fill.

As for flavor: Citrus = lemonade! This has a zing which helps your mouth water.. a welcomed effect when dehydrated. Drink is pre-sweetened to help eliminate any bitter aftertaste.. Flavors seems reminiscent of a diet drink. Simply put, being a concoction of nutrients/minerals to replenish dehydration.. this adds flavor to help make that a pleasant experience! these taste best served with ice.. prior to doing so- add h2o + packet till dissolved completely- then, add ice afterwards! Hoping this info can help your decision- stay safe everyone.

title: Someone needs to get it right- send white not blue gel!!
text:
So upset!! I did the research prior to ordering and was told this was white solid only when it arrived it is that nasty blue gel!!! Doesn’t make any sense and I’m dumbfounded how this cannot be clearly marked and followed..?? FYI, this is happened to us a handful of times with the same old spice deodorant- and we so strongly dislike the gel… so, major disappointment!

Whoever is missing this technicality when shipping, we should really try to step the game and pay attention to the details. How obnoxious that I’m either stuck with returning and going through that headache… Or losing out on my money? Sort of a lose lose situation for the buyer.

As for the product itself… Obviously, it works and smells great if you get what you want. We love both the smell and the performance… However, only when it’s correct can we experience this!!

title: Loved “different” without being too radical.. so many options!!
text:
Wow! For anyone looking for a healthy alternative to what you find in most spreads and add-ons.. this is it!! Don’t be fooled by the category- for some reason we were under the impression this would be a lot like a Biscoff or Nutella spread in taste… We were wrong! My advice, don’t have an expectation or, comparison as to what you “think” this is like- and just embrace it as its own unique taste of hazelnut spread!! TBH, I’ve only experienced “hazelnut” in a coffee flavor so, I found it nice being different (nothing to radical!) from all the others.

I received mine in a commercial size tub and appreciate it is stamped clearly with expiration (1yr from purchase date) *Instructions also state NOT to refrigerate. Tub has a small handle but, I highly suggest carrying by the bottom as well- handle doesn’t feel confidently secure and I can’t imagine having to clean this up if it spilled.. ugh!! (see photos for container and details!)

Before using- make certain to stir (fold!) in mixture completely and do so reaching the bottom- Otherwise, this may taste out of balance (almost waxy + oily in the mouth!) Note: this does have thinner consistency then one would expect from its cousin- peanut butter.. (see photo of actual content) this is more like a thicker syrup- and may take a bit of trial/error to use as a spread.. I suggest using spoon vs. knife! With all the varied possibilities to use this.. i’m sure you’ll figure what’s best in no time! **we used with vanilla ice cream to make milkshakes- wowza!! I highly suggest you try.. ha ha! My husband drizzled atop a single scoop and loved it that way as well..

I’ve done my homework! Coming from a company who seems so passionate about their product, inspired us to try- for the price point and amount plus, shelf life.. it’s a solid deal. Love that I can sneak extra protein into my kiddos diet! Overall, a fun alternative to the usuals and better yet.. one that is healthy. Hoping this info can help in your decision- stay safe!

title: Sky is the limit with this- and I included a few easy uses!!
text:
Sky is the limit with this one..!!! Margaritas you bet! I did love it kicked in some sweet with the salt (may be a hint of Orange zest?) it gives a fun, safe way to branch out and try new things- always a plus for picky kids!!

But- I feel need to share our fav.’s in different genres:

Favorite drink add-on was to a Bloody Mary.. served with a fresh spear of veggies!! Just a dash over the glass added more flavor to the spicy tomato.. and it also grabbed onto the fun veggie ensemble that was accompanying the drink.. it was sublime!! A great way to eat veggies after with the extra flavor!

For any with a palate for eating jacketed potatoes -this is Bomb!! Fully clean baking potato.. add a rub of olive oil.. make a cross incision 1/4” deep on top.. roll potato in spices- place directly into 400° oven on baking rack for 2hr (not foiled.. I put cooking tray below to catch any drip!) Outside skin will be crisp and bursting with flavor.. inside with be lite/fluffy!!!! Of course, you must add butter into your cut before eating.. duh!!

As for kid friendly: We did use on chicken strips as well- we just brushed the uncooked tenders with lite layer of Mayo and rolled into spices in a bowl along with some Panko and crushed butter cracker.. baked on foil tray at 400° 10min per side- they were amazing!! I mentioned this as a kid friendly idea as spices were not too hot!

Overall- this is a really fun spice for numerous uses.. they weren’t lying when they said- it can enhance just about anything! For those who love the trio of sweet, salt and spice- this is definitely one I would recommend trying. At a price point of $20 for two bottles- you were paying for quality and versatility.. both of which we appreciated! I hope this info can help make your decision- Bon appétit!

title: The review shares and says it all.. step by step!!
text:
As a family who loves pickles, I thought this would be a great find… And the actual size of bag is perfect for sack lunch or, on the go snack- However, portions per serving feels more conducive to that a younger adolescent.. at least in individual size bags.

I included a photo series taking you through the process start to finish… these are larger than Frito snack packs but, smaller than grab sack at convenient store- see size comparison next to coke can.

Everyone expects it and I feel we are all over the shock of that excess air packed inside food bags- but, this one is about 1/2 full (ha! At least I wasn’t pessimistic to say half empty ha ha!).. Only, if you notice content poured out on counter- only a few chips survived to big boy sz. with all that air! Most are a sum of their former “chip self!” Not that this is a big deal and could happen in transfer but, in case it matters to some?!

As for the taste and texture… I am perplexed!!? If you notice color disparity in photos- some are soaking up something? Clearly some pieces appear much darker then, the lighter portions. They didn’t taste greasy while eating… So, maybe it’s something to do with dill flavoring? But, issue with that is- these really seemed to be lack real dill flavor!! (So, I think that blows my theory of what is soaked into chip?) For food labeled with “delicious dill” flavor- it’s fair to say, this was hard to detect. What I could taste was a hint of vinegar and albeit subtle, it was there.. this has a dill after taste maybe? But, having nothing to compare it too, besides an actual pickle- I would have to say these fall short in flavor dept. TBH, I’m sad to report this bc, this is a great flavor idea for sure.. The other ingredient we unanimously voted missing while tasting.. was salt! And I’m not a big salt fan!! So, that should tell you something- I feel original lays potato chips are too salty for my liking.. and yet, I felt delicious dill pickle chips needed some flavoring of something but, salt for sure-

Overall, this appears as a quality product- it boasts a nice and professional… Even inviting label that makes you want to indulge. Problem is, it falls short of expectations. If, flavors were corrected or became more savory- I believe this would have different and more positive results. I gave this x1 star for overall look and x1 star for individual taste palettes- bc, someone else may find this more enjoyable.. and I need to recognize that. I hope this info can help in your decision… Feel free to let me know!

title: A Cinnamon bear that can hold its own!!
text:
Delish!! For any who like a taste of cinnamon that’s not too hot but, definitely delivers on taste… I would recommend! Surprisingly, these came relatively fresh and were reasonably priced… Of course, I wish there were a few more per pack for $ but, in the long run it’s probably a good thing there wasn’t!
These have a slightly firmer exterior one expects and a softer center.. i’d like the spark of cinnamon- just when you think it may get too hot… It tables off. Not sure how it does it but, it’s the perfect amount of “tasty spark!” (can’t figure out why that’s the word that comes to mind but it’s true!!?)

Arrived on time as I expected. These would be the perfect amount for snacking during a movie and even big enough to share with a date- Ha! Only if, one so desires! Otherwise, you can snack on just a few for a good handful of days- and this is my personal fav. way to eat them..snag x1-2 and I’m good to go! I received and transferred Immediately to a sealed Ziploc bag just to recapture and then, keep the freshness and lock it in..*** I would recommend you all do the same as, the container these arrive in- does not seal completely. I will say after 24 hours in a sealed Ziploc, these became softer Then, they did on arrival..

Overall, for anyone who loves a cinnamon bear… This is a good contender. Hope this info helps- Feel free to let me know!

title: This is COFFEE... with a subtle hint of flavor!
text:
This is a coffee for those who love coffee.. And not necessarily those who want to become the next Juan Valdez!! If you grind your own beans and are a complete connoisseur- then, you may not appreciate the whimsical fun found in this mix! I am in no way a coffee aficionado... but, with some of these flavors and fun ideas (found with a quick Google search...) I may just play barista in my own kitchen.. ha!!

I would best described this as a solid coffee base- of a light to med. roast (it surpasses grocery store brands but, may not be as rich as some premium brands either!) Expect each specified cup having a different popular donut flavor, that adds a note to your regular cup of coffee. NO- these are not to be confused with their cousin the, “pre-sweetened cappuccino flavors..” these have hints of the flavor specified- and mostly by the aroma and subtle aftertaste. I didn’t find any of them off putting as, the flavors are instilled subtle enough to add slight touches of variation to a “good” solid cup of coffee. This is NoT for example: the chocolate flavor- the equivalent to a cup of cocoa.. Nope! Expect a cup of coffee without any sweetened taste.. that instead provides hints of specific flavor. If, you’re for that sweet and altered flavor- you should be looking into presweetened flavors of cappuccino..
(Think baking chocolate vs. Hershey candy bar..)

**To sum up the flavor- Imagine drinking your morning coffee while eating/dunking your flavored doughnut!! Probably the best way to describe what you’ll be receiving.. (minus the donut- sorry peeps!)
If you’re wanting to meet this sweetened you must add cream and sugar!
The integrity of the K-cup is there.. I had no issue using in my first generation Keurig. Not all cups are created equal.. and this pierces easily without creating an excess in the hole/piercing made to into the cup itself.. (some cup materials are made cheaper allowing unwanted coffee grounds to get through- not these!!)

I love how these come mixed and easy enough to pull out a diff. variation of choice… I included photo showing you, sampling is easy and fun! This is a great mix for any who love Coffee and donuts-

I hope this info helps… Feel free to let me know!

title: Prepping! 10 pouches so light weight- meals/calories for x20 days!!
text:
Order the x1 pouch.. I am on the bug out bag phase- i’ve had a go bag ready but, food included has been fruit roll, jerky, granola bars- Being where the world has taken us with chaotic turns and the coronavirus in full force…(plus, I live in Utah and just today we had a 5.9 earthquake that shook my soul.. and everything around me!! Another reason if I didn’t have enough..) The need to be ready seems evermore knocking on the door.. As a mom of x4 kids.. I know they will turned to me to be ready in a crisis- I won’t let them down, I refuse. Hence, trial/error of different foods to include to make our bag more substantial.. I figure, if I’m stressing for no reason then, will have a camping bag ready for a week of fun- win/win!!

Details:
Comes in a nice bag that feels confidently sealed- it also is a 2 pack and its premeasured that way- making this nice for two people or.. a couple meals for one.
So, I made one pack and tried it- WoW!! Why does survival have to taste so blah?!! tbh, it’s hard to get your mind around when your standing in a kitchen full of food.. it tends to make this less than adequate or, anything I would “want to/crave“ to eat. Now, I am certain without choices.. This would be fantastic and life’s breath!

Instructions are very straight forward and done by adding the hot water.. basically to prepare you need water and a match- (or, just a way to heat the h2o.) I ordered the granola and it came pre-packed with freeze dried blueberry and powdered milk.. (wouldn’t have powdered milk unless, it was only choice- but, this is the mindset I must tweak) So, I am grateful for quickly trying a few items to figure this out-

The caloric intake is great! The need to sustain ones health/energy in a crisis, is the difference between life and death!!! For such a light pack of granola does pack a caloric punch! Blueberries also play a part in the vital nutrients to a survival meal- even though they taste like “candy blueberry” nutritional value is still there.. best part of berries- they can be swallowed without chewing!

The weight of the pouch is nothing in therefor x10 of these would be x20 meals- A little pricey but, worth it for the amount of meals vs. almost no weight or, space taken up... these aren’t cumbersome in any way! I prefer the pre-measured for ease and knowing the calories/nutrients each person would get..
May not be the best cuisine I’ve ever eaten- But, as a mom who’s planning ahead “just in case..” this would be doable for my family w/o issues! In that regard- I can recommend.

I hope this info helps… feel free to let me know!

title: As great as I remember.. but, enhanced probiotic powers!!
text:
Just as I remember from childhood! Growing up this was the equivalent of grabbing a lifeline on the way out the door to high school.. Breakfast wasn’t my strong point and this was such an awesome and good for you energy boost- Wow! i just thought was it was chocolate milk!!
What a total throwback to my epic, always running mach 2 with my hair on fire- late to class with my trusted Tupperware Mixer cup..(um, the hydro flask of the 80’s people!!) Fast forward 30yrs.. and some things never change!! I rarely get breakfast and realized, I could use that same Nutritional boost.. it could help me over the hump to start the day-

In the past, I used Essential- and LOVED Chocolate flavor. BTW- this comes in a few flavors but, hands-down chocolate is best! Being a grown-up now, the probiotics seem to be a bit more fitting… And I can tell you, it holds the same awesome taste- but, with a better nutritional fit..?! What I loved is that it tastes just the same!!
I love pre-packaged measurements… These are great on the go. Love to send them in daughter’s soccer bag when she travels and she makes them when she needs a boost- she gets milk and mixes, and it’s that easy- even on the road!! When ordering this in bulk it is hands-down a better deal then, protein shakes or, the likes.. and if buying in a large tub, the perk is in the ability to make any size glass you want and not just the standard 8-12oz size. I will say.. less is more with the powder and a little more milk then it calls for- doesn’t make it sit so “heavy” in the stomach.. just try a little less/more and you’ll find the flavor you’re after!

Trust me, if you have kids that like chocolate milk… This is a great substitute- I did it with mine when they were little as well (just never thought to use it myself after high school..) not until the last few months of feeling wiped out. Now, with that damn coronavirus knocking on the door step in every city, it’s more than vital.. we boost our immune, stay healthy, energized and ready to put up a good fight to keep it away!! TBH, I’ve got my entire family having one healthy glass to start the day- it may not help save the day… But, I promise- it will not hurt!! As for the health benefits- hands-down this is more than just chocolate milk and yet, it can be enjoyed as such!!!
Hope this info helps- feel free to let me know!

title: Made with simple ease and a total crowd favorite!!
text:
Ordered this chili packet and made it for dinner. perfect night with temps at 16° for a high today.. We made chili and baked potatoes and grabbed fresh french bread.. it’s too easy and straight forward. If, one’s ever made McCormick chili pckt. before… This is equal in comparison in every way, and even calls for same ingredients added! 1lb. meat, x1 can bean(pinto/kidney), x1 tomato sauce.. Boom! The 3 items + flvr. packet and follow simple directions.. and within 15min. you’ll have a pot of chili simmering-
Compared to other chili packets ive used, I did not notice a flavor difference.. maybe a tweak on a sweeter spice? But, I was the only one to notice this. Definitely made with ease and a total crowd favorite. If your kids eat chili… This is kid friendly!

title: Great 30pk of individually wrapped cinnamon snacks!!!
text:
Great snack concept and “can be“ the healthier choice, in the right context!! If looking for an alternative to cookies/chips/candy… This has that possibility! I was in need of a snack to share with a collegiate soccer team on a long bus ride to/from a weekend of competitive gaming. As long as no one has nut allergy, (of course)- this is a great way to utilize and take advantage of the Individually wrapped and easy to disperse packs of protein. Yes, these have sugar/aspartame I’m well aware but, for the need of quick burst of protein.. these worked!

TBH- Im one who tries to strike balance and common sense with healthy. So.. this review may not be for you if hearing that makes you want to rip your hair out.. I was raised by Stanford grad. parents who’s then, med. school degree led us to be healthy and well cared for children- their ideals, I’ve used raising my own x4 kiddos- which is: find balance between health/happiness never letting one-rob you of the other..!! (Hmm.. Food for thought! Don’t let health fanatic suck all the fun out of life and don’t let happiness take your health away- Seek balance!) I am submitting this review from that POV-

For the few concerns seen with added flavoring and odd ingredient- tbh, we had no complaints! I was a bit confused- *Are the ingredients listed just for the specific/flavor/package received? Or, was it a list of combined ingredients that may be found in all 3 different flavor options?? A bit confusing and IF, seller made this clear- I’m sure it would prove beneficial to both, buyer/seller.

Our use was a little different in the fact, 24 players shared the wealth of this bag and not just one individual person.. if, there have been issues with taste/flavor, my group had none?! In fact, I heard chatter about this snack bringing on memories of toasted almonds from Disney?? (but, personally I’ve never tried those to compare..?) I heard positive likes for the texture of each almond- a crisper cinnamon shell with us softer inner almond.. this was personally, my favorite part!! But, at end of bus ride, I had 24 empty wrappers and 2 girls ask for an extra- if that helps?!

I find it hard to get individually wrapped snacks that aren't the 3 standards: jerky/granola bar/fruit gummy-leather and when you need to fill a protein boost that must stay fresh at room temp.. it can get limiting. (trail mix in past= highly $ and hard to disperse- shelled sunflower seeds cause hydration issues w/ amount of salt.) So, this was a far better choice for our need, it exceeded expectations and with majority of votes thinking these rocked!!? (this feedback is from the palettes of a hungry 20yr. old athletes- craving body fuel!) the bottom line- these almonds seemed to deliver.

I loved the pre-packed size (portion control)- and when served with a piece of fruit and water bottle, this was a welcomed little snack, in just the right amount. I was extremely pleased to find 30 individual packets per bag! I needed 24pk- this covered our group with 6 extra. The cinnamon flavor- mixed that sweet/salty combo people seem to crave these days. It was on point striking the balance of both sets of taste buds and I’ve found you’re really hard pressed to find a person that doesn’t like cinnamon!
Note: These are a great classroom size snack bag… Sold in that hard to find # count of 30, yay!! Seems as if there’s a conspiracy where snacks are always prepackaged in the “off” amount, creating a need to buy 2.. it drives me crazy!! Sure, nut allergies or something to deal with in classrooms- but, kudos to the 30 pack!!
I would purchase in future if need arrives and not hesitate to do so. These are a fun and very cleverly wrapped individual package of a quality portion that hit the mark for us- we be happy nuts!

title: Old school Toblerone in mini size- and 3 flavors!!!
text:
So great! Just as I remember as a kid- and the total bonus is, these are mini/snack size! Perfect for curing that little chocolate itch..!! Another great feature… You get three of the neatest triangle shaped boxes, (imagine the original Toblerone box and now, think of it as a fat/large container..) these triangle boxes are fantastic- They have perforated tab to open the flip top… And you can leave these mini candy bars neatly stored inside the box. The amount per box is about 20-25 in each.

These come in 3 flavors and each box is a mix- milk chocolate, dark chocolate and white chocolate. Yes! They are identical to their original recipe… And you have the tiny crumble of toffee inside. I ordered these for the holidays as a bit of nostalgia for all to sample and try- personally, I used to get these as a little girl and now, my family is hooked!!
Being these are a chocolate from Germany- they always had a tad higher price point (even in the individual style of old) but, for the amount and variety this comes with… This is priced decent. This chocolate brand definitely does not disappoint and The bits of toffee… The perfect added bonus!
